pli-tv-pvr1.1:1.1
Pali: Namo tassa Bhagavato Arahato Sammāsambuddhassa. 
English (brahmali, published): Homage to the Buddha, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:2.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena paṭhamaṁ pārājikaṁ kattha paññattaṁ, kaṁ ārabbha, kismiṁ vatthusmiṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): “The first offense entailing expulsion was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down? Whom is it about? What is it about? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:2.2
Pali: Atthi tattha paññatti, anupaññatti, anuppannapaññatti? 
English (brahmali, published): Is there a rule, an addition to the rule, an unprompted rule? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:2.3
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti, padesapaññatti? 
English (brahmali, published): Is it a rule that applies everywhere or in a particular place? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:2.4
Pali: Sādhāraṇapaññatti, asādhāraṇapaññatti? 
English (brahmali, published): Is it a rule that the monks and nuns have in common or not in common? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:2.5
Pali: Ekatopaññatti, ubhatopaññatti? 
English (brahmali, published): Is it a rule for one Sangha or for both? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:2.6
Pali: Pañcannaṁ pātimokkhuddesānaṁ katthogadhaṁ kattha pariyāpannaṁ?

English (brahmali, published): In which recitation is it included?

Pali: Catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ katamā vipatti? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four kinds of failure does it belong? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:2.9
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katamo āpattikkhandho? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the seven classes of offenses does it belong? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:2.10
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti?

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena paṭhamaṁ pārājikaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The first offense entailing expulsion was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.2
Pali: Vesāliyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Vesālī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.4
Pali: Sudinnaṁ kalandaputtaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Sudinna the Kalandian. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.6
Pali: Sudinno kalandaputto purāṇadutiyikāya methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Sudinna having sexual intercourse with his ex-wife. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.7
Pali: Atthi tattha paññatti, anupaññatti, anuppannapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there a rule, an addition to the rule, an unprompted rule?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.8
Pali: Ekā paññatti, dve anupaññattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There are two additions to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.9
Pali: Anuppannapaññatti tasmiṁ natthi. 
English (brahmali, published): There is no unprompted rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.10
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti, padesapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that applies everywhere or in a particular place?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.11
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Everywhere. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.12
Pali: Sādhāraṇapaññatti, asādhāraṇapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that the monks and nuns have in common or not in common?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.13
Pali: Sādhāraṇapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): In common. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.14
Pali: Ekatopaññatti, ubhatopaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule for one Sangha or for both?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.15
Pali: Ubhatopaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): For both. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.16
Pali: Pañcannaṁ pātimokkhuddesānaṁ katthogadhaṁ kattha pariyāpannanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “In which of the five ways of reciting the Monastic Code is it contained and included?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.17
Pali: Nidānogadhaṁ, nidānapariyāpannaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In the introduction. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.18
Pali: Katamena uddesena uddesaṁ āgacchatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “In which recitation is it included?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.19
Pali: Dutiyena uddesena uddesaṁ āgacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): In the second recitation. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.20
Pali: Catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ katamā vipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four kinds of failure does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.21
Pali: Sīlavipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Failure in morality. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.22
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katamo āpattikkhandhoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the seven classes of offenses does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.23
Pali: Pārājikāpattikkhandho. 
English (brahmali, published): The class of offenses entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.24
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhātīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses does it originate?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.25
Pali: Ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.26
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.27
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four kinds of legal issues does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.28
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.29
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues is it settled?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.30
Pali: Dvīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Through two of them: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.31
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.32
Pali: Ko tattha vinayo, ko tattha abhivinayoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the Monastic Law there? What is concerned with the Monastic Law there?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.33
Pali: Paññatti vinayo, vibhatti abhivinayo. 
English (brahmali, published): The rule is the Monastic Law. Its analysis is concerned with the Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.34
Pali: Kiṁ tattha pātimokkhaṁ, kiṁ tattha adhipātimokkhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the Monastic Code there? What is concerned with the Monastic Code there?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.35
Pali: Paññatti pātimokkhaṁ, vibhatti adhipātimokkhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The rule is the Monastic Code. Its analysis is concerned with the Monastic Code. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.36
Pali: Kā vipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is failure?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.37
Pali: Asaṁvaro vipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Lack of restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.38
Pali: Kā sampattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is success?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.39
Pali: Saṁvaro sampatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.40
Pali: Kā paṭipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the practice?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.41
Pali: “Na evarūpaṁ karissāmī”ti yāvajīvaṁ āpāṇakoṭikaṁ samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu. 
English (brahmali, published): Thinking, “I won’t do such a thing,” one undertakes to train in the training rules for life. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.42
Pali: Kati atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā paṭhamaṁ pārājikaṁ paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “For how many reasons did the Buddha lay down the first offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.43
Pali: Dasa atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā paṭhamaṁ pārājikaṁ paññattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): He laid it down for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.44
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya, dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya, samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya, appasannānaṁ pasādāya, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā, vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.45
Pali: Ke sikkhantīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who are those who train?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.46
Pali: Sekkhā ca puthujjanakalyāṇakā ca sikkhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They are the trainees and the good ordinary people. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.47
Pali: Ke sikkhitasikkhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who have finished the training?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.48
Pali: Arahanto sikkhitasikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): The perfected ones. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.49
Pali: Kattha ṭhitanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Established in what?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.50
Pali: Sikkhākāmesu ṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In fondness for the training. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.51
Pali: Ke dhārentīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who master it?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.52
Pali: Yesaṁ vattati te dhārenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those who learn it. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.53
Pali: Kassa vacananti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whose pronouncement was it?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.54
Pali: Bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa. 
English (brahmali, published): It was the pronouncement of the Buddha, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:3.55
Pali: Kenābhatanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who handed it down?”

Pali: Paramparābhataṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The lineage:

pli-tv-pvr1.1:26.0

pli-tv-pvr1.1:26.1

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.1

English (brahmali, published): Seyyasaka masturbating. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.7
Pali: Atthi tattha paññatti, anupaññatti, anuppannapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there a rule, an addition to the rule, an unprompted rule?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.8

English (brahmali, published): There is no unprompted rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.10

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.12

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.15
Pali: Ekatopaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.16
Pali: Pañcannaṁ pātimokkhuddesānaṁ katthogadhaṁ kattha pariyāpannanti?

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.17
Pali: Nidānogadhaṁ nidānapariyāpannaṁ.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.18

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.19

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.20
Pali: Catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ katamā vipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four kinds of failure does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.21
Pali: Sīlavipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Failure in morality. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.22
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katamo āpattikkhandhoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the seven classes of offenses does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.23
Pali: Saṅghādiseso āpattikkhandho. 
English (brahmali, published): The class of offenses entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.24
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhātīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses does it originate?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.25
Pali: Ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.26
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.27
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four kinds of legal issues does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.28
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.29
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues is it settled?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.30
Pali: Dvīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Through two of them: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.31
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.32
Pali: Ko tattha vinayo, ko tattha abhivinayoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the Monastic Law there? What is concerned with the Monastic Law there?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.33
Pali: Paññatti vinayo, vibhatti abhivinayo. 
English (brahmali, published): The rule is the Monastic Law. Its analysis is concerned with the Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.34
Pali: Kiṁ tattha pātimokkhaṁ, kiṁ tattha adhipātimokkhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the Monastic Code there? What is concerned with the Monastic Code there?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.35
Pali: Paññatti pātimokkhaṁ, vibhatti adhipātimokkhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The rule is the Monastic Code. Its analysis is concerned with the Monastic Code. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.36
Pali: Kā vipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is failure?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.37
Pali: Asaṁvaro vipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Lack of restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.38
Pali: Kā sampattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is success?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.39
Pali: Saṁvaro sampatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.40
Pali: Kā paṭipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the practice?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.41
Pali: Na evarūpaṁ karissāmīti yāvajīvaṁ āpāṇakoṭikaṁ samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu. 
English (brahmali, published): Thinking, “I won’t do such a thing,” one undertakes to train in the training rules for life. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.42
Pali: Kati atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā upakkamitvā asuciṁ mocentassa saṅghādiseso paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “For how many reasons did the Buddha lay down the offense entailing suspension for emitting semen by means of effort?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.43
Pali: Dasa atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā upakkamitvā asuciṁ mocentassa saṅghādiseso paññatto—
English (brahmali, published): He laid it down for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.44
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya, dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya, samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya, appasannānaṁ pasādāya, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā, vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.45
Pali: Ke sikkhantīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who are those who train?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.46
Pali: Sekkhā ca puthujjanakalyāṇakā ca sikkhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They are the trainees and the good ordinary people. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.47
Pali: Ke sikkhitasikkhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who have finished the training?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.48
Pali: Arahanto sikkhitasikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): The perfected ones. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:27.49
Pali: Kattha ṭhitanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Established in what?”

pli-tv-pvr1.1:44.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena mātugāmena saddhiṁ kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjantassa saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The offense entailing suspension for making physical contact with a woman was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:44.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:44.3

English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:44.4

English (brahmali, published): Venerable Udāyī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:44.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:44.6
Pali: Āyasmā udāyī mātugāmena saddhiṁ kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajji, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Udāyī making physical contact with a woman. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:44.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:51.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ aññabhāgiyassa adhikaraṇassa kiñci desaṁ lesamattaṁ upādāya pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsentassa saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension for charging a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, using an unrelated legal issue as a pretext. Where was it laid down?”

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?”

pli-tv-pvr1.1:59.4

English (brahmali, published): The thirteen offenses entailing suspension.” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:60.0
Pali: 3. Aniyatakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 3. The chapter on undetermined offenses 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:60.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena paṭhamo aniyato kattha paññatto? 
English (brahmali, published): “The first undetermined offense was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:60.2
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbha? 
English (brahmali, published): Whom is it about? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:60.3
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusmiṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): What is it about? 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:60.4
Pali: Atthi tattha paññatti, anupaññatti, anuppannapaññatti, sabbatthapaññatti padesapaññatti, sādhāraṇapaññatti asādhāraṇapaññatti, ekatopaññatti ubhatopaññatti, pañcannaṁ pātimokkhuddesānaṁ katthogadhaṁ kattha pariyāpannaṁ, katamena uddesena uddesaṁ āgacchati, catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ katamā vipatti, sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katamo āpattikkhandho, channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti, catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ, sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammati, ko tattha vinayo, ko tattha abhivinayo, kiṁ tattha pātimokkhaṁ, kiṁ tattha adhipātimokkhaṁ, kā vipatti, kā sampatti, kā paṭipatti, kati atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā paṭhamo aniyato paññatto, ke sikkhanti, ke sikkhitasikkhā, kattha ṭhitaṁ, ke dhārenti, kassa vacanaṁ kenābhatanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is there a rule, an addition to the rule, an unprompted rule? Is it a rule that applies everywhere or in a particular place? Is it a rule that the monks and nuns have in common or not in common? Is it a rule for one Sangha or for both? In which of the five ways of reciting the Monastic Code is it contained and included? In which recitation is it included? To which of the four kinds of failure does it belong? To which of the seven classes of offenses does it belong? Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses does it originate? To which of the four kinds of legal issues does it belong? Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues is it settled? What is the Monastic Law there? What is concerned with the Monastic Law there? What is the Monastic Code there? What is concerned with the Monastic Code there? What is failure? What is success? What is the practice? For how many reasons did the Buddha lay down the first undetermined offense? Who are those who train? Who have finished the training? Established in what? Who master it? Whose pronouncement was it? Who handed it down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena paṭhamo aniyato kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The first undetermined offense was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Udāyī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.6
Pali: Āyasmā udāyī mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho paṭicchanne āsane alaṅkammaniye nisajjaṁ kappesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Udāyī sitting down in private alone with a woman on a concealed seat suitable for the deed. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.7
Pali: Atthi tattha paññatti, anupaññatti, anuppannapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there a rule, an addition to the rule, an unprompted rule?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.8
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.9
Pali: Anupaññatti anuppannapaññatti tasmiṁ natthi. 
English (brahmali, published): There is no addition to the rule. There is no unprompted rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.10
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti, padesapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that applies everywhere or in a particular place?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.11
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Everywhere. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.12
Pali: Sādhāraṇapaññatti, asādhāraṇapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that the monks and nuns have in common or not in common?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.13
Pali: Asādhāraṇapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Not in common. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.14
Pali: Ekatopaññatti, ubhatopaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule for one Sangha or for both?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.15
Pali: Ekatopaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.16
Pali: Pañcannaṁ pātimokkhuddesānaṁ katthogadhaṁ kattha pariyāpannanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “In which of the five ways of reciting the Monastic Code is it contained and included?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.17
Pali: Nidānogadhaṁ nidānapariyāpannaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In the introduction. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.18
Pali: Katamena uddesena uddesaṁ āgacchatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “In which recitation is it included?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.19
Pali: Catutthena uddesena uddesaṁ āgacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): In the fourth recitation. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.20
Pali: Catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ katamā vipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four kinds of failure does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.21
Pali: Siyā sīlavipatti siyā ācāravipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be failure in morality or failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.22
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katamo āpattikkhandhoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the seven classes of offenses does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.23
Pali: Siyā pārājikāpattikkhandho siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandho siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandho. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion, in the class of offenses entailing suspension, or in the class of offenses entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.24
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhātīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses does it originate?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.25
Pali: Ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.26
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.27
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four kinds of legal issues does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.28
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.29
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues is it settled?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.30
Pali: Tīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Through three of them: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.31
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): it may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or it may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.32
Pali: Ko tattha vinayo, ko tattha abhivinayoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the Monastic Law there? What is concerned with the Monastic Law there?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.33
Pali: Paññatti vinayo, vibhatti abhivinayo. 
English (brahmali, published): The rule is the Monastic Law. Its analysis is concerned with the Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.34
Pali: Kiṁ tattha pātimokkhaṁ, kiṁ tattha adhipātimokkhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the Monastic Code there? What is concerned with the Monastic Code there?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.35
Pali: Paññatti pātimokkhaṁ, vibhatti adhipātimokkhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The rule is the Monastic Code. Its analysis is concerned with the Monastic Code. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.36
Pali: Kā vipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is failure?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.37
Pali: Asaṁvaro vipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Lack of restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.38
Pali: Kā sampattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is success?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.39
Pali: Saṁvaro sampatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.40
Pali: Kā paṭipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the practice?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.41
Pali: Na evarūpaṁ karissāmīti yāvajīvaṁ āpāṇakoṭikaṁ samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu. 
English (brahmali, published): Thinking, “I won’t do such a thing,” one undertakes to train in the training rules for life. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.42
Pali: Kati atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā paṭhamo aniyato paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “For how many reasons did the Buddha lay down the first undetermined offense?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.43
Pali: Dasa atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā paṭhamo aniyato paññatto—
English (brahmali, published): He laid it down for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.44
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya, dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya, samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya, appasannānaṁ pasādāya, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā, vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.45
Pali: Ke sikkhantīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who are those who train?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.46
Pali: Sekkhā ca puthujjanakalyāṇakā ca sikkhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They are the trainees and the good ordinary people. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.47
Pali: Ke sikkhitasikkhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who have finished the training?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.48
Pali: Arahanto sikkhitasikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): The perfected ones. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.49
Pali: Kattha ṭhitanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Established in what?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.50
Pali: Sikkhākāmesu ṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In fondness for the training. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.51
Pali: Ke dhārentīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who master it?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:61.52

pli-tv-pvr1.1:78.1

English (brahmali, published): Through three of them: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:78.33

pli-tv-pvr1.1:85.1
Pali: Aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for having an unrelated nun wash a used robe. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:85.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:85.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:85.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Udāyī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:85.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:85.6
Pali: Āyasmā udāyī aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Udāyī having an unrelated nun wash a used robe. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:85.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:85.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ chahi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in six ways: … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:86.1
Pali: Aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthato cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇhantassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for receiving a robe directly from an unrelated nun. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:86.2
Pali: Rājagahe paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Rājagaha. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:86.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:86.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Udāyī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:86.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:86.6
Pali: Āyasmā udāyī aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthato cīvaraṁ paṭiggahesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Udāyī receiving a robe directly from an unrelated nun. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:86.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti ekā anupaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There is one addition to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:86.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ chahi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in six ways: … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:87.1
Pali: Aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā cīvaraṁ viññāpentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for asking an unrelated male or female householder for a robe. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:87.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:87.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:87.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:87.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:87.6
Pali: Āyasmā upanando sakyaputto aññātakaṁ seṭṭhiputtaṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Upananda asking the son of an unrelated merchant for a robe. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:87.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti ekā anupaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There is one addition to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:87.8

English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in six ways: … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:88.1
Pali: Aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā tatuttari cīvaraṁ viññāpentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for asking an unrelated male or female householder for too many robes. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:88.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:88.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:88.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:88.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:88.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū na mattaṁ jānitvā bahuṁ cīvaraṁ viññāpesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks not knowing moderation and asking for many robes. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:88.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:88.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ chahi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in six ways: …

Pali: Pubbe appavāritassa aññātakaṁ gahapatikaṁ upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjantassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti?

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.0
Pali: 4.2. Kosiyavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on silk 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.1
Pali: Kosiyamissakaṁ santhataṁ kārāpentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for having a blanket made that contains silk. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.2
Pali: Āḷaviyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Āḷavī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū kosiyakārake upasaṅkamitvā evamāhaṁsu “bahū, āvuso, kosakārake pacatha. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks going to the silk-makers and saying, “Please boil a heap of silkworms 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.7
Pali: Amhākampi dassatha. 
English (brahmali, published): and give us silk. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.8
Pali: Mayampi icchāma kosiyamissakaṁ santhataṁ kātun”ti, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): We want to make a blanket containing silk.” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:93.9
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:95.1
Pali: Anādiyitvā tulaṁ odātānaṁ tulaṁ gocariyānaṁ navaṁ santhataṁ kārāpentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for having a new blanket made without using one measure of white wool and one measure of brown. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:95.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī.

English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six.

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:95.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū thokaññeva odātaṁ ante ādiyitvā tatheva suddhakāḷakānaṁ eḷakalomānaṁ santhataṁ kārāpesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks adding just a little bit of white on the edge, effectively having a blanket made entirely of black wool. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:95.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:97.1
Pali: Anādiyitvā purāṇasanthatassa sāmantā sugatavidatthiṁ navaṁ nisīdanasanthataṁ kārāpentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for having a new sitting blanket made without incorporating a piece of one standard handspan from the border of an old blanket. Where was it laid down?”

Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī.

English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): A number of monks. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:97.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:97.6
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhū santhatāni ujjhitvā āraññikaṅgaṁ piṇḍapātikaṅgaṁ paṁsukūlikaṅgaṁ samādiyiṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks discarding their blankets and undertaking the practice of staying in the wilderness, of eating only almsfood, and of wearing rag-robes.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:98.1

pli-tv-pvr1.1:98.7

English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:98.9
Pali: siyā kāyato samuṭṭhāti, na vācato na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body, not from speech or mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:98.10

English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:99.1

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.0
Pali: 4.3. Pattavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on almsbowls 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.1
Pali: Atirekapattaṁ dasāhaṁ atikkāmentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for keeping an extra almsbowl for more than ten days. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū atirekapattaṁ dhāresuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks keeping an extra bowl. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, ekā anupaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There is one addition to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ dvīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.9
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca samuṭṭhāti, na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and speech, not from mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:104.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:105.1
Pali: Ūnapañcabandhanena pattena aññaṁ navaṁ pattaṁ cetāpentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for exchanging an almsbowl with fewer than five mends for a new almsbowl. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:105.2
Pali: Sakkesu paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In the Sakyan country. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:105.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:105.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:105.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū appamattakenapi bhinnena appamattakenapi khaṇḍena vilikhitamattenapi bahū patte viññāpesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks asking for many bowls even when their existing almsbowls only had a minor chip or scratch. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:105.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:110.1
Pali: Pubbe appavāritassa aññātakassa gahapatikassa tantavāye upasaṅkamitvā cīvare vikappaṁ āpajjantassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for going, without first being invited, to an unrelated householder’s weavers and then specifying the kind of robe-cloth one wants. Where was it laid down?”

pli-tv-pvr1.1:112.1
Pali: Tiṇṇaṁ cīvarānaṁ aññataraṁ cīvaraṁ antaraghare nikkhipitvā atirekachārattaṁ vippavasantassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti?

pli-tv-pvr1.1:113.1
Pali: Jānaṁ saṅghikaṁ lābhaṁ pariṇataṁ attano pariṇāmentassa nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing relinquishment and confession for diverting to oneself material support that one knows was intended for the Sangha. Where was it laid down?”

English (brahmali, published): The thirty rules on relinquishment and confession are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:115.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:116.1
Pali: Dasekarattimāso ca, 
English (brahmali, published): “Ten, one day, and a month; 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:116.2
Pali: dhovanañca paṭiggaho; 
English (brahmali, published): And washing, receiving; 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:116.3
Pali: Aññātaṁ tañca uddissa, 
English (brahmali, published): Unrelated, and that one, for the sake of; 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:116.4
Pali: ubhinnaṁ dūtakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Of both, and with messenger. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:117.1
Pali: Kosiyā suddhadvebhāgā, 
English (brahmali, published): Silk, entirely, two parts, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:117.2
Pali: chabbassāni nisīdanaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Six years, sitting blanket; 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:117.3
Pali: Dve ca lomāni uggaṇhe, 
English (brahmali, published): And two on wool, should take, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:117.4
Pali: ubho nānappakārakā. 
English (brahmali, published): Two on various kinds. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:118.1
Pali: Dve ca pattāni bhesajjaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Two on bowls, and tonics, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:118.2
Pali: Vassikā dānapañcamaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Rainy season, the fifth on a gift; 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:118.3
Pali: Sāmaṁ vāyāpanacceko, 
English (brahmali, published): Oneself, having woven, haste, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:118.4
Pali: Sāsaṅkaṁ saṅghikena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Risky, and with the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.0.1
Pali: 5. Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 5. The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.0.2
Pali: 5.1. Musāvādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on lying 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena sampajānamusāvāde pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The offense entailing confession for lying in full awareness was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.4
Pali: Hatthakaṁ sakyaputtaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Hatthaka the Sakyan. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.6
Pali: Āyasmā hatthako sakyaputto titthiyehi saddhiṁ sallapanto avajānitvā paṭijāni, paṭijānitvā avajāni, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Hatthaka, when talking with the monastics of other religions, asserting things after denying them and denying things after asserting them. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ tīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in three ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.9
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:119.10
Pali: siyā vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na kāyato; 
English (brahmali, published): from speech and mind, not from body; or

English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. …

Pali: 5.4. Bhojanavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on eating 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:152.1

English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for eating alms too often at a public guesthouse. Where was it laid down?”

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:152.6

pli-tv-pvr1.1:156.1
Pali: Bhuttāvinā pavāritena anatirittaṁ khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā bhuñjantassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for having finished one’s meal and refused an invitation to eat more, and then eating fresh or cooked food that is not left over. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:156.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:156.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?”

Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:156.6
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhū bhuttāvī pavāritā aññatra bhuñjiṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks having finished their meal and refused an invitation to eat more, and then eating elsewhere. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:156.7

pli-tv-pvr1.1:157.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ bhuttāviṁ pavāritaṁ anatirittena khādanīyena vā bhojanīyena vā abhihaṭṭhuṁ pavārentassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for inviting a monk who has finished his meal and refused an invitation to eat more to eat fresh or cooked food that is not left over. Where was it laid down?”

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?”

pli-tv-pvr1.1:162.1
Pali: Bhojanavaggo catuttho. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth subchapter on eating is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.0
Pali: 5.5. Acelakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on naked ascetics 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.1
Pali: Acelakassa vā paribbājakassa vā paribbājikāya vā sahatthā khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā dentassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for personally giving fresh or cooked food to a naked ascetic, to a male wanderer, or to a female wanderer. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.2
Pali: Vesāliyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Vesālī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Ānanda. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.6
Pali: Āyasmā ānando aññatarissā paribbājikāya ekaṁ maññamāno dve pūve adāsi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Ānanda giving two cookies, thinking they were one, to a certain a female wanderer. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:163.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ dvīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti eḷakalomake …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: … (as in the rule on wool) … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:164.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ “ehāvuso, gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya pavisissāmā”ti tassa dāpetvā vā adāpetvā vā uyyojentassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for saying to a monk, ‘Come, let’s go to the village or town for alms,’ and then, whether he has had food given to him or not, sending him away. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:164.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:164.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:164.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:164.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:164.6
Pali: Āyasmā upanando sakyaputto bhikkhuṁ “ehāvuso, gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pavisissāmā”ti, tassa adāpetvā uyyojesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Upananda saying to a monk, “Come, let’s go to the village or town for alms,” and then, without having had food given to him, sending him away. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:164.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:164.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ tīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in three ways: … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.1
Pali: Sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappentassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for sitting down intruding on a lustful couple. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.6
Pali: Āyasmā upanando sakyaputto sabhojane kule anupakhajja nisajjaṁ kappesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Upananda sitting down intruding on a lustful couple. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:165.9
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. …

Pali: Mātugāmena saddhiṁ eko ekāya raho nisajjaṁ kappentassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for sitting down in private alone with a woman. Where was it laid down?”

English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:168.1
Pali: Nimantitena sabhattena santaṁ bhikkhuṁ anāpucchā purebhattaṁ pacchābhattaṁ kulesu cārittaṁ āpajjantassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for being invited to a meal and then visiting families beforehand or afterwards without informing an available monk. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:168.2
Pali: Rājagahe paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Rājagaha. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:168.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:168.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:168.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:168.6
Pali: Āyasmā upanando sakyaputto nimantito sabhatto samāno purebhattaṁ pacchābhattaṁ kulesu cārittaṁ āpajji, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Upananda having been invited to a meal and then visiting families beforehand and afterwards. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:168.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, catasso anupaññattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There are four additions to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:168.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ dvīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti kathinake …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: … (as in the rule on the robe season) … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:169.1
Pali: Tatuttari bhesajjaṁ viññāpentassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for asking for too many tonics. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:169.2
Pali: Sakkesu paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In the Sakyan country. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:169.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:169.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:169.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:169.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū mahānāmena sakkena “ajjaṇho, bhante, āgamethā”ti vuccamānā nāgamesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks not waiting for one day when asked by Mahānāma the Sakyan. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:169.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:169.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ chahi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in six ways: …

pli-tv-pvr1.1:174.0
Pali: 5.6. Surāpānavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on drinking alcohol 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:174.1
Pali: Surāmerayapāne pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for drinking an alcoholic drink. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:174.2
Pali: Kosambiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Kosambī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:174.3

English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:174.6
Pali: Āyasmā sāgato majjaṁ pivi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Sāgata drinking alcohol. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:174.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule.

English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:174.9
Pali: siyā kāyato samuṭṭhāti, na vācato na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body, not from speech or mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:174.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. …

pli-tv-pvr1.1:177.1
Pali: Anādariye pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for disrespect. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:177.2
Pali: Kosambiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Kosambī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:177.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:177.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ channaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Channa. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:177.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:177.6
Pali: Āyasmā channo anādariyaṁ akāsi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Channa acting disrespectfully. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:177.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:177.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ tīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe….

pli-tv-pvr1.1:178.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ bhiṁsāpentassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for scaring a monk. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:178.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:178.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:178.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:178.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:178.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ bhiṁsāpesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks scaring a monk. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:178.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:179.1
Pali: Jotiṁ samādahitvā visibbentassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for lighting a fire and warming oneself. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:179.2
Pali: Bhaggesu paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In the Bhaggā country. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:179.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:179.4
Pali: Sambahule bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): A number of monks. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:179.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:179.6
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhū jotiṁ samādahitvā visibbesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks warming themselves by lighting a fire. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:179.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, dve anupaññattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There are two additions to the rule.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:180.1
Pali: Orenaddhamāsaṁ nahāyantassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for bathing at intervals of less than a half-month. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:180.2
Pali: Rājagahe paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Rājagaha.

Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:180.4

English (brahmali, published): A number of monks. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:180.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:180.6
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhū rājānampi passitvā na mattaṁ jānitvā nahāyiṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks bathing without moderation, even after seeing the king. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:180.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, cha anupaññattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There are six additions to the rule.

Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti, padesapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that applies everywhere or in a particular place?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:180.9
Pali: Padesapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): In a particular place.

pli-tv-pvr1.1:182.1
Pali: Bhikkhussa vā bhikkhuniyā vā sikkhamānāya vā sāmaṇerassa vā sāmaṇeriyā vā sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ vikappetvā appaccuddhāraṇaṁ paribhuñjantassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for assigning the ownership of a robe to a monk, a nun, a trainee nun, a novice monk, or a novice nun, and then using it without the other first relinquishing it. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:182.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:182.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:182.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ upanandaṁ sakyaputtaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upananda the Sakyan. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:182.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:182.6
Pali: Āyasmā upanando sakyaputto bhikkhussa sāmaṁ cīvaraṁ vikappetvā appaccuddhāraṇaṁ paribhuñji, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Upananda assigning the ownership of a robe to a monk and then using it without that monk first relinquishing it. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:182.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule.

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on containing living beings

pli-tv-pvr1.1:192.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:192.9
Pali: Kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:193.1
Pali: Jānaṁ tathāvādinā bhikkhunā akaṭānudhammena taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ sambhuñjantassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for living with a monk who one knows is saying such things, who has not made amends according to the rule, and who has not given up that view. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:193.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:193.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:193.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:193.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:193.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū jānaṁ tathāvādinā ariṭṭhena bhikkhunā akaṭānudhammena taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissaṭṭhena saddhiṁ sambhuñjiṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks living with the monk Ariṭṭha who they knew was saying such things, who had not made amends according to the rule, and who had not given up that view. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:193.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:193.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ tīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in three ways: …

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?”

pli-tv-pvr1.1:195.1
Pali: Sappāṇakavaggo sattamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh subchapter on containing living beings is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.0
Pali: 5.8. Sahadhammikavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on legitimate correction 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.1
Pali: Bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamānena “na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchissāmī”ti bhaṇantassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “When legitimately corrected by the monks, there is an offense entailing confession for saying, ‘I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who is an expert on the Monastic Law.’ Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.2
Pali: Kosambiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Kosambī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.4
Pali: Āyasmantaṁ channaṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Channa. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.6
Pali: Āyasmā channo bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno “na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchissāmī”ti bhaṇi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Channa, when legitimately corrected by the monks, saying, “I won’t practice this training rule until I’ve questioned a monk who is an expert on the Monastic Law”. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:196.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ tīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in three ways: …

pli-tv-pvr1.1:205.1
Pali: Saṅghe vinicchayakathāya vattamānāya chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamantassa pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for, without first giving one’s consent, getting up from one’s seat and leaving while the Sangha is in the middle of a discussion. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:205.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:205.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:205.4
Pali: Aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): A certain monk. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:205.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:205.6
Pali: Aññataro bhikkhu saṅghe vinicchayakathāya vattamānāya chandaṁ adatvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): That monk getting up from his seat and leaving while the Sangha was in the middle of a discussion, without first giving his consent. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:205.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule.

English (brahmali, published): With the subchapter on kings—these nine.” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.0
Pali: 6. Pāṭidesanīyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 6. The chapter on offenses entailing acknowledgment 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā antaragharaṁ paviṭṭhāya hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā bhuñjantassa pāṭidesanīyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The offense entailing acknowledgment for eating fresh or cooked food that was received directly from an unrelated nun who had entered an inhabited area was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.4
Pali: Aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): A certain monk. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.6
Pali: Aññataro bhikkhu aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā antaragharaṁ paviṭṭhāya hatthato āmisaṁ paṭiggahesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): That monk receiving food directly from an unrelated nun who had entered an inhabited area. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ dvīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.9
Pali: siyā kāyato samuṭṭhāti, na vācato na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body, not from speech or mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:232.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.1
Pali: Bhikkhuniyā vosāsantiyā na nivāretvā bhuñjantassa pāṭidesanīyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing acknowledgment for eating without having restrained a nun who is giving directions. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.2
Pali: Rājagahe paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Rājagaha. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū bhikkhuniyo vosāsantiyo na nivāresuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks not restraining a nun who was giving directions. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ dvīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.9
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca samuṭṭhāti, na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and speech, not from mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:233.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.1
Pali: Sekkhasammatesu kulesu khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā bhuñjantassa pāṭidesanīyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing acknowledgment for eating fresh or cooked food after personally receiving it from families designated as ‘in training’. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.4
Pali: Sambahule bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): A number of monks. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.6
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhū na mattaṁ jānitvā paṭiggahesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks receiving with no sense of moderation. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, dve anupaññattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There are two additions to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ dvīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.9
Pali: siyā kāyato samuṭṭhāti, na vācato na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body, not from speech or mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:234.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.1
Pali: Āraññakesu senāsanesu pubbe appaṭisaṁviditaṁ khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā ajjhārāme sahatthā paṭiggahetvā bhuñjantassa pāṭidesanīyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing acknowledgment for eating fresh or cooked food after personally receiving it inside a wilderness monastery without first making an announcement. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.2
Pali: Sakkesu paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In the Sakyan country. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.4
Pali: Sambahule bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): A number of monks. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti. 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.6
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhū ārāme core paṭivasante nārocesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks not informing that there were bandits staying in the monastery. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, ekā anupaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There is one addition to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ dvīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.9
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca samuṭṭhāti, na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and speech, not from mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:235.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:236.1
Pali: Cattāro pāṭidesanīyā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The four offenses entailing acknowledgment are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:237.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:238.1
Pali: Aññātikāya vosāsaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “From one who is unrelated, giving directions, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:238.2
Pali: sekkhaāraññakena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): In training, and with wilderness—

pli-tv-pvr1.1:238.3
Pali: Pāṭidesanīyā cattāro, 
English (brahmali, published): The four offenses entailing acknowledgment, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:238.4
Pali: sambuddhena pakāsitāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaimed by the Awakened One.” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:239.0.1
Pali: 7. Sekhiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 7. The chapter on training 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:239.0.2
Pali: 7.1. Parimaṇḍalavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on evenly all around 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:239.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena anādariyaṁ paṭicca purato vā pacchato vā olambentena nivāsentassa dukkaṭaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, wearing one’s sarong hanging down in front or behind was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:239.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī.

English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:239.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:239.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū puratopi pacchatopi olambentā nivāsesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks wearing their sarongs hanging down in front and behind. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:239.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:239.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way:

English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. …

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca uccāsaddaṁ mahāsaddaṁ karontena antaraghare gacchantassa dukkaṭaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, being noisy while walking in an inhabited area. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū uccāsaddaṁ mahāsaddaṁ karontā antaraghare gacchiṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks being noisy while walking in an inhabited area. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:252.9
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. …

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca sūpaṁ vā odanaṁ vā agilāno attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjantassa dukkaṭaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, eating bean curry or rice that, when one is not sick, one has requested for oneself. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū sūpampi odanampi attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjiṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks eating bean curry and rice that they had requested for themselves. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, ekā anupaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There is one addition to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ dvīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.9
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech; or 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:278.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:279.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca ujjhānasaññinā paresaṁ pattaṁ olokentassa dukkaṭaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, looking at the almsbowl of another finding fault …” … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:279.2
Pali: ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:279.3
Pali: Ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:279.4
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:280.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca mahantaṁ kabaḷaṁ karontassa dukkaṭaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, making a large mouthful …” … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:280.2
Pali: ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:280.3
Pali: Ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:280.4
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:281.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca dīghaṁ ālopaṁ karontassa dukkaṭaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, making an elongated mouthful …” … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:281.2
Pali: ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:281.3
Pali: Ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:281.4
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:282.1
Pali: Piṇḍapātavaggo catuttho. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth subchapter on almsfood is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:283.0
Pali: 7.5. Kabaḷavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on mouthfuls 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:283.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca anāhaṭe kabaḷe mukhadvāraṁ vivarantassa dukkaṭaṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, opening one’s mouth without bringing a mouthful to it …” …

English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, putting one’s whole hand in one’s mouth while eating …” …

pli-tv-pvr1.1:285.2

pli-tv-pvr1.1:285.7

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.0
Pali: 7.6. Surusuruvagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on slurping 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca surusurukārakaṁ bhuñjantassa dukkaṭaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct for, out of disrespect, eating making a slurping sound. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.2
Pali: Kosambiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Kosambī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.4
Pali: Sambahule bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): A number of monks. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.6
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhū surusurukārakaṁ khīraṁ piviṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks slurping while drinking milk. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:294.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—

English (brahmali, published): Those monks giving a teaching to someone holding a sunshade. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:300.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, ekā anupaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There is one addition to the rule.
