English (brahmali, published): Shoes, and sandals, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:328.2
Pali: yānaseyyāgatassa ca; 
English (brahmali, published): And to one in a vehicle, and to one lying down; 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:328.3

English (brahmali, published): To one seated clasping their knees, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:328.4

English (brahmali, published): To one with a headdress, and to one with a covered head. 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:329.1
Pali: Chamā nīcāsane ṭhāne, 
English (brahmali, published): The ground, on a low seat, standing, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:329.2
Pali: pacchato uppathena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Behind, and next to the path; 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:329.3
Pali: Ṭhitakena na kātabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Not to be done while standing, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:329.4
Pali: harite udakamhi cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): On cultivated plants, and in water.” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:330.1
Pali: Tesaṁ vaggānamuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary of the subchapters: 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:331.1
Pali: Parimaṇḍalaujjagghi, 
English (brahmali, published): “Evenly all around, laughing loudly, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:331.2
Pali: khambhaṁ piṇḍaṁ tatheva ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Hands on hips, and also almsfood; 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:331.3
Pali: Kabaḷā surusuru ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Mouthfuls, and slurping, 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:331.4
Pali: pādukena ca sattamāti. 
English (brahmali, published): And with shoe as the seventh.” 

pli-tv-pvr1.1:332.1
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅge katthapaññattivāro niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The questions and answers on the monks’ Monastic Code and its analysis in the Great Analysis are finished.

pli-tv-pvr1.2:0.1
Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhuvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Monks’ Analysis 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:0.3
Pali: Paṭhamabhāga 
English (brahmali, published): Part one 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:0.4
Pali: 1.2 Katāpattivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The number of offenses within each rule 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:0.5
Pali: 1. Pārājikakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:1.1
Pali: Methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanto kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When having sexual intercourse, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:1.2
Pali: Methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:1.3
Pali: Akkhāyite sarīre methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one has sexual intercourse with an undecomposed corpse, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:1.4
Pali: yebhuyyena khāyite sarīre methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one has sexual intercourse with a mostly decomposed corpse, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:1.5
Pali: vaṭṭakate mukhe acchupantaṁ aṅgajātaṁ paveseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when one inserts one’s penis into a wide open mouth without touching it, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:1.6
Pali: methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanto imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:2.1
Pali: Adinnaṁ ādiyanto kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When stealing, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:2.2
Pali: Adinnaṁ ādiyanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:2.3
Pali: Pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, intending to steal, one steals something worth five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:2.4
Pali: atirekamāsakaṁ vā ūnapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, intending to steal, one steals something worth more than one <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coin but less than five, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:2.5
Pali: māsakaṁ vā ūnamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when, intending to steal, one steals something worth one <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coin or less, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:2.6
Pali: adinnaṁ ādiyanto imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:3.1
Pali: Sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropento kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When intentionally killing a human being, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:3.2
Pali: Sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropento tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:3.3
Pali: Manussaṁ odissa opātaṁ khaṇati “papatitvā marissatī”ti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one digs a pit for a human being, thinking, “Falling into it, they will die,” one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:3.4
Pali: papatite dukkhā vedanā uppajjati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when they experience pain after falling in, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:3.5
Pali: marati, āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): when they die, one commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:3.6
Pali: sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropento imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:4.1
Pali: Asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapanto kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When claiming a non-existent superhuman quality, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:4.2
Pali: Asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:4.3
Pali: Pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, having bad desires, overcome by desire, one claims a non-existent superhuman quality, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:4.4
Pali: “yo te vihāre vasati, so bhikkhu arahā”ti bhaṇati, paṭivijānantassa āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one says, “The monk who stays in your dwelling is a perfected one,” and the listener understands, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:4.5
Pali: na paṭivijānantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when the listener does not understand, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:4.6
Pali: asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapanto imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:5.1
Pali: Cattāro pārājikā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The four offenses entailing expulsion are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:6.0
Pali: 2. Saṅghādisesakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:6.1
Pali: (…) Upakkamitvā asuciṁ mocento tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (…) When emitting semen by means of effort, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:6.2
Pali: Ceteti upakkamati muccati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one intends and makes an effort, and semen is emitted, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:6.3
Pali: ceteti upakkamati na muccati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one intends and makes an effort, but semen is not emitted, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:6.4
Pali: payoge dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:7.1
Pali: Mātugāmena saddhiṁ kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When making physical contact with a woman, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:7.2
Pali: Kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one makes physical contact, body with body, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:7.3
Pali: kāyena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one makes physical contact with something connected to her body, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:7.4
Pali: kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one, with something connected to one’s own body, makes physical contact with something connected to her body, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:8.1
Pali: Mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāsento tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When speaking indecently to a woman, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:8.2
Pali: Vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati, avaṇṇampi bhaṇati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, referring to the anus or the vagina, one praises or disparages, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:8.3
Pali: vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ṭhapetvā adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, referring to any part below the collar bone but above the knees, apart from the anus or the vagina, one praises or disparages, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:8.4
Pali: kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when, referring to anything connected to the body, one praises or disparages, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:9.1
Pali: Attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When encouraging the satisfaction of one’s own desires, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:9.2
Pali: Mātugāmassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one encourages a woman to satisfy one’s own desires, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:9.3
Pali: paṇḍakassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one encourages a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i> to satisfy one’s own desires, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:9.4
Pali: tiracchānagatassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one encourages an animal to satisfy one’s own desires, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:10.1
Pali: Sañcarittaṁ samāpajjanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When acting as a matchmaker, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:10.2
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati paccāharati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one accepts the mission, finds out the response, and reports back, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:10.3
Pali: paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati na paccāharati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one accepts the mission, and finds out the response, but does not report back, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:10.4
Pali: paṭiggaṇhāti na vīmaṁsati na paccāharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one accepts the mission, but neither finds out the response, nor reports back, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:11.1
Pali: Saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kārāpento tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When having a hut built by means of begging, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:11.2
Pali: Kārāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is having it built, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:11.3
Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when there is one piece left to complete the hut, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:11.4
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last piece is finished, one commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:12.1
Pali: Mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kārāpento tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When having a large dwelling built, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:12.2
Pali: Kārāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is having it built, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:12.3
Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when there is one piece left to complete the dwelling, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:12.4
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last piece is finished, one commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:13.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsento tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When groundlessly charging a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:13.2
Pali: Anokāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, without having gotten his permission, one speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, one commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:13.3
Pali: okāsaṁ kārāpetvā akkosādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti omasavādassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when, having gotten his permission, one speaks with the aim of abusing him, one commits an offense for abusive speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:14.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ aññabhāgiyassa adhikaraṇassa kiñci desaṁ lesamattaṁ upādāya pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsento tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When charging a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, using an unrelated legal issue as a pretext, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:14.2
Pali: Anokāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, without having gotten his permission, one speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, one commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:14.3
Pali: okāsaṁ kārāpetvā akkosādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti omasavādassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when, having gotten his permission, one speaks with the aim of abusing him, one commits an offense for abusive speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:15.1
Pali: Saṅghabhedako bhikkhu yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When not stopping when pressed for the third time, a monk who is pursuing schism in the Sangha commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:15.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:15.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:15.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:16.1
Pali: Bhedakānuvattakā bhikkhū yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjantā tisso āpattiyo āpajjanti. 
English (brahmali, published): When not stopping when pressed for the third time, monks who side with a monk who is pursuing schism commit three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:16.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, they commit an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:16.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, they commit a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:16.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, they commit an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:17.1
Pali: Dubbaco bhikkhu yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When not stopping when pressed for the third time, a monk who is difficult to correct commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:17.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:17.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:17.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:18.1
Pali: Kuladūsako bhikkhu yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When not stopping when pressed for the third time, a monk who is a corrupter of families commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:18.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:18.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:18.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:19.1
Pali: Terasa saṅghādisesā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The thirteen rules entailing suspension are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:20.0.1
Pali: 4. Nissaggiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 4. The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:20.0.2
Pali: 4.1. Kathinavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on the robe season 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:20.1
Pali: Atirekacīvaraṁ dasāhaṁ atikkāmento ekaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When keeping an extra robe more than ten days, one commits one kind of offense: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:20.2
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:21.1
Pali: Ekarattaṁ ticīvarena vippavasanto ekaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When staying apart from one’s three robes for one day, one commits one kind of offense: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:21.2
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:22.1
Pali: Akālacīvaraṁ paṭiggahetvā māsaṁ atikkāmento ekaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When receiving out-of-season robe-cloth and then keeping it for more than a month, one commits one kind of offense: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:22.2
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:23.1
Pali: Aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā purāṇacīvaraṁ dhovāpento dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When having an unrelated nun wash a used robe, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:23.2
Pali: Dhovāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is having it washed, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:23.3
Pali: dhovāpite nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has had it washed, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:24.1
Pali: Aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā hatthato cīvaraṁ paṭiggaṇhanto dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When receiving a robe directly from an unrelated nun, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:24.2
Pali: Gaṇhāti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of taking it, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:24.3
Pali: gahite nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has taken it, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:25.1
Pali: Aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā cīvaraṁ viññāpento dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When asking an unrelated male or female householder for a robe, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:25.2
Pali: Viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of asking, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:25.3
Pali: viññāpite nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has asked, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:26.1
Pali: Aññātakaṁ gahapatiṁ vā gahapatāniṁ vā tatuttari cīvaraṁ viññāpento dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When asking an unrelated male or female householder for too many robes, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:26.2
Pali: Viññāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of asking, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:26.3
Pali: viññāpite nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has asked, one commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:27.1

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.0.1
Pali: 5. Pācittiyakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 5. The chapter on offenses entailing confession 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.0.2
Pali: 5.1. Musāvādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on lying 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.1
Pali: Sampajānamusāvādaṁ bhāsanto kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When lying in full awareness, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.2
Pali: Sampajānamusāvādaṁ bhāsanto pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When lying in full awareness, one commits five kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.3
Pali: Pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, having bad desires, overcome by desire, one claims a non-existent superhuman quality, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.4
Pali: bhikkhuṁ amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁseti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one groundlessly charges a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.5
Pali: “yo te vihāre vasati, so bhikkhu arahā”ti bhaṇati, paṭivijānantassa āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one says, “The monk who stays in your dwelling is a perfected one,” and the listener understands, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.6
Pali: na paṭivijānantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when the listener does not understand, one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.7
Pali: sampajānamusāvāde pācittiyaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): when one lies in full awareness, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:53.8
Pali: sampajānamusāvādaṁ bhāsanto imā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:54.1
Pali: Omasanto dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When speaking abusively, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:54.2
Pali: Upasampannaṁ omasati, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one speaks abusively to one who is fully ordained, one commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:54.3
Pali: anupasampannaṁ omasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one speaks abusively to one who is not fully ordained, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:55.1
Pali: Pesuññaṁ upasaṁharanto dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When engaging in malicious talebearing, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:55.2
Pali: Upasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one engages in malicious talebearing to one who is fully ordained, one commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:55.3
Pali: anupasampannassa pesuññaṁ upasaṁharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one engages in malicious talebearing to one who is not fully ordained, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:56.1
Pali: Anupasampannaṁ padaso dhammaṁ vācento dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When instructing a person who is not fully ordained to memorize the Teaching, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:56.2
Pali: Vāceti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of instructing, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:56.3
Pali: pade pade āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): for every line, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:57.1
Pali: Anupasampannena uttaridirattatirattaṁ sahaseyyaṁ kappento dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When lying down more than two or three nights in the same sleeping place as a person who is not fully ordained, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:57.2
Pali: Nipajjati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of lying down, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:57.3
Pali: nipanne āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one is lying down, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:58.1
Pali: Mātugāmena sahaseyyaṁ kappento dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When lying down in the same sleeping place as a woman, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:58.2

English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of lying down, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct;

Pali: nipanne āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one is lying down, one commits an offense entailing confession.

pli-tv-pvr1.2:59.2

pli-tv-pvr1.2:59.3

English (brahmali, published): for every line, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:60.1

Pali: ārocite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has finished telling, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:61.1
Pali: Bhikkhussa duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ anupasampannassa ārocento dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When telling a person who is not fully ordained about a monk’s grave offense, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:61.2
Pali: Āroceti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ;

pli-tv-pvr1.2:61.3
Pali: ārocite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has finished telling, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:62.1
Pali: Pathaviṁ khaṇanto dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When digging the earth, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:62.2
Pali: Khaṇati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of digging, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:62.3
Pali: pahāre pahāre āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): for every strike, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:63.1
Pali: Musāvādavaggo paṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The first subchapter on lying is finished.

pli-tv-pvr1.2:99.2
Pali: Nisīdati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of sitting down, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:99.3
Pali: nisinne āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one is seated, one commits an offense entailing confession.

pli-tv-pvr1.2:119.0
Pali: 5.7. Sappāṇakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on containing living beings 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:119.1
Pali: Sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropento kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When intentionally killing a living being, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:119.2
Pali: Sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropento catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When intentionally killing a living being, one commits four kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:119.3
Pali: Anodissa opātaṁ khaṇati—“yo koci papatitvā marissatī”ti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one digs a non-specific pit, thinking, “Whatever falls into it will die,” one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:119.4
Pali: manusso tasmiṁ papatitvā marati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a person falls into it and dies, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:119.5
Pali: yakkho vā peto vā tiracchānagatamanussaviggaho vā tasmiṁ papatitvā marati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a spirit, ghost, or animal in human form falls into it and dies, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:119.6
Pali: tiracchānagato tasmiṁ papatitvā marati, āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): when an animal falls into it and dies, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:119.7
Pali: sañcicca pāṇaṁ jīvitā voropento imā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:120.1

pli-tv-pvr1.2:120.2
Pali: Paribhuñjati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is using it, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:120.3
Pali: paribhutte āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has used it, one commits an offense entailing confession.

pli-tv-pvr1.2:130.0
Pali: 5.8. Sahadhammikavagga 
English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on legitimate correction 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:130.1
Pali: Bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṁ vuccamāno—“na tāvāhaṁ, āvuso, etasmiṁ sikkhāpade sikkhissāmi yāva na aññaṁ bhikkhuṁ byattaṁ vinayadharaṁ paripucchissāmī”ti bhaṇanto dve āpattiyo āpajjati.

pli-tv-pvr1.2:130.2
Pali: Bhaṇati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is in the process of saying it, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:130.3
Pali: bhaṇite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has said it, one commits an offense entailing confession.

Pali: Vivaṇṇeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ;

pli-tv-pvr1.2:131.3

English (brahmali, published): when one has disparaged it, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:132.1

pli-tv-pvr1.2:132.2
Pali: Anāropite mohe moheti, āpatti dukkaṭassa;

pli-tv-pvr1.2:132.3
Pali: āropite mohe moheti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one deceives after having been charged with deception, one commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): When groundlessly charging a monk with an offense entailing suspension, one commits two kinds of offenses:

Pali: Anuddhaṁseti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ;

Pali: anuddhaṁsite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one has made the charge, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:136.1
Pali: Bhikkhussa sañcicca kukkuccaṁ upadahanto dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When intentionally making a monk anxious, one commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:136.2
Pali: Upadahati, payoge dukkaṭaṁ;

English (brahmali, published): when one has done it, one commits an offense entailing confession.

pli-tv-pvr1.2:154.1

English (brahmali, published): When eating fresh or cooked food that was received directly from an unrelated nun who had entered an inhabited area, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.2:154.2

English (brahmali, published): an offense of wrong conduct.

pli-tv-pvr1.8:0.1
Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:0.2
Pali: Bhikkhuvibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Monks’ Analysis 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:0.3
Pali: Paṭhamabhāga 
English (brahmali, published): Part one 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:0.4
Pali: 1.8 Samuccayavāra 
English (brahmali, published): Summary of the previous six sections 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:1.1
Pali: Methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanto kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When having sexual intercourse, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:1.2
Pali: Methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanto tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:1.3
Pali: Akkhāyite sarīre methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one has sexual intercourse with an undecomposed corpse, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:1.4
Pali: yebhuyyena khāyite sarīre methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one has sexual intercourse with a mostly decomposed corpse, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:1.5
Pali: vaṭṭakate mukhe acchupantaṁ aṅgajātaṁ paveseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when one inserts one’s penis into a wide open mouth without touching it, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:1.6
Pali: methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanto imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti, sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā, channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti, catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ, sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? In how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses do they originate? To which of the four kinds of legal issues do they belong? Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.2
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.3
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.4
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ tīhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in three classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.5
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.6
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.7
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.8
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.9
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:2.10
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:3.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca udake uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā karonto kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When, out of disrespect, one defecates, urinates, or spits in water, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:3.2
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca udake uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā karonto ekaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits one kind of offense: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:3.3
Pali: Dukkaṭaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:3.4
Pali: anādariyaṁ paṭicca udake uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā karonto imaṁ ekaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.1
Pali: Sā āpatti catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajati, sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā, channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti, catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ, sattannaṁ, samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to this offense, to how many of the four kinds of failure does it belong? In how many of the seven classes of offenses is it found? Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses does it originate? To which of the four kinds of legal issues does it belong? Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues is it settled? 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.2
Pali: Sā āpatti catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ ekaṁ vipattiṁ bhajati—
English (brahmali, published): It belongs to one kind of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.3
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.4
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ ekena āpattikkhandhena saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): It is found in one class of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.5
Pali: dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.6
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.7
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.8
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It belongs to legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.9
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): It is settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:4.10
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): it may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or it may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:5.1
Pali: Samuccayavāro niṭṭhito aṭṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The summary of the previous six sections, the eighth, is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:5.2
Pali: Ime aṭṭha vārā sajjhāyamaggena likhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): These eight sections were written down through the method of recitation. 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:6.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:7.1
Pali: Katthapaññatti kati ca, 
English (brahmali, published): “Where was it laid down, and how many, 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:7.2
Pali: Vipattisaṅgahena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Failure, and being found in; 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:7.3
Pali: Samuṭṭhānādhikaraṇā, 
English (brahmali, published): Originations, legal issues, 

pli-tv-pvr1.8:7.4
Pali: Samatho samuccayena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Settling, and with gathering up.”

Pali: Ekatopaññatti, ubhatopaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule for one Sangha or for both?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:11.15
Pali: Ekatopaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one.

English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:25.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:25.9
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.1
Pali: Kuladūsakassa bhikkhuno yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension that is a result of a monk not stopping with being a corrupter of families when pressed for the third time. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.4
Pali: Assajipunabbasuke bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks Assaji and Punabbasuka. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.6
Pali: Assajipunabbasukā bhikkhū saṅghena pabbājanīyakammakatā bhikkhū chandagāmitā dosagāmitā mohagāmitā bhayagāmitā pāpesuṁ, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks, when the Sangha did a legal procedure of banishment against them, slandering the monks as acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:26.9
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.1
Pali: Anādariyaṁ paṭicca udake uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā karaṇapaccayā dukkaṭaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense of wrong conduct that is a result of, out of disrespect, defecating, urinating, or spitting in water. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.4
Pali: Chabbaggiye bhikkhū ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The monks from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhū udake uccārampi passāvampi kheḷampi akaṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Those monks defecating, urinating, and spitting in water. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, ekā anupaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There is one addition to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:27.9
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. … 

pli-tv-pvr1.9:28.1
Pali: Katthapaññattivāro niṭṭhito paṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The questions and answers on the monks’ Monastic Code and its analysis, the first, are finished.

English (brahmali, published): The number of offenses within each rule 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:0.5
Pali: 1. Pārājikakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:1.1
Pali: Methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of having sexual intercourse? 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:1.2
Pali: Methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanapaccayā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits four kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:1.3
Pali: akkhāyite sarīre methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one has sexual intercourse with an undecomposed corpse, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:1.4
Pali: yebhuyyena khāyite sarīre methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one has sexual intercourse with a mostly decomposed corpse, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:1.5
Pali: vaṭṭakate mukhe acchupantaṁ aṅgajātaṁ paveseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one inserts one’s penis into a wide open mouth without touching it, one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:1.6
Pali: jatumaṭṭhake pācittiyaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): when one uses a dildo, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:1.7
Pali: methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevanapaccayā imā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:2.1
Pali: Adinnaṁ ādiyanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of stealing? 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:2.2
Pali: Adinnaṁ ādiyanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:2.3
Pali: Pañcamāsakaṁ vā atirekapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, intending to steal, one steals something worth five <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coins or more, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:2.4
Pali: atirekamāsakaṁ vā ūnapañcamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, intending to steal, one steals something worth more than one <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coin but less than five, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:2.5
Pali: māsakaṁ vā ūnamāsakaṁ vā agghanakaṁ adinnaṁ theyyasaṅkhātaṁ ādiyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when, intending to steal, one steals something worth one <i lang='pi' translate='no'>māsaka</i> coin or less, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:2.6
Pali: adinnaṁ ādiyanapaccayā imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:3.1
Pali: Sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of intentionally killing a human being? 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:3.2
Pali: Sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:3.3
Pali: Manussaṁ odissa opātaṁ khaṇati “papatitvā marissatī”ti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one digs a pit for a human being, thinking, “Falling into it, they will die,” one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:3.4
Pali: papatite dukkhā vedanā uppajjati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when they experience pain after falling in, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:3.5
Pali: marati, āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): when they die, one commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:3.6
Pali: sañcicca manussaviggahaṁ jīvitā voropanapaccayā imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:4.1
Pali: Asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of claiming a non-existent superhuman quality? 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:4.2
Pali: Asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:4.3
Pali: pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, having bad desires, overcome by desire, one claims a non-existent superhuman quality, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:4.4
Pali: “yo te vihāre vasati so bhikkhu arahā”ti bhaṇati, paṭivijānantassa āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one says, “The monk who stays in your dwelling is a perfected one,” and the listener understands, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:4.5
Pali: na paṭivijānantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when the listener does not understand, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:4.6
Pali: asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapanapaccayā imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:5.1
Pali: Cattāro pārājikā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The four offenses entailing expulsion are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:6.0
Pali: 2. Saṅghādisesakaṇḍādi 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The chapter on offenses entailing suspension, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:6.1
Pali: Upakkamitvā asucimocanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of emitting semen by means of effort? 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:6.2
Pali: Upakkamitvā asucimocanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:6.3
Pali: ceteti upakkamati muccati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one intends and makes an effort, and semen is emitted, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:6.4
Pali: ceteti upakkamati na muccati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one intends and makes an effort, but semen is not emitted, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:6.5
Pali: payoge dukkaṭaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:6.6
Pali: upakkamitvā asucimocanapaccayā imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:7.1
Pali: Kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of making physical contact with a woman? 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:7.2
Pali: Kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjanapaccayā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits five kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:7.3
Pali: avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a lustful nun consents to a lustful man taking hold of her anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:7.4
Pali: bhikkhu kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a monk makes physical contact, body with body, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:7.5
Pali: kāyena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, with one’s own body, one makes physical contact with something connected to their body, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:7.6
Pali: kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, with something connected to one’s own body, one makes physical contact with something connected to their body, one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:7.7
Pali: aṅgulipatodake pācittiyaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): for tickling, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:7.8
Pali: kāyasaṁsaggaṁ samāpajjanapaccayā imā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:8.1
Pali: Mātugāmaṁ duṭṭhullāhi vācāhi obhāsanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of speaking indecently to a woman, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:8.2
Pali: vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, referring to the anus or the vagina, one praises or disparages, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:8.3
Pali: vaccamaggaṁ passāvamaggaṁ ṭhapetvā adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, referring to any part below the collar bone but above the knees, apart from the anus or the vagina, one praises or disparages, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:8.4
Pali: kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ ādissa vaṇṇampi bhaṇati avaṇṇampi bhaṇati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when, referring to anything connected to the body, one praises or disparages, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:9.1
Pali: Attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of encouraging the satisfaction of one’s own desires, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:9.2
Pali: mātugāmassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one encourages a woman to satisfy one’s own desires, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:9.3
Pali: paṇḍakassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one encourages a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i> to satisfy one’s own desires, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:9.4
Pali: tiracchānagatassa santike attakāmapāricariyāya vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one encourages an animal to satisfy one’s own desires, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:10.1
Pali: Sañcarittaṁ samāpajjanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of acting as a matchmaker, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:10.2
Pali: paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati paccāharati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one accepts the mission, finds out the response, and reports back, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:10.3
Pali: paṭiggaṇhāti vīmaṁsati na paccāharati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one accepts the mission, and finds out the response, but does not report back, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:10.4
Pali: paṭiggaṇhāti na vīmaṁsati na paccāharati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when one accepts the mission, but neither finds out the response, nor reports back, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:11.1
Pali: Saññācikāya kuṭiṁ kārāpanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of having a hut built by means of begging, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:11.2
Pali: kārāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is having it built, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct;

Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when there is one piece left to complete the hut, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:11.4
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last piece is finished, one commits an offense entailing suspension.

Pali: Mahallakaṁ vihāraṁ kārāpanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of having a large dwelling built, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:12.2
Pali: kārāpeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when one is having it built, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct;

Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when there is one piece left to complete the hut, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:12.4
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last piece is finished, one commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:13.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of groundlessly charging a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:13.2
Pali: anokāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, without having gotten his permission, one speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, one commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:13.3
Pali: okāsaṁ kārāpetvā akkosādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti omasavādassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when, having gotten his permission, one speaks with the aim of abusing him, one commits an offense for abusive speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:14.1
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ aññabhāgiyassa adhikaraṇassa kiñci desaṁ lesamattaṁ upādāya pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁsanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of charging a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, using an unrelated legal issue as a pretext, one commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:14.2
Pali: anokāsaṁ kārāpetvā cāvanādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti saṅghādisesena dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, without having gotten his permission, one speaks with the aim of making him leave the monastic life, one commits one offense entailing suspension and one offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:14.3
Pali: okāsaṁ kārāpetvā akkosādhippāyo vadeti, āpatti omasavādassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when, having gotten his permission, one speaks with the aim of abusing him, one commits an offense for abusive speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:15.1
Pali: Saṅghabhedako bhikkhu yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of not stopping when pressed for the third time, a monk who is pursuing schism in the Sangha commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:15.2
Pali: ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, he commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:15.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:15.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:16.1
Pali: Bhedakānuvattakā bhikkhū yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjanti—
English (brahmali, published): As a result of not stopping when pressed for the third time, monks who side with a monk who is pursuing schism commit three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:16.2
Pali: ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, they commit an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:16.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, they commit a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr1.10:16.4

English (brahmali, published): One commits one kind of offense: 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.3
Pali: Dukkaṭaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.4
Pali: anādariyaṁ paṭicca udake uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā karaṇapaccayā imaṁ ekaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.5
Pali: Sā āpatti catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajati? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to this offense, to how many of the four kinds of failure does it belong? 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.6
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): In how many of the seven classes of offenses is it found? 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses does it originate? 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.8
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four kinds of legal issues does it belong? 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.9
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues is it settled? 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.10
Pali: Sā āpatti catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ ekaṁ vipattiṁ bhajati—
English (brahmali, published): It belongs to one kind of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.11
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.12
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ ekena āpattikkhandhena saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): It is found in one class of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.13
Pali: dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.14
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.15
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.16
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It belongs to legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.17
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): It is settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:2.18
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): it may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or it may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:3.1
Pali: Samuccayavāro niṭṭhito aṭṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The summary of the previous six sections, the eighth, is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:3.2
Pali: Aṭṭhapaccayavārā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The eight sections on “as a result of” are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:3.3
Pali: Mahāvibhaṅge soḷasamahāvārā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixteen great sections in The Great Analysis are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr1.16:4.1
Pali: Bhikkhuvibhaṅgamahāvāro niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The great section of the Monks’ Analysis is finished.

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena bhikkhunīnaṁ pañcamaṁ pārājikaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The nuns’ fifth offense entailing expulsion was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.4
Pali: Sundarīnandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Sundarīnandā. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.6
Pali: Sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The lustful nun Sundarīnandā consenting to a lustful man making physical contact with her. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.7
Pali: Atthi tattha paññatti anupaññatti anuppannapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there a rule, an addition to the rule, an unprompted rule?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.8
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.9
Pali: Anupaññatti anuppannapaññatti tasmiṁ natthi. 
English (brahmali, published): There is no addition to the rule. There is no unprompted rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.10
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti, padesapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that applies everywhere or in a particular place?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.11
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Everywhere. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.12
Pali: Sādhāraṇapaññatti, asādhāraṇapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that the monks and nuns have in common or not in common?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.13
Pali: Asādhāraṇapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Not in common. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.14
Pali: Ekatopaññatti, ubhatopaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule for one Sangha or for both?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.15
Pali: Ekatopaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.16
Pali: Catunnaṁ pātimokkhuddesānaṁ katthogadhaṁ kattha pariyāpannanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “In which of the four ways of reciting the Monastic Code is it contained and included?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.17
Pali: Nidānogadhaṁ nidānapariyāpannaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In the introduction. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.18
Pali: Katamena uddesena uddesaṁ āgacchatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “In which recitation is it included?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.19
Pali: Dutiyena uddesena uddesaṁ āgacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): In the second recitation. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.20
Pali: Catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ katamā vipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four kinds of failure does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.21
Pali: Sīlavipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Failure in morality. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.22
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katamo āpattikkhandhoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the seven classes of offenses does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.23
Pali: Pārājikāpattikkhandho. 
English (brahmali, published): The class of offenses entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.24
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhātīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses does it originate?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.25
Pali: Ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): It originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.26
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.27
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four kinds of legal issues does it belong?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.28
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.29
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues is it settled?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.30
Pali: Dvīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Through two of them: 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.31
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.32
Pali: Ko tattha vinayo, ko tattha abhivinayoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the Monastic Law there? What is concerned with the Monastic Law there?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.33
Pali: Paññatti vinayo, vibhatti abhivinayo. 
English (brahmali, published): The rule is the Monastic Law. Its analysis is concerned with the Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.34
Pali: Kiṁ tattha pātimokkhaṁ, kiṁ tattha adhipātimokkhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the Monastic Code there? What is concerned with the Monastic Code there?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.35
Pali: Paññatti pātimokkhaṁ, vibhatti adhipātimokkhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The rule is the Monastic Code. Its analysis is concerned with the Monastic Code. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.36
Pali: Kā vipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is failure?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.37
Pali: Asaṁvaro vipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Lack of restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.38
Pali: Kā sampattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is success?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.39
Pali: Saṁvaro sampatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.40
Pali: Kā paṭipattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the practice?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.41
Pali: “Na evarūpaṁ karissāmī”ti yāvajīvaṁ āpāṇakoṭikaṁ samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu. 
English (brahmali, published): Thinking, “I won’t do such a thing,” one undertakes to train in the training rules for life. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.42
Pali: Kati atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ pañcamaṁ pārājikaṁ paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “For how many reasons did the Buddha lay down the nun’s fifth offense entailing expulsion?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.43
Pali: Dasa atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā bhikkhunīnaṁ pañcamaṁ pārājikaṁ paññattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): He laid it down for the following ten reasons: 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.44
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya, dummaṅkūnaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya, samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya, appasannānaṁ pasādāya, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā, vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad nuns, for the ease of good nuns, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.45
Pali: Kā sikkhantīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who are those who train?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.46
Pali: Sekkhā ca puthujjanakalyāṇikā ca sikkhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They are the trainees and the good ordinary people. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.47
Pali: Kā sikkhitasikkhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who have finished the training?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.48
Pali: Arahantiyo sikkhitasikkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): The perfected ones. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.49
Pali: Kattha ṭhitanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Established in what?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.50
Pali: Sikkhākāmāsu ṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In fondness for the training. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.51
Pali: Kā dhārentīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who master it?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.52
Pali: Yāsaṁ vattati tā dhārenti. 
English (brahmali, published): Those who learn it. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.53
Pali: Kassa vacananti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whose pronouncement was it?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.54
Pali: Bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa. 
English (brahmali, published): It was the pronouncement of the Buddha, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.55
Pali: Kenābhatanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Who handed it down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:2.56
Pali: Paramparābhataṁ—
English (brahmali, published): The lineage: 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:3.1
Pali: Upāli dāsako ceva, 
English (brahmali, published): “Upāli and Dāsaka, 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:3.2
Pali: soṇako siggavo tathā; 
English (brahmali, published): Soṇaka and so Siggava; 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:3.3
Pali: Moggaliputtena pañcamā, 
English (brahmali, published): With Moggaliputta as the fifth—

pli-tv-pvr2.1:3.4
Pali: ete jambusirivhaye. 
English (brahmali, published): These were in India, the land named after the glorious rose apple. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:4.1
Pali: Tato mahindo iṭṭiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): Then Mahinda, Iṭṭiya, 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:4.2
Pali: uttiyo sambalo tathā; 
English (brahmali, published): Uttiya and so Sambala; 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:4.3
Pali: Bhaddanāmo ca paṇḍito. 
English (brahmali, published): And the wise one named Bhadda: 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:5.1
Pali: Ete nāgā mahāpaññā, 
English (brahmali, published): These mighty beings of great wisdom, 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:5.2
Pali: jambudīpā idhāgatā; 
English (brahmali, published): Came here from India; 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:5.3
Pali: Vinayaṁ te vācayiṁsu, 
English (brahmali, published): They taught the Collection on Monastic Law, 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:5.4
Pali: piṭakaṁ tambapaṇṇiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): In Sri Lanka. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:6.1
Pali: Nikāye pañca vācesuṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): And the five Collections of Discourses, 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:6.2
Pali: satta ceva pakaraṇe; 
English (brahmali, published): And the seven works of philosophy; 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:6.3
Pali: Tato ariṭṭho medhāvī, 
English (brahmali, published): Then Ariṭṭha the discerning, 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:6.4
Pali: tissadatto ca paṇḍito. 
English (brahmali, published): And the wise Tissadatta. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:7.1
Pali: Visārado kāḷasumano, 
English (brahmali, published): The confident Kālasumana, 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:7.2
Pali: thero ca dīghanāmako; 
English (brahmali, published): And the senior monk named Dīgha; 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:7.3
Pali: Dīghasumano ca paṇḍito. 
English (brahmali, published): And the wise Dīghasumana. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:8.1
Pali: Punadeva kāḷasumano, 
English (brahmali, published): Another Kālasumana,

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena bhikkhunīnaṁ chaṭṭhaṁ pārājikaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “The nuns’ sixth offense entailing expulsion was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.4
Pali: Thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.6
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodesi na gaṇassa ārocesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā, knowing that a nun had committed an offense entailing expulsion, neither confronting her herself nor telling the community. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:19.9
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. …

English (brahmali, published): 2. The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:25.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena ussayavādikāya bhikkhuniyā aḍḍaṁ karontiyā saṅghādiseso kattha paññatto? 
English (brahmali, published): “The offense entailing suspension for a litigious nun initiating a lawsuit was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?

English (brahmali, published): Whom is it about?

English (brahmali, published): Who handed it down?”

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:26.6

English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā taking legal action. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:26.7
Pali: Atthi tattha paññatti, anupaññatti, anuppannapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there a rule, an addition to the rule, an unprompted rule?”

English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:26.9
Pali: Anupaññatti anuppannapaññatti tasmiṁ natthi. 
English (brahmali, published): There is no addition to the rule. There is no unprompted rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:26.10
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti, padesapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that applies everywhere or in a particular place?”

Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Everywhere. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:26.12

Pali: Asādhāraṇapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Not in common. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:26.14
Pali: Ekatopaññatti, ubhatopaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule for one Sangha or for both?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:26.15
Pali: Ekatopaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one.

pli-tv-pvr2.1:32.1
Pali: Samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osārentiyā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension for readmitting a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community. English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā readmitting a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community.

pli-tv-pvr2.1:42.1

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī.

English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:63.4
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhuniyo ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): A number of nuns.

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:63.6
