pli-tv-pvr2.1:139.1
Pali: “Alaṁ tāva te, ayye, vuṭṭhāpitenā”ti vuccamānāya “sādhū”ti paṭissuṇitvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajjantiyā pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for verbally consenting when being told, ‘Venerable, you’ve given enough full admissions for now,’ but then criticizing it afterwards. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:139.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:139.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:139.4
Pali: Caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Caṇḍakālī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:139.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:139.6
Pali: Caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī “alaṁ tāva te, ayye, vuṭṭhāpitenā”ti vuccamānā “sādhū”ti paṭissuṇitvā pacchā khīyanadhammaṁ āpajji, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Caṇḍakālī verbally consenting when being told, “Venerable, you’ve given enough full admissions for now,” but then criticizing it afterwards. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:139.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:139.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ tīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in three ways: … 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.1
Pali: Sikkhamānaṁ—“sace me tvaṁ, ayye, cīvaraṁ dassasi, evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti vatvā neva vuṭṭhāpentiyā na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karontiyā pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for telling a trainee nun, ‘If you give me a robe, venerable, I’ll give you the full admission,’ but then neither giving her the full admission nor making any effort to have her fully admitted. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.4
Pali: Thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.6
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī sikkhamānaṁ—“sace me tvaṁ, ayye, cīvaraṁ dassasi, evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti vatvā neva vuṭṭhāpesi na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ akāsi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā telling a trainee nun, “If you give me a robe, venerable, I’ll give you the full admission,” but then neither giving her the full admission nor making any effort to have her fully admitted. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:140.9
Pali: dhuranikkhepe …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): through abandoning one’s duty. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:141.1
Pali: Sikkhamānaṁ—“sace maṁ tvaṁ, ayye, dve vassāni anubandhissasi, evāhaṁ taṁ vuṭṭhāpessāmī”ti vatvā neva vuṭṭhāpentiyā na vuṭṭhāpanāya ussukkaṁ karontiyā pācittiyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing confession for telling a trainee nun, ‘If you follow me for two years, Venerable, I’ll give you the full admission,’ but then neither giving her the full admission nor making any effort to have her fully admitted. Where was it laid down?”

English (brahmali, published): The subchapter on sunshades and sandals

English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing acknowledgment for asking for oil and then eating it. Where was it laid down?”

Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī.

English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): The nuns from the group of six. 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:176.5

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:176.6

English (brahmali, published): Meat, milk, and curd:

English (brahmali, published): The eight offenses entailing acknowledgment, 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:185.6
Pali: sayaṁ buddhena desitāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Taught by the Buddha himself.” 

pli-tv-pvr2.1:186.1

pli-tv-pvr2.1:186.2
Pali: Katthapaññattivāro niṭṭhito paṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): Questions and answers on the nuns’ Monastic Code and its analysis, the first, are finished.

Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium

Pali: Bhikkhunivibhaṅga 
English (brahmali, published): The Nuns’ Analysis

Pali: Paṭhamabhāga 
English (brahmali, published): Part one

Pali: 2.2 Katāpattivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The number of offenses within each rule 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:0.5
Pali: 1. Pārājikakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:1.1
Pali: Avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantī kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When a lustful nun consents to a lustful man making physical contact with her, how many kinds of offenses does she commit? 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:1.2
Pali: Avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): She commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:1.3
Pali: Adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she consents to him taking hold of her anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:1.4
Pali: ubbhakkhakaṁ adhojāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she consents to him taking hold of her above the collar bone or below the knees, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:1.5
Pali: kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when she consents to him taking hold of something connected to her body, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:1.6
Pali: avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantī imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:2.1
Pali: Vajjappaṭicchādikā bhikkhunī vajjaṁ paṭicchādentī kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When a nun conceals an offense, how many kinds of offenses does she commit? 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:2.2
Pali: Vajjappaṭicchādikā bhikkhunī vajjaṁ paṭicchādentī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): She commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:2.3
Pali: Jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she knowingly conceals an offense entailing expulsion, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:2.4
Pali: vematikā paṭicchādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, being unsure, she conceals it, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:2.5
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when she conceals a failure in conduct, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:2.6
Pali: vajjappaṭicchādikā bhikkhunī vajjaṁ paṭicchādentī imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:3.1
Pali: Ukkhittānuvattikā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjantī kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When a nun takes sides with one who has been ejected and does not stop when pressed for the third time, how many kinds of offenses does she commit? 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:3.2
Pali: Ukkhittānuvattikā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjantī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): She commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:3.3
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:3.4
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:3.5
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:3.6
Pali: ukkhittānuvattikā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjantī imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:4.1
Pali: Aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūrentī kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When fulfilling the eight parts, how many kinds of offenses does she commit? 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:4.2
Pali: Aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūrentī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): She commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:4.3
Pali: Purisena—“itthannāmaṁ okāsaṁ āgacchā”ti vuttā gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she goes to such-and-such a place when told by a man to do so, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:4.4
Pali: purisassa hatthapāsaṁ okkantamatte āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she enters within arm’s reach of the man, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:4.5
Pali: aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūreti, āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): when she fulfills the eight parts, she commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:4.6
Pali: aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūrentī imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:5.1
Pali: Pārājikā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The offenses entailing expulsion are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:6.0
Pali: 2. Saṅghādisesakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The chapter on offenses entailing suspension 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:6.1
Pali: Ussayavādikā bhikkhunī aḍḍaṁ karontī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When a litigious nun initiates a lawsuit, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:6.2
Pali: Ekassa āroceti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she tells one other person, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:6.3
Pali: dutiyassa āroceti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she tells a second person, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:6.4
Pali: aḍḍapariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the lawsuit is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:7.1
Pali: Coriṁ vuṭṭhāpentī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When giving the full admission to a female criminal, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:7.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:7.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:7.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:8.1
Pali: Ekā gāmantaraṁ gacchantī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When walking to the next inhabited area by herself, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:8.2
Pali: Gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she is in the process of going, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:8.3
Pali: paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ parikkhepaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she crosses the boundary with her first foot, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:8.4
Pali: dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when she crosses with her second foot, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:9.1
Pali: Samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osārentī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When readmitting a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:9.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:9.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:9.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:10.1
Pali: Avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā bhuñjantī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When a lustful nun eats fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:10.2
Pali: “Khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:10.3
Pali: ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): for every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:10.4
Pali: udakadantaponaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:11.1
Pali: “Kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā. Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā, taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti uyyojentī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When urging a nun on, saying, “Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you,” she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:11.2
Pali: Tassā vacanena khādissāmi bhuñjissāmīti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, because of her statement, the other nun receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:11.3
Pali: ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): for every mouthful swallowed, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:11.4
Pali: bhojanapariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the meal is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension.

English (brahmali, published): 3. The chapter on relinquishment 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:17.1

pli-tv-pvr2.2:18.1

pli-tv-pvr2.2:18.3
Pali: bhājāpite nissaggiyaṁ pācittiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): when she has distributed it, she commits an offense entailing relinquishment and confession.

English (brahmali, published): when she is done, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:35.1
Pali: Bhikkhussa bhuñjantassa pānīyena vā vidhūpanena vā upatiṭṭhantī dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When attending on a monk who is eating with drinking water or a fan, she commits two kinds of offenses:

English (brahmali, published): when standing beyond arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

English (brahmali, published): for every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:37.1
Pali: Uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā saṅkāraṁ vā vighāsaṁ vā tirokuṭṭe vā tiropākāre vā chaḍḍentī dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When disposing of feces, urine, trash, or food scraps over a wall or over an encircling wall, she commits two kinds of offenses:

pli-tv-pvr2.2:37.3
Pali: chaḍḍite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when she has discarded it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:38.1
Pali: Uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā saṅkāraṁ vā vighāsaṁ vā harite chaḍḍentī dve āpattiyo āpajjati.

pli-tv-pvr2.2:38.2
Pali: Chaḍḍeti, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when she is in the process of discarding it, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:38.3
Pali: chaḍḍite āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when she has discarded it, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:39.1

English (brahmali, published): when she is in the process of going, she commits an offense of wrong conduct;

Pali: hatthapāsaṁ vijahitvā tiṭṭhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when she stands beyond arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:43.1
Pali: Ajjhokāse purisena saddhiṁ ekenekā santiṭṭhantī dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When standing alone with a man out in the open, she commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:43.2
Pali: Hatthapāse tiṭṭhati, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she stands within arm’s reach, she commits an offense entailing confession;

Pali: hatthapāsaṁ vijahitvā tiṭṭhati, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when she stands beyond arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct.

English (brahmali, published): when she stands beyond arm’s reach, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:45.1
Pali: Purebhattaṁ kulāni upasaṅkamitvā āsane nisīditvā sāmike anāpucchā pakkamantī dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When visiting families before the meal, sitting down on a seat, and then departing without informing the owners, she commits two kinds of offenses:

English (brahmali, published): when she goes beyond with the second foot, she commits an offense entailing confession.

English (brahmali, published): when she is seated, she commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:47.1

English (brahmali, published): when she is seated, she commits an offense entailing confession.

Pali: Rattandhakāravaggo dutiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The second subchapter on the dark of the night is finished.

pli-tv-pvr2.2:78.1

English (brahmali, published): when she crosses the boundary with her first foot, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.2:81.3
Pali: dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when she crosses with her second foot, she commits an offense entailing confession.

Pali: pade pade āpatti pācittiyassa.

Pali: 2.3 Vipattivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The classes of failure to which each rule belongs 

pli-tv-pvr2.3:1.1
Pali: Avassutāya bhikkhuniyā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantiyā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to the offenses for a lustful nun consenting to a lustful man making physical contact with her, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr2.3:1.2
Pali: Avassutāya bhikkhuniyā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantiyā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr2.3:1.3
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.3:2.1
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjantiyā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to the offenses for asking for curd and then eating it, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr2.3:2.2
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjantiyā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ ekaṁ vipattiṁ bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to one kind of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr2.3:2.3
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.3:3.1
Pali: Vipattivāro niṭṭhito tatiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The classes of failure to which each rule belongs, the third, are finished.

English (brahmali, published): The Compendium

English (brahmali, published): Part one 

pli-tv-pvr2.4:0.4
Pali: 2.4 Saṅgahavāra 
English (brahmali, published): The classes of offenses to which each rule belongs 

pli-tv-pvr2.4:1.1
Pali: Avassutāya bhikkhuniyā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantiyā āpattiyo sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to the offenses for a lustful nun consenting to a lustful man making physical contact with her, in how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr2.4:1.2
Pali: Avassutāya bhikkhuniyā avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantiyā āpattiyo sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ tīhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in three: 

pli-tv-pvr2.4:1.3
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.4:2.1
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjantiyā āpattiyo sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to the offenses for asking for curd and then eating it, in how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr2.4:2.2
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjantiyā āpattiyo sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ dvīhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in two: 

pli-tv-pvr2.4:2.3
Pali: siyā pāṭidesanīyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing acknowledgment; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.4:3.1
Pali: Saṅgahavāro niṭṭhito catuttho. 
English (brahmali, published): The classes of offenses to which each rule belongs, the fourth, are finished.

pli-tv-pvr2.5:2.3

English (brahmali, published): from body, not from speech or mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr2.5:2.4
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca samuṭṭhanti, na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and speech, not from mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr2.5:2.5
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na vācato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech; or 

pli-tv-pvr2.5:2.6
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr2.5:3.1
Pali: Samuṭṭhānavāro niṭṭhito pañcamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The number of originations of each offense, the fifth, are finished.

pli-tv-pvr2.8:1.1
Pali: Avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantī kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): When a lustful nun consents to a lustful man making physical contact with her, how many kinds of offenses does she commit? 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:1.2
Pali: Avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantī tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): She commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:1.3
Pali: Adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she consents to him taking hold of her anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:1.4
Pali: ubbhakkhakaṁ adhojāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she consents to him taking hold of her above the collar bone or below the knees, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:1.5
Pali: kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when she consents to him taking hold of something connected to her body, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:1.6
Pali: avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyantī imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.2
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): In how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.3
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses do they originate? 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.4
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four kinds of legal issues do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.6
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.7
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.8
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ tīhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in three classes of offenses:

Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.10
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.11
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.12
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ āpattādhikaraṇaṁ.

pli-tv-pvr2.8:2.13

English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.8:3.1
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjantī kati āpattiyo āpajjati?

pli-tv-pvr2.8:3.2

English (brahmali, published): She commits two kinds of offenses:

pli-tv-pvr2.9:2.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ.

pli-tv-pvr2.9:6.1
Pali: Vajjappaṭicchādanapaccayā pārājikaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing expulsion that is a result of concealing an offense. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:6.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:6.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:6.4
Pali: Thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:6.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:6.6
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ ajjhāpannaṁ bhikkhuniṁ nevattanā paṭicodesi na gaṇassa ārocesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā, knowing that a nun had committed an offense entailing expulsion, neither confronting her herself nor telling the community. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:6.7

pli-tv-pvr2.9:9.0
Pali: 2. Saṅghādisesakaṇḍādi 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The chapter on offenses entailing suspension, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:9.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena ussayavādikāya bhikkhuniyā aḍḍaṁ karaṇapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññatto? 
English (brahmali, published): “The offense entailing suspension that is a result of a litigious nun initiating a lawsuit was laid down by the Buddha who knows and sees, the Perfected One, the fully Awakened One. Where was it laid down?

Pali: Kaṁ ārabbha? 
English (brahmali, published): Whom is it about? 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:9.3
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusmiṁ …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): What is it about? … 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:9.4
Pali: kenābhatanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Who handed it down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.1
Pali: Yaṁ tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena ussayavādikāya bhikkhuniyā aḍḍaṁ karaṇapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti?

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā taking legal action. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.7
Pali: Atthi tattha paññatti, anupaññatti, anuppannapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is there a rule, an addition to the rule, an unprompted rule?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.8
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.9
Pali: Anupaññatti anuppannapaññatti tasmiṁ natthi. 
English (brahmali, published): There is no addition to the rule. There is no unprompted rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.10
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti, padesapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that applies everywhere or in a particular place?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.11
Pali: Sabbatthapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Everywhere. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.12
Pali: Sādhāraṇapaññatti, asādhāraṇapaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule that the monks and nuns have in common or not in common?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.13
Pali: Asādhāraṇapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): Not in common. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.14
Pali: Ekatopaññatti, ubhatopaññattīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it a rule for one Sangha or for both?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.15
Pali: Ekatopaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): For one. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:10.16

Pali: Nidānogadhaṁ nidānapariyāpannaṁ.

pli-tv-pvr2.9:16.1
Pali: Samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāraṇapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension that is a result of readmitting a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:16.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:16.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:16.4

English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:16.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:16.6
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāresi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā readmitting a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:16.7

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.1
Pali: Avassutāya bhikkhuniyā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā bhuñjanapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension that is a result of a lustful nun eating fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.4

English (brahmali, published): The nun Sundarīnandā. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.6
Pali: Sundarīnandā bhikkhunī avassutā avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato āmisaṁ paṭiggahesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Sundarīnandā, being lustful, receiving food directly from a lustful man. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:17.9
Pali: paṭhamapārājike …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): … (as in the first offense entailing expulsion) … 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:18.1
Pali: “Kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā. Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti uyyojanapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension that is a result of urging a nun on, saying, ‘Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you.’ Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:18.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:18.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:18.4
Pali: Aññataraṁ bhikkhuniṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): A certain nun. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:18.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:18.6
Pali: Aññatarā bhikkhunī—“kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā. Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti uyyojesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A certain nun urging a nun on, saying, “Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you.” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:18.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:18.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ tīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in three ways: … 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.1
Pali: Kupitāya anattamanāya bhikkhuniyā yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension that is a result of an angry nun not stopping when pressed for the third time. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.4
Pali: Caṇḍakāḷiṁ bhikkhuniṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Caṇḍakālī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.6
Pali: Caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca—“buddhaṁ paccācikkhāmi, dhammaṁ paccācikkhāmi, saṅghaṁ paccācikkhāmi, sikkhaṁ paccācikkhāmī”ti, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Caṇḍakālī saying in anger, “I renounce the Buddha, I renounce the Teaching, I renounce the Sangha, I renounce the training!” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:19.9
Pali: dhuranikkhepe …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): through abandoning one’s duty. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.1
Pali: Kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatāya bhikkhuniyā yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension that is a result of a nun who has lost a legal case not stopping when pressed for the third time. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.3

English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.4

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.6
Pali: Caṇḍakāḷī bhikkhunī kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā kupitā anattamanā evaṁ avaca—“chandagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, dosagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, mohagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo, bhayagāminiyo ca bhikkhuniyo”ti, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Caṇḍakālī, who had lost a legal case, saying in anger, “The nuns are acting out of favoritism, ill will, confusion, and fear.” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—

pli-tv-pvr2.9:20.9
Pali: dhuranikkhepe …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): through abandoning one’s duty. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:21.1
Pali: Saṁsaṭṭhānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension that is a result of socializing nuns not stopping when pressed for the third time. Where was it laid down?”

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:21.6
Pali: Sambahulā bhikkhuniyo saṁsaṭṭhā vihariṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A number of nuns socializing. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:21.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:21.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—

pli-tv-pvr2.9:21.9

English (brahmali, published): through abandoning one’s duty. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.1
Pali: “Saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharitthā”ti uyyojentiyā yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā saṅghādiseso kattha paññattoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing suspension that is a result of urging nuns on in this way: ‘Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately,’ and then not stopping when pressed for the third time. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññatto. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.4
Pali: Thullanandaṁ bhikkhuniṁ ārabbha. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.5
Pali: Kismiṁ vatthusminti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.6
Pali: Thullanandā bhikkhunī—“saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha. Mā tumhe nānā viharitthā”ti uyyojesi, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nun Thullanandā urging the nuns on, saying, “Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately.” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:22.9
Pali: dhuranikkhepe …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): through abandoning one’s duty. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:23.1
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjanapaccayā pāṭidesanīyaṁ kattha paññattanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “There is an offense entailing acknowledgment that is a result of asking for curd and then eating it. Where was it laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:23.2
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ paññattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:23.3
Pali: Kaṁ ārabbhāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Whom is it about?”

English (brahmali, published): The nuns from the group of six.

English (brahmali, published): “What is it about?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:23.6
Pali: Chabbaggiyā bhikkhuniyo dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjiṁsu, tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns from the group of six asking for curd and then eating it. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:23.7
Pali: Ekā paññatti, ekā anupaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is one rule. There is one addition to the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:23.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ catūhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhāti …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Of the six kinds of originations of offenses, it originates in four ways: … 

pli-tv-pvr2.9:23.9
Pali: Katthapaññattivāro niṭṭhito paṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): Questions and answers on the nuns’ Monastic Code and its analysis, the first, are finished.

Pali: 2.10 Katāpattivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The number of offenses within each rule 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.0
Pali: 1. Pārājikakaṇḍa 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.1
Pali: Kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of consenting to physical contact? 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.2
Pali: Kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyanapaccayā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits five kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.3
Pali: Avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a lustful nun consents to a lustful man taking hold of her anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.4
Pali: bhikkhu kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a monk makes physical contact, body with body, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.5
Pali: kāyena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, with one’s own body, one makes physical contact with something connected to their body, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.6
Pali: kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, with something connected to one’s own body, one makes physical contact with something connected to their body, one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.7
Pali: aṅgulipatodake pācittiyaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): for tickling, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:1.8
Pali: kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyanapaccayā imā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:2.1
Pali: Vajjappaṭicchādanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of concealing an offense? 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:2.2
Pali: Vajjappaṭicchādanapaccayā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits four kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:2.3
Pali: Bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a nun knowingly conceals an offense entailing expulsion, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:2.4
Pali: vematikā paṭicchādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, being unsure, she conceals it, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:2.5
Pali: bhikkhu saṅghādisesaṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a monk conceals an offense entailing suspension, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:2.6
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when one conceals a failure in conduct, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:2.7
Pali: vajjappaṭicchādanapaccayā imā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:3.1
Pali: Yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of not stopping when pressed for the third time?” 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:3.2
Pali: Yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits five kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:3.3
Pali: Ukkhittānuvattikā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjati, ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when a nun takes sides with one who has been ejected and does not stop when pressed for the third time, then after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:3.4
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:3.5
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:3.6
Pali: bhedakānuvattikā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a nun sides with a monk who is pursuing schism and she does not stop when pressed for the third time, she commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:3.7
Pali: pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): when not giving up a bad view after being pressed for the third time, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:3.8
Pali: yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā imā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:4.1
Pali: Aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūraṇapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does she commit as a result of fulfilling the eight parts? 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:4.2
Pali: Aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūraṇapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): She commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:4.3
Pali: Purisena—“itthannāmaṁ okāsaṁ āgacchā”ti vuttā gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she goes to such-and-such a place when told by a man to do so, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:4.4
Pali: purisassa hatthapāsaṁ okkantamatte āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she enters within arm’s reach of the man, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:4.5
Pali: aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūreti, āpatti pārājikassa—
English (brahmali, published): when she fulfills the eight parts, she commits an offense entailing expulsion. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:4.6
Pali: aṭṭhamaṁ vatthuṁ paripūraṇapaccayā imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:4.7
Pali: Pārājikā niṭṭhitā. 
English (brahmali, published): The offenses entailing expulsion are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:5.0
Pali: 2. Saṅghādisesakaṇḍādi 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The chapter on offenses entailing suspension, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:5.1
Pali: Ussayavādikā bhikkhunī aḍḍaṁ karaṇapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of initiating a lawsuit, a litigious nun commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:5.2
Pali: Ekassa āroceti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she tells one other person, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:5.3
Pali: dutiyassa āroceti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she tells a second person, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:5.4
Pali: aḍḍapariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the lawsuit is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:6.1
Pali: Coriṁ vuṭṭhāpanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of giving the full admission to a female criminal, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:6.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:6.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:6.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:7.1
Pali: Ekā gāmantaraṁ gamanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of walking to the next inhabited area by herself, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:7.2
Pali: Gacchati, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she is in the process of going, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:7.3
Pali: paṭhamaṁ pādaṁ parikkhepaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she crosses the boundary with her first foot, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:7.4
Pali: dutiyaṁ pādaṁ atikkāmeti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when she crosses with her second foot, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:8.1
Pali: Samaggena saṅghena ukkhittaṁ bhikkhuniṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena anapaloketvā kārakasaṅghaṁ anaññāya gaṇassa chandaṁ osāraṇapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of readmitting—without first getting permission from the Sangha that did the legal procedure and without the consent of the community—a nun who had been ejected by a unanimous Sangha in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:8.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:8.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:8.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:9.1
Pali: Avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā bhuñjanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of eating fresh or cooked food after receiving it directly from a lustful man, a lustful nun commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:9.2
Pali: “Khādissāmi bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she receives fresh or cooked food with the intention of eating it, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:9.3
Pali: ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): for every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:9.4
Pali: udakadantaponaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): if she receives water or a tooth cleaner, she commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:10.1
Pali: “Kiṁ te, ayye, eso purisapuggalo karissati avassuto vā anavassuto vā, yato tvaṁ anavassutā. Iṅgha, ayye, yaṁ te eso purisapuggalo deti khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā taṁ tvaṁ sahatthā paṭiggahetvā khāda vā bhuñja vā”ti, uyyojanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of urging a nun on, saying, “Venerable, what can this man do to you, whether he has lust or not, if you’re without? Go on, venerable, receive it with your own hands and then eat whatever fresh or cooked food he gives you,” she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:10.2
Pali: Tassā vacanena khādissāmi bhuñjissāmīti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, because of her statement, the other nun receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:10.3
Pali: ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): for every mouthful swallowed, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:10.4
Pali: bhojanapariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the meal is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:11.1
Pali: Kupitā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of not stopping when pressed for the third time, an angry nun commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:11.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:11.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:11.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:12.1
Pali: Kismiñcideva adhikaraṇe paccākatā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of not stopping when pressed for the third time, a nun who has lost a legal case commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:12.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:12.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:12.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:13.1
Pali: Saṁsaṭṭhā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of not stopping when pressed for the third time, a socializing nun commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:13.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:13.3
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:13.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:14.1
Pali: “Saṁsaṭṭhāva, ayye, tumhe viharatha, mā tumhe nānā viharitthā”ti uyyojentī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of urging the nuns on, saying, “Venerables, you should socialize. Don’t live separately,” and not stopping when pressed for the third time, she commits three kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:14.2
Pali: Ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:14.3

pli-tv-pvr2.10:14.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing suspension. 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:15.1
Pali: Dasa saṅghādisesā niṭṭhitā …pe… . 
English (brahmali, published): The ten rules entailing suspension are finished. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:16.1
Pali: (Yathā heṭṭhā tathā vitthāretabbā paccayameva nānākaraṇaṁ) 
English (brahmali, published): (To be expanded as above, <a href='https://suttacentral.net/pli-tv-pvr2.2#17.0'>Pvr 2.2:17.0</a>–141.3, with the only difference being the addition of “as a result of”.) 

pli-tv-pvr2.10:17.1

pli-tv-pvr2.10:17.2

pli-tv-pvr2.10:17.3

pli-tv-pvr2.10:17.4

pli-tv-pvr2.12:1.1
Pali: Kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyanapaccayā āpattiyo sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to the offenses that are a result of consenting to physical contact, in how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr2.12:1.2

English (brahmali, published): They are found in five: 

pli-tv-pvr2.12:1.3
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.12:2.1
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjanapaccayā āpattiyo sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā?

pli-tv-pvr2.12:2.2
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjanapaccayā āpattiyo sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ dvīhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in two: 

pli-tv-pvr2.12:2.3
Pali: siyā pāṭidesanīyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing acknowledgment; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.12:3.1
Pali: Saṅgahavāro niṭṭhito catuttho. 
English (brahmali, published): The classes of offenses to which each rule belongs, the fourth, are finished.

Pali: 2.16 Samuccayavāra 
English (brahmali, published): Summary of the previous six sections 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:1.1
Pali: Kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of consenting to physical contact, how many kinds of offenses does one commit? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:1.2
Pali: Kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyanapaccayā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One commits five kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:1.3
Pali: Avassutā bhikkhunī avassutassa purisapuggalassa adhakkhakaṁ ubbhajāṇumaṇḍalaṁ gahaṇaṁ sādiyati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a lustful nun consents to a lustful man taking hold of her anywhere below the collar bone but above the knees, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:1.4
Pali: bhikkhu kāyena kāyaṁ āmasati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a monk makes physical contact, body with body, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:1.5
Pali: kāyena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, with one’s own body, one makes physical contact with something connected to their body, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:1.6
Pali: kāyapaṭibaddhena kāyapaṭibaddhaṁ āmasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, with something connected to one’s own body, one makes physical contact with something connected to their body, one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:1.7
Pali: aṅgulipatodake pācittiyaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): for tickling, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:1.8
Pali: kāyasaṁsaggaṁ sādiyanapaccayā imā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.2
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): In how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.3
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses do they originate? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.4
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four kinds of legal issues do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.6
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.7
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.8
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ pañcahi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in five classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.9
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.10
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.11
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.12
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.13
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:2.14
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. … 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:3.1
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjanapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): As a result of asking for curd and then eating it, how many kinds of offenses does she commit? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:3.2
Pali: Dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjanapaccayā dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): She commits two kinds of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:3.3
Pali: “Bhuñjissāmī”ti paṭiggaṇhāti, āpatti dukkaṭassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when she receives it with the intention of eating it, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:3.4
Pali: ajjhohāre ajjhohāre āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa—
English (brahmali, published): for every mouthful swallowed, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:3.5
Pali: dadhiṁ viññāpetvā bhuñjanapaccayā imā dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.2
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): In how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.3
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses do they originate? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.4
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four kinds of legal issues do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.6
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ ekaṁ vipattiṁ bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to one kind of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.7
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.8
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ dvīhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in two classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.9
Pali: siyā pāṭidesanīyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing acknowledgment; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.10
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ catūhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in four ways: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.11
Pali: siyā kāyato samuṭṭhanti, na vācato na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body, not from speech or mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.12
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca samuṭṭhanti, na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and speech, not from mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.13
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti na vācato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech; or 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.14
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.15
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.16
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr2.16:4.17
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass.

English (brahmali, published): The summary of the previous six sections, the eighth, is finished.

Pali: 2.11. Bhūtārocanasamuṭṭhāna 
English (brahmali, published): 11. Originations like the offense for telling truthfully 

pli-tv-pvr3:67.1
Pali: Bhūtaṁ kāyena jāyati, 
English (brahmali, published): Truthfully: produced from the body, 

pli-tv-pvr3:67.2
Pali: na vācā na ca cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): Not from speech or mind; 

pli-tv-pvr3:67.3
Pali: Vācato ca samuṭṭhāti, 
English (brahmali, published): Or it originates from speech, 

pli-tv-pvr3:67.4
Pali: na kāyā na ca cittato. 
English (brahmali, published): Not from body or mind; 

pli-tv-pvr3:68.1
Pali: Kāyavācāya jāyati, 
English (brahmali, published): Or it is produced from body and speech, 

pli-tv-pvr3:68.2
Pali: na tu jāyati cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): Not from mind; 

pli-tv-pvr3:68.3
Pali: Bhūtārocanakā nāma, 
English (brahmali, published): So informing of what is true 

pli-tv-pvr3:68.4
Pali: tīhi ṭhānehi jāyati. 
English (brahmali, published): Is produced for three reasons. 

pli-tv-pvr3:69.1
Pali: Bhūtārocanasamuṭṭhānaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Originations like the offense for telling truthfully are finished.

English (brahmali, published): This offense for admitting a female criminal 

pli-tv-pvr3:70.5

English (brahmali, published): Has two originations, 

pli-tv-pvr3:70.6

English (brahmali, published): As spoken by the King of the Teaching.

English (brahmali, published): For the contraction on originations

English (brahmali, published): Has thirteen classes that have been well taught;

English (brahmali, published): In accordance with the Teaching that guides;

Pali: samuṭṭhāne na muyhatīti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is not confused about origination.”

Pali: Samuṭṭhānasīsasaṅkhepo niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The thirteen groups of originations of offenses is finished.

pli-tv-pvr4:14.1
Pali: Tattha katamāni cha vivādamūlāni? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the six roots of disputes? 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.2
Pali: Idha bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) It may be that a monk is angry and resentful. 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.3
Pali: Yo so bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): One who is angry and resentful is disrespectful and undeferential toward the Teacher, the Teaching, and the Sangha, and he doesn’t fulfill the training. 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.4
Pali: Yo so bhikkhu satthari agāravo viharati appatisso, dhamme …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Such a person 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.5
Pali: saṅghe …pe… 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.6
Pali: sikkhāya na paripūrakārī, so saṅghe vivādaṁ janeti. 
English (brahmali, published): creates disputes in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.7
Pali: Yo hoti vivādo bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Disputes are unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; they are harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans. 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.8
Pali: Evarūpañce tumhe vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā samanupasseyyātha tatra tumhe tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa pahānāya vāyameyyātha. 
English (brahmali, published): When you see such a root of disputes either in yourself or in others, you should make an effort to get rid of it. 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.9
Pali: Evarūpañce tumhe vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā na samanupasseyyātha tatra tumhe tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavāya paṭipajjeyyātha. 
English (brahmali, published): If you don’t see such a root either in yourself or in others, you should practice so that it does not emerge in the future. 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.10
Pali: Evametassa pāpakassa vivādamūlassa pahānaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way that bad root of disputes is abandoned 

pli-tv-pvr4:14.11
Pali: Evametassa pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): and does not emerge in the future. 

pli-tv-pvr4:15.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ bhikkhu makkhī hoti paḷāsī …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (2) Or it may be that a monk is denigrating and domineering,

Pali: issukī hoti maccharī, saṭho hoti māyāvī, pāpiccho hoti micchādiṭṭhi, sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) envious and stingy, (4) treacherous and deceitful, (5) one who has bad desires and wrong views, or (6) one who obstinately grasps his own views and only gives them up with difficulty. 

pli-tv-pvr4:15.3

English (brahmali, published): Any of these is disrespectful and undeferential toward the Teacher, the Teaching, and the Sangha, and he doesn’t fulfill the training.

English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.1
Pali: Tattha katamāni cha anuvādamūlāni? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the six roots of accusations? 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.2
Pali: Idha bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) It may be that a monk is angry and resentful. 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.3
Pali: Yo so bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): One who is angry and resentful is disrespectful and undeferential toward the Teacher, the Teaching, and the Sangha, and he doesn’t fulfill the training. 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.4
Pali: Yo so bhikkhu satthari agāravo viharati appatisso dhamme …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Such a person 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.5
Pali: saṅghe …pe… 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.6
Pali: sikkhāya na paripūrakārī so saṅghe anuvādaṁ janeti. 
English (brahmali, published): makes accusations in the Sangha. 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.7
Pali: Yo hoti anuvādo bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Accusations are unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; they are harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans. 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.8
Pali: Evarūpañce tumhe anuvādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā samanupasseyyātha tatra tumhe tasseva pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa pahānāya vāyameyyātha. 
English (brahmali, published): When you see such a root of accusations either in yourself or in others, you should make an effort to get rid of it. 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.9
Pali: Evarūpañce tumhe anuvādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā na samanupasseyyātha tatra tumhe tasseva pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavāya paṭipajjeyyātha. 
English (brahmali, published): If you don’t see such a root either in yourself or in others, you should practice so that it does not emerge in the future. 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.10
Pali: Evametassa pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa pahānaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way that bad root of accusations is abandoned 

pli-tv-pvr4:16.11
Pali: Evametassa pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): and does not emerge in the future. 

pli-tv-pvr4:17.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ bhikkhu makkhī hoti palāsī …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): (2) Or it may be that a monk is denigrating and domineering, 

pli-tv-pvr4:17.2
Pali: issukī hoti maccharī, saṭho hoti māyāvī, pāpiccho hoti micchādiṭṭhi, sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) envious and stingy, (4) treacherous and deceitful, (5) one who has bad desires and wrong views, or (6) one who obstinately grasps his own views and only gives them up with difficulty. 

pli-tv-pvr4:17.3
Pali: Yo so bhikkhu sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Any of these is disrespectful and undeferential toward the Teacher, the Teaching, and the Sangha, and he doesn’t fulfill the training.
