Pali: Imāni cha anuvādamūlāni. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:18.1
Pali: Tattha katame cha sāraṇīyā dhammā? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the six aspects of friendliness? 

pli-tv-pvr4:18.2
Pali: Idha bhikkhuno mettaṁ kāyakammaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti sabrahmacārīsu āvi ceva raho ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) As to this, a monk acts with good will toward his fellow monastics, both in public and in private. 

pli-tv-pvr4:18.3
Pali: Ayampi dhammo sāraṇīyo piyakaraṇo garukaraṇo saṅgahāya avivādāya sāmaggiyā ekībhāvāya saṁvattati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:19.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ bhikkhuno mettaṁ vacīkammaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti sabrahmacārīsu āvi ceva raho ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (2) Furthermore, a monk speaks with good will to his fellow monastics, both in public and in private. 

pli-tv-pvr4:19.2
Pali: Ayampi dhammo sāraṇīyo piyakaraṇo garukaraṇo saṅgahāya avivādāya sāmaggiyā ekībhāvāya saṁvattati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:20.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ bhikkhuno mettaṁ manokammaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti sabrahmacārīsu āvi ceva raho ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) Furthermore, a monk thinks with good will about his fellow monastics, both in public and in private. 

pli-tv-pvr4:20.2
Pali: Ayampi dhammo sāraṇīyo piyakaraṇo garukaraṇo saṅgahāya avivādāya sāmaggiyā ekībhāvāya saṁvattati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:21.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ bhikkhu ye te lābhā dhammikā dhammaladdhā antamaso pattapariyāpannamattampi tathārūpehi lābhehi appaṭivibhattabhogī hoti sīlavantehi sabrahmacārīhi sādhāraṇabhogī. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) Furthermore, whatever a monk has gained in an appropriate manner, even the content of his almsbowl, he shares without reservation with his virtuous fellow monastics. 

pli-tv-pvr4:21.2
Pali: Ayampi dhammo sāraṇīyo piyakaraṇo garukaraṇo saṅgahāya avivādāya sāmaggiyā ekībhāvāya saṁvattati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:22.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ bhikkhu yāni tāni sīlāni akhaṇḍāni acchiddāni asabalāni akammāsāni bhujissāni viññupasatthāni aparāmaṭṭhāni samādhisaṁvattanikāni, tathārūpesu sīlesu sīlasāmaññagato viharati sabrahmacārīhi āvi ceva raho ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (5) Furthermore, a monk lives with his fellow monastics, both in public and in private, with moral conduct that is unbroken, consistent, spotless, pure, liberating, praised by the wise, ungrasped, and leading to stillness. 

pli-tv-pvr4:22.2
Pali: Ayampi dhammo sāraṇīyo piyakaraṇo garukaraṇo saṅgahāya avivādāya sāmaggiyā ekībhāvāya saṁvattati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:23.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ bhikkhu yāyaṁ diṭṭhi ariyā niyyānikā niyyāti takkarassa sammā dukkhakkhayāya tathārūpāya diṭṭhiyā diṭṭhisāmaññagato viharati sabrahmacārīhi āvi ceva raho ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (6) Furthermore, a monk lives with his fellow monastics, both in public and in private, with that noble view that is liberating and leads one who acts in accordance with it to the complete end of suffering. 

pli-tv-pvr4:23.2
Pali: Ayampi dhammo sāraṇīyo piyakaraṇo garukaraṇo saṅgahāya avivādāya sāmaggiyā ekībhāvāya saṁvattati. 
English (brahmali, published): These aspects of friendliness create love and respect, and lead to coming together, concord, harmony, and unity. 

pli-tv-pvr4:23.3
Pali: Ime cha sāraṇīyā dhammā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:24.1
Pali: Tattha katamāni aṭṭhārasa bhedakaravatthūni? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the eighteen grounds for schism? 

pli-tv-pvr4:24.2
Pali: Idha bhikkhu adhammaṁ “dhammo”ti dīpeti, dhammaṁ “adhammo”ti dīpeti, avinayaṁ “vinayo”ti dīpeti, vinayaṁ “avinayo”ti dīpeti, abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatena “bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenā”ti dīpeti, bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatena “abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenā”ti dīpeti, anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena “āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenā”ti dīpeti, āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena “anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenā”ti dīpeti, apaññattaṁ tathāgatena “paññattaṁ tathāgatenā”ti dīpeti, paññattaṁ tathāgatena “apaññattaṁ tathāgatenā”ti dīpeti, āpattiṁ “anāpattī”ti dīpeti, anāpattiṁ “āpattī”ti dīpeti, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ “garukā āpattī”ti dīpeti, garukaṁ āpattiṁ “lahukā āpattī”ti dīpeti, sāvasesaṁ āpattiṁ “anavasesā āpattī”ti dīpeti, anavasesaṁ āpattiṁ “sāvasesā āpattī”ti dīpeti, duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ “aduṭṭhullā āpattī”ti dīpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ “duṭṭhullā āpattī”ti dīpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this case a monk proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it and what is in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it. He proclaims what is contrary to the Monastic Law as being in accordance with it, and what is in accordance with the Monastic Law as contrary to it. He proclaims what hasn’t been spoken by the Buddha as spoken by him, and what has been spoken by the Buddha as not spoken by him. He proclaims what was not practiced by the Buddha as practiced by him, and what was practiced by the Buddha as not practiced by him. He proclaims what was not laid down by the Buddha as laid down by him, and what was laid down by the Buddha as not laid down by him. He proclaims a non-offense as an offense, and an offense as a non-offense. He proclaims a light offense as heavy, and a heavy offense as light. He proclaims a curable offense as incurable, and an incurable offense as curable. He proclaims a grave offense as minor, and a minor offense as grave. 

pli-tv-pvr4:24.3
Pali: Imāni aṭṭhārasa bhedakaravatthūni. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:25.1
Pali: Tattha katamāni cattāri adhikaraṇāni? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the four kinds of legal issues? 

pli-tv-pvr4:25.2
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr4:25.3
Pali: imāni cattāri adhikaraṇāni. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:26.1
Pali: Tattha katame satta samathā? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the seven principles for settling them? 

pli-tv-pvr4:26.2
Pali: Sammukhāvinayo, sativinayo, amūḷhavinayo, paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ, yebhuyyasikā, tassapāpiyasikā, tiṇavatthārako—
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, majority decision, further penalty, covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:26.3
Pali: ime satta samathā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:27.1
Pali: Katipucchāvāro niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The section on questioning “how many?” is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr4:28.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr4:29.1
Pali: Āpatti āpattikkhandhā, 
English (brahmali, published): “Offense, classes of offenses, 

pli-tv-pvr4:29.2
Pali: vinītā sattadhā puna; 
English (brahmali, published): Training, again sevenfold; 

pli-tv-pvr4:29.3
Pali: Vinītāgāravā ceva, 
English (brahmali, published): Training, and disrespect, 

pli-tv-pvr4:29.4
Pali: gāravā mūlameva ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Respect, and root. 

pli-tv-pvr4:30.1
Pali: Puna vinītā vipatti, 
English (brahmali, published): Again training, failure, 

pli-tv-pvr4:30.2
Pali: samuṭṭhānā vivādanā; 
English (brahmali, published): Originations, disputes; 

pli-tv-pvr4:30.3
Pali: Anuvādā sāraṇīyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Accusations, friendliness, 

pli-tv-pvr4:30.4
Pali: bhedādhikaraṇena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Schism, and with legal issues; 

pli-tv-pvr4:30.5
Pali: Satteva samathā vuttā, 
English (brahmali, published): Seven are said on settling, 

pli-tv-pvr4:30.6
Pali: padā sattarasā imeti. 
English (brahmali, published): These are the seventeen items.”

English (brahmali, published): 1. The section on the six kinds of originations of offenses 

pli-tv-pvr4:31.1
Pali: Paṭhamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena pārājikaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it possible to commit an offense entailing expulsion through the first kind of origination of offenses?”—

pli-tv-pvr4:31.2
Pali: Na hīti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:31.3
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ āpajjeyyāti?

pli-tv-pvr4:31.4
Pali: Siyāti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:31.5

pli-tv-pvr4:31.6
Pali: Siyāti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:34.12

English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:34.13
Pali: Dubbhāsitaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “An offense of wrong speech?”—

pli-tv-pvr4:34.14
Pali: Na hīti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-pvr4:35.1
Pali: Pañcamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena pārājikaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it possible to commit an offense entailing expulsion through the fifth kind of origination of offenses?”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.2
Pali: Siyāti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.3
Pali: Saṅghādisesaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “An offense entailing suspension?”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.4
Pali: Siyāti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.5
Pali: Thullaccayaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “A serious offense?”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.6
Pali: Siyāti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.7
Pali: Pācittiyaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “An offense entailing confession?”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.8
Pali: Siyāti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.9
Pali: Pāṭidesanīyaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “An offense entailing acknowledgment?”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.10
Pali: Na hīti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “No.”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.11
Pali: Dukkaṭaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “An offense of wrong conduct?”—

pli-tv-pvr4:35.14
Pali: Siyāti vattabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One might.” 

pli-tv-pvr4:36.1
Pali: Chaṭṭhena āpattisamuṭṭhānena pārājikaṁ āpajjeyyāti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Is it possible to commit an offense entailing expulsion through the sixth kind of origination of offenses?”—

English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

English (brahmali, published): “One might.”—

English (brahmali, published): “No.” 

pli-tv-pvr4:37.1
Pali: Chaāpattisamuṭṭhānavāro niṭṭhito paṭhamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The first section on the six kinds of originations of offenses is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.0
Pali: 4.2. Katāpattivāra 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The section on “how many kinds of offenses?” 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.1
Pali: Paṭhamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many kinds of offenses does one commit through the first kind of origination of offenses? 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.2
Pali: Paṭhamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Five: 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.3
Pali: Bhikkhu kappiyasaññī saññācikāya kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when a monk—perceiving it as allowable and by means of begging—builds a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks a space on all sides, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.4
Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) when there is one piece left to complete the hut, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.5
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) when the last piece is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.6
Pali: bhikkhu kappiyasaññī vikāle bhojanaṁ bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (4) when a monk, perceiving it as allowable, eats cooked food at the wrong time, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.7
Pali: bhikkhu kappiyasaññī aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā antaragharaṁ paviṭṭhāya hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa—
English (brahmali, published): (5) when a monk, perceiving it as allowable, receives fresh or cooked food directly from an unrelated nun who has entered an inhabited area, and then eats it, he commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-pvr4:38.8
Pali: paṭhamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena imā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.2
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): In how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.3
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses do they originate? 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.4
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four kinds of legal issues do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.6
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.7
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.8
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ pañcahi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in five classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.9
Pali: siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā pāṭidesanīyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses entailing acknowledgment; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.10
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.11
Pali: kāyato samuṭṭhanti, na vācato na cittato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, not from speech or mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.12
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.13
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:39.14
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:40.1
Pali: Dutiyena āpattisamuṭṭhānena kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit through the second kind of origination of offenses? 

pli-tv-pvr4:40.2
Pali: Dutiyena āpattisamuṭṭhānena catasso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): Four: 

pli-tv-pvr4:40.3
Pali: bhikkhu kappiyasaññī samādisati—“kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ. Payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when a monk, perceiving it as allowable, appoints someone to build him a hut, and they build a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks a space on all sides, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr4:40.4
Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) when there is one piece left to complete the hut, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:40.5
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) when the last piece is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:40.6
Pali: Bhikkhu kappiyasaññī anupasampannaṁ padaso dhammaṁ vāceti, āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): (4) when a monk, perceiving it as allowable, instructs a person who is not fully ordained to memorize the Teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr4:40.7
Pali: dutiyena āpattisamuṭṭhānena imā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.4
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ catūhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in four classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.6
Pali: siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.8
Pali: vācato samuṭṭhanti, na kāyato na cittato. 
English (brahmali, published): from speech, not from body or mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:41.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:42.1
Pali: Tatiyena āpattisamuṭṭhānena kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit through the third kind of origination of offenses? 

pli-tv-pvr4:42.2
Pali: Tatiyena āpattisamuṭṭhānena pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Five: 

pli-tv-pvr4:42.3
Pali: Bhikkhu kappiyasaññī saṁvidahitvā kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ. Payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when a monk—perceiving it as allowable and having appointed someone—builds a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks a space on all sides, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr4:42.4
Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) when there is one piece left to complete the hut, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:42.5
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) when the last piece is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:42.6
Pali: Bhikkhu kappiyasaññī paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) when a monk, perceiving it as allowable, eats fine foods that he has requested for himself, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:42.7
Pali: Bhikkhu kappiyasaññī bhikkhuniyā vosāsantiyā na nivāretvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa—
English (brahmali, published): (5) when a monk, perceiving it as allowable, eats without having restrained a nun who is giving directions, he commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-pvr4:42.8
Pali: tatiyena āpattisamuṭṭhānena imā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.4
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ pañcahi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in five classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.6
Pali: siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā pāṭidesanīyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses entailing acknowledgment; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.8
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca samuṭṭhanti, na cittato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and speech, not from mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:43.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.1
Pali: Catutthena āpattisamuṭṭhānena kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit through the fourth kind of origination of offenses? 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.2
Pali: Catutthena āpattisamuṭṭhānena cha āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): Six: 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.3
Pali: bhikkhu methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when a monk has sexual intercourse, he commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.4
Pali: bhikkhu akappiyasaññī saññācikāya kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) when a monk—perceiving it as unallowable and by means of begging—builds a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks a space on all sides, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.5
Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) when there is one piece left to complete the hut, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.6
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) when the last piece is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.7
Pali: Bhikkhu akappiyasaññī vikāle bhojanaṁ bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (5) when a monk, perceiving it as unallowable, eats cooked food at the wrong time, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.8
Pali: Bhikkhu akappiyasaññī aññātikāya bhikkhuniyā antaragharaṁ paviṭṭhāya hatthato khādanīyaṁ vā bhojanīyaṁ vā sahatthā paṭiggahetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (6) when a monk, perceiving it as unallowable, receives fresh or cooked food directly from an unrelated nun who has entered an inhabited area, and then eats it, he commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-pvr4:44.9
Pali: Catutthena āpattisamuṭṭhānena imā cha āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.4
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ chahi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in six classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.6
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā pāṭidesanīyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses entailing acknowledgment; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.8
Pali: kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na vācato. 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech. 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:45.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.1
Pali: Pañcamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit through the fifth kind of origination of offenses? 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.2
Pali: Pañcamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena cha āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Six: 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.3
Pali: Bhikkhu pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when a monk, having bad desires, overcome by desire, claims a non-existent superhuman quality, he commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.4
Pali: bhikkhu akappiyasaññī samādisati—“kuṭiṁ me karothā”ti. Tassa kuṭiṁ karonti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ. Payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) when a monk, perceiving it as unallowable, appoints someone to build him a hut, and they build a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks a space on all sides, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.5
Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) when there is one piece left to complete the hut, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.6
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) when the last piece is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.7
Pali: Bhikkhu akappiyasaññī anupasampannaṁ padaso dhammaṁ vāceti, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (5) when a monk, perceiving it as unallowable, instructs a person who is not fully ordained to memorize the Teaching, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.8
Pali: Na khuṁsetukāmo na vambhetukāmo na maṅkukattukāmo davakamyatā hīnena hīnaṁ vadeti, āpatti dubbhāsitassa—
English (brahmali, published): (6) when—not wishing to revile, not wishing to insult, not wishing to humiliate, but wanting to have fun—one says what is low to one who is low, one commits an offense of wrong speech. 

pli-tv-pvr4:46.9
Pali: pañcamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena imā cha āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.4
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ chahi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in six classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.6
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena, siyā dubbhāsitāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong speech. 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.8
Pali: vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na kāyato. 
English (brahmali, published): from speech and mind, not from body. 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:47.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.1
Pali: Chaṭṭhena āpattisamuṭṭhānena kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit through the sixth kind of origination of offenses? 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.2
Pali: Chaṭṭhena āpattisamuṭṭhānena cha āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): Six: 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.3
Pali: bhikkhu saṁvidahitvā bhaṇḍaṁ avaharati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when a monk, having agreed with others, steals goods, he commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.4
Pali: bhikkhu akappiyasaññī saṁvidahitvā kuṭiṁ karoti adesitavatthukaṁ pamāṇātikkantaṁ sārambhaṁ aparikkamanaṁ, payoge dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) when a monk—perceiving it as unallowable and having appointed someone—builds a hut whose site has not been approved, which exceeds the right size, where harm will be done, and which lacks a space on all sides, then for the effort there is an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.5
Pali: ekaṁ piṇḍaṁ anāgate āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) when there is one piece left to complete the hut, he commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.6
Pali: tasmiṁ piṇḍe āgate, āpatti saṅghādisesassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) when the last piece is finished, he commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.7
Pali: Bhikkhu akappiyasaññī paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (5) when a monk, perceiving it as unallowable, eats fine foods that he has requested for himself, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.8
Pali: Bhikkhu akappiyasaññī bhikkhuniyā vosāsantiyā na nivāretvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa—
English (brahmali, published): (6) when a monk, perceiving it as unallowable, eats without having restrained a nun who is giving directions, he commits an offense entailing acknowledgment. 

pli-tv-pvr4:48.9
Pali: chaṭṭhena āpattisamuṭṭhānena imā cha āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.2
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): In how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.3
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi āpattisamuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses do they originate? 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.4
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four kinds of legal issues do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.6
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.7
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.8
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ chahi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in six classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.9
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā pāṭidesanīyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses entailing acknowledgment; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.10
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.11
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.12
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.13
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:49.14
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass.” 

pli-tv-pvr4:50.1
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katāpattivāro niṭṭhito dutiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The second section on “how many kinds of offenses?” for the six kinds of originations of offenses is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr4:51.0
Pali: 4.3. Āpattisamuṭṭhānagāthā 
English (brahmali, published): 3. The verses on the kinds of originations of offenses 

pli-tv-pvr4:51.1
Pali: Samuṭṭhānā kāyikā anantadassinā, 
English (brahmali, published): “Origination from body has been declared by the one who benefits the world, 

pli-tv-pvr4:51.2
Pali: Akkhātā lokahitena vivekadassinā; 
English (brahmali, published): The one of boundless vision, seeing seclusion: 

pli-tv-pvr4:51.3
Pali: Āpattiyo tena samuṭṭhitā kati, 
English (brahmali, published): I ask how many kinds of offenses originate from that—

pli-tv-pvr4:51.4
Pali: Pucchāmi taṁ brūhi vibhaṅgakovida. 
English (brahmali, published): You who are skilled in analysis, please say. 

pli-tv-pvr4:52.1
Pali: Samuṭṭhānā kāyikā anantadassinā, 
English (brahmali, published): Origination from body has been declared by the one who benefits the world,

Pali: Akkhātā lokahitena vivekadassinā; 
English (brahmali, published): The one of boundless vision, seeing seclusion: 

pli-tv-pvr4:52.3
Pali: Āpattiyo tena samuṭṭhitā pañca, 
English (brahmali, published): Five kinds of offenses originate from that—

pli-tv-pvr4:52.4
Pali: Etaṁ te akkhāmi vibhaṅgakovida. 
English (brahmali, published): I declare this to you, you who are skilled in analysis.

pli-tv-pvr4:64.0
Pali: 4.4. Vipattipaccayavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 4. The section on “as a result of failure” 

pli-tv-pvr4:64.1
Pali: Sīlavipattipaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of failure in morality? 

pli-tv-pvr4:64.2
Pali: Sīlavipattipaccayā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): Four: 

pli-tv-pvr4:64.3
Pali: bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a nun knowingly conceals an offense entailing expulsion, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr4:64.4
Pali: vematikā paṭicchādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, being unsure, she conceals it, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:64.5
Pali: bhikkhu saṅghādisesaṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a monk conceals an offense entailing suspension, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:64.6
Pali: attano duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when he conceals a grave offense of his own, he commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:64.7
Pali: sīlavipattipaccayā imā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.4
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ catūhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in four classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.6
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.8
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:65.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:66.1
Pali: Ācāravipattipaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of failure in conduct? 

pli-tv-pvr4:66.2
Pali: Ācāravipattipaccayā ekaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): One: 

pli-tv-pvr4:66.3
Pali: Ācāravipattiṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when one conceals a failure in conduct, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:66.4
Pali: ācāravipattipaccayā imaṁ ekaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.1
Pali: Sā āpatti catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to this offense, to how many of the four kinds of failure does it belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues is it settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.3
Pali: Sā āpatti catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ ekaṁ vipattiṁ bhajati—
English (brahmali, published): It belongs to one kind of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.4
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ ekena āpattikkhandhena saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): It is found in one class of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.6
Pali: dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.8
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): It belongs to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:67.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:68.1
Pali: Diṭṭhivipattipaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of failure in view? 

pli-tv-pvr4:68.2
Pali: Diṭṭhivipattipaccayā dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Two: 

pli-tv-pvr4:68.3
Pali: Pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjati, ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): when not giving up a bad view after being pressed for the third time, then after the motion, one commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr4:68.4
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): when the last announcement is finished, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr4:68.5
Pali: diṭṭhivipattipaccayā imā dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ ekaṁ vipattiṁ bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to one kind of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.4
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ dvīhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in two classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.6
Pali: siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.8
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:69.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.1
Pali: Ājīvavipattipaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of failure in livelihood? 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.2
Pali: Ājīvavipattipaccayā cha āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): Six: 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.3
Pali: ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when, to make a living, having bad desires, overcome by desire, one claims a non-existent superhuman quality, one commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.4
Pali: ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā sañcarittaṁ samāpajjati, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) when, to make a living, one acts as a matchmaker, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.5
Pali: ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā “yo te vihāre vasati, so bhikkhu arahā”ti bhaṇati, paṭivijānantassa āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) when, to make a living, one says, “The monk who stays in your dwelling is a perfected one,” and the listener understands, one commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.6
Pali: ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā bhikkhu paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (4) when, to make a living, a monk eats fine foods that he has requested for himself, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.7
Pali: ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā bhikkhunī paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti pāṭidesanīyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (5) when, to make a living, a nun eats fine foods that she has requested for herself, she commits an offense entailing acknowledgment; 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.8
Pali: ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā sūpaṁ vā odanaṁ vā agilāno attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): (6) when, to make a living, one eats bean curry or rice that one has requested for oneself, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:70.9
Pali: ājīvavipattipaccayā imā cha āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.4
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ chahi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in six classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.6
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā pāṭidesanīyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses entailing acknowledgment; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ chahi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in six ways: 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.8
Pali: siyā kāyato samuṭṭhanti, na vācato na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body, not from speech or mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.9
Pali: siyā vācato samuṭṭhanti, na kāyato na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from speech, not from body or mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca samuṭṭhanti, na cittato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and speech, not from mind; or 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.11
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na vācato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech; or 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.12
Pali: siyā vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na kāyato; 
English (brahmali, published): from speech and mind, not from body; or 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.13
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.14
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.15
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:71.16
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:72.1
Pali: Vipattipaccayavāro niṭṭhito catuttho. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth section on “as a result of failure” is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr4:73.0
Pali: 4.5. Adhikaraṇapaccayavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 5. The section on “as a result of legal issues” 

pli-tv-pvr4:73.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of legal issues arising from disputes? 

pli-tv-pvr4:73.2
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇapaccayā dve āpattiyo āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): Two: 

pli-tv-pvr4:73.3
Pali: upasampannaṁ omasati, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one speaks abusively to one who is fully ordained, one commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:73.4
Pali: anupasampannaṁ omasati, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when one speaks abusively to one who is not fully ordained, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:73.5
Pali: vivādādhikaraṇapaccayā imā dve āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:74.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? …

Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled?

English (brahmali, published): They belong to one kind of failure:

Pali: ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): failure in conduct.

Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ dvīhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in two classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:74.6
Pali: siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:74.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ tīhi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in three ways: 

pli-tv-pvr4:74.8
Pali: siyā kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na vācato; 
English (brahmali, published): from body and mind, not from speech; or 

pli-tv-pvr4:74.9
Pali: siyā vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti, na kāyato; 
English (brahmali, published): from speech and mind, not from body; or 

pli-tv-pvr4:74.10
Pali: siyā kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind.

Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ.

English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:75.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of legal issues arising from accusations? 

pli-tv-pvr4:75.2
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇapaccayā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Three: 

pli-tv-pvr4:75.3
Pali: Bhikkhuṁ amūlakena pārājikena dhammena anuddhaṁseti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one groundlessly charges a monk with an offense entailing expulsion, one commits an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:75.4
Pali: amūlakena saṅghādisesena anuddhaṁseti, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when one groundlessly charges him with an offense entailing suspension, one commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:75.5
Pali: amūlikāya ācāravipattiyā anuddhaṁseti, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when one groundlessly charges him with failure in conduct, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:75.6
Pali: anuvādādhikaraṇapaccayā imā tisso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:76.1

English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:77.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of legal issues arising from offenses? 

pli-tv-pvr4:77.2
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇapaccayā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Four: 

pli-tv-pvr4:77.3
Pali: Bhikkhunī jānaṁ pārājikaṁ dhammaṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti pārājikassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a nun knowingly conceals an offense entailing expulsion, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr4:77.4
Pali: vematikā paṭicchādeti, āpatti thullaccayassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when, being unsure, she conceals it, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:77.5
Pali: bhikkhu saṅghādisesaṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti pācittiyassa; 
English (brahmali, published): when a monk conceals an offense entailing suspension, he commits an offense entailing confession; 

pli-tv-pvr4:77.6
Pali: ācāravipattiṁ paṭicchādeti, āpatti dukkaṭassa—
English (brahmali, published): when one conceals a failure in conduct, one commits an offense of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:77.7
Pali: āpattādhikaraṇapaccayā imā catasso āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.4
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ catūhi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in four classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.6
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.8
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:78.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:79.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇapaccayā kati āpattiyo āpajjati? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses does one commit as a result of legal issues arising from business? 

pli-tv-pvr4:79.2
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇapaccayā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Five: 

pli-tv-pvr4:79.3
Pali: Ukkhittānuvattikā bhikkhunī yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjati, ñattiyā dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when a nun takes sides with one who has been ejected and does not stop when pressed for the third time, then after the motion, she commits an offense of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr4:79.4
Pali: dvīhi kammavācāhi thullaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) after each of the first two announcements, she commits a serious offense; 

pli-tv-pvr4:79.5
Pali: kammavācāpariyosāne āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) when the last announcement is finished, she commits an offense entailing expulsion; 

pli-tv-pvr4:79.6
Pali: Bhedakānuvattakā bhikkhū yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjanti, āpatti saṅghādisesassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (4) when monks who side with a monk who is pursuing schism do not stop when pressed for the third time, they commit an offense entailing suspension; 

pli-tv-pvr4:79.7
Pali: pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā yāvatatiyaṁ samanubhāsanāya na paṭinissajjati, āpatti pācittiyassa—
English (brahmali, published): (5) when not giving up a bad view after being pressed for the third time, one commits an offense entailing confession. 

pli-tv-pvr4:79.8
Pali: kiccādhikaraṇapaccayā imā pañca āpattiyo āpajjati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.1
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): When it comes to these offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? … 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.2
Pali: sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.3
Pali: Tā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ dve vipattiyo bhajanti—
English (brahmali, published): They belong to two kinds of failure: 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.4
Pali: siyā sīlavipattiṁ, siyā ācāravipattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be failure in morality; they may be failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ pañcahi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā—
English (brahmali, published): They are found in five classes of offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.6
Pali: siyā pārājikāpattikkhandhena, siyā saṅghādisesāpattikkhandhena, siyā thullaccayāpattikkhandhena, siyā pācittiyāpattikkhandhena, siyā dukkaṭāpattikkhandhena. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be in the class of offenses entailing expulsion; they may be in the class of offenses entailing suspension; they may be in the class of serious offenses; they may be in the class of offenses entailing confession; they may be in the class of offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.7
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ ekena samuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti—
English (brahmali, published): They originate in one way: 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.8
Pali: kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): from body, speech, and mind. 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They belong to legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tīhi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): They are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr4:80.11
Pali: siyā sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, siyā sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what has been admitted; or they may be settled by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.1
Pali: Ṭhapetvā satta āpattiyo satta āpattikkhandhe, avasesā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ kati vipattiyo bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Apart from the seven kinds of offenses and the seven classes of offenses, when it comes to the rest of the offenses, to how many of the four kinds of failure do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.2
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ katihi āpattikkhandhehi saṅgahitā? 
English (brahmali, published): In how many of the seven classes of offenses are they found? 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.3
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ katihi samuṭṭhānehi samuṭṭhanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the six kinds of originations of offenses do they originate? 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.4
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four kinds of legal issues do they belong? 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.5
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many of the seven principles for settling legal issues are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.6
Pali: Ṭhapetvā satta āpattiyo satta āpattikkhandhe avasesā āpattiyo catunnaṁ vipattīnaṁ na katamaṁ vipattiṁ bhajanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Apart from the seven kinds of offenses and the seven classes of offenses, the rest of the offenses do not belong to any of the four kinds of failure. 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.7
Pali: Sattannaṁ āpattikkhandhānaṁ na katamena āpattikkhandhena saṅgahitā. 
English (brahmali, published): They are not found in any of the seven classes of offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.8
Pali: Channaṁ āpattisamuṭṭhānānaṁ na katamena āpattisamuṭṭhānena samuṭṭhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They do not originate through any of the six kinds of originations of offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.9
Pali: Catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ na katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): They do not belong to any of the four kinds of legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.10
Pali: Sattannaṁ samathānaṁ na katamena samathena sammanti. 
English (brahmali, published): They are not settled through any of the seven principles for settling legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.11
Pali: Taṁ kissa hetu? 
English (brahmali, published): Why is that? 

pli-tv-pvr4:81.12
Pali: Ṭhapetvā satta āpattiyo satta āpattikkhandhe, natthaññā āpattiyoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Apart from the seven kinds of offenses and the seven classes of offenses, there are no other offenses.” 

pli-tv-pvr4:82.1
Pali: Adhikaraṇapaccayavāro niṭṭhito pañcamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifth section on “as a result of legal issues” is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr4:83.1
Pali: Antarapeyyālaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Various topics with a focus on the origination of offenses is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr4:84.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr4:85.1
Pali: Katipucchā samuṭṭhānā, 
English (brahmali, published): “Questioning ‘how many?’, originations, 

pli-tv-pvr4:85.2
Pali: katāpatti tatheva ca; 
English (brahmali, published): And so ‘how many kinds of offenses?’ 

pli-tv-pvr4:85.3
Pali: Samuṭṭhānā vipatti ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Originations, and failure, 

pli-tv-pvr4:85.4
Pali: tathādhikaraṇena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): And so with legal issues.”

English (brahmali, published): What is the forerunner?

pli-tv-pvr5:5.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇassa kiṁ pubbaṅgamanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the forerunner of legal issues arising from disputes?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.2
Pali: Lobho pubbaṅgamo, doso pubbaṅgamo, moho pubbaṅgamo, alobho pubbaṅgamo, adoso pubbaṅgamo, amoho pubbaṅgamo. 
English (brahmali, published): Desire is a forerunner, ill will is a forerunner, confusion is a forerunner, non-desire is a forerunner, non-ill will is a forerunner, non-confusion is a forerunner. 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.3
Pali: Kati ṭhānānīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many reasons are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.4
Pali: Aṭṭhārasa bhedakaravatthūni ṭhānāni. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighteen grounds for schism. 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.5
Pali: Kati vatthūnīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many grounds are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.6
Pali: Aṭṭhārasa bhedakaravatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighteen grounds for schism. 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.7
Pali: Kati bhūmiyoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many foundations are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.8
Pali: Aṭṭhārasa bhedakaravatthūni bhūmiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The eighteen grounds for schism. 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.9
Pali: Kati hetūti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many causes are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.10
Pali: Nava hetū—
English (brahmali, published): Nine: 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.11
Pali: tayo kusalahetū, tayo akusalahetū, tayo abyākatahetū. 
English (brahmali, published): there are three wholesome causes, three unwholesome causes, and three indeterminate causes. 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.12
Pali: Kati mūlānīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many roots are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.13
Pali: Dvādasa mūlāni. 
English (brahmali, published): Twelve. 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.14
Pali: Katihākārehi vivadatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many motives does one dispute?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.15
Pali: Dvīhākārehi vivadati—
English (brahmali, published): Through two: 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.16
Pali: dhammadiṭṭhi vā adhammadiṭṭhi vā. 
English (brahmali, published): through a view that accords with the Teaching or through a view that is contrary to the Teaching. 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.17
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from disputes settled?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.18
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ dvīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Through two of them: 

pli-tv-pvr5:5.19
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca yebhuyyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face and through majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇassa kiṁ pubbaṅgamanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the forerunner of legal issues arising from accusations?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.2
Pali: Lobho pubbaṅgamo, doso pubbaṅgamo, moho pubbaṅgamo, alobho pubbaṅgamo, adoso pubbaṅgamo, amoho pubbaṅgamo. 
English (brahmali, published): Desire is a forerunner, ill will is a forerunner, confusion is a forerunner, non-desire is a forerunner, non-ill will is a forerunner, non-confusion is a forerunner. 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.3
Pali: Kati ṭhānānīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many reasons are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.4
Pali: Catasso vipattiyo ṭhānāni. 
English (brahmali, published): The four failures. 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.5
Pali: Kati vatthūnīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many grounds are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.6
Pali: Catasso vipattiyo vatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): The four failures. 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.7
Pali: Kati bhūmiyoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many foundations are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.8
Pali: Catasso vipattiyo bhūmiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The four failures. 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.9
Pali: Kati hetūti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many causes are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.10
Pali: Nava hetū—
English (brahmali, published): Nine: 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.11
Pali: tayo kusalahetū, tayo akusalahetū, tayo abyākatahetū. 
English (brahmali, published): there are three wholesome causes, three unwholesome causes, and three indeterminate causes. 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.12
Pali: Kati mūlānīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many roots are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.13
Pali: Cuddasa mūlāni. 
English (brahmali, published): Fourteen. 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.14
Pali: Katihākārehi anuvadatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many motives does one accuse?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.15
Pali: Dvīhākārehi anuvadati—
English (brahmali, published): Through two: 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.16
Pali: vatthuto vā āpattito vā. 
English (brahmali, published): through action or through offense. 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.17
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from accusations settled?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.18
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ catūhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Through four of them: 

pli-tv-pvr5:6.19
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, and through further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇassa kiṁ pubbaṅgamanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the forerunner of legal issues arising from offenses?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.2
Pali: Lobho pubbaṅgamo, doso pubbaṅgamo, moho pubbaṅgamo, alobho pubbaṅgamo, adoso pubbaṅgamo, amoho pubbaṅgamo. 
English (brahmali, published): Desire is a forerunner, ill will is a forerunner, confusion is a forerunner, non-desire is a forerunner, non-ill will is a forerunner, non-confusion is a forerunner. 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.3
Pali: Kati ṭhānānīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many reasons are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.4
Pali: Satta āpattikkhandhā ṭhānāni. 
English (brahmali, published): The seven classes of offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.5
Pali: Kati vatthūnīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many grounds are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.6
Pali: Satta āpattikkhandhā vatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): The seven classes of offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.7
Pali: Kati bhūmiyoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many foundations are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.8
Pali: Satta āpattikkhandhā bhūmiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The seven classes of offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.9
Pali: Kati hetūti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many causes are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.10
Pali: Cha hetū—
English (brahmali, published): Six: 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.11
Pali: tayo akusalahetū, tayo abyākatahetū. 
English (brahmali, published): three unwholesome causes and three indeterminate causes. 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.12
Pali: Kati mūlānīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many roots are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.13
Pali: Cha āpattisamuṭṭhānāni mūlāni. 
English (brahmali, published): The six originations of offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.14
Pali: Katihākārehi āpattiṁ āpajjatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many motives does one commit an offense?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.15
Pali: Chahākārehi āpattiṁ āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): Through six: 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.16
Pali: alajjitā, aññāṇatā, kukkuccapakatatā, akappiye kappiyasaññitā, kappiye akappiyasaññitā, satisammosā. 
English (brahmali, published): through shamelessness, through ignorance, through being overcome by anxiety, through perceiving what is unallowable as allowable, through perceiving what is allowable as unallowable, through absentmindedness. 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.17
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from offenses settled?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.18
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ tīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Through three of them: 

pli-tv-pvr5:7.19
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face and through acting according to what has been admitted, and through resolution face-to-face and through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇassa kiṁ pubbaṅgamanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What is the forerunner of legal issues arising from business?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.2

Pali: Kati ṭhānānīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many reasons are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.4
Pali: Cattāri kammāni ṭhānāni. 
English (brahmali, published): The four legal procedures. 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.5
Pali: Kati vatthūnīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many grounds are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.6
Pali: Cattāri kammāni vatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): The four legal procedures. 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.7
Pali: Kati bhūmiyoti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many foundations are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.8
Pali: Cattāri kammāni bhūmiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The four legal procedures. 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.9
Pali: Kati hetūti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many causes are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.10
Pali: Nava hetū—
English (brahmali, published): Nine: 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.11
Pali: tayo kusalahetū, tayo akusalahetū, tayo abyākatahetū. 
English (brahmali, published): there are three wholesome causes, three unwholesome causes, and three indeterminate causes. 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.12
Pali: Kati mūlānīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many roots are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.13
Pali: Ekaṁ mūlaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): One: 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.14
Pali: saṅgho. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha. 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.15
Pali: Katihākārehi kiccaṁ jāyatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many motives does one give rise to business?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.16
Pali: Dvīhākārehi kiccaṁ jāyati—
English (brahmali, published): Through two: 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.17
Pali: ñattito vā apalokanato vā. 
English (brahmali, published): through a motion or through getting permission. 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.18
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from business settled?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.19
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ ekena samathena sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Through one: 

pli-tv-pvr5:8.20
Pali: sammukhāvinayena. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:9.1
Pali: Kati samathā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many principles for settling are there? 

pli-tv-pvr5:9.2
Pali: Satta samathā. 
English (brahmali, published): Seven: 

pli-tv-pvr5:9.3
Pali: Sammukhāvinayo, sativinayo, amūḷhavinayo, paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ, yebhuyyasikā, tassapāpiyasikā, tiṇavatthārako—
English (brahmali, published): resolution face-to-face, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, majority decision, further penalty, and covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:9.4
Pali: ime satta samathā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:10.1
Pali: Siyā ime satta samathā dasa samathā honti, dasa samathā satta samathā honti vatthuvasena pariyāyena? 
English (brahmali, published): With a different presentation, might the seven principles for settling become ten, and the ten become seven? 

pli-tv-pvr5:10.2
Pali: Siyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): They might. 

pli-tv-pvr5:11.1
Pali: Kathañca siyā? 
English (brahmali, published): How? 

pli-tv-pvr5:11.2
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇassa dve samathā, anuvādādhikaraṇassa cattāro samathā, āpattādhikaraṇassa tayo samathā, kiccādhikaraṇassa eko samatho. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from disputes are settled through two principles, legal issues arising from accusations are settled through four principles, legal issues arising from offenses are settled through three principles, legal issues arising from business are settled through one principle. 

pli-tv-pvr5:11.3
Pali: Evaṁ ime satta samathā dasa samathā honti, dasa samathā satta samathā honti vatthuvasena pariyāyena. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, the seven principles for settling become ten, and the ten become seven. 

pli-tv-pvr5:12.1
Pali: Pariyāyavāro niṭṭhito chaṭṭho. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixth section on “a different presentation” is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:13.0
Pali: 2. Sādhāraṇavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The section on “in common” 

pli-tv-pvr5:13.1
Pali: Kati samathā vivādādhikaraṇassa sādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many of the principles for settling legal issues arising from disputes do the monks and the nuns have in common?

Pali: Kati samathā vivādādhikaraṇassa asādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many do they not have in common?
