Pali: Kati samathā anuvādādhikaraṇassa sādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many of the principles for settling legal issues arising from accusations do the monks and the nuns have in common? 

pli-tv-pvr5:13.4
Pali: Kati samathā anuvādādhikaraṇassa asādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many do they not have in common?

Pali: Kati samathā āpattādhikaraṇassa sādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many of the principles for settling legal issues arising from offenses do the monks and the nuns have in common? 

pli-tv-pvr5:13.6
Pali: Kati samathā āpattādhikaraṇassa asādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many do they not have in common? 

pli-tv-pvr5:13.7
Pali: Kati samathā kiccādhikaraṇassa sādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many of the principles for settling legal issues arising from business do the monks and the nuns have in common? 

pli-tv-pvr5:13.8
Pali: Kati samathā kiccādhikaraṇassa asādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many do they not have in common? 

pli-tv-pvr5:14.1
Pali: Dve samathā vivādādhikaraṇassa sādhāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): They have two principles for settling legal issues arising from disputes in common: 

pli-tv-pvr5:14.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayo, yebhuyyasikā. 
English (brahmali, published): resolution face-to-face and majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:14.3
Pali: Pañca samathā vivādādhikaraṇassa asādhāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): And they have five not in common: 

pli-tv-pvr5:14.4
Pali: sativinayo, amūḷhavinayo, paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ, tassapāpiyasikā, tiṇavatthārako. 
English (brahmali, published): resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, and covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:15.1
Pali: Cattāro samathā anuvādādhikaraṇassa sādhāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): They have four principles for settling legal issues arising from accusations in common: 

pli-tv-pvr5:15.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayo, sativinayo, amūḷhavinayo, tassapāpiyasikā. 
English (brahmali, published): resolution face-to-face, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, and further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:15.3
Pali: Tayo samathā anuvādādhikaraṇassa asādhāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): And they have three not in common: 

pli-tv-pvr5:15.4
Pali: yebhuyyasikā, paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ, tiṇavatthārako. 
English (brahmali, published): majority decision, acting according to what has been admitted, and covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:16.1
Pali: Tayo samathā āpattādhikaraṇassa sādhāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): They have three principles for settling legal issues arising from offenses in common: 

pli-tv-pvr5:16.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayo, paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ, tiṇavatthārako. 
English (brahmali, published): resolution face-to-face, acting according to what has been admitted, and covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:16.3
Pali: Cattāro samathā āpattādhikaraṇassa asādhāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): And they have four not in common: 

pli-tv-pvr5:16.4
Pali: yebhuyyasikā, sativinayo, amūḷhavinayo, tassapāpiyasikā. 
English (brahmali, published): majority decision, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, and further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:17.1
Pali: Eko samatho kiccādhikaraṇassa sādhāraṇo—
English (brahmali, published): They have one principle for settling legal issues arising from business in common: 

pli-tv-pvr5:17.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayo. 
English (brahmali, published): resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:17.3
Pali: Cha samathā kiccādhikaraṇassa asādhāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): And they have six not in common: 

pli-tv-pvr5:17.4
Pali: yebhuyyasikā, sativinayo, amūḷhavinayo, paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ, tassapāpiyasikā, tiṇavatthārako. 
English (brahmali, published): majority decision, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, and covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:18.1
Pali: Sādhāraṇavāro niṭṭhito sattamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventh section on “in common” is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:19.0
Pali: 3. Tabbhāgiyavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 3. The section on “belonging to that”

English (brahmali, published): How many belong to something else?

English (brahmali, published): How many belong to something else?

pli-tv-pvr5:25.0
Pali: 4. Samathāsamathassasādhāraṇavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 4. The section on different principles for settling used in common 

pli-tv-pvr5:25.1
Pali: Samathā samathassa sādhāraṇā, samathā samathassa asādhāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): One principle for settling is used in common with another principle for settling, and one principle for settling is not used in common with another principle for settling. 

pli-tv-pvr5:25.2
Pali: Siyā samathā samathassa sādhāraṇā, siyā samathā samathassa asādhāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that one principle for settling is used in common with another principle for settling, and it may be that one principle for settling is not used in common with another principle for settling. 

pli-tv-pvr5:26.1
Pali: Kathaṁ siyā samathā samathassa sādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How may it be that one principle for settling is used in common with another principle for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:26.2
Pali: Kathaṁ siyā samathā samathassa asādhāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): How may it be that one principle for settling is not used in common with another principle for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:26.3
Pali: Yebhuyyasikā sammukhāvinayassa sādhāraṇā, sativinayassa amūḷhavinayassa paṭiññātakaraṇassa tassapāpiyasikāya tiṇavatthārakassa asādhāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): Majority decision is used in common with resolution face-to-face. It is not used in common with resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, or covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:27.1
Pali: Sativinayo sammukhāvinayassa sādhāraṇo, amūḷhavinayassa paṭiññātakaraṇassa tassapāpiyasikāya tiṇavatthārakassa yebhuyyasikāya asādhāraṇo. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution through recollection is used in common with resolution face-to-face. It is not used in common with resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, covering over as if with grass, or majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:28.1
Pali: Amūḷhavinayo sammukhāvinayassa sādhāraṇo, paṭiññātakaraṇassa tassapāpiyasikāya tiṇavatthārakassa yebhuyyasikāya sativinayassa asādhāraṇo. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution because of past insanity is used in common with resolution face-to-face. It is not used in common with acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, covering over as if with grass, majority decision, or resolution through recollection. 

pli-tv-pvr5:29.1
Pali: Paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ sammukhāvinayassa sādhāraṇaṁ, tassapāpiyasikāya tiṇavatthārakassa yebhuyyasikāya sativinayassa amūḷhavinayassa asādhāraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Acting according to what has been admitted is used in common with resolution face-to-face. It is not used in common with further penalty, covering over as if with grass, majority decision, resolution through recollection, or resolution because of past insanity. 

pli-tv-pvr5:30.1
Pali: Tassapāpiyasikā sammukhāvinayassa sādhāraṇā, tiṇavatthārakassa yebhuyyasikāya sativinayassa amūḷhavinayassa paṭiññātakaraṇassa asādhāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): Further penalty is used in common with resolution face-to-face. It is not used in common with covering over as if with grass, majority decision, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, or acting according to what has been admitted. 

pli-tv-pvr5:31.1
Pali: Tiṇavatthārako sammukhāvinayassa sādhāraṇo, yebhuyyasikāya sativinayassa amūḷhavinayassa paṭiññātakaraṇassa tassapāpiyasikāya asādhāraṇo. 
English (brahmali, published): Covering over as if with grass is used in common with resolution face-to-face. It is not used in common with majority decision, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, or further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:32.1
Pali: Evaṁ siyā samathā samathassa sādhāraṇā; 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:32.2
Pali: evaṁ siyā samathā samathassa asādhāraṇā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:33.1
Pali: Samathā samathassa sādhāraṇavāro niṭṭhito navamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The ninth section on different principles for settling used in common is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:34.0
Pali: 5. Samathāsamathassatabbhāgiyavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 5. The section on different principles for settling belonging with each other 

pli-tv-pvr5:34.1
Pali: Samathā samathassa tabbhāgiyā, samathā samathassa aññabhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): One principle for settling belongs with another principle for settling, and one principle for settling does not belong with another principle for settling. 

pli-tv-pvr5:34.2
Pali: Siyā samathā samathassa tabbhāgiyā, siyā samathā samathassa aññabhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that one principle for settling belongs with another principle for settling, and it may be that one principle for settling does not belong with another principle for settling. 

pli-tv-pvr5:35.1
Pali: Kathaṁ siyā samathā samathassa tabbhāgiyā? 
English (brahmali, published): How may it be that one principle for settling belongs with another principle for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:35.2
Pali: Kathaṁ siyā samathā samathassa aññabhāgiyā? 
English (brahmali, published): How may it be that one principle for settling does not belong with another principle for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:35.3
Pali: Yebhuyyasikā sammukhāvinayassa tabbhāgiyā, sativinayassa amūḷhavinayassa paṭiññātakaraṇassa tassapāpiyasikāya tiṇavatthārakassa aññabhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): Majority decision belongs with resolution face-to-face. It does not belong with resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, or covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:36.1
Pali: Sativinayo sammukhāvinayassa tabbhāgiyo, amūḷhavinayassa paṭiññātakaraṇassa tassapāpiyasikāya tiṇavatthārakassa yebhuyyasikāya aññabhāgiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution through recollection belongs with resolution face-to-face. It does not belong with resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, covering over as if with grass, or majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:37.1
Pali: Amūḷhavinayo sammukhāvinayassa tabbhāgiyo, paṭiññātakaraṇassa tassapāpiyasikāya tiṇavatthārakassa yebhuyyasikāya sativinayassa aññabhāgiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution because of past insanity belongs with resolution face-to-face. It does not belong with acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, covering over as if with grass, majority decision, or resolution through recollection. 

pli-tv-pvr5:38.1
Pali: Paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ sammukhāvinayassa tabbhāgiyaṁ, tassapāpiyasikāya tiṇavatthārakassa yebhuyyasikāya sativinayassa amūḷhavinayassa aññabhāgiyaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Acting according to what has been admitted belongs with resolution face-to-face. It does not belong with further penalty, covering over as if with grass, majority decision, resolution through recollection, or resolution because of past insanity. 

pli-tv-pvr5:39.1
Pali: Tassapāpiyasikā sammukhāvinayassa tabbhāgiyā, tiṇavatthārakassa yebhuyyasikāya sativinayassa amūḷhavinayassa paṭiññātakaraṇassa aññabhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): Further penalty belongs with resolution face-to-face. It does not belong with covering over as if with grass, majority decision, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, or acting according to what has been admitted. 

pli-tv-pvr5:40.1
Pali: Tiṇavatthārako sammukhāvinayassa tabbhāgiyo, yebhuyyasikāya sativinayassa amūḷhavinayassa paṭiññātakaraṇassa tassapāpiyasikāya aññabhāgiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): Covering over as if with grass belongs with resolution face-to-face. It does not belong with majority decision, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, or further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:41.1
Pali: Evaṁ siyā samathā samathassa tabbhāgiyā, evaṁ siyā samathā samathassa aññabhāgiyā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:42.1
Pali: Samathā samathassa tabbhāgiyavāro niṭṭhito dasamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The tenth section on different principles for settling belonging with each other is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:43.0

English (brahmali, published): 6. The section on “is a principle for settling also resolution face-to-face?”

English (brahmali, published): Is a principle for settling also a further penalty, and is a further penalty also a principle for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:43.7
Pali: Samatho tiṇavatthārako, tiṇavatthārako samatho? 
English (brahmali, published): Is a principle for settling also covering over as if with grass, and is covering over as if with grass also a principle for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:44.1
Pali: Yebhuyyasikā sativinayo amūḷhavinayo paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ tassapāpiyasikā tiṇavatthārako—
English (brahmali, published): Majority decision, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, and covering over as if with grass: 

pli-tv-pvr5:44.2
Pali: ime samathā samathā, no sammukhāvinayo. 
English (brahmali, published): these principles for settling are principles for settling, but they are not resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:44.3
Pali: Sammukhāvinayo samatho ceva sammukhāvinayo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face is both a principle for settling and also resolution face-to-face.

English (brahmali, published): Acting according to what has been admitted is both a principle for settling and also acting according to what has been admitted.

pli-tv-pvr5:61.0
Pali: 8. Kusalavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 8. The section on the wholesome 

pli-tv-pvr5:61.1
Pali: Sammukhāvinayo kusalo akusalo abyākato? 
English (brahmali, published): Is resolution face-to-face wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:61.2
Pali: Yebhuyyasikā kusalā akusalā abyākatā? 
English (brahmali, published): Is a majority decision wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:61.3
Pali: Sativinayo kusalo akusalo abyākato? 
English (brahmali, published): Is resolution through recollection wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:61.4
Pali: Amūḷhavinayo kusalo akusalo abyākato? 
English (brahmali, published): Is resolution because of past insanity wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:61.5
Pali: Paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ kusalaṁ akusalaṁ abyākataṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Is acting according to what has been admitted wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:61.6
Pali: Tassapāpiyasikā kusalā akusalā abyākatā? 
English (brahmali, published): Is a further penalty wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:61.7
Pali: Tiṇavatthārako kusalo akusalo abyākato? 
English (brahmali, published): Is covering over as if with grass wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:62.1
Pali: Sammukhāvinayo siyā kusalo, siyā abyākato. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face may be wholesome or indeterminate; resolution face-to-face is never unwholesome. A majority decision may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. Resolution through recollection may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. Resolution because of past insanity may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. Acting according to what has been admitted may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. A further penalty may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. Covering over as if with grass, may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:62.2
Pali: Natthi sammukhāvinayo akusalo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:63.1
Pali: Yebhuyyasikā siyā kusalā, siyā akusalā, siyā abyākatā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:64.1
Pali: Sativinayo siyā kusalo, siyā akusalo, siyā abyākato. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:65.1
Pali: Amūḷhavinayo siyā kusalo, siyā akusalo, siyā abyākato. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:66.1
Pali: Paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ siyā kusalaṁ, siyā akusalaṁ, siyā abyākataṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:67.1
Pali: Tassapāpiyasikā siyā kusalā, siyā akusalā siyā abyākatā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:68.1
Pali: Tiṇavatthārako siyā kusalo, siyā akusalo, siyā abyākato. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:69.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ kusalaṁ akusalaṁ abyākataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Are legal issues arising from disputes wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:69.2
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ kusalaṁ akusalaṁ abyākataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Are legal issues arising from accusations wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:69.3
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ kusalaṁ akusalaṁ abyākataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Are legal issues arising from offenses wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:69.4
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ kusalaṁ akusalaṁ abyākataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Are legal issues arising from business wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:70.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ siyā kusalaṁ, siyā akusalaṁ, siyā abyākataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from disputes may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. Legal issues arising from accusations may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. Legal issues arising from offenses may be unwholesome or indeterminate; legal issues arising from offenses are never wholesome. Legal issues arising from business may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:71.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ siyā kusalaṁ, siyā akusalaṁ, siyā abyākataṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:72.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ siyā akusalaṁ, siyā abyākataṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:72.2
Pali: Natthi āpattādhikaraṇaṁ kusalaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:73.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ siyā kusalaṁ, siyā akusalaṁ, siyā abyākataṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:74.1
Pali: Kusalavāro niṭṭhito terasamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The thirteenth section on the wholesome is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:75.0
Pali: 9. Yatthavāra, pucchāvāra 
English (brahmali, published): 9. The section on “where”, the section on questioning 

pli-tv-pvr5:75.1
Pali: Yattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati, tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati; 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face is appropriate where majority decision is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:75.2
Pali: yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): Majority decision is appropriate where resolution face-to-face is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:75.3
Pali: Na tattha sativinayo labbhati, na tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati, na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati, na tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there, resolution through recollection is not appropriate, nor is resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, or covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:76.1
Pali: Yattha sativinayo labbhati, tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati; 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face is appropriate where resolution through recollection is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:76.2
Pali: yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, tattha sativinayo labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution through recollection is appropriate where resolution face-to-face is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:76.3
Pali: Na tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati, na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati, na tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati, na tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there, resolution because of past insanity is not appropriate, nor is acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, covering over as if with grass, or majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:77.1
Pali: Yattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati, tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati; 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face is appropriate where resolution because of past insanity is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:77.2
Pali: yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution because of past insanity is appropriate where resolution face-to-face is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:77.3
Pali: Na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati, na tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati, na tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati, na tattha sativinayo labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there, acting according to what has been admitted is not appropriate, nor is further penalty, covering over as if with grass, majority decision, or resolution through recollection.

Pali: Yattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati, tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati; 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face is appropriate where acting according to what has been admitted is appropriate.

English (brahmali, published): Acting according to what has been admitted is appropriate where resolution face-to-face is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:78.3

English (brahmali, published): But there, further penalty is not appropriate, nor is covering over as if with grass, majority decision, resolution through recollection, or resolution because of past insanity.

English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face is appropriate where covering over as if with grass is appropriate.

English (brahmali, published): Covering over as if with grass is appropriate where resolution face-to-face is appropriate.

English (brahmali, published): But there, majority decision is not appropriate, nor is resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, or further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:81.1
Pali: Yattha yebhuyyasikā tattha sammukhāvinayo; 
English (brahmali, published): Where there is majority decision, there is resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:81.2
Pali: yattha sammukhāvinayo tattha yebhuyyasikā. 
English (brahmali, published): Where there is resolution face-to-face, there is majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:81.3
Pali: Na tattha sativinayo, na tattha amūḷhavinayo, na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā, na tattha tiṇavatthārako. 
English (brahmali, published): But there is no resolution through recollection there, nor resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, or covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:82.1
Pali: Yattha sativinayo tattha sammukhāvinayo; 
English (brahmali, published): Where there is resolution through recollection, there is resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:82.2
Pali: yattha sammukhāvinayo tattha sativinayo. 
English (brahmali, published): Where there is resolution face-to-face, there is resolution through recollection. 

pli-tv-pvr5:82.3
Pali: Na tattha amūḷhavinayo, na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā, na tattha tiṇavatthārako, na tattha yebhuyyasikā. 
English (brahmali, published): But there is no resolution because of past insanity there, nor acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, covering over as if with grass, or majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:82.4
Pali: Sammukhāvinayaṁ kātuna mūlaṁ …pe…. 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face to be done as the basis …

Pali: Yattha tiṇavatthārako tattha sammukhāvinayo; 
English (brahmali, published): Where there is covering over as if with grass, there is  resolution face-to-face.

English (brahmali, published): But there is no majority decision there, nor resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, or further penalty.

pli-tv-pvr5:85.0
Pali: 10. Samathavāra, vissajjanāvāra 
English (brahmali, published): 10. The section on settling, the section on responding 

pli-tv-pvr5:85.1
Pali: Yasmiṁ samaye sammukhāvinayena ca yebhuyyasikāya ca adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati, yattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): On an occasion when a legal issue is being resolved through resolution face-to-face and majority decision, then, where majority decision is appropriate, there resolution face-to-face is appropriate, and where resolution face-to-face is appropriate, there majority decision is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:85.2
Pali: Na tattha sativinayo labbhati, na tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati, na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati, na tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there resolution through recollection is not appropriate, nor is resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, or covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:86.1
Pali: Yasmiṁ samaye sammukhāvinayena ca sativinayena ca adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati, yattha sativinayo labbhati tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati tattha sativinayo labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): On an occasion when a legal issue is being resolved through resolution face-to-face and resolution through recollection, then, where resolution through recollection is appropriate, there resolution face-to-face is appropriate, and where resolution face-to-face is appropriate, there resolution through recollection is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:86.2
Pali: Na tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati, na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati, na tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati, na tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there resolution because of past insanity is not appropriate, nor is acting according to what has been admitted, further penalty, covering over as if with grass, or majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:87.1
Pali: Yasmiṁ samaye sammukhāvinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati, yattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): On an occasion when a legal issue is being resolved through resolution face-to-face and resolution because of past insanity, then, where resolution because of past insanity is appropriate, there resolution face-to-face is appropriate, and where resolution face-to-face is appropriate, there resolution because of past insanity is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:87.2
Pali: Na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati, na tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati, na tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati, na tattha sativinayo labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there acting according to what has been admitted is not appropriate, nor is further penalty, covering over as if with grass, majority decision, or resolution through recollection. 

pli-tv-pvr5:88.1
Pali: Yasmiṁ samaye sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati, yattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): On an occasion when a legal issue is being resolved through resolution face-to-face and acting according to what has been admitted, then, where acting according to what has been admitted is appropriate, there resolution face-to-face is appropriate, and where resolution face-to-face is appropriate, there acting according to what has been admitted is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:88.2
Pali: Na tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati, na tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati, na tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati, na tattha sativinayo labbhati, na tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there further penalty is not appropriate, nor is covering over as if with grass, majority decision, resolution through recollection, or resolution because of past insanity. 

pli-tv-pvr5:89.1
Pali: Yasmiṁ samaye sammukhāvinayena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati, yattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): On an occasion when a legal issue is being resolved through resolution face-to-face and further penalty, then, where further penalty is appropriate, there resolution face-to-face is appropriate, and where resolution face-to-face is appropriate, there further penalty is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:89.2
Pali: Na tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati, na tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati, na tattha sativinayo labbhati, na tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati, na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there covering over as if with grass is not appropriate, nor is majority decision, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, or acting according to what has been admitted. 

pli-tv-pvr5:90.1
Pali: Yasmiṁ samaye sammukhāvinayena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati, yattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati tattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati, yattha sammukhāvinayo labbhati tattha tiṇavatthārako labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): On an occasion when a legal issue is being resolved through resolution face-to-face and covering over as if with grass, then, where covering over as if with grass is appropriate, there resolution face-to-face is appropriate, and where resolution face-to-face is appropriate, there covering over as if with grass is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr5:90.2
Pali: Na tattha yebhuyyasikā labbhati, na tattha sativinayo labbhati, na tattha amūḷhavinayo labbhati, na tattha paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ labbhati, na tattha tassapāpiyasikā labbhati. 
English (brahmali, published): But there majority decision is not appropriate, nor is resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, or further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:91.1
Pali: Samathavāro niṭṭhito pannarasamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifteenth section on settling is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:92.0
Pali: 11. Saṁsaṭṭhavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 11. The section on connection 

pli-tv-pvr5:92.1
Pali: Adhikaraṇanti vā samathāti vā ime dhammā saṁsaṭṭhā udāhu visaṁsaṭṭhā? 
English (brahmali, published): “Are ‘legal issue’ and ‘principle for settling’ connected or disconnected? 

pli-tv-pvr5:92.2
Pali: Labbhā ca panimesaṁ dhammānaṁ vinibbhujitvā vinibbhujitvā nānākaraṇaṁ paññāpetunti? 
English (brahmali, published): Is it possible to completely separate them and point to their difference?” 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.1
Pali: Adhikaraṇanti vā samathāti vā ime dhammā visaṁsaṭṭhā, no saṁsaṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘Legal issue’ and ‘principle for settling’ are disconnected, not connected, 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.2
Pali: Labbhā ca panimesaṁ dhammānaṁ vinibbhujitvā vinibbhujitvā nānākaraṇaṁ paññāpetunti. 
English (brahmali, published): and it’s possible to completely separate them and point to their difference”: 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.3
Pali: So—“mā hevan”tissa vacanīyo. 
English (brahmali, published): they should be told not to say this. 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.4
Pali: Adhikaraṇanti vā samathāti vā ime dhammā saṁsaṭṭhā, no visaṁsaṭṭhā. 
English (brahmali, published): “‘Legal issue’ and ‘principle for settling’ are connected, not disconnected, 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.5
Pali: No ca labbhā imesaṁ dhammānaṁ vinibbhujitvā vinibbhujitvā nānākaraṇaṁ paññāpetuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): and it’s impossible to completely separate them and point to their difference. 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.6
Pali: Taṁ kissa hetu? 
English (brahmali, published): Why’s that? 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.7
Pali: Nanu vuttaṁ bhagavatā—
English (brahmali, published): Didn’t the Buddha say that 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.8
Pali: “cattārimāni, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇāni, satta samathā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are four kinds of legal issues and seven principles for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.9
Pali: Adhikaraṇā samathehi sammanti, samathā adhikaraṇehi sammanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues are settled through the principles for settling; the principles for settling are settled through legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.10
Pali: Evaṁ, ime dhammā saṁsaṭṭhā no visaṁsaṭṭhā; 
English (brahmali, published): In this way they are connected, not disconnected, 

pli-tv-pvr5:93.11
Pali: no ca labbhā imesaṁ dhammānaṁ vinibbhujitvā vinibbhujitvā nānākaraṇaṁ paññāpetun”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): and it’s impossible to completely separate them and point to their difference.” 

pli-tv-pvr5:94.1
Pali: Saṁsaṭṭhavāro niṭṭhito soḷasamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The sixteenth section on connection is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:95.0
Pali: 12. Sammativāra 
English (brahmali, published): 12. The section on settling 

pli-tv-pvr5:95.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from disputes settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:95.2
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from accusations settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:95.3
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from offenses settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:95.4
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from business settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:96.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ dvīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from disputes are settled through two principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:96.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca yebhuyyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face and through majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:97.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ catūhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from accusations are settled through four principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:97.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, and through further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:98.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ tīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from offenses are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:98.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through acting according to what has been admitted, and through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:99.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ ekena samathena sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from business are settled through one principle: 

pli-tv-pvr5:99.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:100.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇañca anuvādādhikaraṇañca katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from disputes and legal issues arising from accusations settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:100.2
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇañca anuvādādhikaraṇañca pañcahi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): Through five: 

pli-tv-pvr5:100.3

English (brahmali, published): Through six:

Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇañca anuvādādhikaraṇañca kiccādhikaraṇañca pañcahi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): Through five: 

pli-tv-pvr5:107.3
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca yebhuyyasikāya ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through majority decision, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, and through further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:108.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇañca āpattādhikaraṇañca kiccādhikaraṇañca katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, and legal issues arising from business settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:108.2
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇañca āpattādhikaraṇañca kiccādhikaraṇañca chahi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): Through six: 

pli-tv-pvr5:108.3
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, through acting according to what has been admitted, through further penalty, and through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:109.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇañca anuvādādhikaraṇañca āpattādhikaraṇañca kiccādhikaraṇañca katihi samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from disputes, legal issues arising from accusations, legal issues arising from offenses, and legal issues arising from business settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:109.2
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇañca anuvādādhikaraṇañca āpattādhikaraṇañca kiccādhikaraṇañca sattahi samathehi sammanti—
English (brahmali, published): Through seven: 

pli-tv-pvr5:109.3
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca yebhuyyasikāya ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through majority decision, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, through acting according to what has been admitted, through further penalty, and through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:110.1
Pali: Sammativāro niṭṭhito sattarasamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The seventeenth section on settling is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:111.0
Pali: 13. Sammantinasammantivāra 
English (brahmali, published): 13. The section on settling and not settling 

pli-tv-pvr5:111.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammati, katihi samathehi na sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from disputes settled, and through how many principles are they not settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:111.2
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammati, katihi samathehi na sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from accusations settled, and through how many principles are they not settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:111.3
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammati, katihi samathehi na sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from offenses settled, and through how many principles are they not settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:111.4
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ katihi samathehi sammati, katihi samathehi na sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many principles for settling are legal issues arising from business settled, and through how many principles are they not settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:112.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ dvīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from disputes are settled through two principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:112.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca yebhuyyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, and through majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:112.3
Pali: Pañcahi samathehi na sammati—
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through five principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:112.4
Pali: sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, through acting according to what has been admitted, through further penalty, and through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:113.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ catūhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from accusations are settled through four principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:113.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, and through further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:113.3
Pali: Tīhi samathehi na sammati—
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:113.4
Pali: yebhuyyasikāya ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through majority decision, through acting according to what has been admitted, and through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:114.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ tīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from offenses are settled through three principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:114.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through acting according to what has been admitted, and through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:114.3
Pali: Catūhi samathehi na sammati—
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through four principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:114.4
Pali: yebhuyyasikāya ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through majority decision, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, and through further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:115.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ ekena samathena sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from business are settled through one principle: 

pli-tv-pvr5:115.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:115.3
Pali: Chahi samathehi na sammati—
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through six principles: 

pli-tv-pvr5:115.4
Pali: yebhuyyasikāya ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through majority decision, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, through acting according to what has been admitted, through further penalty, and through covering over as if with grass.

pli-tv-pvr5:127.0
Pali: 14. Samathādhikaraṇavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 14. The section on principles for settling and legal issues 

pli-tv-pvr5:127.1
Pali: Samathā samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Are principles for settling settled through principles for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:127.2
Pali: Samathā adhikaraṇehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Are principles for settling settled through legal issues? 

pli-tv-pvr5:127.3
Pali: Adhikaraṇā samathehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Are legal issues settled through principles for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:127.4
Pali: Adhikaraṇā adhikaraṇehi sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Are legal issues settled through legal issues? 

pli-tv-pvr5:128.1
Pali: Siyā samathā samathehi sammanti, siyā samathā samathehi na sammanti. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that principles for settling are settled through principles for settling; it may be that principles for settling are not settled through principles for settling. 

pli-tv-pvr5:128.2
Pali: Siyā samathā adhikaraṇehi sammanti, siyā samathā adhikaraṇehi na sammanti. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that principles for settling are settled through legal issues; it may be that principles for settling are not settled through legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr5:128.3
Pali: Siyā adhikaraṇā samathehi sammanti, siyā adhikaraṇā samathehi na sammanti. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that legal issues are settled through principles for settling; it may be that legal issues are not settled through principles for settling. 

pli-tv-pvr5:128.4
Pali: Siyā adhikaraṇā adhikaraṇehi sammanti, siyā adhikaraṇā adhikaraṇehi na sammanti. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that legal issues are settled through legal issues; it may be that legal issues are not settled through legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr5:129.1
Pali: Kathaṁ siyā samathā samathehi sammanti, kathaṁ siyā samathā samathehi na sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): How may it be that principles for settling are settled through principles for settling? And how may it be that principles for settling are not settled through principles for settling? 

pli-tv-pvr5:129.2
Pali: Yebhuyyasikā sammukhāvinayena sammati; 
English (brahmali, published): A majority decision is settled through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:129.3
Pali: sativinayena na sammati, amūḷhavinayena na sammati, paṭiññātakaraṇena na sammati, tassapāpiyasikāya na sammati, tiṇavatthārakena na sammati. 
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, through acting according to what has been admitted, through further penalty, or through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:130.1
Pali: Sativinayo sammukhāvinayena sammati; 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution through recollection is settled through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:130.2
Pali: amūḷhavinayena na sammati, paṭiññātakaraṇena na sammāti, tassapāpiyasikāya na sammati, tiṇavatthārakena na sammati, yebhuyyasikāya na sammati. 
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through resolution because of past insanity, through acting according to what has been admitted, through further penalty, through covering over as if with grass, or through majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:131.1
Pali: Amūḷhavinayo sammukhāvinayena sammati; 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution because of past insanity is settled through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:131.2
Pali: paṭiññātakaraṇena na sammati, tassapāpiyasikāya na sammati, tiṇavatthārakena na sammati, yebhuyyasikāya na sammati, sativinayena na sammati. 
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through acting according to what has been admitted, through further penalty, through covering over as if with grass, through majority decision, or through resolution through recollection. 

pli-tv-pvr5:132.1
Pali: Paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ sammukhāvinayena sammati; 
English (brahmali, published): Acting according to what has been admitted is settled through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:132.2
Pali: tassapāpiyasikāya na sammati, tiṇavatthārakena na sammati, yebhuyyasikāya na sammati, sativinayena na sammati, amūḷhavinayena na sammati. 
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through further penalty, through covering over as if with grass, through majority decision, through resolution through recollection, or through resolution because of past insanity. 

pli-tv-pvr5:133.1
Pali: Tassapāpiyasikā sammukhāvinayena sammati; 
English (brahmali, published): A further penalty is settled through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:133.2
Pali: tiṇavatthārakena na sammati, yebhuyyasikāya na sammati, sativinayena na sammati, amūḷhavinayena na sammati, paṭiññātakaraṇena na sammati. 
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through covering over as if with grass, through majority decision, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, or through acting according to what has been admitted. 

pli-tv-pvr5:134.1
Pali: Tiṇavatthārako sammukhāvinayena sammati; 
English (brahmali, published): Covering over as if with grass is settled through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:134.2
Pali: yebhuyyasikāya na sammati, sativinayena na sammati, amūḷhavinayena na sammati, paṭiññātakaraṇena na sammati, tassapāpiyasikāya na sammati. 
English (brahmali, published): It is not settled through majority decision, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, through acting according to what has been admitted, or through further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:134.3
Pali: Evaṁ siyā samathā samathehi sammanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:134.4
Pali: Evaṁ siyā samathā samathehi na sammanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:135.1
Pali: Kathaṁ siyā samathā adhikaraṇehi sammanti, kathaṁ siyā samathā adhikaraṇehi na sammanti? 
English (brahmali, published): How may it be that principles for settling are settled through legal issues? How may it be that principles for settling are not settled through legal issues? 

pli-tv-pvr5:135.2
Pali: Sammukhāvinayo vivādādhikaraṇena na sammati, anuvādādhikaraṇena na sammati, āpattādhikaraṇena na sammati; 
English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face is not settled through a legal issue arising from a dispute, a legal issue arising from an accusation, or a legal issue arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr5:135.3
Pali: kiccādhikaraṇena sammati. 
English (brahmali, published): It is settled through a legal issue arising from business.

English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from disputes are settled through resolution face-to-face and through majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:142.3

pli-tv-pvr5:143.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ sammukhāvinayena ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca sammati;

Pali: Chāpi samathā cattāropi adhikaraṇā sammukhāvinayena sammanti; 
English (brahmali, published): The six principles for settling and the four legal issues are settled through resolution face-to-face. 

pli-tv-pvr5:150.2

English (brahmali, published): Resolution face-to-face is not settled through anything. 

pli-tv-pvr5:151.1

English (brahmali, published): The nineteenth section on principles for settling and legal issues is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.0
Pali: 15. Samuṭṭhāpanavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 15. The section on causing to originate 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ samuṭṭhāpeti? 
English (brahmali, published): Which of the four legal issues causes legal issues arising from disputes to originate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.2
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ na katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ samuṭṭhāpeti; 
English (brahmali, published): None of them. 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.3
Pali: api ca vivādādhikaraṇapaccayā cattāro adhikaraṇā jāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Nevertheless, the four legal issues are produced from legal issues arising from disputes. 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.4
Pali: Yathā kathaṁ viya? 
English (brahmali, published): How is that? 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.5
Pali: Idha bhikkhū vivadanti—
English (brahmali, published): It may be that monks are disputing, saying, 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.6
Pali: “dhammo”ti vā, “adhammo”ti vā, “vinayo”ti vā, “avinayo”ti vā, “abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenā”ti vā, “bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenā”ti vā, “anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenā”ti vā, “āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenā”ti vā, “apaññattaṁ tathāgatenā”ti vā, “paññattaṁ tathāgatenā”ti vā, “āpattī”ti vā, “anāpattī”ti vā, “lahukā āpattī”ti vā, “garukā āpattī”ti vā, “sāvasesā āpattī”ti vā, “anavasesā āpattī”ti vā, “duṭṭhullā āpattī”ti vā “aduṭṭhullā āpattī”ti vā. 
English (brahmali, published): “This is the Teaching”, “This is contrary to the Teaching”, “This is the Monastic Law”, “This is contrary to the Monastic Law”, “This was spoken by the Buddha”, “This wasn’t spoken by the Buddha”, “This was practiced by the Buddha”, “This wasn’t practiced by the Buddha”, “This was laid down by the Buddha”, “This wasn’t laid down by the Buddha”, “This is an offense”, “This isn’t an offense”, “This is a light offense”, “This is a heavy offense”, “This is a curable offense”, “This is an incurable offense”, “This is a grave offense”, or “This is a minor offense.” 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.7
Pali: Yaṁ tattha bhaṇḍanaṁ kalaho viggaho vivādo nānāvādo aññathāvādo vipaccatāya vohāro medhakaṁ, idaṁ vuccati vivādādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this, whatever there is of quarreling, arguing, conflict, disputing, variety in opinion, difference in opinion, heated speech, or strife—this is called a legal issue arising from a dispute. 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.8
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇe saṅgho vivadati. 
English (brahmali, published): When, during a legal issue arising from a dispute, the Sangha disputes, there is a legal issue arising from a dispute. 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.9
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ vivadamāno anuvadati. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who is disputing makes an accusation, there is a legal issue arising from an accusation. 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.10
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ anuvadamāno āpattiṁ āpajjati āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who is accusing commits an offense, there is a legal issue arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.11
Pali: Tāya āpattiyā saṅgho kammaṁ karoti kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When the Sangha does a legal procedure because of that offense, there is a legal issue arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr5:152.12
Pali: Evaṁ vivādādhikaraṇapaccayā cattāro adhikaraṇā jāyanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ samuṭṭhāpeti? 
English (brahmali, published): Which of the four legal issues causes legal issues arising from accusations to originate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.2
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ na katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ samuṭṭhāpeti; 
English (brahmali, published): None of them. 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.3
Pali: api ca anuvādādhikaraṇapaccayā cattāro adhikaraṇā jāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Nevertheless, the four legal issues are produced from legal issues arising from accusations. 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.4
Pali: Yathā kathaṁ viya? 
English (brahmali, published): How is that? 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.5
Pali: Idha bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ anuvadanti sīlavipattiyā vā ācāravipattiyā vā diṭṭhivipattiyā vā ājīvavipattiyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that monks accuse a monk of failure in morality, failure in conduct, failure in view, or failure in livelihood. 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.6
Pali: Yo tattha anuvādo anuvadanā anullapanā anubhaṇanā anusampavaṅkatā abbhussahanatā anubalappadānaṁ, idaṁ vuccati anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): In regard to this, whatever there is of accusations, accusing, allegations, blame, taking sides because of friendship, taking part in the accusation, or supporting the accusation—this is called a legal issue arising from an accusation. 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.7
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇe saṅgho vivadati. 
English (brahmali, published): When, during a legal issue arising from an accusation, the Sangha disputes, there is a legal issue arising from a dispute. 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.8
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ vivadamāno anuvadati. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who is disputing makes an accusation, there is a legal issue arising from an accusation. 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.9
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ anuvadamāno āpattiṁ āpajjati āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who is accusing commits an offense, there is a legal issue arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.10
Pali: Tāya āpattiyā saṅgho kammaṁ karoti kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When the Sangha does a legal procedure because of that offense, there is a legal issue arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr5:153.11
Pali: Evaṁ anuvādādhikaraṇapaccayā cattāro adhikaraṇā jāyanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ samuṭṭhāpeti? 
English (brahmali, published): Which of the four legal issues causes legal issues arising from offenses to originate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.2
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ na katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ samuṭṭhāpeti; 
English (brahmali, published): None of them. 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.3
Pali: api ca āpattādhikaraṇapaccayā cattāro adhikaraṇā jāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Nevertheless, the four legal issues are produced from legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.4
Pali: Yathā kathaṁ viya? 
English (brahmali, published): How is that? 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.5
Pali: Pañcapi āpattikkhandhā āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, sattapi āpattikkhandhā āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, idaṁ vuccati āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): There are legal issues arising from offenses because of the five classes of offenses, and there are legal issues arising from offenses because of the seven classes of offenses—these are called legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.6
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇe saṅgho vivadati. 
English (brahmali, published): When, during a legal issue arising from an offense, the Sangha disputes, there is a legal issue arising from a dispute. 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.7
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ vivadamāno anuvadati. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who is disputing makes an accusation, there is a legal issue arising from an accusation. 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.8
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ anuvadamāno āpattiṁ āpajjati āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who is accusing commits an offense, there is a legal issue arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.9
Pali: Tāya āpattiyā saṅgho kammaṁ karoti kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When the Sangha does a legal procedure because of that offense, there is a legal issue arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr5:154.10
Pali: Evaṁ āpattādhikaraṇapaccayā cattāro adhikaraṇā jāyanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ samuṭṭhāpeti? 
English (brahmali, published): Which of the four legal issues causes legal issues arising from business to originate? 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.2
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ na katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ samuṭṭhāpeti, 
English (brahmali, published): None of them. 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.3
Pali: api ca kiccādhikaraṇapaccayā cattāro adhikaraṇā jāyanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Nevertheless, the four legal issues are produced from legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.4
Pali: Yathā kathaṁ viya? 
English (brahmali, published): How is that? 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.5
Pali: Yā saṅghassa kiccayatā karaṇīyatā apalokanakammaṁ ñattikammaṁ ñattidutiyakammaṁ ñatticatutthakammaṁ, idaṁ vuccati kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Whatever is the duty or the business of the Sangha—a legal procedure consisting of getting permission, a legal procedure consisting of one motion, a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements—this is called a legal issue arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.6
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇe saṅgho vivadati. 
English (brahmali, published): When, during a legal issue arising from business, the Sangha disputes, there is a legal issue arising from a dispute. 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.7
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ vivadamāno anuvadati. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who is disputing makes an accusation, there is a legal issue arising from an accusation. 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.8
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ anuvadamāno āpattiṁ āpajjati āpattādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who is accusing commits an offense, there is a legal issue arising from an offense. 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.9
Pali: Tāya āpattiyā saṅgho kammaṁ karoti kiccādhikaraṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When the Sangha does a legal procedure because of that offense, there is a legal issue arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr5:155.10
Pali: Evaṁ kiccādhikaraṇapaccayā cattāro adhikaraṇā jāyanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr5:156.1
Pali: Samuṭṭhāpanavāro niṭṭhito vīsatimo. 
English (brahmali, published): The twentieth section on causing to originate is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:157.0
Pali: 16. Bhajativāra 
English (brahmali, published): 16. The section on “belonging to” 

pli-tv-pvr5:157.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ bhajati? 
English (brahmali, published): “To which of the four legal issues do legal issues arising from disputes belong? 

pli-tv-pvr5:157.2
Pali: Katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ upanissitaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues do they depend on? 

pli-tv-pvr5:157.3
Pali: Katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ pariyāpannaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues are they included in? 

pli-tv-pvr5:157.4
Pali: Katamena adhikaraṇena saṅgahitaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues are they grouped with? 

pli-tv-pvr5:158.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ bhajati? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four legal issues do legal issues arising from accusations belong? 

pli-tv-pvr5:158.2
Pali: Katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ upanissitaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues do they depend on? 

pli-tv-pvr5:158.3
Pali: Katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ pariyāpannaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues are they included in? 

pli-tv-pvr5:158.4
Pali: Katamena adhikaraṇena saṅgahitaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues are they grouped with? 

pli-tv-pvr5:159.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ bhajati? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four legal issues do legal issues arising from offenses belong? 

pli-tv-pvr5:159.2
Pali: Katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ upanissitaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues do they depend on? 

pli-tv-pvr5:159.3

English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues are they included in? 

pli-tv-pvr5:159.4
Pali: Katamena adhikaraṇena saṅgahitaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues are they grouped with? 

pli-tv-pvr5:160.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ bhajati? 
English (brahmali, published): To which of the four legal issues do legal issues arising from business belong? 

pli-tv-pvr5:160.2
Pali: Katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ upanissitaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues do they depend on? 

pli-tv-pvr5:160.3
Pali: Katamaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ pariyāpannaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues are they included in? 

pli-tv-pvr5:160.4
Pali: Katamena adhikaraṇena saṅgahitaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Which legal issues are they grouped with? 

pli-tv-pvr5:161.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ vivādādhikaraṇaṁ bhajati, vivādādhikaraṇaṁ upanissitaṁ, vivādādhikaraṇaṁ pariyāpannaṁ, vivādādhikaraṇena saṅgahitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from disputes belong to legal issues arising from disputes; they depend on legal issues arising from disputes; they are included in legal issues arising from disputes; they are grouped with legal issues arising from disputes. 

pli-tv-pvr5:162.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ bhajati, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ upanissitaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ pariyāpannaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇena saṅgahitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from accusations belong to legal issues arising from accusations; they depend on legal issues arising from accusations; they are included in legal issues arising from accusations; they are grouped with legal issues arising from accusations. 

pli-tv-pvr5:163.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ āpattādhikaraṇaṁ bhajati, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ upanissitaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ pariyāpannaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇena saṅgahitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from offenses belong to legal issues arising from offenses; they depend on legal issues arising from offenses; they are included in legal issues arising from offenses; they are grouped with legal issues arising from offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr5:164.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ catunnaṁ adhikaraṇānaṁ kiccādhikaraṇaṁ bhajati, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ upanissitaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ pariyāpannaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇena saṅgahitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from business belong to legal issues arising from business; they depend on legal issues arising from business; they are included in legal issues arising from business; they are grouped with legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr5:165.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ sattannaṁ samathānaṁ kati samathe bhajati, kati samathe upanissitaṁ, kati samathe pariyāpannaṁ, katihi samathehi saṅgahitaṁ, katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): To how many of the seven principles for settling do legal issues arising from disputes belong? On how many principles for settling do they depend? In how many principles for settling are they included? With how many principles for settling are they grouped? Through how many principles for settling are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:166.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ sattannaṁ samathānaṁ kati samathe bhajati, kati samathe upanissitaṁ, kati samathe pariyāpannaṁ, katihi samathehi saṅgahitaṁ, katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): To how many of the seven principles for settling do legal issues arising from accusations belong? On how many principles for settling do they depend? In how many principles for settling are they included? With how many principles for settling are they grouped? Through how many principles for settling are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:167.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ sattannaṁ samathānaṁ kati samathe bhajati, kati samathe upanissitaṁ, kati samathe pariyāpannaṁ, katihi samathehi saṅgahitaṁ, katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): To how many of the seven principles for settling do legal issues arising from offenses belong? On how many principles for settling do they depend? In how many principles for settling are they included? With how many principles for settling are they grouped? Through how many principles for settling are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:168.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ sattannaṁ samathānaṁ kati samathe bhajati, kati samathe upanissitaṁ, kati samathe pariyāpannaṁ, katihi samathehi saṅgahitaṁ, katihi samathehi sammati? 
English (brahmali, published): To how many of the seven principles for settling do legal issues arising from business belong? On how many principles for settling do they depend? In how many principles for settling are they included? With how many principles for settling are they grouped? Through how many principles for settling are they settled? 

pli-tv-pvr5:169.1
Pali: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ sattannaṁ samathānaṁ dve samathe bhajati, dve samathe upanissitaṁ, dve samathe pariyāpannaṁ, dvīhi samathehi saṅgahitaṁ, dvīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from disputes belong to two principles for settling; they depend on two principles for settling; they are included in two principles for settling; they are grouped with two principles for settling; they are settled through two principles for settling: 

pli-tv-pvr5:169.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca yebhuyyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face and through majority decision. 

pli-tv-pvr5:170.1
Pali: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ sattannaṁ samathānaṁ cattāro samathe bhajati, cattāro samathe upanissitaṁ, cattāro samathe pariyāpannaṁ, catūhi samathehi saṅgahitaṁ, catūhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from accusations belong to four principles for settling; they depend on four principles for settling; they are included in four principles for settling; they are grouped with four principles for settling; they are settled through four principles for settling: 

pli-tv-pvr5:170.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca sativinayena ca amūḷhavinayena ca tassapāpiyasikāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through resolution through recollection, through resolution because of past insanity, and through further penalty. 

pli-tv-pvr5:171.1
Pali: Āpattādhikaraṇaṁ sattannaṁ samathānaṁ tayo samathe bhajati, tayo samathe upanissitaṁ, tayo samathe pariyāpannaṁ, tīhi samathehi saṅgahitaṁ tīhi samathehi sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from offenses belong to three principles for settling; they depend on three principles for settling; they are included in three principles for settling; they are grouped with three principles for settling; they are settled through three principles for settling: 

pli-tv-pvr5:171.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayena ca paṭiññātakaraṇena ca tiṇavatthārakena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face, through acting according to what has been admitted, and through covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr5:172.1
Pali: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ sattannaṁ samathānaṁ ekaṁ samathaṁ bhajati, ekaṁ samathaṁ upanissitaṁ, ekaṁ samathaṁ pariyāpannaṁ, ekena samathena saṅgahitaṁ, ekena samathena sammati—
English (brahmali, published): Legal issues arising from business belong to one principle for settling; they depend on one principle for settling; they are included in one principle for settling; they are grouped with one principle for settling; they are settled through one principle for settling: 

pli-tv-pvr5:172.2
Pali: sammukhāvinayenāti. 
English (brahmali, published): through resolution face-to-face.” 

pli-tv-pvr5:173.1
Pali: Bhajativāro niṭṭhito ekavīsatimo. 
English (brahmali, published): The twenty-first section on “belonging to” is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:174.1
Pali: Samathabhedo niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The legal issues and their settling are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr5:175.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr5:176.1
Pali: Adhikaraṇaṁ pariyāyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Legal issues, a different presentation, 

pli-tv-pvr5:176.2
Pali: sādhāraṇā ca bhāgiyā; 
English (brahmali, published): And in common, belonging to; 

pli-tv-pvr5:176.3
Pali: Samathā sādhāraṇikā, 
English (brahmali, published): Principles for settling used in common, 

pli-tv-pvr5:176.4
Pali: samathassa tabbhāgiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): Principles for settling that belong with that. 

pli-tv-pvr5:177.1
Pali: Samathā sammukhā ceva, 
English (brahmali, published): Principle for settling is also resolution face-to-face, 

pli-tv-pvr5:177.2
Pali: vinayena kusalena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): On resolution, and on the wholesome; 

pli-tv-pvr5:177.3
Pali: Yattha samathasaṁsaṭṭhā, 
English (brahmali, published): Where, on settling, on connection, 

pli-tv-pvr5:177.4
Pali: sammanti na sammanti ca; 
English (brahmali, published): On settling, and on not settling; 

pli-tv-pvr5:177.5
Pali: Samathādhikaraṇañceva, 
English (brahmali, published): And on principles for settling and legal issues, 

pli-tv-pvr5:177.6
Pali: samuṭṭhānaṁ bhajanti cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Origination, and belonging to.”

pli-tv-pvr7:1.1
Pali: Āpattikarā dhammā jānitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “The things that produce offenses should be known.

English (brahmali, published): The things that do not produce offenses should be known.

English (brahmali, published): Light offenses should be known.

English (brahmali, published): Curable offenses should be known.

English (brahmali, published): Grave offenses should be known.

pli-tv-pvr7:7.0
Pali: 2. Dukavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The section on twos 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.1
Pali: Atthāpatti saññā vimokkhā, atthāpatti no saññāvimokkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses for which perception is a factor, and offenses for which it is not. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.2
Pali: Atthāpatti laddhasamāpattikassa, atthāpatti na laddhasamāpattikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses for which the attainment has been achieved, and offenses for which it has not. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.3
Pali: Atthāpatti saddhammapaṭisaññuttā, atthāpatti asaddhammapaṭisaññuttā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses that are connected with the true Teaching, and offenses that are not. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.4
Pali: Atthāpatti saparikkhārapaṭisaññuttā, atthāpatti paraparikkhārapaṭisaññuttā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses that are connected with one’s own requisites, and offenses that are connected with someone else’s requisites. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.5
Pali: Atthāpatti sapuggalapaṭisaññuttā, atthāpatti parapuggalapaṭisaññuttā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses that are connected with oneself, and offenses that are connected with others. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.6
Pali: Atthi saccaṁ bhaṇanto garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati, musā bhaṇanto lahukaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): There are heavy offenses committed by one speaking the truth, and light offenses committed by one speaking falsely. 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.7
Pali: Atthi musā bhaṇanto garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati, saccaṁ bhaṇanto lahukaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): There are heavy offenses committed by one speaking falsely, and light offenses committed by one speaking the truth. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.8
Pali: Atthāpatti bhūmigato āpajjati, no vehāsagato. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by one on the ground, not by one above ground. 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.9
Pali: Atthāpatti vehāsagato āpajjati, no bhūmigato. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by one above ground, not by one on the ground. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.10
Pali: Atthāpatti nikkhamanto āpajjati, no pavisanto. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by one who is leaving, not by one who is entering. 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.11
Pali: Atthāpatti pavisanto āpajjati, no nikkhamanto. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by one who is entering, not by one who is leaving. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.12
Pali: Atthāpatti ādiyanto āpajjati, atthāpatti anādiyanto āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by applying, and offenses committed by not applying. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.13
Pali: Atthāpatti samādiyanto āpajjati, atthāpatti na samādiyanto āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by undertaking, and offenses committed by not undertaking. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.14
Pali: Atthāpatti karonto āpajjati, atthāpatti na karonto āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by doing, and offenses committed by not doing. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.15
Pali: Atthāpatti dento āpajjati, atthāpatti na dento āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by giving, and offenses committed by not giving. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.16
Pali: Atthāpatti desento āpajjati, atthāpatti na desento āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by teaching, and offenses committed by not teaching. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.17
Pali: Atthāpatti paṭiggaṇhanto āpajjati, atthāpatti na paṭiggaṇhanto āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by receiving, and offenses committed by not receiving. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.18
Pali: Atthāpatti paribhogena āpajjati, atthāpatti na paribhogena āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by using, and offenses committed by not using. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.19
Pali: Atthāpatti rattiṁ āpajjati, no divā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed at night, not by day. 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.20
Pali: Atthāpatti divā āpajjati, no rattiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by day, not at night. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.21
Pali: Atthāpatti aruṇugge āpajjati, atthāpatti na aruṇugge āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed at dawn, and offenses committed not at dawn. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.22
Pali: Atthāpatti chindanto āpajjati, atthāpatti na chindanto āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by cutting, and offenses committed by not cutting. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.23
Pali: Atthāpatti chādento āpajjati, atthāpatti na chādento āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by covering, and offenses committed by not covering. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:7.24
Pali: Atthāpatti dhārento āpajjati, atthāpatti na dhārento āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses committed by wearing, and offenses committed by not wearing.

Pali: Dve uposathā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two observance days: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.2
Pali: cātuddasiko ca pannarasiko ca. 
English (brahmali, published): the fourteenth and the fifteenth day of the lunar half-month. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.3

English (brahmali, published): There are two invitation days: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.4
Pali: cātuddasikā ca pannarasikā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): the fourteenth and the fifteenth day of the lunar half-month. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.5
Pali: Dve kammāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.6
Pali: apalokanakammaṁ, ñattikammaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the procedure consisting of getting permission, and the procedure consisting of one motion. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.7
Pali: Aparānipi dve kammāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are two other kinds of legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.8
Pali: ñattidutiyakammaṁ, ñatticatutthakammaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, and the procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.9
