English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of cases of legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.10
Pali: apalokanakammassa vatthu, ñattikammassa vatthu. 
English (brahmali, published): the cases of procedures consisting of getting permission, and the cases of procedures consisting of one motion. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.11
Pali: Aparānipi dve kammavatthūni—
English (brahmali, published): There are two other kinds of cases of legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.12
Pali: ñattidutiyakammassa vatthu, ñatticatutthakammassa vatthu. 
English (brahmali, published): the cases of procedures consisting of one motion and one announcement, and the cases of procedures consisting of one motion and three announcements. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.13
Pali: Dve kammadosā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of flaws in legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.14
Pali: apalokanakammassa doso, ñattikammassa doso. 
English (brahmali, published): the flaw in a procedure consisting of getting permission, and the flaw in a procedure consisting of one motion. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.15
Pali: Aparepi dve kammadosā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two other kinds of flaws in legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.16
Pali: ñattidutiyakammassa doso, ñatticatutthakammassa doso. 
English (brahmali, published): the flaw in a procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, and the flaw in a procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.17
Pali: Dve kammasampattiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of successes in legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.18
Pali: apalokanakammassa sampatti, ñattikammassa sampatti. 
English (brahmali, published): the success of a procedure consisting of getting permission, and the success of a procedure consisting of one motion. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.19
Pali: Aparāpi dve kammasampattiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are two other kinds of successes in legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.20
Pali: ñattidutiyakammassa sampatti, ñatticatutthakammassa sampatti. 
English (brahmali, published): the success of a procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, and the success of a procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements. 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.21
Pali: Dve nānāsaṁvāsakabhūmiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are two grounds for belonging to a different Buddhist sect: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.22
Pali: attanā vā attānaṁ nānāsaṁvāsakaṁ karoti, samaggo vā naṁ saṅgho ukkhipati adassane vā appaṭikamme vā appaṭinissagge vā. 
English (brahmali, published): either one makes oneself belong to a different Buddhist sect, or a unanimous assembly ejects one for not recognizing an offense, for not making amends for an offense, or for not giving up a bad view. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.23
Pali: Dve samānasaṁvāsakabhūmiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are two grounds for belonging to the same Buddhist sect: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.24
Pali: attanā vā attānaṁ samānasaṁvāsakaṁ karoti, samaggo vā naṁ saṅgho ukkhittaṁ osāreti dassane vā paṭikamme vā paṭinissagge vā. 
English (brahmali, published): either one makes oneself belong to the same Buddhist sect, or a unanimous assembly readmits one who has been ejected for recognizing an offense, for making amends for an offense, or for giving up a bad view. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.25
Pali: Dve pārājikā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of offenses entailing expulsion: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.26
Pali: bhikkhūnañca bhikkhunīnañca. 
English (brahmali, published): for monks and for nuns. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.27
Pali: Dve saṅghādisesā, dve thullaccayā, dve pācittiyā, dve pāṭidesanīyā, dve dukkaṭā, dve dubbhāsitā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of offenses entailing suspension, two kinds of serious offenses, two kinds of offenses entailing confession, two kinds of offenses entailing acknowledgment, two kinds of offenses of wrong conduct, two kinds of offenses of wrong speech: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.28
Pali: bhikkhūnañca bhikkhunīnañca. 
English (brahmali, published): for monks and for nuns. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.29
Pali: Satta āpattiyo, satta āpattikkhandhā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven kinds of offenses and seven classes of offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.30
Pali: Dvīhākārehi saṅgho bhijjati—
English (brahmali, published): Schism in the Sangha occurs in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:8.31
Pali: kammena vā salākaggāhena vā. 
English (brahmali, published): through a legal procedure or through a vote. 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.1
Pali: Dve puggalā na upasampādetabbā—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people should not be given the full ordination: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.2
Pali: addhānahīno, aṅgahīno. 
English (brahmali, published): one lacking in age, and one lacking in limbs. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.3
Pali: Aparepi dve puggalā na upasampādetabbā—
English (brahmali, published): Another two kinds of people should not be given the full ordination: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.4
Pali: vatthuvipanno, karaṇadukkaṭako. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is deficient as object, and one who has acted wrongly. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.5
Pali: Aparepi dve puggalā na upasampādetabbā—
English (brahmali, published): Another two kinds of people should not be given the full ordination: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.6
Pali: aparipūro paripūro no ca yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is incomplete, and one who is complete but who has not asked for it. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.7
Pali: Dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ nissāya na vatthabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): One should not live with formal support from two kinds of persons: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.8
Pali: alajjissa ca bālassa ca. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is shameless, and one who is ignorant. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.9
Pali: Dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ nissayo na dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): One should not give formal support to two kinds of people: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.10
Pali: alajjissa ca lajjino ca na yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is shameless, and one who has a sense of conscience but who has not asked for it. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.11
Pali: Dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ nissayo dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): One should give formal support to two kinds of people: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.12
Pali: bālassa ca lajjissa ca yācati. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is ignorant, and one who has a sense of conscience and who has asked for it. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.13
Pali: Dve puggalā abhabbā āpattiṁ āpajjituṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people are incapable of committing an offense: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.14
Pali: buddhā ca paccekabuddhā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Buddhas and solitary Buddhas. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.15
Pali: Dve puggalā bhabbā, āpattiṁ āpajjituṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people are capable of committing an offense: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.16
Pali: bhikkhū ca bhikkhuniyo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): monks and nuns. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.17
Pali: Dve puggalā abhabbā sañcicca āpattiṁ āpajjituṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people are incapable of intentionally committing an offense: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.18
Pali: bhikkhū ca bhikkhuniyo ca ariyapuggalā. 
English (brahmali, published): monks and nuns who are noble persons. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.19
Pali: Dve puggalā bhabbā sañcicca āpattiṁ āpajjituṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people are capable of intentionally committing an offense: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.20
Pali: bhikkhū ca bhikkhuniyo ca puthujjanā. 
English (brahmali, published): monks and nuns who are ordinary persons. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.21
Pali: Dve puggalā abhabbā sañcicca sātisāraṁ vatthuṁ ajjhācarituṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people are incapable of intentionally committing an action that goes too far: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.22
Pali: bhikkhū ca bhikkhuniyo ca ariyapuggalā. 
English (brahmali, published): monks and nuns who are noble persons. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.23
Pali: Dve puggalā bhabbā sañcicca sātisāraṁ vatthuṁ ajjhācarituṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people are capable of intentionally committing an action that goes too far: 

pli-tv-pvr7:9.24
Pali: bhikkhū ca bhikkhuniyo ca puthujjanā. 
English (brahmali, published): monks and nuns who are ordinary persons. 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.1
Pali: Dve paṭikkosā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of objections: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.2
Pali: kāyena vā paṭikkosati vācāya vā paṭikkosati. 
English (brahmali, published): one objects by body, or one objects by speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.3
Pali: Dve nissāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of sending away: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.4
Pali: atthi puggalo appatto nissāraṇaṁ tañce saṅgho nissāreti ekacco sunissārito, ekacco dunnissārito. 
English (brahmali, published): if the Sangha sends away those who don’t have the attributes needed for being sent away, the sending away of some succeeds, while the sending away of others fails. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.5
Pali: Dve osāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of admittance: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.6
Pali: atthi puggalo appatto osāraṇaṁ tañce saṅgho osāreti ekacco sosārito, ekacco dosārito. 
English (brahmali, published): if the Sangha admits those who don’t have the attributes needed for being admitted, the admittance of some succeeds, while the admittance of others fails. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.7
Pali: Dve paṭiññā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of admitting: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.8
Pali: kāyena vā paṭijānāti vācāya vā paṭijānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): one admits by body, or one admits by speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.9
Pali: Dve paṭiggahā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of receiving: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.10
Pali: kāyena vā paṭiggaṇhāti kāyapaṭibaddhena vā paṭiggaṇhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): one receives by body, or one receives by what is connected to the body. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.11
Pali: Dve paṭikkhepā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of prohibitions: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.12
Pali: kāyena vā paṭikkhipati vācāya vā paṭikkhipati. 
English (brahmali, published): one prohibits by body, or one prohibits by speech. —

pli-tv-pvr7:10.14
Pali: sikkhūpaghātikā ca bhogūpaghātikā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): harming of the training, and harming of possessions. —

Pali: Dve codanā—

pli-tv-pvr7:10.16

English (brahmali, published): one accuses by body, or one accuses by speech.

Pali: Dve cīvarāni—

pli-tv-pvr7:10.22
Pali: gahapatikañca paṁsukūlañca. 
English (brahmali, published): from householders, and from rags. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.23

English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of almsbowls: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.24
Pali: ayopatto mattikāpatto. 
English (brahmali, published): iron bowls, and ceramic bowls. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.25

Pali: tipumayaṁ, sīsamayaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): bowl-rests made of tin, and bowl-rests made of lead. —

English (brahmali, published): There are two ways of determining an almsbowl: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.28
Pali: kāyena vā adhiṭṭheti vācāya vā adhiṭṭheti. 
English (brahmali, published): one determines it by body, or one determines it by speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.29
Pali: Dve cīvarassa adhiṭṭhānā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two ways of determining a robe: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.30
Pali: kāyena vā adhiṭṭheti vācāya vā adhiṭṭheti. 
English (brahmali, published): one determines it by body, or one determines it by speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.31
Pali: Dve vikappanā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of assignment: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.32
Pali: sammukhāvikappanā ca parammukhāvikappanā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): assignment in the presence of, and assignment in the absence of.

Pali: Dve vinayā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two Monastic Laws: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.34
Pali: bhikkhūnañca bhikkhunīnañca. 
English (brahmali, published): for the monks, and for the nuns. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.35

English (brahmali, published): There are two things that belong to the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.36
Pali: paññattañca paññattānulomañca. 
English (brahmali, published): the rules, and what accords with the rules. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.37
Pali: Dve vinayassa sallekhā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of self-effacement through the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.38
Pali: akappiye setughāto, kappiye mattakāritā. 
English (brahmali, published): ending access to what is unallowable, and moderation in what is allowable. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.39
Pali: Dvīhākārehi āpattiṁ āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits an offense in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.40
Pali: kāyena vā āpajjati vācāya vā āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): one commits it by body, or one commits it by speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.41
Pali: Dvīhākārehi āpattiyā vuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): One is cleared of an offense in two ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.42
Pali: kāyena vā vuṭṭhāti vācāya vā vuṭṭhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): one is cleared by body, or one is cleared by speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.43
Pali: Dve parivāsā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of probation: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.44
Pali: paṭicchannaparivāso, appaṭicchannaparivāso. 
English (brahmali, published): probation for concealed offenses, and probation for unconcealed offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.45
Pali: Aparepi dve parivāsā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two other kinds of probation: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.46
Pali: suddhantaparivāso samodhānaparivāso. 
English (brahmali, published): purifying probation, and simultaneous probation. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.47
Pali: Dve mānattā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of trial periods: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.48
Pali: paṭicchannamānattaṁ, appaṭicchannamānattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): trial periods for concealed offenses, and trial periods for unconcealed offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.49
Pali: Aparepi dve mānattā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two other kinds of trial periods: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.50
Pali: pakkhamānattaṁ, samodhānamānattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): trial periods for a half-month, and simultaneous trial periods. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.51
Pali: Dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ ratticchedo—
English (brahmali, published): Not counting a day is for two kinds of people: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.52
Pali: pārivāsikassa ca mānattacārikassa ca. 
English (brahmali, published): for one on probation, and for one undertaking the trial period. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.53
Pali: Dve anādariyāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of disrespect: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.54
Pali: puggalānādariyañca dhammānādariyañca. 
English (brahmali, published): disrespect for the person, and disrespect for the rule. 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.55
Pali: Dve loṇāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of salt: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.56
Pali: jātimañca kārimañca. 
English (brahmali, published): natural, and artificial. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.57
Pali: Aparānipi dve loṇāni—

pli-tv-pvr7:10.58
Pali: sāmuddaṁ kāḷaloṇaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): sea salt, and black salt. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.59
Pali: Aparānipi dve loṇāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are two other kinds of salt: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.60
Pali: sindhavaṁ, ubbhidaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): hill salt, and soil salt. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.61
Pali: Aparānipi dve loṇāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are two other kinds of salt: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.62
Pali: romakaṁ, pakkālakaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): salt from the Roma country, and grain salt. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.63
Pali: Dve paribhogā—
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of using: 

pli-tv-pvr7:10.64
Pali: abbhantaraparibhogo ca bāhiraparibhogo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): using internally, and using externally. —

pli-tv-pvr7:10.68
Pali: piyakamyassa vā bhedādhippāyassa vā. 
English (brahmali, published): for one wanting to endear himself, or for one aiming at division. —

pli-tv-pvr7:25.0
Pali: 3. Tikavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 3. The section on threes 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.1
Pali: Atthāpatti tiṭṭhante bhagavati āpajjati, no parinibbute; 
English (brahmali, published): “(1) There are offenses one commits while the Buddha is alive, not after his extinguishment. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.2
Pali: atthāpatti parinibbute bhagavati āpajjati, no tiṭṭhante; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses one commits after the Buddha’s extinguishment, not while he is alive. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.3
Pali: atthāpatti tiṭṭhantepi bhagavati āpajjati parinibbutepi. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses one commits both while the Buddha is alive and also after his extinguishment. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.4
Pali: Atthāpatti kāle āpajjati, no vikāle; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses one commits at the right time, not at the wrong time. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.5
Pali: atthāpatti vikāle āpajjati, no kāle; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses one commits at the wrong time, not at the right time. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.6
Pali: atthāpatti kāle ceva āpajjati vikāle ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses one commits both at the right time and also at the wrong time. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.7
Pali: Atthāpatti rattiṁ āpajjati, no divā; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses one commits at night, not by day. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.8
Pali: atthāpatti divā āpajjati, no rattiṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses one commits by day, not at night. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.9
Pali: atthāpatti rattiñceva āpajjati divā ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses one commits both at night and also by day. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.10
Pali: Atthāpatti dasavasso āpajjati, no ūnadasavasso; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses one commits when one has ten years of seniority, not less. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.11
Pali: atthāpatti ūnadasavasso āpajjati, no dasavasso; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses one commits when one has less than ten years of seniority, not ten. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.12
Pali: atthāpatti dasavasso ceva āpajjati ūnadasavasso ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses one commits both when one has ten years of seniority and also when one has less. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.13
Pali: Atthāpatti pañcavasso āpajjati, no ūnapañcavasso; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses one commits when one has five years of seniority, not less. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.14
Pali: atthāpatti ūnapañcavasso āpajjati, no pañcavasso; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses one commits when one has less than five years of seniority, not five. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.15
Pali: atthāpatti pañcavasso ceva āpajjati ūnapañcavasso ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses one commits both when one has five years of seniority and also when one has less. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.16
Pali: Atthāpatti kusalacitto āpajjati; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses one commits with a wholesome mind. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.17
Pali: atthāpatti akusalacitto āpajjati; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses one commits with an unwholesome mind. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.18
Pali: atthāpatti abyākatacitto āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses one commits with an indeterminate mind. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.19
Pali: Atthāpatti sukhavedanāsamaṅgī āpajjati; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses one commits while experiencing pleasant feelings. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.20
Pali: atthāpatti dukkhavedanāsamaṅgī āpajjati; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses one commits while experiencing unpleasant feelings. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.21
Pali: atthāpatti adukkhamasukhavedanāsamaṅgī āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses one commits while experiencing neither pleasant nor unpleasant feelings. 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.22
Pali: Tīṇi codanāvatthūni—
English (brahmali, published): There are three grounds for an accusation: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.23
Pali: diṭṭhena, sutena, parisaṅkāya. 
English (brahmali, published): what is seen, what is heard, and what is suspected. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.24
Pali: Tayo salākaggāhā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three ways of voting: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.25
Pali: guḷhako, vivaṭako, sakaṇṇajappako. 
English (brahmali, published): a secret ballot, an open vote, and whispering in the ear. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.26
Pali: Tayo paṭikkhepā—
English (brahmali, published): Three things are opposed: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.27
Pali: mahicchatā, asantuṭṭhitā, asallekhatā. 
English (brahmali, published): great desires, discontent, and self-inflation. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.28
Pali: Tayo anuññātā—
English (brahmali, published): Three things are allowed: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.29
Pali: appicchatā, santuṭṭhitā, sallekhatā. 
English (brahmali, published): fewness of wishes, contentment, and self-effacement. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.30
Pali: Aparepi tayo paṭikkhepā—
English (brahmali, published): Three other things are opposed: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.31
Pali: mahicchatā, asantuṭṭhitā, amattaññutā. 
English (brahmali, published): great desires, discontent, and lacking a sense of moderation. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.32
Pali: Tayo anuññātā—
English (brahmali, published): And three things are allowed: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.33
Pali: appicchatā, santuṭṭhitā, mattaññutā. 
English (brahmali, published): fewness of wishes, contentment, and having a sense of moderation. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.34
Pali: Tisso paññattiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of rules: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.35
Pali: paññatti, anupaññatti, anuppannapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): a rule, an addition to a rule, and an unprompted rule. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.36
Pali: Aparāpi tisso paññattiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are three other kinds of rules: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.37
Pali: sabbatthapaññatti, padesapaññatti, sādhāraṇapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): a rule that applies everywhere, a rule that applies in a particular place, and a rule that the monks and nuns have in common. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.38
Pali: Aparāpi tisso paññattiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are three other kinds of rules: 

pli-tv-pvr7:25.39
Pali: asādhāraṇapaññatti, ekatopaññatti, ubhatopaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): a rule the monks and nuns do not have in common, a rule for one Sangha, and a rule for both Sanghas. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.1
Pali: Atthāpatti bālo āpajjati, no paṇḍito; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses committed by fools, not by the wise. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.2
Pali: atthāpatti paṇḍito āpajjati, no bālo; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses committed by the wise, not by fools. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.3
Pali: atthāpatti bālo ceva āpajjati paṇḍito ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses committed by both by fools and the wise. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.4
Pali: Atthāpatti kāḷe āpajjati, no juṇhe; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses committed during the waning phase of the moon, not during the waxing phase. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.5
Pali: atthāpatti juṇhe āpajjati, no kāḷe; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses committed during the waxing phase of the moon, not during the waning phase. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.6
Pali: atthāpatti kāḷe ceva āpajjati juṇhe ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses committed both during the waning and the waxing phases of the moon. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.7
Pali: Atthi kāḷe kappati, no juṇhe; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are things that are allowable during the waning phase of the moon, not during the waxing phase. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.8
Pali: atthi juṇhe kappati, no kāḷe; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are things that are allowable during the waxing phase of the moon, not during the waning phase. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.9
Pali: atthi kāḷe ceva kappati juṇhe ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are things that are allowable both during the waning and the waxing phases of the moon. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.10
Pali: Atthāpatti hemante āpajjati, no gimhe no vasse; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses committed during winter, not during summer or the rainy season. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.11
Pali: atthāpatti gimhe āpajjati, no hemante no vasse; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses committed during summer, not during winter or the rainy season. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.12
Pali: atthāpatti vasse āpajjati, no hemante no gimhe. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses committed during the rainy season, not during winter or summer. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.13
Pali: Atthāpatti saṅgho āpajjati, na gaṇo na puggalo; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses committed by a sangha, not by several monastics or an individual. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.14
Pali: atthāpatti gaṇo āpajjati, na saṅgho na puggalo; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses committed by several monastics, not by a sangha or an individual. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.15
Pali: atthāpatti puggalo āpajjati, na saṅgho na gaṇo. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses committed by an individual, not by a sangha or several monastics. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.16
Pali: Atthi saṅghassa kappati, na gaṇassa na puggalassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are things allowable for a sangha, not for several monastics or an individual. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.17
Pali: atthi gaṇassa kappati, na saṅghassa na puggalassa; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are things allowable for several monastics, not for a sangha or an individual. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.18
Pali: atthi puggalassa kappati, na saṅghassa na gaṇassa. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are things allowable for an individual, not for a sangha or several monastics. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.19
Pali: Tisso chādanā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of concealing: 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.20
Pali: vatthuṁ chādeti, no āpattiṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) one conceals the action that was the basis for the offense, not the offense. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.21
Pali: āpattiṁ chādeti, no vatthuṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) one conceals the offense, not the action that was the basis for the offense. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.22
Pali: vatthuñceva chādeti āpattiñca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) one conceals both the action that was the basis for the offense and also the offense. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.23
Pali: Tisso paṭicchādiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are three coverings: 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.24
Pali: jantāgharapaṭicchādi, udakapaṭicchādi, vatthapaṭicchādi. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) a sauna, (2) water, and (3) a cloth. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.25
Pali: Tīṇi paṭicchannāni vahanti, no vivaṭāni—
English (brahmali, published): Three things happen concealed, not openly: 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.26
Pali: mātugāmo paṭicchanno vahati, no vivaṭo; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) Women are married with a veil, not unveiled. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.27
Pali: brāhmaṇānaṁ mantā paṭicchannā vahanti, no vivaṭā; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) The mantras of the brahmins are transmitted in secret, not openly. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.28
Pali: micchādiṭṭhi paṭicchannā vahati, no vivaṭā. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) Wrong view is transmitted in secret, not openly. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.29
Pali: Tīṇi vivaṭāni virocanti, no paṭicchannāni—
English (brahmali, published): Three things shine in the open, not when concealed: 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.30
Pali: candamaṇḍalaṁ vivaṭaṁ virocati, no paṭicchannaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) The disc of the moon shines in the open, not when concealed. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.31
Pali: sūriyamaṇḍalaṁ vivaṭaṁ virocati, no paṭicchannaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) The disc of the sun shines in the open, not when concealed. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.32
Pali: tathāgatappavedito dhammavinayo vivaṭo virocati, no paṭicchanno. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) The spiritual path proclaimed by the Buddha shines in the open, not when concealed. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.33
Pali: Tayo senāsanaggāhā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three times for the allocation of dwellings: 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.34
Pali: purimako, pacchimako, antarāmuttako. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) the first, (2) the second, and (3) when given up in between. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.35
Pali: Atthāpatti gilāno āpajjati, no agilāno; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses that one commits when sick, not when not sick. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.36
Pali: atthāpatti agilāno āpajjati, no gilāno; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses that one commits when not sick, not when sick. 

pli-tv-pvr7:26.37
Pali: atthāpatti gilāno ceva āpajjati agilāno ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses that one commits both when sick and when not sick. 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.1
Pali: Tīṇi adhammikāni pātimokkhaṭṭhapanāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of illegitimate cancellations of the Monastic Code. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.2
Pali: Tīṇi dhammikāni pātimokkhaṭṭhapanāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of legitimate cancellations of the Monastic Code. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.3
Pali: Tayo parivāsā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of probation: 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.4
Pali: paṭicchannaparivāso, appaṭicchannaparivāso, suddhantaparivāso. 
English (brahmali, published): probation for concealed offenses, probation for unconcealed offenses, and purifying probation. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.5
Pali: Tayo mānattā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of trial period: 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.6
Pali: paṭicchannamānattaṁ, appaṭicchannamānattaṁ, pakkhamānattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): trial period for concealed offenses, trial period for unconcealed offenses, and trial period for a half-month. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.7
Pali: Tayo pārivāsikassa bhikkhuno ratticchedā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three things that stop a monk on probation from counting a particular day toward his probationary period: 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.8
Pali: sahavāso, vippavāso, anārocanā. 
English (brahmali, published): he stays in the same room as a regular monk; he stays apart from other monks; he doesn’t inform other monks of his status. 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.9
Pali: Atthāpatti anto āpajjati, no bahi; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses that one commits inside, not outside. 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.10
Pali: atthāpatti bahi āpajjati, no anto; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses that one commits outside, not inside. 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.11
Pali: atthāpatti anto ceva āpajjati bahi ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses that one commits both inside and outside. 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.12
Pali: Atthāpatti antosīmāya āpajjati, no bahisīmāya; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses that one commits inside the monastery zone, not outside. 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.13
Pali: atthāpatti bahisīmāya āpajjati, no antosīmāya; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses that one commits outside the monastery zone, not inside. 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.14
Pali: atthāpatti antosīmāya ceva āpajjati bahisīmāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses that one commits both inside and outside the monastery zone. 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.15
Pali: Tīhākārehi āpattiṁ āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits an offense in three ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.16
Pali: kāyena āpajjati, vācāya āpajjati, kāyena vācāya āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): one commits an offense by body, by speech, or by body and speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.17
Pali: Aparehipi tīhākārehi āpattiṁ āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits an offense in three other ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.18
Pali: saṅghamajjhe, gaṇamajjhe, puggalassa santike. 
English (brahmali, published): in the midst of the Sangha, in the midst of a group, or in the presence of an individual. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.19
Pali: Tīhākārehi āpattiyā vuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): One clears an offense in three ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.20
Pali: kāyena vuṭṭhāti, vācāya vuṭṭhāti, kāyena vācāya vuṭṭhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): one clears an offense by body, by speech, or by body and speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.21
Pali: Aparehipi tīhākārehi āpattiyā vuṭṭhāti—
English (brahmali, published): One clears an offense in three other ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.22
Pali: saṅghamajjhe, gaṇamajjhe, puggalassa santike. 
English (brahmali, published): in the midst of the Sangha, in the midst of a group, or in the presence of an individual. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.23
Pali: Tīṇi adhammikāni amūḷhavinayassa dānāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are three illegitimate grantings of resolution because of past insanity. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:27.24
Pali: Tīṇi dhammikāni amūḷhavinayassa dānāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are three legitimate grantings of resolution because of past insanity. 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.1
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho tajjanīyakammaṁ kareyya—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, the Sangha may, if it wishes, do a procedure of condemnation against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.2
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārako hoti kalahakārako vivādakārako bhassakārako saṅghe adhikaraṇakārako, bālo hoti abyatto, āpattibahulo anapadāno gihisaṁsaṭṭho viharati ananulomikehi gihisaṁsaggehi. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he is quarrelsome, argumentative, and a creator of legal issues in the Sangha; (2) he is ignorant and incompetent, often committing offenses, and lacking in boundaries; (3) he is constantly and improperly socializing with householders. 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.3
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho niyassakammaṁ kareyya—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, the Sangha may, if it wishes, do a procedure of demotion against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.4
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārako hoti …pe… saṅghe adhikaraṇakārako, bālo hoti abyatto, āpattibahulo anapadāno gihisaṁsaṭṭho viharati ananulomikehi gihisaṁsaggehi. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he is quarrelsome, argumentative, and a creator of legal issues in the Sangha; (2) he is ignorant and incompetent, often committing offenses, and lacking in boundaries; (3) he is constantly and improperly socializing with householders. 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.5
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho pabbājanīyakammaṁ kareyya—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, the Sangha may, if it wishes, do a procedure of banishment against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.6
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārako hoti …pe… saṅghe adhikaraṇakārako, bālo hoti abyatto, āpattibahulo anapadāno kuladūsako hoti pāpasamācāro pāpasamācārā dissanti ceva suyyanti ca. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he is quarrelsome, argumentative, and a creator of legal issues in the Sangha; (2) he is ignorant and incompetent, often committing offenses, and lacking in boundaries; (3) he is a corrupter of families and badly behaved, and his bad behavior has been seen and heard about. 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.7
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho paṭisāraṇīyakammaṁ kareyya—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, the Sangha may, if it wishes, do a procedure of reconciliation against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.8
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārako hoti …pe… saṅghe adhikaraṇakārako, bālo hoti abyatto, āpattibahulo anapadāno gihī akkosati paribhāsati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he is quarrelsome, argumentative, and a creator of legal issues in the Sangha; (2) he is ignorant and incompetent, often committing offenses, and lacking in boundaries; (3) he abuses and reviles householders. 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.9
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṁ kareyya—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, the Sangha may, if it wishes, do a procedure of ejection against him for not recognizing an offense: 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.10
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārako hoti …pe… saṅghe adhikaraṇakārako, bālo hoti abyatto, āpattibahulo anapadāno āpattiṁ āpajjitvā na icchati āpattiṁ passituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he is quarrelsome, argumentative, and a creator of legal issues in the Sangha; (2) he is ignorant and incompetent, often committing offenses, and lacking in boundaries; (3) after committing an offense, he refuses to recognize it. 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.11
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammaṁ kareyya—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, the Sangha may, if it wishes, do a procedure of ejection against him for not making amends for an offense: 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.12
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārako hoti …pe… saṅghe adhikaraṇakārako, bālo hoti abyatto, āpattibahulo anapadāno āpattiṁ āpajjitvā na icchati āpattiṁ paṭikātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he is quarrelsome, argumentative, and a creator of legal issues in the Sangha; (2) he is ignorant and incompetent, often committing offenses, and lacking in boundaries; (3) after committing an offense, he refuses to make amends for it. 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.13
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho pāpikāya diṭṭhiyā appaṭinissagge ukkhepanīyakammaṁ kareyya—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, the Sangha may, if it wishes, do a procedure of ejection against him for not giving up a bad view: 

pli-tv-pvr7:28.14
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārako hoti …pe… saṅghe adhikaraṇakārako, bālo hoti abyatto, āpattibahulo anapadāno na icchati pāpikaṁ diṭṭhiṁ paṭinissajjituṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he is quarrelsome, argumentative, and a creator of legal issues in the Sangha; (2) he is ignorant and incompetent, often committing offenses, and lacking in boundaries; (3) he refuses to give up a bad view. 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.1
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho āgāḷhāya ceteyya—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, the Sangha may, if it wishes, plan a strong action against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.2
Pali: bhaṇḍanakārako hoti kalahakārako vivādakārako bhassakārako saṅghe adhikaraṇakārako, bālo hoti abyatto āpattibahulo anapadāno, gihisaṁsaṭṭho viharati ananulomikehi gihisaṁsaggehi. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he is quarrelsome, argumentative, and a creator of legal issues in the Sangha; (2) he is ignorant and incompetent, often committing offenses, and lacking in boundaries; (3) he is constantly and improperly socializing with householders. 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.3
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.4
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.5
Pali: Aparehipi tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three other qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.6
Pali: adhisīle sīlavipanno hoti, ajjhācāre ācāravipanno hoti, atidiṭṭhiyā diṭṭhivipanno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he has failed in the higher morality; he has failed in the higher conduct; he has failed in the higher view. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.7
Pali: Aparehipi tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three other qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.8
Pali: kāyikena davena samannāgato hoti, vācasikena davena samannāgato hoti, kāyikavācasikena davena samannāgato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): his bodily conduct is frivolous; his verbal conduct is frivolous; his bodily and verbal conduct are frivolous. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.9
Pali: Aparehipi tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three other qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.10
Pali: kāyikena anācārena samannāgato hoti, vācasikena anācārena samannāgato hoti, kāyikavācasikena anācārena samannāgato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he is improperly behaved by body; he is improperly behaved by speech; he is improperly behaved by body and speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.11
Pali: Aparehipi tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three other qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.12
Pali: kāyikena upaghātikena samannāgato hoti, vācasikena upaghātikena samannāgato hoti, kāyikavācasikena upaghātikena samannāgato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): his bodily conduct is harmful; his verbal conduct is harmful; his bodily and verbal conduct are harmful. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.13
Pali: Aparehipi tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three other qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.14
Pali: kāyikena micchājīvena samannāgato hoti, vācasikena micchājīvena samannāgato hoti, kāyikavācasikena micchājīvena samannāgato hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he has wrong livelihood by body; he has wrong livelihood by speech; he has wrong livelihood by body and speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.15
Pali: Aparehipi tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three other qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.16
Pali: āpattiṁ āpanno kammakato upasampādeti, nissayaṁ deti, sāmaṇeraṁ upaṭṭhāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): if, after committing an offense and having a legal procedure done against him, he gives the full ordination, gives formal support, has a novice monk attend on him. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.17
Pali: Aparehipi tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three other qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.18
Pali: yāya āpattiyā saṅghena kammaṁ kataṁ hoti taṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati, aññaṁ vā tādisikaṁ, tato vā pāpiṭṭhataraṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he commits the same offense for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him; he commits an offense similar to the one for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him; he commits an offense worse than the one for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.19
Pali: Aparehipi tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three other qualities a legal procedure may be done against him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:29.20
Pali: buddhassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, dhammassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, saṅghassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati. 
English (brahmali, published): he disparages the Buddha; he disparages the Teaching; he disparages the Sangha. 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.1
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno saṅghamajjhe uposathaṁ ṭhapentassa—“alaṁ, bhikkhu, mā bhaṇḍanaṁ mā kalahaṁ mā viggahaṁ mā vivādan”ti omadditvā saṅghena uposatho kātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities and is having the observance-day ceremony canceled in the midst of the Sangha, then, after pressing him by saying, ‘Enough, no more arguing and disputing,’ the Sangha should do the observance-day ceremony. These are the three qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.2
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.3
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno saṅghamajjhe pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapentassa—“alaṁ, bhikkhu, mā bhaṇḍanaṁ mā kalahaṁ mā viggahaṁ mā vivādan”ti omadditvā saṅghena pavāretabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities and is having the invitation ceremony canceled in the midst of the Sangha, then, after pressing him by saying, ‘Enough, no more arguing and disputing,’ the Sangha should do the invitation ceremony. These are the three qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.4
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.5
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno na kāci saṅghasammuti dātabbā—
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha should not give any formal approval to a monk who has three qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.6
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.7
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā saṅghe na voharitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he should not speak in the Sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.8
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.9
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu na kismiñci paccekaṭṭhāne ṭhapetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he should not be put in any position of authority: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.10
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.11
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno nissāya na vatthabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, one should not live with formal support from him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.12
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.13
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno nissayo na dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he should not give formal support: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.14
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.15
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno okāsakammaṁ kārāpentassa nālaṁ okāsakammaṁ kātuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he is not qualified to ask for permission to correct another: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.16
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.17
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno savacanīyaṁ nādātabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he should not be allowed to direct anyone: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.18
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.19
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno vinayo na pucchitabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he should not be asked about the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.20
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. —

Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā vinayo na pucchitabbo—

English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. —

English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, his questions about the Monastic Law should not be replied to: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.24
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.25
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā vinayo na vissajjetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he should not reply to questions about the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.26
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.27
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno anuyogo na dātabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he should not be allowed to ask questions:

Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. —

Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā saddhiṁ vinayo na sākacchitabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, one should not discuss the Monastic Law with him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.30
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.31
Pali: Tīhaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā na upasampādetabbaṁ na nissayo dātabbo na sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has three qualities, he should not give the full ordination, give formal support, or have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:30.32
Pali: alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca. 
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk. 

pli-tv-pvr7:31.1
Pali: Tayo uposathā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of observance-day ceremonies: 

pli-tv-pvr7:31.2
Pali: cātuddasiko, pannarasiko, sāmaggiuposatho. 
English (brahmali, published): on the fourteenth, on the fifteenth, and the observance-day ceremony for the sake of unity. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:31.3
Pali: Aparepi tayo uposathā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three other kinds of observance-day ceremonies: 

pli-tv-pvr7:31.4
Pali: saṅgheuposatho, gaṇeuposatho, puggaleuposatho. 
English (brahmali, published): the observance-day ceremony for a sangha, the observance-day ceremony for a group, and the observance-day ceremony for an individual. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:31.5
Pali: Aparepi tayo uposathā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three other kinds of observance-day ceremonies: 

pli-tv-pvr7:31.6
Pali: suttuddesouposatho, pārisuddhiuposatho, adhiṭṭhānuposatho. 
English (brahmali, published): the observance-day ceremony which consists of reciting the Monastic Code, the observance-day ceremony which consists of declaring purity, and the observance-day ceremony which consists of a determination. 

pli-tv-pvr7:32.1
Pali: Tisso pavāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of invitation ceremonies: 

pli-tv-pvr7:32.2
Pali: cātuddasikā, pannarasikā, sāmaggipavāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): on the fourteenth, on the fifteenth, and the invitation ceremony for the sake of unity. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:32.3
Pali: Aparāpi tisso pavāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three other kinds of invitation ceremonies: 

pli-tv-pvr7:32.4
Pali: saṅghepavāraṇā, gaṇepavāraṇā, puggalepavāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): the invitation ceremony for a sangha, the invitation ceremony for a group, and the invitation ceremony for an individual. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:32.5
Pali: Aparāpi tisso pavāraṇā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three other kinds of invitation ceremonies: 

pli-tv-pvr7:32.6
Pali: tevācikāpavāraṇā, dvevācikāpavāraṇā, samānavassikāpavāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): the invitation ceremony done by means of three statements, the invitation ceremony done by means of two statements, the invitation ceremony done by means of groups according to the year of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.1
Pali: Tayo āpāyikā nerayikā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of persons bound for hell: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.2
Pali: idamappahāya, yo ca abrahmacārī brahmacāripaṭiñño, yo ca suddhaṁ brahmacāriṁ parisuddhabrahmacariyaṁ carantaṁ amūlakena abrahmacariyena anuddhaṁseti, yo cāyaṁ evaṁvādī evaṁdiṭṭhi—“natthi kāmesu doso”ti so kāmesu pātabyataṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) one who, not having abandoned it, does not abstain from sexuality, while claiming to do so; (2) one who groundlessly charges someone who lives a pure spiritual life with not abstaining from sexuality; (3) one who has and declares a view such as this: ‘There is no fault in worldly pleasures,’ and then indulges in them. 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.3
Pali: Tīṇi akusalamūlāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are three unwholesome roots: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.4
Pali: lobho akusalamūlaṁ, doso akusalamūlaṁ, moho akusalamūlaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): desire, ill will, and confusion. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.5
Pali: Tīṇi kusalamūlāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are three wholesome roots: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.6
Pali: alobho kusalamūlaṁ, adoso kusalamūlaṁ, amoho kusalamūlaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): non-desire, non-ill will, and non-confusion. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.7
Pali: Tīṇi duccaritāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of misconduct: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.8
Pali: kāyaduccaritaṁ, vacīduccaritaṁ, manoduccaritaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): misconduct by body, misconduct by speech, and misconduct by mind. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.9
Pali: Tīṇi sucaritāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of good conduct: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.10
Pali: kāyasucaritaṁ, vacīsucaritaṁ, manosucaritaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): good conduct by body, good conduct by speech, and good conduct by mind. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.11
Pali: Tayo atthavase paṭicca bhagavatā kulesu tikabhojanaṁ paññattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): There are three reasons why the Buddha laid down the rule on eating in groups of no more than three: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.12
Pali: dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, “mā pāpicchā pakkhaṁ nissāya saṅghaṁ bhindeyyun”ti kulānuddayatāya ca. 
English (brahmali, published): for the restraint of bad people; for the ease of good monks, stopping those with bad desires from creating a faction and then splitting the Sangha; and out of compassion for families. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.13
Pali: Tīhi asaddhammehi abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho pāpicchatā pāpamittatā oramattakena visesādhigamena antarā vosānaṁ āpādi. 
English (brahmali, published): It’s because he was overcome and consumed by three bad qualities that Devadatta was irredeemably destined to an eon in hell: bad desires; bad friends; and after trifling successes, he stopped short of the goal. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.14
Pali: Tisso sammutiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of approval: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.15
Pali: daṇḍasammuti, sikkāsammuti, daṇḍasikkāsammuti. 
English (brahmali, published): approval to use a staff, approval to use a carrying net, and approval to use both a staff and a carrying net. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.16
Pali: Tisso pādukā dhuvaṭṭhānikā asaṅkamanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of foot stands that are fixed in place and immobile: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.17
Pali: vaccapādukā, passāvapādukā, ācamanapādukā. 
English (brahmali, published): foot stands for defecating, foot stands for urinating, foot stands for restroom ablutions. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.18
Pali: Tisso pādaghaṁsaniyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of foot scrubbers: 

pli-tv-pvr7:33.19
Pali: sakkharaṁ, kathalā, samuddapheṇakoti. 
English (brahmali, published): stones, pebbles, and pumice.” 

pli-tv-pvr7:34.1
Pali: Tikaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The section on threes is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr7:35.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr7:36.1
Pali: Tiṭṭhante kāle rattiñca, 
English (brahmali, published): “While he is alive, at the right time, and at night, 

pli-tv-pvr7:36.2
Pali: dasa pañca kusalena; 
English (brahmali, published): Ten, five, with wholesome; 

pli-tv-pvr7:36.3
Pali: Vedanā codanā vatthu, 
English (brahmali, published): Feelings, grounds for an accusation, 

pli-tv-pvr7:36.4
Pali: salākā dve paṭikkhipā. 
English (brahmali, published): Voting, two on prohibited. 

pli-tv-pvr7:37.1
Pali: Paññatti apare dve ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Rule, and two others, 

pli-tv-pvr7:37.2
Pali: bālo kāḷe ca kappati; 
English (brahmali, published): Fools, and during the waning phase of the moon, it is allowable; 

pli-tv-pvr7:37.3
Pali: Hemante saṅgho saṅghassa, 
English (brahmali, published): During winter, a sangha, for a sangha, 

pli-tv-pvr7:37.4
Pali: chādanā ca paṭicchādi. 
English (brahmali, published): And concealings, a covering. 

pli-tv-pvr7:38.1
Pali: Paṭicchannā vivaṭā ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Concealed, and in the open, 

pli-tv-pvr7:38.2
Pali: senāsanagilāyanā; 
English (brahmali, published): Dwelling, sick; 

pli-tv-pvr7:38.3
Pali: Pātimokkhaṁ parivāsaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Monastic Code, probation, 

pli-tv-pvr7:38.4
Pali: mānattā pārivāsikā. 
English (brahmali, published): Trial period, those on probation. 

pli-tv-pvr7:39.1
Pali: Anto anto ca sīmāya, 
English (brahmali, published): Inside, and inside the monastery zone, 

pli-tv-pvr7:39.2
Pali: āpajjati punāpare; 
English (brahmali, published): One commits, again another; 

pli-tv-pvr7:39.3
Pali: Vuṭṭhāti apare ceva, 
English (brahmali, published): One clears, and another, 

pli-tv-pvr7:39.4
Pali: amūḷhavinayā duve. 
English (brahmali, published): Two on resolution because of past insanity. 

pli-tv-pvr7:40.1
Pali: Tajjanīyā niyassā ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Condemnation, and demotion, 

pli-tv-pvr7:40.2
Pali: pabbājapaṭisāraṇī; 
English (brahmali, published): Banishment, reconciliation; 

pli-tv-pvr7:40.3
Pali: Adassanā paṭikamme, 
English (brahmali, published): Not recognizing, making amends, 

pli-tv-pvr7:40.4
Pali: anissagge ca diṭṭhiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): And not giving up a view. 

pli-tv-pvr7:41.1
Pali: Āgāḷhakammādhisīle, 
English (brahmali, published): Strong, legal procedure, in the higher morality, 

pli-tv-pvr7:41.2
Pali: davānācāraghātikā; 
English (brahmali, published): Frivolous, improperly behaved, harmful; 

pli-tv-pvr7:41.3
Pali: Ājīvāpannā tādisikā, 
English (brahmali, published): Livelihood, committing, similar, 

pli-tv-pvr7:41.4
Pali: avaṇṇuposathena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Disparages, and with observance-day ceremony. 

pli-tv-pvr7:42.1
Pali: Pavāraṇā sammuti ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Invitation ceremony, and formal approval, 

pli-tv-pvr7:42.2
Pali: vohārapaccekena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Speak, and with authority; 

pli-tv-pvr7:42.3
Pali: Na vatthabbaṁ na dātabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Should not live, should not give, 

pli-tv-pvr7:42.4
Pali: okāsaṁ na kare tathā. 
English (brahmali, published): So one should not ask for permission. 

pli-tv-pvr7:43.1
Pali: Na kare savacanīyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): One should not direct, 

pli-tv-pvr7:43.2
Pali: na pucchitabbakā duve; 
English (brahmali, published): Two on those who should not be asked; 

pli-tv-pvr7:43.3
Pali: Na vissajje duve ceva, 
English (brahmali, published): And two on one should not reply, 

pli-tv-pvr7:43.4
Pali: anuyogampi no dade. 
English (brahmali, published): And one should not be allowed to ask. 

pli-tv-pvr7:44.1
Pali: Sākacchā upasampadā, 
English (brahmali, published): Discussion, full ordination, 

pli-tv-pvr7:44.2
Pali: nissayasāmaṇerā ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Formal support, and novice monk; 

pli-tv-pvr7:44.3
Pali: Uposathatikā tīṇi, 
English (brahmali, published): Three on three observance-day ceremonies, 

pli-tv-pvr7:44.4
Pali: pavāraṇatikā tayo. 
English (brahmali, published): Three on three invitation ceremonies. 

pli-tv-pvr7:45.1
Pali: Āpāyikā akusalā, 
English (brahmali, published): Bound for the lower, unwholesome, 

pli-tv-pvr7:45.2
Pali: kusalā caritā duve; 
English (brahmali, published): Wholesome, two on conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr7:45.3
Pali: Tikabhojanasaddhamme, 
English (brahmali, published): Eating in groups of no more than three, in bad qualities, 

pli-tv-pvr7:45.4
Pali: sammuti pādukena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Approval, and with foot stands; 

pli-tv-pvr7:45.5
Pali: Pādaghaṁsanikā ceva, 
English (brahmali, published): And foot scrubbers—

pli-tv-pvr7:45.6
Pali: uddānaṁ tikake idanti. 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary for the threes.” 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.0
Pali: 4. Catukkavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 4. The section on fours 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.1
Pali: Atthāpatti sakavācāya āpajjati, paravācāya vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses that one commits through one’s own speech, but clears through someone else’s speech. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.2
Pali: atthāpatti paravācāya āpajjati, sakavācāya vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses that one commits through someone else’s speech, but clears through one’s own speech. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.3
Pali: atthāpatti sakavācāya āpajjati, sakavācāya vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses that one commits through one’s own speech and clears through one’s own speech. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.4
Pali: atthāpatti paravācāya āpajjati, paravācāya vuṭṭhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) There are offenses that one commits through someone else’s speech and clears through someone else’s speech. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.5
Pali: Atthāpatti kāyena āpajjati, vācāya vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses that one commits by body, but clears by speech. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.6
Pali: atthāpatti vācāya āpajjati, kāyena vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses that one commits by speech, but clears by body. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.7
Pali: atthāpatti kāyena āpajjati, kāyena vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses that one commits by body and clears by body. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.8
Pali: atthāpatti vācāya āpajjati, vācāya vuṭṭhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) There are offenses that one commits by speech and clears by speech. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.9
Pali: Atthāpatti pasutto āpajjati, paṭibuddho vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses that one commits while sleeping, but clears while awake. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.10
Pali: atthāpatti paṭibuddho āpajjati, pasutto vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses that one commits while awake, but clears while sleeping. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.11
Pali: atthāpatti pasutto āpajjati, pasutto vuṭṭhāti; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses that one commits while sleeping and clears while sleeping. 

pli-tv-pvr7:46.12
Pali: atthāpatti paṭibuddho āpajjati, paṭibuddho vuṭṭhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) There are offenses that one commits while awake and clears while awake.

pli-tv-pvr7:49.1
Pali: Catūhākārehi āpattiṁ āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits offenses in four ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:49.2

English (brahmali, published): by body, by speech, by body and speech, through a legal procedure. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:49.3
Pali: Aparehipi catūhākārehi āpattiṁ āpajjati—
English (brahmali, published): One commits offenses in four other ways:

English (brahmali, published): in the midst of the Sangha, in the midst of a group, in the presence of an individual, through the appearance of sexual characteristics. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:49.5

English (brahmali, published): One clears offenses in four ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:49.6

pli-tv-pvr7:49.7

English (brahmali, published): One clears offenses in four other ways: 

pli-tv-pvr7:49.8

English (brahmali, published): in the midst of the Sangha, in the midst of a group, in the presence of an individual, through the appearance of sexual characteristics. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:49.9
Pali: Saha paṭilābhena purimaṁ jahati, pacchime patiṭṭhāti, viññattiyo paṭippassambhanti, paṇṇattiyo nirujjhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): When one gets it: one abandons the former, one is established in the latter, asking for things comes to a stop, rules come to an end. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:49.10

English (brahmali, published): There are four kinds of people who corrupt a gathering:

English (brahmali, published): a moral monk with good qualities, a moral nun with good qualities, a moral male lay follower with good qualities, a moral female lay follower with good qualities. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.1
Pali: Atthāpatti āgantuko āpajjati, no āvāsiko; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses committed by new arrivals, not by residents. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.2
Pali: atthāpatti āvāsiko āpajjati, no āgantuko; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses committed by residents, not by new arrivals. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.3
Pali: atthāpatti āgantuko ceva āpajjati āvāsiko ca; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses committed both by new arrivals and by residents. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.4
Pali: atthāpatti neva āgantuko āpajjati, no āvāsiko. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) There are offenses committed neither by new arrivals nor by residents. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.5
Pali: Atthāpatti gamiko āpajjati, no āvāsiko; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses committed by those departing, not by residents. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.6
Pali: atthāpatti āvāsiko āpajjati, no gamiko; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses committed by residents, not by those departing. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.7
Pali: atthāpatti gamiko ceva āpajjati āvāsiko ca; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses committed both by those departing and by residents. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.8
Pali: atthāpatti neva gamiko āpajjati no āvāsiko. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) There are offenses committed neither by those departing nor by residents. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.9
Pali: Atthi vatthunānattatā no āpattinānattatā, atthi āpattinānattatā no vatthunānattatā, atthi vatthunānattatā ceva āpattinānattatā ca, atthi neva vatthunānattatā no āpattinānattatā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are rules that have variety in the action that is the basis for the offense, but not in the offense. (2) There are rules that have variety in the offense, but not in the action that is the basis for the offense. (3) There are rules that have variety both in the action that is the basis for the offense and in the offense. (4) There are rules that have variety neither in the action that is the basis for the offense nor in the offense. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.10
Pali: Atthi vatthusabhāgatā no āpattisabhāgatā, atthi āpattisabhāgatā no vatthusabhāgatā, atthi vatthusabhāgatā ceva āpattisabhāgatā ca atthi neva vatthusabhāgatā no āpattisabhāgatā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are rules where the action that is the basis for the offense is shared, but not the offense. (2) There are rules where the offense is shared, but not the action that is the basis for the offense. (3) There are rules where both the action that is the basis for the offense and the offense are shared. (4) There are rules where neither the action that is the basis for the offense nor the offense is shared. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.11
Pali: Atthāpatti upajjhāyo āpajjati no saddhivihāriko, atthāpatti saddhivihāriko āpajjati no upajjhāyo, atthāpatti upajjhāyo ceva āpajjati saddhivihāriko ca, atthāpatti neva upajjhāyo āpajjati no saddhivihāriko. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses committed by the preceptor, but not the student. (2) There are offenses committed by the student, but not the preceptor. (3) There are offenses committed by both the preceptor and the student. (4) There are offenses committed by neither the preceptor nor the student. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.12
Pali: Atthāpatti ācariyo āpajjati no antevāsiko, atthāpatti antevāsiko āpajjati no ācariyo, atthāpatti ācariyo ceva āpajjati antevāsiko ca, atthāpatti neva ācariyo āpajjati no antevāsiko. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses committed by the teacher, but not the pupil. (2) There are offenses committed by the pupil, but not the teacher. (3) There are offenses committed by both the teacher and the pupil. (4) There are offenses committed by neither the teacher nor the pupil. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.13
Pali: Cattāro paccayā anāpatti vassacchedassa—
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense for breaking the rainy-season residence for these four reasons: 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.14
Pali: saṅgho vā bhinno hoti, saṅghaṁ vā bhinditukāmā honti, jīvitantarāyo vā hoti, brahmacariyantarāyo vā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): there is a schism in the Sangha; there are some who want to cause a schism in the Sangha; there is a threat to life; there is a threat to the monastic life. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.15
Pali: Cattāri vacīduccaritāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are four kinds of bad conduct by speech:

Pali: musāvādo, pisuṇā vācā, pharusā vācā, samphappalāpo. 
English (brahmali, published): lying, divisive speech, harsh speech, and idle speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.17

English (brahmali, published): There are four kinds of good conduct by speech: 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.18
Pali: saccavācā, apisuṇā vācā, saṇhā vācā, mantā bhāsā. 
English (brahmali, published): truthful speech, non-divisive speech, gentle speech, and meaningful speech. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.19
Pali: Atthi ādiyanto garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati, payojento lahukaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are offenses that are serious when taking for oneself, but light when inciting someone else. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.20
Pali: atthi ādiyanto lahukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati, payojento garukaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are offenses that are light when taking for oneself, but serious when inciting someone else. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.21
Pali: atthi ādiyantopi payojentopi garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are offenses that are serious both when taking for oneself and when inciting someone else. 

pli-tv-pvr7:50.22
Pali: atthi ādiyantopi payojentopi lahukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) There are offenses that are light both when taking for oneself and when inciting someone else. 

pli-tv-pvr7:51.1
Pali: Atthi puggalo abhivādanāraho, no paccuṭṭhānāraho; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are people who deserve being bowed down to, but not being stood up for. 

pli-tv-pvr7:51.2
Pali: atthi puggalo paccuṭṭhānāraho, no abhivādanāraho; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) There are people who deserve being stood up for, but not to being bowed down to. 

pli-tv-pvr7:51.3
Pali: atthi puggalo abhivādanāraho ceva paccuṭṭhānāraho ca; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) There are people who deserve both being bowed down to and being stood up for. 

pli-tv-pvr7:51.4
Pali: atthi puggalo neva abhivādanāraho no paccuṭṭhānāraho. 
English (brahmali, published): (4) There are people who deserve neither being bowed down to nor being stood up for. 

pli-tv-pvr7:51.5
Pali: Atthi puggalo āsanāraho, no abhivādanāraho; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) There are people who deserve a seat, but not being bowed down to.

pli-tv-pvr7:63.0
Pali: 5. Pañcakavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 5. The section on fives 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.1
Pali: Pañca āpattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are five kinds of offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.2
Pali: Pañca āpattikkhandhā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five classes of offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.3
Pali: Pañca vinītavatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five grounds of training. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.4
Pali: Pañca kammāni ānantarikāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of actions with results in the next life. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.5
Pali: Pañca puggalā niyatā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people with fixed rebirth. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.6
Pali: Pañca chedanakā āpattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five offenses involving cutting. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.7
Pali: Pañcahākārehi āpattiṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five reasons for committing an offense. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.8
Pali: Pañca āpattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of offenses because of lying. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.9
Pali: Musāvādapaccayā pañcahākārehi kammaṁ na upeti—
English (brahmali, published): There are five reasons why a legal procedure is invalid: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.10
Pali: sayaṁ vā kammaṁ na karoti, paraṁ vā na ajjhesati, chandaṁ vā pārisuddhiṁ vā na deti, kayiramāne kamme paṭikkosati, kate vā pana kamme adhammadiṭṭhi hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) one does not do the legal procedure oneself; (2) one does not request someone else; (3) one does not give one’s consent or declare one’s purity; (4) one objects while the legal procedure is being carried out; or (5) one has the view that the completed legal procedure is illegitimate. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.11
Pali: Pañcahākārehi kammaṁ upeti—
English (brahmali, published): There are five reasons why a legal procedure is valid: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.12
Pali: sayaṁ vā kammaṁ karoti, paraṁ vā ajjhesati, chandaṁ vā pārisuddhiṁ vā deti, kayiramāne kamme nappaṭikkosati, kate vā pana kamme dhammadiṭṭhi hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) one does the legal procedure oneself; (2) one requests someone else; (3) one gives one’s consent or declares one’s purity; (4) one does not object while the legal procedure is being carried out; or (5) one has the view that the completed legal procedure is legitimate. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.13
Pali: Pañca piṇḍapātikassa bhikkhuno kappanti—
English (brahmali, published): There are five things that are allowable for a monk who only eats almsfood: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.14
Pali: anāmantacāro, gaṇabhojanaṁ, paramparabhojanaṁ, anadhiṭṭhānaṁ, avikappanā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) visiting families before or after a meal invitation, (2) eating in a group, (3) eating a meal before another, (4) non-determination, (5) non-assignment to another. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.15
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ussaṅkitaparisaṅkito hoti—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has five qualities, whether he is a bad monk or firm in morality, he is suspected and mistrusted: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.16
Pali: pāpabhikkhupi akuppadhammopi vesiyāgocaro vā hoti, vidhavāgocaro vā hoti, thullakumārigocaro vā hoti, paṇḍakagocaro vā hoti, bhikkhunigocaro vā hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): he regularly associates with sex workers, widows, single women, <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍakas</i>, or nuns. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.17
Pali: Pañca telāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of oil: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.18
Pali: tilatelaṁ, sāsapatelaṁ, madhukatelaṁ, eraṇḍakatelaṁ, vasātelaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): sesame oil, mustard oil, honey-tree oil, castor oil, and oil from fat. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.19
Pali: Pañca vasāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of fat: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.20
Pali: acchavasaṁ, macchavasaṁ, susukāvasaṁ, sūkaravasaṁ, gadrabhavasaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): bear fat, fish fat, alligator fat, pig fat, and donkey fat. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.21
Pali: Pañca byasanāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of losses: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.22
Pali: ñātibyasanaṁ, bhogabyasanaṁ, rogabyasanaṁ, sīlabyasanaṁ, diṭṭhibyasanaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): loss of relatives, loss of property, loss of health, loss of morality, and loss of view. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.23
Pali: Pañca sampadā—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of successes: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.24
Pali: ñātisampadā, bhogasampadā, arogasampadā, sīlasampadā, diṭṭhisampadā. 
English (brahmali, published): success in relatives, success in property, success in health, success in morality, and success in view. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.25
Pali: Pañca nissayapaṭippassaddhiyo upajjhāyamhā—
English (brahmali, published): There are five reasons why the formal support from a preceptor comes to an end: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.26
Pali: upajjhāyo pakkanto vā hoti, vibbhanto vā, kālaṅkato vā, pakkhasaṅkanto vā, āṇattiyeva pañcamī. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) the preceptor goes away; (2) the preceptor disrobes; (3) the preceptor dies; (4) the preceptor joins another religion or sect; or (5) the preceptor says so. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.27
Pali: Pañca puggalā na upasampādetabbā—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who should not be given the full ordination: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.28
Pali: addhānahīno, aṅgahīno, vatthuvipanno, karaṇadukkaṭako, aparipūro. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) one lacking in age, (2) one lacking in limbs, (3) one who is deficient as object, (4) one who has acted wrongly, (5) one who is incomplete. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.29
Pali: Pañca paṁsukūlāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of rags: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.30
Pali: sosānikaṁ, pāpaṇikaṁ, undūrakkhāyikaṁ, upacikakkhāyikaṁ, aggidaḍḍhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) those from a charnel ground, (2) those from a shop, (3) those eaten by rats, (4) those eaten by termites, (5) those burned by fire. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.31
Pali: Aparānipi pañca paṁsukūlāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are five other kinds of rags: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.32
Pali: gokhāyikaṁ, ajakkhāyikaṁ, thūpacīvaraṁ, ābhisekikaṁ, gatapaṭiyāgataṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) those chewed by cattle, (2) those chewed by goats, (3) those left at a stupa, (4) those discarded from a king’s consecration, (5) those taken to and then brought back from a charnel ground. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.33
Pali: Pañca avahārā—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of removing: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.34
Pali: theyyāvahāro, pasayhāvahāro, parikappāvahāro, paṭicchannāvahāro, kusāvahāro. 
English (brahmali, published): removing by theft, removing by force, removing dependent on conditions, removing by concealing, removing by drawing lots. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.35
Pali: Pañca mahācorā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five notorious gangsters to be found in the world. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.36
Pali: Pañca avissajjaniyāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five things not to be given away. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.37
Pali: Pañca avebhaṅgiyāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five things not to be distributed. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.38
Pali: Pañcāpattiyo kāyato samuṭṭhanti, na vācato na cittato. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of offenses that originate from body, not from speech and mind. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.39

English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of offenses that originate from body and speech, not from mind. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.40
Pali: Pañcāpattiyo desanāgāminiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of offenses that are confessable. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.41
Pali: Pañca saṅghā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of sangha. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.42
Pali: Pañca pātimokkhuddesā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five ways of reciting the Monastic Code. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.43
Pali: Sabbapaccantimesu janapadesu vinayadharapañcamena gaṇena upasampādetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Outside the central Ganges plain, the full ordination is to be given by a group of five, including one expert on the Monastic Law. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.44
Pali: Pañcānisaṁsā kathinatthāre. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five benefits of performing the robe-making ceremony. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.45
Pali: Pañca kammāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of legal procedures. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.46
Pali: Yāvatatiyake pañca āpattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five ‘for the third time’ offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.47
Pali: Pañcahākārehi adinnaṁ ādiyantassa āpatti pārājikassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When five factors are fulfilled, there is an offense entailing expulsion for one who steals. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.48
Pali: Pañcahākārehi adinnaṁ ādiyantassa āpatti thullaccayassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When five factors are fulfilled, there is a serious offense for one who steals. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.49
Pali: Pañcahākārehi adinnaṁ ādiyantassa āpatti dukkaṭassa. 
English (brahmali, published): When five factors are fulfilled, there is an offense of wrong conduct for one who steals. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.50
Pali: Pañca akappiyāni na paribhuñjitabbāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are five unallowable things that should not be used:

English (brahmali, published): what has not been given, what is not known about, what is not allowable, what has not been received, what has not been made left over. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.52
