English (brahmali, published): There are five allowable things that may be used: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.53
Pali: dinnañca hoti, viditañca hoti, kappiyañca hoti, paṭiggahitañca hoti, katātirittañca hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): what has been given, what is known about, what is allowable, what has been received, what has been made left over. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.54
Pali: Pañca dānāni apuññāni puññasammatāni lokasmiṁ—
English (brahmali, published): There are five gifts without merit that are considered meritorious in the world:

English (brahmali, published): desire, ill will, confusion, feeling inspired to speak, and the thought of departing. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.58
Pali: Pañcānisaṁsā sammajjaniyā—
English (brahmali, published): There are five benefits of sweeping: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.59
Pali: sakacittaṁ pasīdati, paracittaṁ pasīdati, devatā attamanā honti, pāsādikasaṁvattanikakammaṁ upacinati, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) one’s own mind becomes serene, (2) the minds of others become serene, (3) the gods are pleased, (4) one accumulates actions that lead to being inspiring, (5) at the break-up of the body after death one is reborn in heaven. 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.60
Pali: Aparepi pañcānisaṁsā sammajjaniyā—
English (brahmali, published): There are five other benefits of sweeping: 

pli-tv-pvr7:63.61
Pali: sakacittaṁ pasīdati, paracittaṁ pasīdati, devatā attamanā honti, satthusāsanaṁ kataṁ hoti, pacchimā janatā diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) one’s own mind becomes serene, (2) the minds of others become serene, (3) the gods are pleased, (4) one carries out the Teacher’s instruction, (5) later generations follow one’s example. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.1
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.2
Pali: attano bhāsapariyantaṁ na uggaṇhāti, parassa bhāsapariyantaṁ na uggaṇhāti, attano bhāsapariyantaṁ na uggahetvā parassa bhāsapariyantaṁ na uggahetvā adhammena kāreti appaṭiññāya. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not grasp what is proper for himself to say; (2) he does not grasp what is proper for others to say; (3-5) not grasping either, he makes them act illegitimately, without their admission. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.3
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.4
Pali: attano bhāsapariyantaṁ uggaṇhāti, parassa bhāsapariyantaṁ uggaṇhāti, attano bhāsapariyantaṁ uggahetvā parassa bhāsapariyantaṁ uggahetvā dhammena kāreti paṭiññāya. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he grasps what is proper for himself to say; (2) he grasps what is proper for others to say; (3-5) grasping both, he makes them act legitimately, in accordance with their admission. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.5
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has five other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.6
Pali: āpattiṁ na jānāti, āpattiyā mūlaṁ na jānāti, āpattisamudayaṁ na jānāti, āpattinirodhaṁ na jānāti, āpattinirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ na jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the offenses; (2) he does not know the roots of the offenses; (3) he does not know the origins of the offenses; (4) he does not know the ending of the offenses; (5) he does not know the way leading to the ending of the offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.7
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.8
Pali: āpattiṁ jānāti, āpattiyā mūlaṁ jānāti, āpattisamudayaṁ jānāti, āpattinirodhaṁ jānāti, āpattinirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses; (2) he knows the roots of the offenses; (3) he knows the origins of the offenses; (4) he knows the ending of the offenses; (5) he knows the way leading to the ending of the offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.9
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has five other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.10
Pali: adhikaraṇaṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇasamudayaṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇanirodhaṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ na jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the legal issues; (2) he does not know the root of the legal issues; (3) he does not know the origin of the legal issues; (4) he does not know the ending of the legal issues; (5) he does not know the way leading to the ending of the legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.11
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.12
Pali: adhikaraṇaṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇasamudayaṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇanirodhaṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the legal issues; (2) he knows the root of the legal issues; (3) he knows the origin of the legal issues; (4) he knows the ending of the legal issues; (5) he knows the way leading to the ending of the legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.13
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has five other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.14
Pali: vatthuṁ na jānāti, nidānaṁ na jānāti, paññattiṁ na jānāti, anupaññattiṁ na jānāti, anusandhivacanapathaṁ na jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the actions that are the bases for offenses; (2) he does not know the origin stories; (3) he does not know the rules; (4) he does not know the additions to the rules; (5) he does not know the sequence of statements. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.15
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.16
Pali: vatthuṁ jānāti, nidānaṁ jānāti, paññattiṁ jānāti, anupaññattiṁ jānāti, anusandhivacanapathaṁ jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the actions that are the bases for offenses; (2) he knows the origin stories; (3) he knows the rules; (4) he knows the additions to the rules; (5) he knows the sequence of statements. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.17
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has five other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.18
Pali: ñattiṁ na jānāti, ñattiyā karaṇaṁ na jānāti, na pubbakusalo hoti, na aparakusalo hoti, akālaññū ca hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the motion; (2) he does not know how the motion is done; (3) he is not skilled in what comes first; (4) he is not skilled in what comes afterwards; (5) he does not know the right time. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.19
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.20
Pali: ñattiṁ jānāti, ñattiyā karaṇaṁ jānāti, pubbakusalo hoti, aparakusalo hoti, kālaññū ca hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the motion; (2) he knows how the motion is done; (3) he is skilled in what comes first; (4) he is skilled in what comes afterwards; (5) he knows the right time. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.21
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has five other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.22
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, ācariyaparamparā kho panassa na suggahitā hoti na sumanasikatā na sūpadhāritā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he does not know the light and heavy offenses; (3) he does not know the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he does not know the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has not well-learned and well-remembered his teachers’ tradition. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.23
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.24
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ jānati, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, ācariyaparamparā kho panassa suggahitā hoti sumanasikatā sūpadhāritā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he knows the light and heavy offenses; (3) he knows the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he knows the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has well-learned and well-remembered his teachers’ tradition. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.25
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has five other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.26
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni na vitthārena svāgatāni honti na suvibhattāni na suppavattīni na suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he does not know the light and heavy offenses; (3) he does not know the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he does not know the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has not properly learned either Monastic Codes in detail, and he has not analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, or investigated them well, either in terms of the rules or their detailed exposition. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.27
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.28
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he knows the light and heavy offenses; (3) he knows the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he knows the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has properly learned both Monastic Codes in detail, and has analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, and investigated them well, both in terms of the rules and their detailed exposition. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.29
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has five other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.30
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇe ca na vinicchayakusalo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he does not know the light and heavy offenses; (3) he does not know the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he does not know the grave and minor offenses; (5) he is not skilled in deciding legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.31
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has five qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:64.32
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇe ca vinicchayakusalo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he knows the light and heavy offenses; (3) he knows the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he knows the grave and minor offenses; (5) he is skilled in deciding legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.1
Pali: Pañca āraññikā—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of wilderness dwellers: 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.2
Pali: mandattā momūhattā āraññiko hoti, pāpiccho icchāpakato āraññiko hoti, ummādā cittakkhepā āraññiko hoti, “vaṇṇitaṁ buddhehi buddhasāvakehī”ti āraññiko hoti, api ca appicchaññeva nissāya santuṭṭhiññeva nissāya sallekhaññeva nissāya pavivekaññeva nissāya idamatthitaññeva nissāya āraññiko hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): one who is a wilderness dweller (1) because of stupidity and folly; (2) because one is overcome by bad desires; (3) because of insanity and derangement; (4) because it is praised by the Buddhas and their disciples; or (5) because of fewness of wishes, contentment, self-effacement, seclusion, and not needing anything else. 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.3
Pali: Pañca piṇḍapātikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who only eat almsfood … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.4
Pali: pañca paṁsukūlikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of rag-robe wearers … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.5
Pali: pañca rukkhamūlikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who live at the foot of a tree … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.6
Pali: pañca sosānikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who live in charnel grounds … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.7
Pali: pañca abbhokāsikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who live out in the open … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.8
Pali: pañca tecīvarikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who only have three robes … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.9
Pali: pañca sapadānacārikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who go on continuous almsround … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.10
Pali: pañca nesajjikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who never lie down … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.11
Pali: pañca yathāsanthatikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who accept any kind of resting place … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.12
Pali: pañca ekāsanikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who eat in one sitting per day … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.13
Pali: pañca khalupacchābhattikā …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who refuse to accept food offered after the meal has begun … 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.14
Pali: pañca pattapiṇḍikā—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of people who eat only from the almsbowl: 

pli-tv-pvr7:65.15
Pali: mandattā momūhattā pattapiṇḍiko hoti, pāpiccho icchāpakato pattapiṇḍiko hoti, ummādā cittakkhepā pattapiṇḍiko hoti, “vaṇṇitaṁ buddhehi buddhasāvakehī”ti pattapiṇḍiko hoti, api ca appicchaññeva nissāya santuṭṭhiññeva nissāya sallekhaññeva nissāya pavivekaññeva nissāya idamatthitaññeva nissāya pattapiṇḍiko hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): one who eats only from the almsbowl (1) because of stupidity and folly; (2) because one is overcome by bad desires; (3) because of insanity and derangement; (4) because it is praised by the Buddhas and their disciples; or (5) because of fewness of wishes, contentment, self-effacement, seclusion, and not needing anything else. 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.1
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā nānissitena vatthabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has five qualities, he should not live without formal support: 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.2
Pali: uposathaṁ na jānāti, uposathakammaṁ na jānāti, pātimokkhaṁ na jānāti, pātimokkhuddesaṁ na jānāti, ūnapañcavasso hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know about the observance-day ceremony; (2) he does not know the observance-day procedure; (3) he does not know the Monastic Code; (4) he does not know the recitation of the Monastic Code; (5) he has less than five years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.3
Pali: Pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā anissitena vatthabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): But when a monk has five qualities, he may live without formal support: 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.4
Pali: uposathaṁ jānāti, uposathakammaṁ jānāti, pātimokkhaṁ jānāti, pātimokkhuddesaṁ jānāti, pañcavasso vā hoti atirekapañcavasso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows about the observance-day ceremony; (2) he knows the observance-day procedure; (3) he knows the Monastic Code; (4) he knows the recitation of the Monastic Code; (5) he has five or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.5
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā nānissitena vatthabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has five other qualities, he should not live without formal support: 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.6
Pali: pavāraṇaṁ na jānāti, pavāraṇākammaṁ na jānāti, pātimokkhaṁ na jānāti, pātimokkhuddesaṁ na jānāti, ūnapañcavasso hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know about the invitation ceremony; (2) he does not know the invitation procedure; (3) he does not know the Monastic Code; (4) he does not know the recitation of the Monastic Code; (5) he has less than five years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.7

English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows about the invitation ceremony; (2) he knows the invitation procedure; (3) he knows the Monastic Code; (4) he knows the recitation of the Monastic Code; (5) he has five or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.9
Pali: Aparehipi pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā nānissitena vatthabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has five other qualities, he should not live without formal support: 

pli-tv-pvr7:66.10
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, ūnapañcavasso hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he does not know the light and heavy offenses; (3) he does not know the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he does not know the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has less than five years of seniority.

pli-tv-pvr7:67.1
Pali: Pañca ādīnavā apāsādike—
English (brahmali, published): There are five dangers in being uninspiring: 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.2
Pali: attāpi attānaṁ upavadati, anuviccapi viññū garahanti, pāpako kittisaddo abbhuggacchati, sammūḷho kālaṁ karoti, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) you criticize yourself; (2) after investigating, wise people condemn you; (3) you get a bad reputation; (4) you die confused; (5) after death, you are reborn in a lower realm. 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.3
Pali: Pañcānisaṁsā pāsādike—
English (brahmali, published): There are five benefits in being inspiring: 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.4
Pali: attāpi attānaṁ na upavadati, anuviccapi viññū pasaṁsanti, kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggacchati, asammūḷho kālaṁ karoti, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) you do not criticize yourself; (2) after investigating, wise people praise you; (3) you get a good reputation; (4) you die unconfused; (5) after death, you are reborn in heaven. 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.5
Pali: Aparepi pañca ādīnavā apāsādike—
English (brahmali, published): There are five other dangers in being uninspiring: 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.6
Pali: appasannā na pasīdanti, pasannānaṁ ekaccānaṁ aññathattaṁ hoti, satthusāsanaṁ akataṁ hoti, pacchimā janatā diṭṭhānugatiṁ nāpajjati, cittamassa na pasīdati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) you do not give rise to confidence in those without it; (2) you cause some to lose their confidence; (3) you don’t carry out the Teacher’s instructions; (4) later generations follow your example; (5) your mind doesn’t become serene. 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.7
Pali: Pañcānisaṁsā pāsādike—
English (brahmali, published): And there are five benefits in being inspiring: 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.8
Pali: appasannā pasīdanti, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya hoti, satthusāsanaṁ kataṁ hoti, pacchimā janatā diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjati, cittamassa pasīdati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) you give rise to confidence in those without it; (2) you increase the confidence of those who have it; (3) you carry out the Teacher’s instructions; (4) later generations follow your example; (5) your mind becomes serene. 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.9
Pali: Pañca ādīnavā kulūpake—
English (brahmali, published): There are five dangers of associating with families: 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.10
Pali: anāmantacāre āpajjati, raho nisajjāya āpajjati, paṭicchanne āsane āpajjati, mātugāmassa uttarichappañcavācāhi dhammaṁ desento āpajjati, kāmasaṅkappabahulo ca viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) one commits the offense of visiting families before or after a meal invitation; (2) one commits the offense of sitting down in private; (3) one commits the offense of sitting down on a concealed seat; (4) one commits the offense of teaching more than five or six sentences to a woman; (5) one has a lot of worldly thoughts. 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.11
Pali: Pañca ādīnavā kulūpakassa bhikkhuno—
English (brahmali, published): There are five dangers for a monk who associates with families: 

pli-tv-pvr7:67.12
Pali: ativelaṁ kulesu saṁsaṭṭhassa viharato mātugāmassa abhiṇhadassanaṁ, dassane sati saṁsaggo, saṁsagge sati vissāso, vissāse sati otāro, otiṇṇacittassetaṁ bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ anabhirato vā brahmacariyaṁ carissati aññataraṁ vā saṅkiliṭṭhaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjissati sikkhaṁ vā paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattissati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) when he associates too much with families, he often sees women; (2) because of seeing them, he associates with them; (3) because of associating with them, there is intimacy; (4) because of intimacy, there is lust; (5) because his mind is overcome by lust, it is to be expected that he will be dissatisfied with the spiritual life, that he will commit a certain defiled offense, or that he will renounce the training and return to the lower life. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.1
Pali: Pañca bījajātāni—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of propagation: 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.2
Pali: mūlabījaṁ, khandhabījaṁ, phaḷubījaṁ, aggabījaṁ, bījabījaññeva pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) propagation from roots, (2) propagation from stems, (3) propagation from joints, (4) propagation from cuttings, (5) propagation from regular seeds as the fifth. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.3
Pali: Pañcahi samaṇakappehi phalaṁ paribhuñjitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): When fruit is allowable for monastics for any of five reasons, it may be eaten: 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.4
Pali: aggiparijitaṁ, satthaparijitaṁ, nakhaparijitaṁ, abījaṁ, nibbattabījaññeva pañcamaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) it has been damaged by fire, (2) it has been damaged by a knife, (3) it has been damaged by a fingernail, (4) it’s seedless, (5) the seeds have been removed. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.5
Pali: Pañca visuddhiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of purification: 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.6
Pali: nidānaṁ uddisitvā avasesaṁ sutena sāvetabbaṁ, ayaṁ paṭhamā visuddhi; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) After reciting the introduction, the rest is announced as if heard. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.7
Pali: nidānaṁ uddisitvā cattāri pārājikāni uddisitvā avasesaṁ sutena sāvetabbaṁ, ayaṁ dutiyā visuddhi; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) After reciting the introduction and the four rules entailing expulsion, the rest is announced as if heard. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.8
Pali: nidānaṁ uddisitvā cattāri pārājikāni uddisitvā terasa saṅghādisese uddisitvā avasesaṁ sutena sāvetabbaṁ, ayaṁ tatiyā visuddhi; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) After reciting the introduction, the four rules entailing expulsion, and the thirteen rules entailing suspension, the rest is announced as if heard. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.9
Pali: nidānaṁ uddisitvā cattāri pārājikāni uddisitvā terasa saṅghādisese uddisitvā dve aniyate uddisitvā avasesaṁ sutena sāvetabbaṁ, ayaṁ catutthā visuddhi; 
English (brahmali, published): (4) After reciting the introduction, the four rules entailing expulsion, the thirteen rules entailing suspension, and the two undetermined rules, the rest is announced as if heard. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.10
Pali: vitthāreneva pañcamī. 
English (brahmali, published): (5) In full is the fifth. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.11
Pali: Aparāpi pañca visuddhiyo—
English (brahmali, published): There are five other kinds of purification: 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.12
Pali: suttuddeso, pārisuddhiuposatho, adhiṭṭhānuposatho, pavāraṇā, sāmaggīuposathoyeva pañcamo. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) the observance-day ceremony which consists of reciting the Monastic Code, (2) the observance-day ceremony which consists of declaring purity, (3) the observance-day ceremony which consists of a determination, (4) the invitation ceremony, (5) the observance-day ceremony for the sake of unity as the fifth. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.13
Pali: Pañcānisaṁsā vinayadhare—
English (brahmali, published): There are five benefits of being an expert on the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.14
Pali: attano sīlakkhandho sugutto hoti surakkhito, kukkuccapakatānaṁ paṭisaraṇaṁ hoti, visārado saṅghamajjhe voharati, paccatthike sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggaṇhāti, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā paṭipanno hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) your own morality is well guarded; (2) you are a refuge for those who are habitually anxious; (3) you speak with confidence in the midst of the Sangha; (4) you can legitimately and properly refute an opponent; (5) you are practicing for the longevity of the true Teaching. 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.15
Pali: Pañca adhammikāni pātimokkhaṭṭhapanāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of illegitimate cancellations of the Monastic Code. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:68.16
Pali: Pañca dhammikāni pātimokkhaṭṭhapanānīti. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five kinds of legitimate cancellations of the Monastic Code.”

Pali: Pañcakaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The section on fives is finished.

pli-tv-pvr7:81.0

English (brahmali, published): 6. The section on sixes 

pli-tv-pvr7:81.1
Pali: Cha agāravā. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are six kinds of disrespect. —

English (brahmali, published): There are six kinds of respect. —

English (brahmali, published): There are six proper ways. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:81.5

English (brahmali, published): There are six originations of offenses. —

English (brahmali, published): There are six offenses involving cutting. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:81.7

English (brahmali, published): There are six ways of committing an offense. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:81.8
Pali: Chānisaṁsā vinayadhare. 
English (brahmali, published): There are six benefits of being an expert on the Monastic Law. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:81.9

English (brahmali, published): There are six rules about ‘at the most’. —

English (brahmali, published): There are six kinds of robe-cloth. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:81.12

English (brahmali, published): There are six kinds of dye. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:81.13

English (brahmali, published): There are six kinds of offenses that originate from body and mind, not from speech. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:81.14

English (brahmali, published): There are six aspects of friendliness. —

pli-tv-pvr7:82.1
Pali: Chahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ nissayo dātabbo sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has six qualities, he may give the full ordination, give formal support, and have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:82.2
Pali: asekkhena sīlakkhandhena samannāgato hoti, asekkhena samādhikkhandhena samannāgato hoti, asekkhena paññakkhandhena samannāgato hoti, asekkhena vimuttikkhandhena samannāgato hoti, asekkhena vimuttiñāṇadassanakkhandhena samannāgato hoti, dasavasso vā hoti atirekadasavasso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): he has (1) the virtue, (2) stillness, (3) wisdom, (4) freedom, and (5) the knowledge and vision of freedom of one who is fully trained, and (6) he has ten or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:83.1
Pali: Aparehipi chahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ nissayo dātabbo sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has six other qualities, he may give the full ordination, give formal support, and have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:83.2

English (brahmali, published): (1) he has the virtue of one who is fully trained himself and encourages others in it; 

pli-tv-pvr7:83.3
Pali: attanā asekkhena samādhikkhandhena samannāgato hoti, paraṁ asekkhe samādhikkhandhe samādapetā;

pli-tv-pvr7:83.4

English (brahmali, published): (3) he has the wisdom of one who is fully trained himself and encourages others in it; 

pli-tv-pvr7:83.5
Pali: attanā asekkhena vimuttikkhandhena samannāgato hoti, paraṁ asekkhe vimuttikkhandhe samādapetā; 
English (brahmali, published): (4) he has the freedom of one who is fully trained himself and encourages others in it; 

pli-tv-pvr7:83.6
Pali: attanā asekkhena vimuttiñāṇadassanakkhandhena samannāgato hoti, paraṁ asekkhe vimuttiñāṇadassanakkhandhe samādapetā; 
English (brahmali, published): (5) he has the knowledge and vision of freedom of one who is fully trained himself and encourages others in it; 

pli-tv-pvr7:83.7
Pali: dasavasso vā hoti atirekadasavasso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): (6) he has ten or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:84.1
Pali: Aparehipi chahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ nissayo dātabbo sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has six other qualities, he may give the full ordination, give formal support, and have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:84.2
Pali: saddho hoti, hirimā hoti, ottappī hoti, āraddhavīriyo hoti, upaṭṭhitassati hoti, dasavasso vā hoti atirekadasavasso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): he has (1) faith, (2) conscience, (3) moral prudence, (4) energy, (5) mindfulness, and (6) ten or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:85.1
Pali: Aparehipi chahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ nissayo dātabbo sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has six other qualities, he may give the full ordination, give formal support, and have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:85.2
Pali: na adhisīle sīlavipanno hoti, na ajjhācāre ācāravipanno hoti, na atidiṭṭhiyā diṭṭhivipanno hoti, bahussuto hoti, paññavā hoti, dasavasso vā hoti atirekadasavasso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he has not failed in the higher morality; (2) he has not failed in the higher conduct; (3) he has not failed in the higher view; (4) he is learned; (5) he is wise; (6) he has ten or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:86.1
Pali: Aparehipi chahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ nissayo dātabbo sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has six other qualities, he may give the full ordination, give formal support, and have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:86.2
Pali: paṭibalo hoti antevāsiṁ vā saddhivihāriṁ vā gilānaṁ upaṭṭhātuṁ vā upaṭṭhāpetuṁ vā, anabhirataṁ vūpakāsetuṁ vā vūpakāsāpetuṁ vā, uppannaṁ kukkuccaṁ dhammato vinodetuṁ, āpattiṁ jānāti, āpattivuṭṭhānaṁ jānāti, dasavasso vā hoti atirekadasavasso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): he is capable of three things in regard to a student: (1) to nurse him or have him nursed when he is sick; (2) to send him away or have him sent away when he is discontent with the spiritual life; (3) to use the Teaching to dispel anxiety. And (4) he knows the offenses; (5) he knows how offenses are cleared; and (6) he has ten or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:87.1
Pali: Aparehipi chahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ nissayo dātabbo sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has six other qualities, he may give the full ordination, give formal support, and have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:87.2
Pali: paṭibalo hoti antevāsiṁ vā saddhivihāriṁ vā ābhisamācārikāya sikkhāya sikkhāpetuṁ, ādibrahmacāriyakāya sikkhāya vinetuṁ, abhidhamme vinetuṁ, abhivinaye vinetuṁ, uppannaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ dhammato vivecetuṁ, dasavasso vā hoti atirekadasavasso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): he is capable of five things in regard to a student: (1) to train him in good conduct; (2) to train him in the basics of the spiritual life; (3) to train him in the Teaching; (4) to train him in the Monastic Law; and (5) to use the Teaching to make him give up wrong views. And (6) he has ten or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr7:88.1
Pali: Aparehipi chahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ nissayo dātabbo sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has six other qualities, he may give the full ordination, give formal support, and have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr7:88.2
Pali: āpattiṁ jānāti, anāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, garukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso, dasavasso vā hoti atirekadasavasso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses; (2) he knows the non-offenses; (3) he knows the light offenses; (4) he knows the heavy offenses; (5) he has properly learned both Monastic Codes in detail, and he has analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, and investigated them well, both in terms of the rules and their detailed exposition; (6) he has ten or more years of seniority.

pli-tv-pvr7:96.0
Pali: 7. Sattakavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 7. The section on sevens 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.1
Pali: Sattāpattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are seven kinds of offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.2
Pali: Sattāpattikkhandhā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven classes of offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.3
Pali: Satta vinītavatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven grounds of training. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.4
Pali: Satta sāmīciyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven proper ways. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.5
Pali: Satta adhammikā paṭiññātakaraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven illegitimate ways of acting according to what has been admitted. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.6
Pali: Satta dhammikā paṭiññātakaraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven legitimate ways of acting according to what has been admitted. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.7
Pali: Sattannaṁ anāpatti sattāhakaraṇīyena gantuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): There is no offense in going for seven days to seven kinds of people. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.8
Pali: Sattānisaṁsā vinayadhare. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven benefits of being an expert on the Monastic Law. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.9
Pali: Satta paramāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven rules about ‘at the most’. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.10
Pali: Sattame aruṇuggamane nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): There is becoming subject to relinquishment at dawn on the seventh day. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.11
Pali: Satta samathā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven principles for settling legal issues. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.12
Pali: Satta kammāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven kinds of legal procedures. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.13
Pali: Satta āmakadhaññāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven kinds of raw grain. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.14
Pali: Tiriyaṁ sattantarā. 
English (brahmali, published): It is seven wide inside. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.15
Pali: Gaṇabhojane satta anupaññattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven additions to the rule on eating in a group. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.16
Pali: Bhesajjāni paṭiggahetvā sattāhaparamaṁ sannidhikārakaṁ paribhuñjitabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): After being received, the tonics should be used from storage for at most seven days. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.17
Pali: Katacīvaraṁ ādāya pakkamati. 
English (brahmali, published): One takes a finished robe and leaves the monastery. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.18

English (brahmali, published): One leaves the monastery with a finished robe. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.19
Pali: Bhikkhussa na hoti āpatti daṭṭhabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk doesn’t have any offense he needs to recognize. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.20
Pali: Bhikkhussa hoti āpatti daṭṭhabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk does have an offense he needs to recognize. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.21
Pali: Bhikkhussa hoti āpatti daṭṭhabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk has an offense he needs to make amends for. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.22
Pali: Satta adhammikāni pātimokkhaṭṭhapanāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven kinds of illegitimate cancellations of the Monastic Code. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:96.23
Pali: Satta dhammikāni pātimokkhaṭṭhapanāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are seven kinds of legitimate cancellations of the Monastic Code. 

pli-tv-pvr7:97.1
Pali: Sattahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu vinayadharo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has seven qualities, he is an expert on the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:97.2
Pali: āpattiṁ jānāti, anāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, garukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sīlavā hoti, pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu, catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) He knows the offenses. (2) He knows the non-offenses. (3) He knows the light offenses. (4) He knows the heavy offenses. (5) He is virtuous and restrained by the Monastic Code. His conduct is good, he associates with the right people, and he sees danger in minor faults. And he undertakes and trains in the training rules. (6) Whenever he wants, he accesses the four absorptions, those pleasant meditations of the higher mind. (7) And because of the ending of the corruptions, he has realized with his own insight, in this very life, the liberation by mind and the liberation by wisdom. 

pli-tv-pvr7:98.1
Pali: Aparehipi sattahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu vinayadharo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has seven other qualities, he is an expert on the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:98.2
Pali: āpattiṁ jānāti, anāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, garukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti tathārūpassa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā, catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) He knows the offenses. (2) He knows the non-offenses. (3) He knows the light offenses. (4) He knows the heavy offenses. (5) He has learned much, and he retains and accumulates what he has learned. Those teachings that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, that have a true goal and are well articulated, and that set out the perfectly complete and pure spiritual life—he has learned many such teachings, retained them in mind, recited them verbally, mentally investigated them, and penetrated them well by view. (6) Whenever he wants, he accesses the four absorptions, those pleasant meditations of the higher mind. (7) And because of the ending of the corruptions, he has realized with his own insight, in this very life, the liberation by mind and the liberation by wisdom. 

pli-tv-pvr7:99.1
Pali: Aparehipi sattahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu vinayadharo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has seven other qualities, he is an expert on the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:99.2
Pali: āpattiṁ jānāti, anāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, garukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso, catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) He knows the offenses. (2) He knows the non-offenses. (3) He knows the light offenses. (4) He knows the heavy offenses. (5) He has properly learned both Monastic Codes in detail; he has analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, and investigated them well, both in terms of the rules and their detailed exposition. (6) Whenever he wants, he accesses the four absorptions, those pleasant meditations of the higher mind. (7) And because of the ending of the corruptions, he has realized with his own insight, in this very life, the liberation by mind and the liberation by wisdom. 

pli-tv-pvr7:100.1
Pali: Aparehipi sattahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu vinayadharo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has seven other qualities, he is an expert on the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr7:100.2
Pali: āpattiṁ jānāti; anāpattiṁ jānāti; lahukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti; garukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti; anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ dvepi jātiyo tissopi jātiyo catassopi jātiyo pañcapi jātiyo dasapi jātiyo vīsampi jātiyo tiṁsampi jātiyo cattālīsampi jātiyo paññāsampi jātiyo jātisatampi jātisahassampi jātisatasahassampi anekepi saṁvaṭṭakappe anekepi vivaṭṭakappe anekepi saṁvaṭṭavivaṭṭakappe—“amutrāsiṁ evaṁnāmo evaṅgotto evaṁvaṇṇo evamāhāro evaṁsukhadukkhappaṭisaṁvedī evamāyupariyanto, so tato cuto amutra udapādiṁ; tatrāpāsiṁ evaṁnāmo evaṅgotto evaṁvaṇṇo evamāhāro evaṁsukhadukkhappaṭisaṁvedī evamāyupariyanto, so tato cuto idhūpapanno”ti iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati; dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena satte passati cavamāne upapajjamāne hīne paṇīte suvaṇṇe dubbaṇṇe, sugate duggate yathākammūpage satte pajānāti—“ime vata bhonto sattā kāyaduccaritena samannāgatā, vacīduccaritena samannāgatā, manoduccaritena samannāgatā, ariyānaṁ upavādakā, micchādiṭṭhikā, micchādiṭṭhikammasamādānā, te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapannā, ime vā pana bhonto sattā kāyasucaritena samannāgatā, vacīsucaritena samannāgatā, manosucaritena samannāgatā, ariyānaṁ anupavādakā, sammādiṭṭhikā, sammādiṭṭhikammasamādānā, te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapannā”ti iti dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena satte passati cavamāne upapajjamāne hīne paṇīte suvaṇṇe dubbaṇṇe sugate duggate yathākammūpage satte pajānāti; āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) He knows the offenses. (2) He knows the non-offenses. (3) He knows the light offenses. (4) He knows the heavy offenses. (5) He recollects many past lives, that is, one birth, two births, three births, four births, five births, ten births, twenty births, thirty births, forty births, fifty births, a hundred births, a thousand births, a hundred thousand births; many eons of world dissolution, many eons of world evolution, many eons of both dissolution and evolution; and he knows: ‘There I had such a name, such a family, such an appearance, such food, such an experience of pleasure and pain, and such a lifespan. Passing away from there, I was reborn elsewhere, and there I had such a name, such a family, such an appearance, such food, such an experience of pleasure and pain, and such a lifespan. Passing away from there, I was reborn here.’ In this way he recollects many past lives with their characteristics and particulars. (6) With superhuman and purified clairvoyance, he sees beings passing away and getting reborn, inferior and superior, beautiful and ugly, gone to good destinations and to bad destinations, and he understands how beings pass on according to their actions: ‘These beings who engaged in misconduct by body, speech, and mind, who abused the noble ones, who had wrong views and acted accordingly, at the breaking up of the body after death, have been reborn in a lower realm, a bad destination, a world of misery, hell. But these beings who engaged in good conduct of body, speech, and mind, who did not abuse the noble ones, who held right view and acted accordingly, at the breaking up of the body after death, have been reborn in a good destination, a heaven world.’ In this way, with superhuman and purified clairvoyance, he sees beings passing away and getting reborn, inferior and superior, beautiful and ugly, gone to good destinations and to bad destinations, and he understands how beings pass on according to their actions. (7) And because of the ending of the corruptions, he has realized with his own insight, in this very life, the liberation by mind and the liberation by wisdom.

pli-tv-pvr7:113.1

Pali: Aṭṭhānisaṁse sampassamānena paresampi saddhāya sā āpatti desetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): When you see eight benefits, you should confess an offense even out of confidence in the others. —

English (brahmali, published): There are eight ‘after the third’. —

pli-tv-pvr7:117.1
Pali: Lokadhammā garudhammā,

pli-tv-pvr7:120.0
Pali: 9. Navakavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 9. The section on nines 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.1
Pali: Nava āghātavatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are nine grounds for resentment. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.2
Pali: Nava āghātapaṭivinayā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine ways of getting rid of resentment. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.3
Pali: Nava vinītavatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine grounds of training. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.4
Pali: Nava paṭhamāpattikā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine immediate offenses. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.5
Pali: Navahi saṅgho bhijjati. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha is split by a group of nine. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.6
Pali: Nava paṇītabhojanāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine fine foods. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.7
Pali: Navamaṁsehi dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): There is an offense of wrong conduct for eating nine kinds of meat. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.8
Pali: Nava pātimokkhuddesā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine ways of reciting the Monastic Code. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.9
Pali: Nava paramāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine rules about ‘at the most’. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.10
Pali: Nava taṇhāmūlakā dhammā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine things rooted in craving. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.11
Pali: Nava vidhamānā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine kinds of conceit. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.12
Pali: Nava cīvarāni adhiṭṭhātabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine kinds of robes that should be determined. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.13
Pali: Nava cīvarāni na vikappetabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine kinds of robes that should not be assigned to another. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.14
Pali: Dīghaso nava vidatthiyo sugatavidatthiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): Nine standard handspans long. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.15
Pali: Nava adhammikāni dānāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine illegitimate kinds of gifts. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.16
Pali: Nava adhammikā paṭiggahā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine illegitimate kinds of receiving. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.17
Pali: Nava adhammikā paribhogā—
English (brahmali, published): There are nine illegitimate kinds of possession. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.18
Pali: tīṇi dhammikāni dānāni, tayo dhammikā paṭiggahā, tayo dhammikā paribhogā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are three legitimate kinds of gifts, three legitimate kinds of receiving, and three legitimate kinds of possession. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.19
Pali: Nava adhammikā saññattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine illegitimate ways of winning over. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.20
Pali: Nava dhammikā saññattiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine legitimate ways of winning over. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.21
Pali: Adhammakamme dve navakāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are two groups of nine on illegitimate legal procedures. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.22
Pali: Dhammakamme dve navakāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are two groups of nine on legitimate legal procedures. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.23
Pali: Nava adhammikāni pātimokkhaṭṭhapanāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine kinds of illegitimate cancellations of the Monastic Code. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:120.24
Pali: Nava dhammikāni pātimokkhaṭṭhapanānīti. 
English (brahmali, published): There are nine kinds of legitimate cancellations of the Monastic Code.” 

pli-tv-pvr7:121.1
Pali: Navakaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The section on nines is finished.

pli-tv-pvr7:126.0
Pali: 10. Dasakavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 10. The section on tens 

pli-tv-pvr7:126.1
Pali: Dasa āghātavatthūni. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are ten grounds for resentment. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:126.2
Pali: Dasa āghātapaṭivinayā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are ten ways of getting rid of resentment. —

English (brahmali, published): There are ten grounds of training. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:126.4

English (brahmali, published): There are ten subject matters of wrong view. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:126.5

English (brahmali, published): There are ten ways of doing skillful deeds. —

English (brahmali, published): There are ten reasons why a vote is illegitimate. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:126.12

English (brahmali, published): There are ten reasons why a vote is legitimate. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:126.13
Pali: Sāmaṇerānaṁ dasa sikkhāpadāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are ten training rules for novice monks. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:126.14
Pali: Dasahaṅgehi samannāgato sāmaṇero nāsetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): A novice monk who has ten qualities should be expelled. 

pli-tv-pvr7:127.1
Pali: Dasahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has ten qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:127.2
Pali: attano bhāsapariyantaṁ na uggaṇhāti, parassa bhāsapariyantaṁ na uggaṇhāti, attano bhāsapariyantaṁ anuggahetvā parassa bhāsapariyantaṁ anuggahetvā adhammena kāreti, appaṭiññāya āpattiṁ na jānāti, āpattiyā mūlaṁ na jānāti, āpattisamudayaṁ na jānāti, āpattinirodhaṁ na jānāti, āpattinirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ na jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not grasp what is proper for himself to say; (2) he does not grasp what is proper for others to say; (3-5) not grasping either, he makes them act illegitimately, without their admission; (6) he does not know the offenses; (7) he does not know the roots of the offenses; (8) he does not know the origin of the offenses; (9) he does not know the ending of the offenses; (10) he does not know the way leading to the ending of the offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr7:128.1
Pali: Dasahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has ten qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:128.2
Pali: attano bhāsapariyantaṁ uggaṇhāti, parassa bhāsapariyantaṁ uggaṇhāti, attano bhāsapariyantaṁ uggahetvā parassa bhāsapariyantaṁ uggahetvā dhammena kāreti, paṭiññāya āpattiṁ jānāti, āpattiyā mūlaṁ jānāti, āpattisamudayaṁ jānāti, āpattinirodhaṁ jānāti, āpattinirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he grasps what is proper for himself to say; (2) he grasps what is proper for others to say; (3-5) grasping both, he makes them act legitimately, in accordance with their admission; (6) he knows the offenses; (7) he knows the roots of the offenses; (8) he knows the origin of the offenses; (9) he knows the ending of the offenses; (10) he knows the way leading to the ending of the offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr7:129.1
Pali: Aparehipi dasahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has ten other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:129.2
Pali: adhikaraṇaṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇasamudayaṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇanirodhaṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ na jānāti, vatthuṁ na jānāti, nidānaṁ na jānāti, paññattiṁ na jānāti, anupaññattiṁ na jānāti, anusandhivacanapathaṁ na jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the legal issues; (2) he does not know the root of the legal issues; (3) he does not know the origin of the legal issues; (4) he does not know the ending of the legal issues; (5) he does not know the way leading to the ending of the legal issues; (6) he does not know the actions that are the bases for offenses; (7) he does not know the origin stories; (8) he does not know the rules; (9) he does not know the additions to the rules; (10) he does not know the sequence of statements. 

pli-tv-pvr7:130.1
Pali: Dasahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has ten qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:130.2
Pali: adhikaraṇaṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇasamudayaṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇanirodhaṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ jānāti, vatthuṁ jānāti, nidānaṁ jānāti, paññattiṁ jānāti, anupaññattiṁ jānāti, anusandhivacanapathaṁ jānāti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows legal issues; (2) he knows the root of the legal issues; (3) he knows the origin of the legal issues; (4) he knows the ending of the legal issues; (5) he knows the way leading to the ending of the legal issues; (6) he knows the actions that are the bases for offenses; (7) he knows the origin stories; (8) he knows the rules; (9) he knows the additions to the rules; (10) he knows the sequence of statements. 

pli-tv-pvr7:131.1
Pali: Aparehipi dasahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has ten other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:131.2
Pali: ñattiṁ na jānāti, ñattiyā karaṇaṁ na jānāti, na pubbakusalo hoti, na aparakusalo hoti, akālaññū ca hoti, āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, ācariyaparamparā kho panassa na suggahitā hoti na sumanasikatā na sūpadhāritā. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the motion; (2) he does not know how the motion is done; (3) he is not skilled in what comes first; (4) he is not skilled in what comes afterwards; (5) he does not know the right time; (6) he does not know the offenses and non-offenses; (7) he does not know the light and heavy offenses; (8) he does not know the curable and incurable offenses; (9) he does not know the grave and minor offenses; (10) he has not well-learned or well-remembered his teachers’ tradition. 

pli-tv-pvr7:132.1
Pali: Dasahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has ten qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:132.2

English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the motion; (2) he knows how the motion is done; (3) he is skilled in what comes first; (4) he is skilled in what comes afterwards; (5) he knows the right time; (6) he knows the offenses and non-offenses; (7) he knows the light and heavy offenses; (8) he knows the curable and incurable offenses; (9) he knows the grave and minor offenses; (10) he has well-learned and well-remembered his teachers’ tradition. 

pli-tv-pvr7:133.1
Pali: Aparehipi dasahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “bālo” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): When an expert on the Monastic Law has ten other qualities, he is considered ignorant: 

pli-tv-pvr7:133.2
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena na svāgatāni honti na suvibhattāni na suppavattīni na suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso, āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, adhikaraṇe ca na vinicchayakusalo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he does not know the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he does not know the light and heavy offenses; (3) he does not know the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he does not know the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has not properly learned both Monastic Codes in detail, not having analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, and investigated them well, both in terms of the rules and their detailed exposition; (6) he does not know the offenses and non-offenses; (7) he does not know the light and heavy offenses; (8) he does not know the curable and incurable offenses; (9) he does not know the grave and minor offenses; (10) he is not skilled in deciding legal issues. 

pli-tv-pvr7:134.1
Pali: Dasahaṅgehi samannāgato vinayadharo “paṇḍito” tveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati—
English (brahmali, published): But when an expert on the Monastic Law has ten qualities, he is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr7:134.2
Pali: āpattānāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso, āpattānāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, adhikaraṇe ca vinicchayakusalo hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he knows the light and heavy offenses; (3) he knows the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he knows the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has properly learned both Monastic Codes in detail, having analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, and investigated them well, both in terms of the rules and their detailed exposition; (6) he knows the offenses and non-offenses; (7) he knows the light and heavy offenses; (8) he knows the curable and incurable offenses; (9) he knows the grave and minor offenses; (10) he is skilled in deciding legal issues.

English (brahmali, published): A competent and capable monk who has ten years of seniority may give the going forth, the full ordination, and formal support, and may have a novice monk attend on him. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:135.26

pli-tv-pvr7:144.0
Pali: 11. Ekādasakavāra 
English (brahmali, published): 11. The section on elevens 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.1
Pali: Ekādasa puggalā anupasampannā na upasampādetabbā, upasampannā nāsetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are eleven kinds of people who should not be ordained, and if they have been ordained, they should be expelled. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.2
Pali: Ekādasa pādukā akappiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of unallowable shoes. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.3
Pali: Ekādasa pattā akappiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of unallowable almsbowls. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.4
Pali: Ekādasa cīvarāni akappiyāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of unallowable robes. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.5
Pali: Ekādasa yāvatatiyakā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven ‘after the third’. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.6
Pali: Bhikkhunīnaṁ ekādasa antarāyikā dhammā pucchitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The nuns should be asked about the eleven obstacles. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.7
Pali: Ekādasa cīvarāni adhiṭṭhātabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of robes that should be determined. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.8
Pali: Ekādasa cīvarāni na vikappetabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of robes that should not be assigned to another. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.9
Pali: Ekādase aruṇuggamane nissaggiyaṁ hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): Becoming subject to relinquishment at dawn on the eleventh day. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.10
Pali: Ekādasa gaṇṭhikā kappiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of allowable toggles. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.11
Pali: Ekādasa vidhā kappiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of allowable buckles. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.12
Pali: Ekādasa pathavī akappiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of unallowable earth. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.13
Pali: Ekādasa pathavī kappiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of allowable earth. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.14
Pali: Ekādasa nissayapaṭippassaddhiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven reasons why formal support comes to an end. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.15
Pali: Ekādasa puggalā avandiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of people a monk should not pay respect to. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.16
Pali: Ekādasa paramāni. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven rules on ‘at the most’. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.17
Pali: Ekādasa varāni yāciṁsu. 
English (brahmali, published): They asked for eleven favors. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.18
Pali: Ekādasa sīmādosā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven kinds of flaws in monastery zones. — 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.19
Pali: Akkosakaparibhāsake puggale ekādasādīnavā pāṭikaṅkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven dangers to be expected for people who abuse and revile. 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.20
Pali: Mettāya cetovimuttiyā āsevitāya bhāvitāya bahulīkatāya yānīkatāya vatthukatāya anuṭṭhitāya paricitāya susamāraddhāya ekādasānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven benefits to be expected for one who practices the liberation of mind through love, who develops, cultivates, and makes it a vehicle and basis, who keeps it up, accumulates, and properly implements it: 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.21
Pali: Sukhaṁ supati, sukhaṁ paṭibujjhati, na pāpakaṁ supinaṁ passati, manussānaṁ piyo hoti, amanussānaṁ piyo hoti, devatā rakkhanti, nāssa aggi vā visaṁ vā satthaṁ vā kamati, tuvaṭṭaṁ cittaṁ samādhiyati, mukhavaṇṇo vippasīdati, asammūḷho kālaṁ karoti, uttari appaṭivijjhanto brahmalokūpago hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) you sleep well, (2) you wake up rested, and (3) you have no nightmares; (4) you are loved by humans and (5) spirits; (6) you are protected by the gods; (7) you cannot be harmed by fire, poison, or weapons; (8) your mind is quickly stilled; (9) your face is serene; (10) you die unconfused; and (11) if you do not go any further, you are reborn in the world of the supreme beings.” 

pli-tv-pvr7:144.22
Pali: mettāya cetovimuttiyā āsevitāya bhāvitāya bahulīkatāya yānīkatāya vatthukatāya anuṭṭhitāya paricitāya susamāraddhāya ime ekādasānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhāti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr7:145.1
Pali: Ekādasakaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ.

English (brahmali, published): Determination, assigning to another.

Pali: Aruṇā gaṇṭhikā vidhā, 
English (brahmali, published): Dawn, toggles, buckles, 

pli-tv-pvr7:148.2

English (brahmali, published): And unallowable, allowable;

English (brahmali, published): What is the beginning, the middle, and the end of the observance-day procedure?

English (brahmali, published): of rehabilitation? 

pli-tv-pvr8:5.9
Pali: Abbhānassa vatthu ca puggalo ca ādi, ñatti majjhe, kammavācā pariyosānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The action that is the basis for the offense and the person who has done it are the beginning of rehabilitation, the motion is the middle, and the announcement is the end.

pli-tv-pvr9:0.1
Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr9:0.2
Pali: 9. Atthavasapakaraṇa 
English (brahmali, published): The ten reasons for the laying down of training rules 

pli-tv-pvr9:1.1
Pali: Dasa atthavase paṭicca tathāgatena sāvakānaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): “The Buddha laid down the training rules for his disciples for ten reasons: 

pli-tv-pvr9:1.2
Pali: saṅghasuṭṭhutāya, saṅghaphāsutāya, dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ phāsuvihārāya, diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya, samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya, appasannānaṁ pasādāya, pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): for the well-being of the Sangha, for the comfort of the Sangha, for the restraint of bad people, for the ease of good monks, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life, for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives, to give rise to confidence in those without it, to increase the confidence of those who have it, for the longevity of the true Teaching, and for supporting the training. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.1
Pali: Yaṁ saṅghasuṭṭhu taṁ saṅghaphāsu. 
English (brahmali, published): The well-being of the Sangha is the comfort of the Sangha. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.2
Pali: Yaṁ saṅghaphāsu taṁ dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The comfort of the Sangha is for the restraint of bad people. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.3
Pali: Yaṁ dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahāya taṁ pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ phāsuvihārāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The restraint of bad people is for the ease of good monks. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.4
Pali: Yaṁ pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ phāsuvihārāya taṁ diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The ease of good monks is for the restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.5
Pali: Yaṁ diṭṭhadhammikānaṁ āsavānaṁ saṁvarāya taṁ samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The restraint of the corruptions relating to the present life is for the restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.6
Pali: Yaṁ samparāyikānaṁ āsavānaṁ paṭighātāya taṁ appasannānaṁ pasādāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The restraint of the corruptions relating to future lives is to give rise to confidence in those without it. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.7
Pali: Yaṁ appasannānaṁ pasādāya taṁ pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The giving rise to confidence in those without it is to increase the confidence of those who have it. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.8
Pali: Yaṁ pasannānaṁ bhiyyobhāvāya taṁ saddhammaṭṭhitiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): The increase in confidence of those who have it is for the longevity of the true Teaching. 

pli-tv-pvr9:2.9
Pali: Yaṁ saddhammaṭṭhitiyā taṁ vinayānuggahāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The longevity of the true Teaching is for supporting the training.

  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-pvr/pli-tv-pvr10_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-pvr10:0.1
Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr10:0.2
Pali: 10. Gāthāsaṅgaṇika 
English (brahmali, published): Verses on the training rules 

pli-tv-pvr10:0.3
Pali: 1. Sattanagaresupaññattasikkhāpada 
English (brahmali, published): 1. Training rules laid down in seven towns 

pli-tv-pvr10:1.1
Pali: “Ekaṁsaṁ cīvaraṁ katvā, 
English (brahmali, published): “Arranging your robe over one shoulder, 

pli-tv-pvr10:1.2
Pali: paggaṇhitvāna añjaliṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Raising your joined palms—

pli-tv-pvr10:1.3
Pali: Āsīsamānarūpova, 
English (brahmali, published): What are you hoping for, 

pli-tv-pvr10:1.4
Pali: kissa tvaṁ idha māgato”. 
English (brahmali, published): That you have come here?” 

pli-tv-pvr10:2.1
Pali: “Dvīsu vinayesu ye paññattā, 
English (brahmali, published): “What has been laid down in the two Monastic Laws, 

pli-tv-pvr10:2.2
Pali: Uddesaṁ āgacchanti uposathesu; 
English (brahmali, published): Which come up for recitation on the observance days—

pli-tv-pvr10:2.3
Pali: Kati te sikkhāpadā honti, 
English (brahmali, published): How many training rules do they have? 

pli-tv-pvr10:2.4
Pali: Katisu nagaresu paññattā”. 
English (brahmali, published): In how many towns were they laid down?” 

pli-tv-pvr10:3.1
Pali: “Bhaddako te ummaṅgo, 
English (brahmali, published): “Your approach is excellent, 

pli-tv-pvr10:3.2
Pali: yoniso paripucchasi; 
English (brahmali, published): You question sensibly; 

pli-tv-pvr10:3.3
Pali: Taggha te ahamakkhissaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): And so I will tell you, 

pli-tv-pvr10:3.4
Pali: yathāsi kusalo tathā”. 
English (brahmali, published): According to your skill. 

pli-tv-pvr10:4.1
Pali: “Dvīsu vinayesu ye paññattā, 
English (brahmali, published): What has been laid down in the two Monastic Laws, 

pli-tv-pvr10:4.2
Pali: Uddesaṁ āgacchanti uposathesu; 
English (brahmali, published): Which come up for recitation on the observance days—

pli-tv-pvr10:4.3
Pali: Aḍḍhuḍḍhasatāni te honti, 
English (brahmali, published): They are three hundred and fifty, 

pli-tv-pvr10:4.4
Pali: Sattasu nagaresu paññattā”. 
English (brahmali, published): Laid down in seven towns.” 

pli-tv-pvr10:5.1
Pali: “Katamesu sattasu nagaresu paññattā, 
English (brahmali, published): “In which seven towns were they laid down? 

pli-tv-pvr10:5.2
Pali: Iṅgha me tvaṁ byākara naṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Please tell me this; 

pli-tv-pvr10:5.3
Pali: Taṁ vacanapathaṁ nisāmayitvā, 
English (brahmali, published): And after attending carefully to your explanation, 

pli-tv-pvr10:5.4
Pali: Paṭipajjema hitāya no siyā”. 
English (brahmali, published): We will practice for our own benefit.” 

pli-tv-pvr10:6.1
Pali: “Vesāliyaṁ rājagahe, 
English (brahmali, published): “They were laid down at Vesālī, at Rājagaha, 

pli-tv-pvr10:6.2
Pali: sāvatthiyañca āḷaviyaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): At Sāvatthī, at Āḷavī; 

pli-tv-pvr10:6.3
Pali: Kosambiyañca sakkesu, 
English (brahmali, published): And at Kosambī, and in the Sakyan country, 

pli-tv-pvr10:6.4
Pali: bhaggesu ceva paññattā”. 
English (brahmali, published): And also among the Bhaggas.” 

pli-tv-pvr10:7.1
Pali: “Kati vesāliyaṁ paññattā, 
English (brahmali, published): “How many were laid down at Vesālī? 

pli-tv-pvr10:7.2
Pali: kati rājagahe katā; 
English (brahmali, published): How many were pronounced at Rājagaha? 

pli-tv-pvr10:7.3
Pali: Sāvatthiyaṁ kati honti, 
English (brahmali, published): How many were there at Sāvatthī?

pli-tv-pvr10:27.0
Pali: 2. Catuvipatti 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The four kinds of failure 

pli-tv-pvr10:27.1
Pali: “Yaṁ taṁ pucchimha akittayi no, 
English (brahmali, published): “What we have asked, you have answered, 

pli-tv-pvr10:27.2
Pali: Taṁ taṁ byākataṁ anaññathā; 
English (brahmali, published): All is explained, not otherwise; 

pli-tv-pvr10:27.3
Pali: Aññaṁ taṁ pucchāmi tadiṅgha brūhi, 
English (brahmali, published): I wish to ask you another question—please tell me this: 

pli-tv-pvr10:27.4
Pali: Garuka lahukañcāpi sāvasesaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Serious, and light, curable; 

pli-tv-pvr10:27.5
Pali: Anavasesaṁ duṭṭhullañca aduṭṭhullaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Incurable, and grave, minor, 

pli-tv-pvr10:27.6
Pali: Ye ca yāvatatiyakā. 
English (brahmali, published): And those that are ‘after the third’. 

pli-tv-pvr10:28.1
Pali: Sādhāraṇaṁ asādhāraṇaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): In common, not in common, 

pli-tv-pvr10:28.2
Pali: Vibhattiyo ca yehi samathehi sammanti; 
English (brahmali, published): The ways that failures are settled—

pli-tv-pvr10:28.3
Pali: Sabbānipetāni viyākarohi, 
English (brahmali, published): Please explain all these too, 

pli-tv-pvr10:28.4
Pali: Handa vākyaṁ suṇoma te”. 
English (brahmali, published): And we will listen to you.” 

pli-tv-pvr10:29.1
Pali: “Ekatiṁsā ye garukā, 
English (brahmali, published): “There are thirty-one serious ones, 

pli-tv-pvr10:29.2
Pali: Aṭṭhettha anavasesā; 
English (brahmali, published): And eight here are incurable; 

pli-tv-pvr10:29.3
Pali: Ye garukā te duṭṭhullā, 
English (brahmali, published): Those that are serious are grave, 

pli-tv-pvr10:29.4
Pali: Ye duṭṭhullā sā sīlavipatti; 
English (brahmali, published): Those that are grave are failures in morality; 

pli-tv-pvr10:29.5
Pali: Pārājikaṁ saṅghādiseso, 
English (brahmali, published): Offenses entailing expulsion, offenses entailing suspension—

pli-tv-pvr10:29.6
Pali: ‘Sīlavipattī’ti vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): They are called ‘failure in morality’. 

pli-tv-pvr10:30.1
Pali: Thullaccayaṁ pācittiyā, 
English (brahmali, published): Serious offenses, offenses entailing confession, 

pli-tv-pvr10:30.2
Pali: Pāṭidesanīyaṁ dukkaṭaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Offenses entailing acknowledgment, offenses of wrong conduct; 

pli-tv-pvr10:30.3
Pali: Dubbhāsitaṁ yo cāyaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Offenses of wrong speech, 

pli-tv-pvr10:30.4
Pali: Akkosati hasādhippāyo; 
English (brahmali, published): And whoever calls another names for fun—

pli-tv-pvr10:30.5
Pali: Ayaṁ sā ācāravipattisammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): This is considered failure in conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr10:31.1
Pali: Viparītadiṭṭhiṁ gaṇhanti, 
English (brahmali, published): Holding on to distorted views, 

pli-tv-pvr10:31.2
Pali: Asaddhammehi purakkhatā; 
English (brahmali, published): Preferring what is contrary to the true Dhamma; 

pli-tv-pvr10:31.3
Pali: Abbhācikkhanti sambuddhaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Misrepresenting the Awakened One, 

pli-tv-pvr10:31.4
Pali: Duppaññā mohapārutā; 
English (brahmali, published): Being foolish, enveloped in delusion—

pli-tv-pvr10:31.5
Pali: Ayaṁ sā diṭṭhivipattisammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): This is considered failure in view. 

pli-tv-pvr10:32.1
Pali: Ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati, ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā sañcarittaṁ samāpajjati, ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā ‘yo te vihāre vasati, so bhikkhu arahā’ti bhaṇati, ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā bhikkhu paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā bhikkhunī paṇītabhojanāni attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati, ājīvahetu ājīvakāraṇā sūpaṁ vā odanaṁ vā agilāno attano atthāya viññāpetvā bhuñjati. 
English (brahmali, published): When, to make a living—having bad desires, overcome by desire—one claims to have a non-existent superhuman quality; when, to make a living, one acts as a matchmaker; when, to make a living, one says, ‘The monk who stays in your dwelling is a perfected one;’ when, to make a living, a monk eats fine foods that he has requested for himself; when, to make a living, a nun eats fine foods that she herself has asked for; when, to make a living, one eats bean curry or rice that one has requested for oneself—

pli-tv-pvr10:32.2
Pali: Ayaṁ sā ājīvavipatti sammatā. 
English (brahmali, published): this is considered failure in livelihood. 

pli-tv-pvr10:33.1
Pali: Ekādasa yāvatatiyakā, 
English (brahmali, published): There are eleven ‘after the thirds’, 

pli-tv-pvr10:33.2
Pali: Te suṇohi yathātathaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Listen to them as they truly are: 

pli-tv-pvr10:33.3
Pali: Ukkhittānuvattikā, 
English (brahmali, published): A nun who takes sides with one who has been ejected, 

pli-tv-pvr10:33.4
Pali: Aṭṭha yāvatatiyakā; 
English (brahmali, published): Eight ‘after the thirds’; 

pli-tv-pvr10:33.5
Pali: Ariṭṭho caṇḍakāḷī ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Ariṭṭha, and Caṇḍakālī—

pli-tv-pvr10:33.6
Pali: Ime te yāvatatiyakā”. 
English (brahmali, published): These are those ‘after the thirds’.”

pli-tv-pvr10:37.2
Pali: buddhenādiccabandhunā”.

English (brahmali, published): Listen to it as it truly is:

English (brahmali, published): This is why it is called that.

pli-tv-pvr10:73.1

pli-tv-pvr10:75.3

English (brahmali, published): They declare to be wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr10:75.4
Pali: tenetaṁ iti vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): This is why it is called that. 

pli-tv-pvr10:76.1
Pali: “Dubbhāsitan”ti yaṁ vuttaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): It is said, ‘An offense of wrong speech’. 

pli-tv-pvr10:76.2
Pali: taṁ suṇohi yathātathaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Listen to it as it truly is: 

pli-tv-pvr10:76.3
Pali: Dubbhāsitaṁ durābhaṭṭhaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Wrong speech, wrong utterance, 

pli-tv-pvr10:76.4
Pali: saṅkiliṭṭhañca yaṁ padaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Whatever sentence is defiled; 

pli-tv-pvr10:76.5
Pali: Yañca viññū garahanti, 
English (brahmali, published): And condemned by the wise—

pli-tv-pvr10:76.6
Pali: tenetaṁ iti vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): This is why it is called that. 

pli-tv-pvr10:77.1
Pali: “Sekhiyan”ti yaṁ vuttaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): It is said, ‘Of training’. 

pli-tv-pvr10:77.2
Pali: taṁ suṇohi yathātathaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): Listen to it as it truly is: 

pli-tv-pvr10:77.3
Pali: Sekkhassa sikkhamānassa, 
English (brahmali, published): For a trainee who is training, 

pli-tv-pvr10:77.4
Pali: ujumaggānusārino. 
English (brahmali, published): Following the straight path, 

pli-tv-pvr10:78.1
Pali: Ādi cetaṁ caraṇañca, 
English (brahmali, published): This is the beginning and the right conduct, 

pli-tv-pvr10:78.2
Pali: mukhaṁ saññamasaṁvaro; 
English (brahmali, published): With a controlled and restrained mouth—

pli-tv-pvr10:78.3
Pali: Sikkhā etādisī natthi, 
English (brahmali, published): There is no training like this. 

pli-tv-pvr10:78.4
Pali: tenetaṁ iti vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): This is why it is called that. 

pli-tv-pvr10:79.1
Pali: Channamativassati, 
English (brahmali, published): It rains on what’s concealed, 

pli-tv-pvr10:79.2
Pali: vivaṭaṁ nātivassati; 
English (brahmali, published): Not on what’s revealed; 

pli-tv-pvr10:79.3
Pali: Tasmā channaṁ vivaretha, 
English (brahmali, published): Therefore, reveal the concealed, 

pli-tv-pvr10:79.4
Pali: evaṁ taṁ nātivassati. 
English (brahmali, published): And it won’t be rained upon. 

pli-tv-pvr10:80.1
Pali: Gati migānaṁ pavanaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): The forest is the destination of deer, 

pli-tv-pvr10:80.2
Pali: Ākāso pakkhinaṁ gati; 
English (brahmali, published): The air is the destination of birds; 

pli-tv-pvr10:80.3
Pali: Vibhavo gati dhammānaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Non-existence is the destination of phenomena, 

pli-tv-pvr10:80.4
Pali: Nibbānaṁ arahato gatīti. 
English (brahmali, published): Extinguishment is the destination of a Perfected One.” 

pli-tv-pvr10:81.1
Pali: Gāthāsaṅgaṇikaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The verses on the training rules are finished. 

pli-tv-pvr10:82.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr10:83.1
Pali: Sattanagaresu paññattā, 
English (brahmali, published): “Laid down in seven towns, 

pli-tv-pvr10:83.2
Pali: vipatti caturopi ca; 
English (brahmali, published): And also the four kinds of failure; 

pli-tv-pvr10:83.3
Pali: Bhikkhūnaṁ bhikkhunīnañca, 
English (brahmali, published): In common, not in common, 

pli-tv-pvr10:83.4
Pali: sādhāraṇā asādhāraṇā; 
English (brahmali, published): Between the monks and the nuns; 

pli-tv-pvr10:83.5
Pali: Sāsanaṁ anuggahāya, 
English (brahmali, published): To support Buddhism, 

pli-tv-pvr10:83.6
Pali: gāthāsaṅgaṇikaṁ idanti. 
English (brahmali, published): There are these verses on the training rules.” 

pli-tv-pvr10:84.1
Pali: Gāthāsaṅgaṇikaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The verses on the training rules are finished.
