pli-tv-pvr13:5.1
Pali: Codanāya ko ādi, kiṁ majjhe, kiṁ pariyosānaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): What is the beginning, the middle, and the end of an accusation? 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.2
Pali: Codanāya okāsakammaṁ ādi, kiriyā majjhe, samatho pariyosānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Getting permission for the accusation is the beginning, doing it is the middle, settling it is the end. 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.3
Pali: Codanāya kati mūlāni, kati vatthūni, kati bhūmiyo, katihākārehi codeti? 
English (brahmali, published): How many roots does accusing have, how many bases, and how many grounds? And in how many ways does one accuse? 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.4
Pali: Codanāya dve mūlāni, tīṇi vatthūni, pañca bhūmiyo, dvīhākārehi codeti. 
English (brahmali, published): It has two roots, three bases, and five grounds. And one accuses in two ways. 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.5
Pali: Codanāya katamāni dve mūlāni? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the two roots? 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.6
Pali: Samūlikā vā amūlikā vā—
English (brahmali, published): With a root and without a root. 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.7
Pali: codanāya imāni dve mūlāni. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.8
Pali: Codanāya katamāni tīṇi vatthūni? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the three bases? 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.9
Pali: Diṭṭhena sutena parisaṅkāya—
English (brahmali, published): The seen, the heard, and the suspected. 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.10
Pali: codanāya imāni tīṇi vatthūni. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.11
Pali: Codanāya katamā pañca bhūmiyo? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the five grounds? 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.12
Pali: Kālena vakkhāmi no akālena, bhūtena vakkhāmi no abhūtena, saṇhena vakkhāmi no pharusena, atthasaṁhitena vakkhāmi no anatthasaṁhitena, mettācitto vakkhāmi no dosantaroti—
English (brahmali, published): “I’ll speak at an appropriate time, not at an inappropriate one; I’ll speak the truth, not falsehood; I’ll speak gently, not harshly; I’ll speak what’s beneficial, not what’s unbeneficial; I’ll speak with a mind of good will, not with ill will.” 

pli-tv-pvr13:5.13
Pali: codanāya imā pañca bhūmiyo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr13:6.1
Pali: Katamehi dvīhākārehi codeti? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the two ways of accusing? 

pli-tv-pvr13:6.2
Pali: Kāyena vā codeti vācāya vā codeti—
English (brahmali, published): One accuses by body or by speech. 

pli-tv-pvr13:6.3
Pali: imehi dvīhākārehi codeti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.0
Pali: 2. Codakādipaṭipatti 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The proceeding of an accuser, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.1
Pali: Codakena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): How should the accuser proceed? 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.2
Pali: Cuditakena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): How should the accused proceed? 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.3
Pali: Saṅghena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): How should the Sangha proceed? 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.4
Pali: Anuvijjakena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): How should the investigator proceed? 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.5
Pali: Codakena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How should the accuser proceed?” 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.6
Pali: Codakena pañcasu dhammesu patiṭṭhāya paro codetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): The accuser should set up five qualities before accusing another: 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.7
Pali: Kālena vakkhāmi no akālena, bhūtena vakkhāmi no abhūtena, saṇhena vakkhāmi no pharusena, atthasaṁhitena vakkhāmi no anatthasaṁhitena, mettācitto vakkhāmi no dosantaroti—
English (brahmali, published): “I’ll speak at an appropriate time, not at an inappropriate one; I’ll speak the truth, not falsehood; I’ll speak gently, not harshly; I’ll speak what’s beneficial, not what’s unbeneficial; I’ll speak with a mind of good will, not with ill will.” 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.8
Pali: codakena evaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.9
Pali: Cuditakena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How should the accused proceed?” 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.10
Pali: Cuditakena dvīsu dhammesu paṭipajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The accused should set up two qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.11
Pali: Sacce ca akuppe ca—
English (brahmali, published): truth and composure. 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.12
Pali: cuditakena evaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.13
Pali: Saṅghena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How should the Sangha proceed?” 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.14
Pali: Saṅghena otiṇṇānotiṇṇaṁ jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha should know what has been brought before it and what has not. 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.15
Pali: Saṅghena evaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.16
Pali: Anuvijjakena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbanti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How should the investigator proceed?” 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.17
Pali: Anuvijjakena yena dhammena yena vinayena yena satthusāsanena taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati tathā taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The investigator resolves that legal issue in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. 

pli-tv-pvr13:7.18
Pali: Anuvijjakena evaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr13:8.1
Pali: Uposatho kimatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): What is the purpose of the observance-day ceremony? 

pli-tv-pvr13:8.2
Pali: Pavāraṇā kissa kāraṇā; 
English (brahmali, published): Why is there an invitation ceremony? 

pli-tv-pvr13:8.3
Pali: Parivāso kimatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): What is the purpose of probation? 

pli-tv-pvr13:8.4
Pali: Mūlāyapaṭikassanā kissa kāraṇā; 
English (brahmali, published): Why is there a sending back to the beginning? 

pli-tv-pvr13:8.5
Pali: Mānattaṁ kimatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): What is the purpose of the trial period? 

pli-tv-pvr13:8.6
Pali: Abbhānaṁ kissa kāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): Why is there rehabilitation? 

pli-tv-pvr13:9.1
Pali: Uposatho sāmaggatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): The purpose of the observance-day ceremony is unity. 

pli-tv-pvr13:9.2
Pali: Visuddhatthāya pavāraṇā; 
English (brahmali, published): The purpose of the invitation ceremony is purity. 

pli-tv-pvr13:9.3
Pali: Parivāso mānattatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): The purpose of probation is the trial period. 

pli-tv-pvr13:9.4
Pali: Mūlāyapaṭikassanā niggahatthāya; 
English (brahmali, published): The purpose of sending back to the beginning is restraint. 

pli-tv-pvr13:9.5
Pali: Mānattaṁ abbhānatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): The purpose of the trial period is rehabilitation. 

pli-tv-pvr13:9.6
Pali: Visuddhatthāya abbhānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The purpose of rehabilitation is purity.

English (brahmali, published): Being stupid, goes to hell,

English (brahmali, published): Giving up both of these, 

pli-tv-pvr13:11.4

pli-tv-pvr14:0.1
Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr14:0.2
Pali: Cūḷasaṅgāma 
English (brahmali, published): The short chapter on the resolution of conflicts 

pli-tv-pvr14:0.3
Pali: 14. Anuvijjakassapaṭipatti 
English (brahmali, published): The procedure for an investigator 

pli-tv-pvr14:1.1
Pali: Saṅgāmāvacarena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamantena: 
English (brahmali, published): “When a monk who is involved in a conflict is about to approach the Sangha, he should: 

pli-tv-pvr14:1.2
Pali: nīcacittena saṅgho upasaṅkamitabbo; 
English (brahmali, published): be humble; 

pli-tv-pvr14:1.3
Pali: rajoharaṇasamena cittena; 
English (brahmali, published): be intent on removing defilements; 

pli-tv-pvr14:1.4
Pali: āsanakusalena bhavitabbaṁ nisajjakusalena; there bhikkhū anupakhajjantena, nave bhikkhū āsanena appaṭibāhantena, yathāpatirūpe āsane nisīditabbaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): be skilled in appropriate seating and where to sit down, taking a seat without encroaching on the senior monks and without blocking the junior monks; 

pli-tv-pvr14:1.5
Pali: anānākathikena bhavitabbaṁ atiracchānakathikena; sāmaṁ vā dhammo bhāsitabbo paro vā ajjhesitabbo ariyo vā tuṇhībhāvo nātimaññitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): not ramble or engage in worldly talk, but speak according to the Teaching or invite others to speak or value noble silence. 

pli-tv-pvr14:2.1
Pali: Saṅghena anumatena puggalena anuvijjakena anuvijjitukāmena na upajjhāyo pucchitabbo, na ācariyo pucchitabbo, na saddhivihāriko pucchitabbo, na antevāsiko pucchitabbo, na samānupajjhāyako pucchitabbo, na samānācariyako pucchitabbo, na jāti pucchitabbā, na nāmaṁ pucchitabbaṁ, na gottaṁ pucchitabbaṁ, na āgamo pucchitabbo, na kulapadeso pucchitabbo, na jātibhūmi pucchitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): An investigator—who has been approved by the Sangha and who wishes to investigate—should not ask about preceptor, teacher, student, pupil, co-student, co-pupil, caste, name, family, reciter tradition, home address, or nationality. 

pli-tv-pvr14:2.2
Pali: Taṁ kiṁ kāraṇā? 
English (brahmali, published): What is the reason for that? 

pli-tv-pvr14:2.3
Pali: Atrassa pemaṁ vā doso vā. 
English (brahmali, published): In these cases there is affection or ill will. 

pli-tv-pvr14:2.4
Pali: Peme vā sati dose vā, chandāpi gaccheyya, dosāpi gaccheyya, mohāpi gaccheyya, bhayāpi gaccheyya. 
English (brahmali, published): When there is affection or ill will, one might be biased by favoritism, ill will, confusion, or fear. 

pli-tv-pvr14:3.1
Pali: Saṅghena anumatena puggalena anuvijjakena anuvijjitukāmena saṅghagarukena bhavitabbaṁ no puggalagarukena, saddhammagarukena bhavitabbaṁ no āmisagarukena, atthavasikena bhavitabbaṁ no parisakappikena, kālena anuvijjitabbaṁ no akālena, bhūtena anuvijjitabbaṁ no abhūtena, saṇhena anuvijjitabbaṁ no pharusena, atthasaṁhitena anuvijjitabbaṁ no anatthasaṁhitena, mettācittena anuvijjitabbaṁ no dosantarena, na upakaṇṇakajappinā bhavitabbaṁ, na jimhaṁ pekkhitabbaṁ, na akkhi nikhaṇitabbaṁ, na bhamukaṁ ukkhipitabbaṁ, na sīsaṁ ukkhipitabbaṁ, na hatthavikāro kātabbo, na hatthamuddā dassetabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): An investigator—who has been approved by the Sangha and who wishes to investigate—should respect the Sangha, not individuals; should value the true Dhamma, not worldly things; should value the goal, not conforming to the gathering; should investigate at an appropriate time, not at an inappropriate one; should investigate truthfully, not falsely; should investigate gently, not harshly; should investigate beneficially, not unbeneficially; should investigate with a mind of good will, not with ill will; should not whisper in the ear; should not look for flaws; should not wink; should not raise an eyebrow; should not raise the head; should not signal with the hand; should not gesture with the hand. 

pli-tv-pvr14:4.1
Pali: Āsanakusalena bhavitabbaṁ nisajjakusalena, yugamattaṁ pekkhantena atthaṁ anuvidhiyantena sake āsane nisīditabbaṁ, na ca āsanā vuṭṭhātabbaṁ, na vītihātabbaṁ, na kummaggo sevitabbo, na bāhāvikkhepakaṁ bhaṇitabbaṁ, aturitena bhavitabbaṁ asāhasikena, acaṇḍikatena bhavitabbaṁ vacanakkhamena, mettācittena bhavitabbaṁ hitānukampinā, kāruṇikena bhavitabbaṁ hitaparisakkinā, asamphappalāpinā bhavitabbaṁ pariyantabhāṇinā, averavasikena bhavitabbaṁ anasuruttena, attā pariggahetabbo, paro pariggahetabbo, codako pariggahetabbo, cuditako pariggahetabbo, adhammacodako pariggahetabbo, adhammacuditako pariggahetabbo, dhammacodako pariggahetabbo, dhammacuditako pariggahetabbo, vuttaṁ ahāpentena avuttaṁ apakāsentena otiṇṇāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ pariggahetvā paro paṭipucchitvā yathā paṭiññāya kāretabbo, mando hāsetabbo, bhīrū assāsetabbo, caṇḍo nisedhetabbo, asuci vibhāvetabbo, ujumaddavena na chandāgatiṁ gantabbaṁ, na dosāgatiṁ gantabbaṁ, na mohāgatiṁ gantabbaṁ, na bhayāgatiṁ gantabbaṁ, majjhattena bhavitabbaṁ dhammesu ca puggalesu ca. 
English (brahmali, published): He should be skilled in appropriate seating and where to sit down. Looking a plow’s length ahead, acting in line with his aim, he should sit down on his own seat. He should not get up from his seat, bungle the investigation, take a wrong path, or gesticulate. He should proceed without haste or force, not be fierce, and be patient with others’ speech. He should have mind of loving kindness with compassion and empathy, and strive for what is beneficial. He should not speak idly but to the point, without being angry or argumentative. He should assess himself, the others, the accuser, the accused, one who accuses illegitimately, one who is accused illegitimately, one who accuses legitimately, and one who is accused legitimately. Not omitting what has been said, nor announcing what has not been said, he should carefully scrutinize the sentences and words under consideration, question the others, and deal with them according to what they have admitted. He should gladden those who are confused, comfort those who are frightened, restrain those who are fierce, and expose those who are impure. Being upright and gentle, he should not be biased by favoritism, anger, confusion, or fear. He should be impartial in regard to the Teaching and the people involved. 

pli-tv-pvr14:4.2
Pali: Evañca pana anuvijjako anuvijjamāno satthu ceva sāsanakaro hoti, viññūnañca sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way an investigator is acting in accordance with the instruction of the Teacher. And they are dear, agreeable, respected, and esteemed by their discerning fellow monastics. 

pli-tv-pvr14:5.1
Pali: Suttaṁ saṁsandanatthāya, opammaṁ nidassanatthāya, attho viññāpanatthāya, paṭipucchā ṭhapanatthāya, okāsakammaṁ codanatthāya, codanā sāraṇatthāya, sāraṇā savacanīyatthāya, savacanīyaṁ palibodhatthāya, palibodho vinicchayatthāya, vinicchayo santīraṇatthāya, santīraṇaṁ ṭhānāṭhānagamanatthāya, ṭhānāṭhānagamanaṁ dummaṅkūnaṁ puggalānaṁ niggahatthāya, pesalānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ sampaggahatthāya, saṅgho sampariggahasampaṭicchanatthāya, saṅghena anumatā puggalā paccekaṭṭhāyino avisaṁvādakaṭṭhāyino. 
English (brahmali, published): The Monastic Code is for the sake of concluding, a simile for the sake of illustration, the goal is to be made known, and questioning is for sake of establishing. Getting permission is for the sake of accusing, accusing for reminding, reminding for directing, directing for obstructing, obstructing for investigating, investigating for weighing up, weighing up for deciding what is and is not the case, and deciding what is and is not the case is for the sake of restraining bad people and to support the good monks. The Sangha has the purpose of examining and accepting the decision. The Sangha should appoint people who are trustworthy to positions of authority. 

pli-tv-pvr14:6.1
Pali: Vinayo saṁvaratthāya, saṁvaro avippaṭisāratthāya, avippaṭisāro pāmujjatthāya, pāmujjaṁ pītatthāya, pīti passaddhatthāya, passaddhi sukhatthāya, sukhaṁ samādhatthāya, samādhi yathābhūtañāṇadassanatthāya, yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ nibbidatthāya, nibbidā virāgatthāya, virāgo vimuttatthāya, vimutti vimuttiñāṇadassanatthāya, vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ anupādāparinibbānatthāya. 
English (brahmali, published): The Monastic Law is for the sake of restraint, restraint for non-regret, non-regret for joy, joy for rapture, rapture for tranquility, tranquility for bliss, bliss for stillness, stillness for seeing things according to reality, seeing things according to reality for repulsion, repulsion for dispassion, dispassion for liberation, liberation for knowledge and vision of liberation, and knowledge and vision of liberation is for the sake of extinguishment without grasping. 

pli-tv-pvr14:6.2
Pali: Etadatthā kathā, etadatthā mantanā, etadatthā upanisā, etadatthaṁ sotāvadhānaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): This is the purpose of discussion, this is the purpose of consultation, this is the purpose of vital conditions, this is the purpose of listening, 

pli-tv-pvr14:6.3
Pali: yadidaṁ anupādācittassa vimokkhoti. 
English (brahmali, published): that is, the release of mind without grasping.” 

pli-tv-pvr14:7.1
Pali: Anuyogavattaṁ nisāmaya, 
English (brahmali, published): “Consider carefully the proper procedure of examination, 

pli-tv-pvr14:7.2
Pali: Kusalena buddhimatā kataṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): As formulated by the skilled Awakened One, 

pli-tv-pvr14:7.3
Pali: Suvuttaṁ sikkhāpadānulomikaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Well-spoken in line with the training rules—

pli-tv-pvr14:7.4
Pali: Gatiṁ na nāsento samparāyikaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Do not ruin your future rebirth. 

pli-tv-pvr14:8.1
Pali: Vatthuṁ vipattiṁ āpattiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Ignorant about basis, failure, and offense, 

pli-tv-pvr14:8.2
Pali: Nidānaṁ ākāraakovido; 
English (brahmali, published): As well as source and ways; 

pli-tv-pvr14:8.3
Pali: Pubbāparaṁ na jānāti, 
English (brahmali, published): Not knowing the right order, 

pli-tv-pvr14:8.4
Pali: Katākataṁ samena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Nor what has and has not been done. 

pli-tv-pvr14:9.1
Pali: Kammañca adhikaraṇañca, 
English (brahmali, published): Ignorant about legal procedures and legal issues, 

pli-tv-pvr14:9.2
Pali: samathe cāpi akovido; 
English (brahmali, published): As well as their settling. 

pli-tv-pvr14:9.3
Pali: Ratto duṭṭho ca mūḷho ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Greedy, angry, and confused, 

pli-tv-pvr14:9.4
Pali: bhayā mohā ca gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): Biased by fear and confusion, 

pli-tv-pvr14:10.1
Pali: Na ca saññattikusalo, 
English (brahmali, published): Not skilled in persuasion, 

pli-tv-pvr14:10.2
Pali: nijjhattiyā ca akovido; 
English (brahmali, published): Nor in making others understand; 

pli-tv-pvr14:10.3
Pali: Laddhapakkho ahiriko, 
English (brahmali, published): A shameless one who has obtained supporters, 

pli-tv-pvr14:10.4
Pali: kaṇhakammo anādaro; 
English (brahmali, published): Disrespectful and doing dark deeds—

pli-tv-pvr14:10.5
Pali: Sa ve tādisako bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): A monk such as this 

pli-tv-pvr14:10.6
Pali: appaṭikkhoti vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): Is called ‘not worthy of attention’. 

pli-tv-pvr14:11.1
Pali: Vatthuṁ vipattiṁ āpattiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Understanding basis, failure, and offense, 

pli-tv-pvr14:11.2
Pali: nidānaṁ ākārakovido; 
English (brahmali, published): As well as source and ways; 

pli-tv-pvr14:11.3
Pali: Pubbāparañca jānāti, 
English (brahmali, published): Knowing the right order, 

pli-tv-pvr14:11.4
Pali: katākataṁ samena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): And also what has and has not been done. 

pli-tv-pvr14:12.1
Pali: Kammañca adhikaraṇañca, 
English (brahmali, published): Understanding legal procedures and legal issues, 

pli-tv-pvr14:12.2
Pali: samathe cāpi kovido; 
English (brahmali, published): As well as their settling. 

pli-tv-pvr14:12.3
Pali: Aratto aduṭṭho amūḷho, 
English (brahmali, published): Not greedy, angry, or confused, 

pli-tv-pvr14:12.4
Pali: bhayā mohā na gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): Biased neither by fear nor confusion, 

pli-tv-pvr14:13.1
Pali: Saññattiyā ca kusalo, 
English (brahmali, published): Skilled in persuasion, 

pli-tv-pvr14:13.2
Pali: nijjhattiyā ca kovido; 
English (brahmali, published): And in making others understand; 

pli-tv-pvr14:13.3
Pali: Laddhapakkho hirimano, 
English (brahmali, published): A conscientious one who has obtained supporters, 

pli-tv-pvr14:13.4
Pali: sukkakammo sagāravo; 
English (brahmali, published): Respectful and doing bright deeds—

pli-tv-pvr14:13.5
Pali: Sa ve tādisako bhikkhu, 
English (brahmali, published): A monk such as this 

pli-tv-pvr14:13.6
Pali: sappaṭikkhoti vuccatīti. 
English (brahmali, published): Is called ‘worthy of attention’.” 

pli-tv-pvr14:14.1
Pali: Cūḷasaṅgāmaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The short chapter on the resolution of conflicts is finished 

pli-tv-pvr14:15.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr14:16.1
Pali: Nīcacittena puccheyya, 
English (brahmali, published): “Humble, one may ask, 

pli-tv-pvr14:16.2
Pali: garu saṅghe na puggale; 
English (brahmali, published): Respect for the Sangha, not individuals; 

pli-tv-pvr14:16.3
Pali: Suttaṁ saṁsandanatthāya, 
English (brahmali, published): The Monastic Code is for the sake of concluding, 

pli-tv-pvr14:16.4
Pali: vinayānuggahena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): And through supporting the training—

pli-tv-pvr14:16.5
Pali: uddānaṁ cūḷasaṅgāme, 
English (brahmali, published): The summary of the short chapter on the resolution of conflicts, 

pli-tv-pvr14:16.6
Pali: ekuddeso idaṁ katanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Made into one recitation.”

pli-tv-pvr15
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-pvr/pli-tv-pvr15_root-pli-ms.json

pli-tv-pvr15:0.1
Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr15:0.2
Pali: 15. Mahāsaṅgāma 
English (brahmali, published): The great chapter on the resolution of conflicts 

pli-tv-pvr15:0.3
Pali: 1. Voharantenajānitabbādi 
English (brahmali, published): 1. What is to be known by one who is speaking, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr15:1.1
Pali: Saṅgāmāvacarena bhikkhunā saṅghe voharantena vatthu jānitabbaṁ, vipatti jānitabbā, āpatti jānitabbā, nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ, ākāro jānitabbo, pubbāparaṁ jānitabbaṁ, katākataṁ jānitabbaṁ, kammaṁ jānitabbaṁ, adhikaraṇaṁ jānitabbaṁ, samatho jānitabbo, na chandāgati gantabbā, na dosāgati gantabbā, na mohāgati gantabbā, na bhayāgati gantabbā, saññāpanīye ṭhāne saññāpetabbaṁ, nijjhāpanīye ṭhāne nijjhāpetabbaṁ, pekkhanīye ṭhāne pekkhitabbaṁ, pasādanīye ṭhāne pasādetabbaṁ, “laddhapakkhomhī”ti parapakkho nāvajānitabbo, bahussutomhīti appassuto nāvajānitabbo, therataromhīti navakataro nāvajānitabbo, asampattaṁ na byāhātabbaṁ, sampattaṁ dhammato vinayato na parihāpetabbaṁ, yena dhammena yena vinayena yena satthusāsanena taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati, tathā taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): When a monk who is involved in a conflict is speaking in the Sangha, he should know the actions that are the bases for offenses, as well as the failures, the offenses, the origin stories, the attributes, the right order, what has and has not been done, the legal procedures, the legal issues, and their settling. He should not be biased by favoritism, ill will, confusion, or fear. He should persuade when persuasion is appropriate, should make others understand when making understand is appropriate, should look on when looking on is appropriate, and should inspire when inspiration is appropriate. Thinking, “I’ve obtained supporters,” he should not despise the supporters of others. Thinking, “I’m learned,” he should not despise those who are ignorant. Thinking, “I’m more senior,” he should not despise those who are junior. He should not speak about what has not been reached, and he should not use the Teaching or the Monastic Law to neglect what has been reached. He should resolve that legal issue in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. 

pli-tv-pvr15:2.1
Pali: Vatthu jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the actions that are the bases for offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr15:2.2
Pali: aṭṭhapārājikānaṁ vatthu jānitabbaṁ, tevīsasaṅghādisesānaṁ vatthu jānitabbaṁ, dveaniyatānaṁ vatthu jānitabbaṁ, dvecattārīsanissaggiyānaṁ vatthu jānitabbaṁ, aṭṭhāsītisatapācittiyānaṁ vatthu jānitabbaṁ, dvādasapāṭidesanīyānaṁ vatthu jānitabbaṁ, dukkaṭānaṁ vatthu jānitabbaṁ, dubbhāsitānaṁ vatthu jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know the actions that are the bases for the eight offenses entailing expulsion, for the twenty-three offenses entailing suspension, for the two undetermined offenses, for the forty-two offenses entailing relinquishment, for the one hundred and eighty-eight offenses entailing confession, for the twelve offenses entailing acknowledgment, for the offenses of wrong conduct, and for the offenses of wrong speech. 

pli-tv-pvr15:3.1
Pali: Vipatti jānitabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the failures: 

pli-tv-pvr15:3.2
Pali: sīlavipatti jānitabbā, ācāravipatti jānitabbā, diṭṭhivipatti jānitabbā, ājīvavipatti jānitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know failure in morality, failure in conduct, failure in view, and failure in livelihood. 

pli-tv-pvr15:4.1
Pali: Āpatti jānitabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the offenses: 

pli-tv-pvr15:4.2
Pali: pārājikāpatti jānitabbā, saṅghādisesāpatti jānitabbā, thullaccayāpatti jānitabbā, pācittiyāpatti jānitabbā, pāṭidesanīyāpatti jānitabbā, dukkaṭāpatti jānitabbā, dubbhāsitāpatti jānitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know the offenses entailing expulsion, the offenses entailing suspension, the serious offenses, the offenses entailing confession, the offenses entailing acknowledgement, the offenses of wrong conduct, and the offenses of wrong speech. 

pli-tv-pvr15:5.1
Pali: Nidānaṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the origin stories: 

pli-tv-pvr15:5.2
Pali: aṭṭhapārājikānaṁ nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ, tevīsasaṅghādisesānaṁ nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ, dveaniyatānaṁ nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ, dvecattārīsanissaggiyānaṁ nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ, aṭṭhāsītisatapācittiyānaṁ nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ, dvādasapāṭidesanīyānaṁ nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ, dukkaṭānaṁ nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ, dubbhāsitānaṁ nidānaṁ jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know the origin stories to the eight offenses entailing expulsion, to the twenty-three offenses entailing suspension, to the two undetermined offenses, to the forty-two offenses entailing relinquishment, to the one hundred and eighty-eight offenses entailing confession, to the twelve offenses entailing acknowledgment, to the offenses of wrong conduct, and to the offenses of wrong speech. 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.1
Pali: Ākāro jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the attributes: 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.2
Pali: saṅgho ākārato jānitabbo, gaṇo ākārato jānitabbo, puggalo ākārato jānitabbo, codako ākārato jānitabbo, cuditako ākārato jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know the attributes of a sangha, of a group, of an individual, of an accuser, of an accused. 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.3
Pali: Saṅgho ākārato jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the attributes of a sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.4
Pali: paṭibalo nu kho ayaṁ saṅgho imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena udāhu noti, evaṁ saṅgho ākārato jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): is this sangha capable of resolving this legal issue according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, or is it not? 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.5
Pali: Gaṇo ākārato jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the attributes of a group: 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.6
Pali: paṭibalo nu kho ayaṁ gaṇo imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena udāhu noti, evaṁ gaṇo ākārato jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): is this group capable of resolving this legal issue according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, or is it not? 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.7
Pali: Puggalo ākārato jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the attributes of an individual: 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.8
Pali: paṭibalo nu kho ayaṁ puggalo imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena udāhu noti, evaṁ puggalo ākārato jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): is this individual capable of resolving this legal issue according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, or is he not? 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.9
Pali: Codako ākārato jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the attributes of an accuser: 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.10
Pali: kacci nu kho ayamāyasmā pañcasu dhammesu patiṭṭhāya paraṁ codeti udāhu noti, evaṁ codako ākārato jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): is this venerable established in the five qualities before accusing another, or is he not? 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.11
Pali: Cuditako ākārato jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the attributes of an accused: 

pli-tv-pvr15:6.12
Pali: kacci nu kho ayamāyasmā dvīsu dhammesu patiṭṭhito sacce ca akuppe ca udāhu noti, evaṁ cuditako ākārato jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): is this venerable established in the two qualities of truth and composure, or is he not? 

pli-tv-pvr15:7.1
Pali: Pubbāparaṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the right order: 

pli-tv-pvr15:7.2
Pali: kacci nu kho ayamāyasmā vatthuto vā vatthuṁ saṅkamati, vipattito vā vipattiṁ saṅkamati, āpattito vā āpattiṁ saṅkamati, avajānitvā vā paṭijānāti, paṭijānitvā vā avajānāti, aññena vā aññaṁ paṭicarati, udāhu noti, evaṁ pubbāparaṁ jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): does this venerable go from one action that is the basis for an offense to another action, from one failure to another, from one offense to another? Does he assert things after denying them, deny things after asserting them, or evade the issue? Or does he not? 

pli-tv-pvr15:8.1
Pali: Katākataṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know what has and has not been done: 

pli-tv-pvr15:8.2
Pali: methunadhammo jānitabbo, methunadhammassa anulomaṁ jānitabbaṁ, methunadhammassa pubbabhāgo jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know sexual intercourse, he should know what amounts to sexual intercourse, he should know the preliminaries of sexual intercourse. 

pli-tv-pvr15:8.3
Pali: Methunadhammo jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know sexual intercourse: 

pli-tv-pvr15:8.4
Pali: dvayaṁdvayasamāpatti jānitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know that which is done wherever there are couples. 

pli-tv-pvr15:8.5
Pali: Methunadhammassa anulomaṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know what amounts to sexual intercourse: 

pli-tv-pvr15:8.6
Pali: bhikkhu attano mukhena parassa aṅgajātaṁ gaṇhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): a monk taking the genitals of another in his mouth. 

pli-tv-pvr15:8.7
Pali: Methunadhammassa pubbabhāgo jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the preliminaries to sexual intercourse: 

pli-tv-pvr15:8.8
Pali: vaṇṇāvaṇṇo, kāyasaṁsaggo, duṭṭhullavācā, attakāmapāricariyā, vacanamanuppadānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): various colors, physical contact, indecent speech, satisfying one’s own desires, encouraging through speech. 

pli-tv-pvr15:9.1
Pali: Kammaṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr15:9.2
Pali: soḷasakammāni jānitabbāni—
English (brahmali, published): he should know the sixteen legal procedures: 

pli-tv-pvr15:9.3
Pali: cattāri apalokanakammāni jānitabbāni, cattāri ñattikammāni jānitabbāni, cattāri ñattidutiyakammāni jānitabbāni, cattāri ñatticatutthakammāni jānitabbāni. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know the four kinds of legal procedures consisting of getting permission, the four kinds of legal procedures consisting of one motion, the four kinds of legal procedures consisting one motion and one announcement, and the four kinds of legal procedures consisting of one motion and three announcements. 

pli-tv-pvr15:10.1
Pali: Adhikaraṇaṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know the legal issues: 

pli-tv-pvr15:10.2
Pali: cattāri adhikaraṇāni jānitabbāni—
English (brahmali, published): he should know the four kinds of legal issues: 

pli-tv-pvr15:10.3
Pali: vivādādhikaraṇaṁ jānitabbaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ jānitabbaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ jānitabbaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know the legal issues arising from disputes, the legal issues arising from accusations, the legal issues arising from offenses, and the legal issues arising from business. 

pli-tv-pvr15:11.1
Pali: Samatho jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): He should know settling: 

pli-tv-pvr15:11.2
Pali: satta samathā jānitabbā—
English (brahmali, published): he should know the seven principles for settling legal issues: 

pli-tv-pvr15:11.3
Pali: sammukhāvinayo jānitabbo, sativinayo jānitabbo, amūḷhavinayo jānitabbo, paṭiññātakaraṇaṁ jānitabbaṁ, yebhuyyasikā jānitabbā, tassapāpiyasikā jānitabbā, tiṇavatthārako jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): he should know resolution face-to-face, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, acting according to what has been admitted, majority decision, further penalty, and covering over as if with grass. 

pli-tv-pvr15:12.0
Pali: 2. Agatiagantabba 
English (brahmali, published): 2. Not to be biased 

pli-tv-pvr15:12.1
Pali: Na chandāgati gantabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He should not be biased by favoritism: 

pli-tv-pvr15:12.2
Pali: chandāgatiṁ gacchanto kathaṁ chandāgatiṁ gacchati? 
English (brahmali, published): How is one biased by favoritism? 

pli-tv-pvr15:12.3
Pali: Idhekacco—“ayaṁ me upajjhāyo vā ācariyo vā saddhivihāriko vā antevāsiko vā samānupajjhāyako vā samānācariyako vā sandiṭṭho vā sambhatto vā ñātisālohito vā”ti, tassānukampāya tassānurakkhāya adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti, avinayaṁ vinayoti dīpeti, vinayaṁ avinayoti dīpeti, abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatena bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatena abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, apaññattaṁ tathāgatena paññattaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, paññattaṁ tathāgatena apaññattaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, anāpattiṁ āpattīti dīpeti, āpattiṁ anāpattīti dīpeti, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ garukā āpattīti dīpeti, garukaṁ āpattiṁ lahukā āpattīti dīpeti, sāvasesaṁ āpattiṁ anavasesā āpattīti dīpeti, anavasesaṁ āpattiṁ sāvasesā āpattīti dīpeti, duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that someone thinks, “This is my preceptor, teacher, student, pupil, co-student, co-pupil, friend, companion, or relative.” To be compassionate toward and protect this person, he proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it and what is in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it. He proclaims what is contrary to the Monastic Law as being in accordance with it, and what is in accordance with the Monastic Law as contrary to it. He proclaims what hasn’t been spoken by the Buddha as spoken by him, and what has been spoken by the Buddha as not spoken by him. He proclaims what was not practiced by the Buddha as practiced by him, and what was practiced by the Buddha as not practiced by him. He proclaims what was not laid down by the Buddha as laid down by him, and what was laid down by the Buddha as not laid down by him. He proclaims a non-offense as an offense, and an offense as a non-offense. He proclaims a light offense as heavy, and a heavy offense as light. He proclaims a curable offense as incurable, and an incurable offense as curable. He proclaims a grave offense as minor, and a minor offense as grave. 

pli-tv-pvr15:12.4
Pali: Imehi aṭṭhārasahi vatthūhi chandāgatiṁ gacchanto bahujanāhitāya paṭipanno hoti bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is biased by favoritism by way of these eighteen grounds, then his behavior is unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; it is harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans. 

pli-tv-pvr15:12.5
Pali: Imehi aṭṭhārasahi vatthūhi chandāgatiṁ gacchanto khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is biased by favoritism by way of these eighteen grounds, then he is damaged and impaired, blamed and criticized by sensible people, and makes much demerit. 

pli-tv-pvr15:12.6
Pali: Chandāgatiṁ gacchanto evaṁ chandāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:13.1
Pali: Na dosāgati gantabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He should not be biased by ill will: 

pli-tv-pvr15:13.2
Pali: dosāgatiṁ gacchanto kathaṁ dosāgatiṁ gacchati? 
English (brahmali, published): How is one biased by ill will? 

pli-tv-pvr15:13.3
Pali: Idhekacco anatthaṁ me acarīti āghātaṁ bandhati, anatthaṁ me caratīti āghātaṁ bandhati, anatthaṁ me carissatīti āghātaṁ bandhati, piyassa me manāpassa anatthaṁ acari … anatthaṁ carati … anatthaṁ carissatīti āghātaṁ bandhati, appiyassa me amanāpassa atthaṁ acari … atthaṁ carati … atthaṁ carissatīti āghātaṁ bandhati. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that someone thinks, “They’ve harmed me,” and he feels resentful. Or he thinks, “They’re harming me,” and he feels resentful. Or he thinks, “They’ll harm me,” and he feels resentful. Or he thinks, “They’ve harmed someone who’s dear to me” … “They’re harming someone who’s dear to me” … “They’ll harm someone who’s dear to me” … “They’ve benefited someone I dislike” … “They’re benefiting someone I dislike” … “They’ll benefit someone I dislike,” and he feels resentful. 

pli-tv-pvr15:13.4
Pali: Imehi navahi āghātavatthūhi āghāto paṭighāto kuddho kodhābhibhūto adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Because of these nine grounds for resentment, he is resentful, hostile, angry, and overcome by anger, and then proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it and what is in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it … 

pli-tv-pvr15:13.5
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): He proclaims a grave offense as minor, and a minor offense as grave. 

pli-tv-pvr15:13.6
Pali: Imehi aṭṭhārasahi vatthūhi dosāgatiṁ gacchanto bahujanāhitāya paṭipanno hoti bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is biased by ill will by way of these eighteen grounds, then his behavior is unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; it is harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans. 

pli-tv-pvr15:13.7
Pali: Imehi aṭṭhārasahi vatthūhi dosāgatiṁ gacchanto khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is biased by ill will by way of these eighteen grounds, then he is damaged and impaired, blamed and criticized by sensible people, and makes much demerit. 

pli-tv-pvr15:13.8
Pali: Dosāgatiṁ gacchanto evaṁ dosāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:14.1
Pali: Na mohāgati gantabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He should not be biased by confusion: 

pli-tv-pvr15:14.2
Pali: mohāgatiṁ gacchanto kathaṁ mohāgatiṁ gacchati? 
English (brahmali, published): How is one biased by confusion? 

pli-tv-pvr15:14.3
Pali: Ratto rāgavasena gacchati, duṭṭho dosavasena gacchati, mūḷho mohavasena gacchati, parāmaṭṭho diṭṭhivasena gacchati, mūḷho sammūḷho mohābhibhūto adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Biased by favoritism, ill will, or confusion, or by a grasped view, he is confused, deluded, and overcome by confusion, and then proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it and what is in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it … 

pli-tv-pvr15:14.4
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): He proclaims a grave offense as minor, and a minor offense as grave. 

pli-tv-pvr15:14.5
Pali: Imehi aṭṭhārasahi vatthūhi mohāgatiṁ gacchanto bahujanāhitāya paṭipanno hoti bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is biased by confusion by way of these eighteen grounds, then his behavior is unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; it is harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans. 

pli-tv-pvr15:14.6
Pali: Imehi aṭṭhārasahi vatthūhi mohāgatiṁ gacchanto khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is biased by confusion by way of these eighteen grounds, then he is damaged and impaired, blamed and criticized by sensible people, and makes much demerit. 

pli-tv-pvr15:14.7
Pali: Mohāgatiṁ gacchanto evaṁ mohāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:15.1
Pali: Na bhayāgati gantabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): He should not be biased by fear: 

pli-tv-pvr15:15.2
Pali: bhayāgatiṁ gacchanto kathaṁ bhayāgatiṁ gacchati? 
English (brahmali, published): How is one biased by fear? 

pli-tv-pvr15:15.3
Pali: Idhekacco—“ayaṁ visamanissito vā gahananissito vā balavanissito vā kakkhaḷo pharuso jīvitantarāyaṁ vā brahmacariyantarāyaṁ vā karissatī”ti, tassa bhayā bhīto adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti, avinayaṁ vinayoti dīpeti, vinayaṁ avinayoti dīpeti, abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatena bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatena abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, apaññattaṁ tathāgatena paññattaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, paññattaṁ tathāgatena apaññattaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpeti, anāpattiṁ āpattīti dīpeti, āpattiṁ anāpattīti dīpeti, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ garukā āpattīti dīpeti, garukaṁ āpattiṁ lahukā āpattīti dīpeti, sāvasesaṁ āpattiṁ anavasesā āpattīti dīpeti, anavasesaṁ āpattiṁ sāvasesā āpattīti dīpeti, duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that someone thinks, “This one relies on the uneven, on thick covers, and on powerful individuals; he’s cruel and harsh, and might be a threat to life or the monastic life.” Fearful or frightened of him, he proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it and what is in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it. He proclaims what is contrary to the Monastic Law as being in accordance with it, and what is in accordance with the Monastic Law as contrary to it. He proclaims what hasn’t been spoken by the Buddha as spoken by him, and what has been spoken by the Buddha as not spoken by him. He proclaims what was not practiced by the Buddha as practiced by him, and what was practiced by the Buddha as not practiced by him. He proclaims what was not laid down by the Buddha as laid down by him, and what was laid down by the Buddha as not laid down by him. He proclaims a non-offense as an offense, and an offense as a non-offense. He proclaims a light offense as heavy, and a heavy offense as light. He proclaims a curable offense as incurable, and an incurable offense as curable. He proclaims a grave offense as minor, and a minor offense as grave. 

pli-tv-pvr15:15.4
Pali: Imehi aṭṭhārasahi vatthūhi bhayāgatiṁ gacchanto bahujanāhitāya paṭipanno hoti bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is biased by fear by way of these eighteen grounds, then his behavior is unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; it is harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans. 

pli-tv-pvr15:15.5
Pali: Imehi aṭṭhārasahi vatthūhi bhayāgatiṁ gacchanto khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti, sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati. 
English (brahmali, published): If he is biased by fear by way of these eighteen grounds, then he is damaged and impaired, blamed and criticized by sensible people, and makes much demerit. 

pli-tv-pvr15:15.6
Pali: Bhayāgatiṁ gacchanto evaṁ bhayāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:16.1
Pali: Chandā dosā bhayā mohā, 
English (brahmali, published): “If, because of favoritism, ill will, fear, or confusion, 

pli-tv-pvr15:16.2
Pali: yo dhammaṁ ativattati; 
English (brahmali, published): He goes beyond the Teaching, 

pli-tv-pvr15:16.3
Pali: Nihīyati tassa yaso, 
English (brahmali, published): Then his reputation is harmed, 

pli-tv-pvr15:16.4
Pali: kāḷapakkheva candimāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Like the moon during the waning half-month.” 

pli-tv-pvr15:17.0
Pali: 3. Agatiagamana 
English (brahmali, published): 3. Not being biased 

pli-tv-pvr15:17.1
Pali: Kathaṁ na chandāgatiṁ gacchati? 
English (brahmali, published): How is one not biased by favoritism? 

pli-tv-pvr15:17.2
Pali: Adhammaṁ adhammoti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, dhammaṁ dhammoti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, avinayaṁ avinayoti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, vinayaṁ vinayoti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatena abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatena bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, apaññattaṁ tathāgatena apaññattaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, paññattaṁ tathāgatena paññattaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, anāpattiṁ anāpattīti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, āpattiṁ āpattīti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ lahukā āpattīti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, garukaṁ āpattiṁ garukā āpattīti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, sāvasesaṁ āpattiṁ sāvasesā āpattīti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, anavasesaṁ āpattiṁ anavasesā āpattīti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento na chandāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): One is not biased by favoritism if one proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as such, and what is in accordance with the Teaching as such; if one proclaims what is contrary to the Monastic Law as such, and what is in accordance with the Monastic Law as such; if one proclaims what hasn’t been spoken by the Buddha as such, and what has been spoken by the Buddha as such; if one proclaims what was not practiced by the Buddha as such, and what was practiced by the Buddha as such; if one proclaims what was not laid down by the Buddha as such, and what was laid down by the Buddha as such; if one proclaims a non-offense as such, and an offense as such; if one proclaims a light offense as light, and a heavy offense as heavy; if one proclaims a curable offense as curable, and an incurable offense as incurable; if one proclaims a grave offense as grave, and a minor offense as minor. 

pli-tv-pvr15:17.3
Pali: Evaṁ na chandāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:18.1
Pali: Kathaṁ na dosāgatiṁ gacchati? 
English (brahmali, published): How is one not biased by ill will? 

pli-tv-pvr15:18.2
Pali: Adhammaṁ adhammoti dīpento na dosāgatiṁ gacchati, dhammaṁ dhammoti dīpento na dosāgatiṁ gacchati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): One is not biased by ill will if one proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as such, and what is in accordance with the Teaching as such … 

pli-tv-pvr15:18.3
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento na dosāgatiṁ gacchati, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento na dosāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): if one proclaims a grave offense as grave, and a minor offense as minor. 

pli-tv-pvr15:18.4
Pali: Evaṁ na dosāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:19.1
Pali: Kathaṁ na mohāgatiṁ gacchati? 
English (brahmali, published): How is one not biased by confusion?

English (brahmali, published): if one proclaims a grave offense as grave, and a minor offense as minor. 

pli-tv-pvr15:19.4
Pali: Evaṁ na mohāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:20.1
Pali: Kathaṁ na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati? 
English (brahmali, published): How is one not biased by fear? 

pli-tv-pvr15:20.2
Pali: Adhammaṁ adhammoti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, dhammaṁ dhammoti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, avinayaṁ avinayoti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, vinayaṁ vinayoti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatena abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatena bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatena āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, apaññattaṁ tathāgatena apaññattaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, paññattaṁ tathāgatena paññattaṁ tathāgatenāti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, anāpattiṁ anāpattīti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, āpattiṁ āpattīti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, lahukaṁ āpattiṁ lahukā āpattīti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, garukaṁ āpattiṁ garukā āpattīti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, sāvasesaṁ āpattiṁ sāvasesā āpattīti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, anavasesaṁ āpattiṁ anavasesā āpattīti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati.

pli-tv-pvr15:20.3
Pali: Evaṁ na bhayāgatiṁ gacchati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:21.1
Pali: Chandā dosā bhayā mohā, 
English (brahmali, published): “If, because of favoritism, ill will, fear, or confusion, 

pli-tv-pvr15:21.2
Pali: yo dhammaṁ nātivattati; 
English (brahmali, published): He does not go beyond the Teaching, 

pli-tv-pvr15:21.3
Pali: Āpūrati tassa yaso, 
English (brahmali, published): Then his reputation grows, 

pli-tv-pvr15:21.4
Pali: sukkapakkheva candimāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Like the moon during the waxing fortnight.” 

pli-tv-pvr15:22.0
Pali: 4. Saññāpanīyādi 
English (brahmali, published): 4. To be persuaded, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr15:22.1
Pali: Kathaṁ saññāpanīye ṭhāne saññāpeti? 
English (brahmali, published): How does one persuade when persuasion is appropriate? 

pli-tv-pvr15:22.2
Pali: Adhammaṁ adhammoti dīpento saññāpanīye ṭhāne saññāpeti, dhammaṁ dhammoti dīpento saññāpanīye ṭhāne saññāpeti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaiming what is contrary to the Teaching as such, and what is in accordance with the Teaching as such, he persuades others when persuasion is appropriate. … 

pli-tv-pvr15:22.3
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento saññāpanīye ṭhāne saññāpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento saññāpanīye ṭhāne saññāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaiming a grave offense as grave and a minor offense as minor, he persuades others when persuasion is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr15:22.4
Pali: Evaṁ saññāpanīye ṭhāne saññāpeti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:23.1
Pali: Kathaṁ nijjhāpanīye ṭhāne nijjhāpeti? 
English (brahmali, published): How does one make others understand when making understand is appropriate? 

pli-tv-pvr15:23.2
Pali: Adhammaṁ adhammoti dīpento nijjhāpanīye ṭhāne nijjhāpeti, dhammaṁ dhammoti dīpento nijjhāpanīye ṭhāne nijjhāpeti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaiming what is contrary to the Teaching as such, and what is in accordance with the Teaching as such, he makes others understand when making understand is appropriate. … 

pli-tv-pvr15:23.3
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento nijjhāpanīye ṭhāne nijjhāpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento nijjhāpanīye ṭhāne nijjhāpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaiming a grave offense as grave and a minor offense as minor, he makes others understand when making understand is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr15:23.4
Pali: Evaṁ nijjhāpanīye ṭhāne nijjhāpeti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:24.1
Pali: Kathaṁ pekkhanīye ṭhāne pekkhati? 
English (brahmali, published): How does one look on when looking on is appropriate? 

pli-tv-pvr15:24.2
Pali: Adhammaṁ adhammoti dīpento pekkhanīye ṭhāne pekkhati, dhammaṁ dhammoti dīpento pekkhanīye ṭhāne pekkhati …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaiming what is contrary to the Teaching as such, and what is in accordance with the Teaching as such, he looks on when looking on is appropriate. … 

pli-tv-pvr15:24.3
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento pekkhanīye ṭhāne pekkhati, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento pekkhanīye ṭhāne pekkhati. 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaiming a grave offense as grave and a minor offense as minor, he looks on when looking on is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr15:24.4
Pali: Evaṁ pekkhanīye ṭhāne pekkhati. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:25.1
Pali: Kathaṁ pasādanīye ṭhāne pasādeti? 
English (brahmali, published): How does one inspire when inspiration is appropriate? 

pli-tv-pvr15:25.2
Pali: Adhammaṁ adhammoti dīpento pasādanīye ṭhāne pasādeti, dhammaṁ dhammoti dīpento pasādanīye ṭhāne pasādeti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaiming what is contrary to the Teaching as such, and what is in accordance with the Teaching as such, he inspires when inspiration is appropriate. … 

pli-tv-pvr15:25.3
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento pasādanīye ṭhāne pasādeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpento pasādanīye ṭhāne pasādeti. 
English (brahmali, published): Proclaiming a grave offense as grave and a minor offense as minor, he inspires when inspiration is appropriate. 

pli-tv-pvr15:25.4
Pali: Evaṁ pasādanīye ṭhāne pasādeti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:26.0
Pali: 5. Parapakkhādiavajānana 
English (brahmali, published): 5. Despising the supporters of others, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr15:26.1
Pali: Kathaṁ laddhapakkhomhīti parapakkhaṁ avajānāti? 
English (brahmali, published): Thinking, “I’ve obtained supporters,” how does one despise the supporters of others? 

pli-tv-pvr15:26.2
Pali: Idhekacco laddhapakkho hoti laddhaparivāro pakkhavā ñātimā. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that someone has obtained supporters and a group of followers, and has relatives. 

pli-tv-pvr15:26.3
Pali: “Ayaṁ aladdhapakkho aladdhaparivāro na pakkhavā na ñātimā”ti tassa avajānanto adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He thinks, “This one doesn’t have supporters or a group of followers, and doesn’t have relatives,” and despising him, he proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it and what is in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it. … 

pli-tv-pvr15:26.4
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): He proclaims a grave offense as minor, and a minor offense as grave. 

pli-tv-pvr15:26.5
Pali: Evaṁ laddhapakkhomhīti parapakkhaṁ avajānāti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:27.1
Pali: Kathaṁ bahussutomhīti appassutaṁ avajānāti? 
English (brahmali, published): Thinking, “I’m learned,” how does one despise those who are ignorant? 

pli-tv-pvr15:27.2
Pali: Idhekacco bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo. 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that someone is learned, one who has retained and accumulated what he has learned. 

pli-tv-pvr15:27.3
Pali: “Ayaṁ appassuto appāgamo appadharo”ti tassa avajānanto adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): He thinks, “This one is ignorant; he has learned little and remembers little,” and despising him, he proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it and what is in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it. … 

pli-tv-pvr15:27.4
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): He proclaims a grave offense as minor, and a minor offense as grave. 

pli-tv-pvr15:27.5
Pali: Evaṁ bahussutomhīti appassutaṁ avajānāti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:28.1
Pali: Kathaṁ therataromhīti navakataraṁ avajānāti? 
English (brahmali, published): Thinking, “I’m more senior,” how does one despise those who are more junior? 

pli-tv-pvr15:28.2
Pali: Idhekacco thero hoti rattaññū cirapabbajito “ayaṁ navako appaññāto appakataññū imassa vacanaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī”ti tassa avajānanto adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti …pe… 
English (brahmali, published): It may be that someone is a senior monk of long standing. He thinks, “This is an unknown and ignorant junior monk; one shouldn’t do as he asks,” and despising him, he proclaims what is contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it and what is in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it. … 

pli-tv-pvr15:28.3
Pali: duṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ aduṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti, aduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ duṭṭhullā āpattīti dīpeti. 
English (brahmali, published): He proclaims a grave offense as minor, and a minor offense as grave. 

pli-tv-pvr15:28.4
Pali: Evaṁ therataromhīti navakataraṁ avajānāti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:29.1
Pali: Asampattaṁ na byāharitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should not speak about what has not been reached: 

pli-tv-pvr15:29.2
Pali: anotiṇṇaṁ bhāraṁ na otāretabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): he should not bring up an issue not under consideration. 

pli-tv-pvr15:29.3
Pali: Sampattaṁ dhammato vinayato na parihāpetabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): He should not use the Teaching or the Monastic Law to neglect what has been reached: 

pli-tv-pvr15:29.4
Pali: yaṁatthāya saṅgho sannipatito hoti, taṁ atthaṁ dhammato vinayato na parihāpetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the purpose for which the Sangha has gathered should not be neglected using the Teaching or the Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-pvr15:30.1
Pali: Yena dhammenāti 
English (brahmali, published): In accordance with the Teaching: 

pli-tv-pvr15:30.2
Pali: bhūtena vatthunā. 
English (brahmali, published): in according with truth, in accordance with the action that was the basis for the offense. 

pli-tv-pvr15:30.3
Pali: Yena vinayenāti 
English (brahmali, published): In accordance with the Monastic Law: 

pli-tv-pvr15:30.4
Pali: codetvā sāretvā. 
English (brahmali, published): having accused and having reminded. 

pli-tv-pvr15:30.5
Pali: Yena satthusāsanenāti 
English (brahmali, published): In accordance with the Teacher’s instruction: 

pli-tv-pvr15:30.6
Pali: ñattisampadāya anussāvanasampadāya, yena dhammena yena vinayena yena satthusāsanena taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati, tathā taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametabbanti. 
English (brahmali, published): he resolves that legal issue, complete in motion and complete in announcement, in accordance with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction. 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.0
Pali: 6. Anuvijjakassaanuyoga 
English (brahmali, published): 6. Questioning by the investigator 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.1
Pali: Anuvijjakena codako pucchitabbo—
English (brahmali, published): The investigator should ask the accuser, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.2
Pali: “yaṁ kho tvaṁ, āvuso, imassa bhikkhuno pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapesi, kimhi naṁ ṭhapesi, sīlavipattiyā vā ṭhapesi, ācāravipattiyā vā ṭhapesi, diṭṭhivipattiyā vā ṭhapesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Are you canceling this monk’s invitation because he has failed in morality, in conduct, or in view?” 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.3
Pali: So ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If he says, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.4
Pali: “sīlavipattiyā vā ṭhapemi, ācāravipattiyā vā ṭhapemi, diṭṭhivipattiyā vā ṭhapemī”ti, so evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): “I’m canceling it because he has failed in morality,” “I’m canceling it because he has failed in conduct,” or “I’m canceling it because he has failed in view,” he should be asked, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.5
Pali: “jānāti panāyasmā sīlavipattiṁ, jānāti ācāravipattiṁ, jānāti diṭṭhivipattin”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Do you know what failure in morality is?” “Do you know what failure in conduct is?” or “Do you know what failure in view is?” 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.6
Pali: So ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If he says, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.7
Pali: “jānāmi kho ahaṁ, āvuso, sīlavipattiṁ, jānāmi ācāravipattiṁ, jānāmi diṭṭhivipattin”ti, so evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): “I do,” he should be asked what they are. 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.8
Pali: “katamā panāvuso, sīlavipatti, katamā ācāravipatti, katamā diṭṭhivipattī”ti? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.9
Pali: So ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If he says, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.10
Pali: “cattāri pārājikāni terasa saṅghādisesā—ayaṁ sīlavipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The four offenses entailing expulsion and the thirteen entailing suspension are failure in morality,” 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.11
Pali: Thullaccayaṁ pācittiyaṁ pāṭidesanīyaṁ dukkaṭaṁ dubbhāsitaṁ—ayaṁ ācāravipatti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The serious offenses, the offenses entailing confession, the offenses entailing acknowledgment, the offenses of wrong conduct, and the offenses of wrong speech are failure in conduct,” 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.12
Pali: Micchādiṭṭhi antaggāhikādiṭṭhi—ayaṁ diṭṭhivipattī”ti, so evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): “Wrong views and extreme views are failure in view,” he should be asked, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.13
Pali: “yaṁ kho tvaṁ, āvuso, imassa bhikkhuno pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapesi, diṭṭhena vā ṭhapesi, sutena vā ṭhapesi, parisaṅkāya vā ṭhapesī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Are you canceling this monk’s invitation because of what you’ve seen, what you’ve heard, or what you suspect?” 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.14
Pali: So ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If he says, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.15
Pali: “diṭṭhena vā ṭhapemi, sutena vā ṭhapemi, parisaṅkāya vā ṭhapemī”ti, so evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): “I’m canceling it because of what I’ve seen,” “I’m canceling it because of what I’ve heard,” or “I’m canceling it because of what I suspect,” he should be asked, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.16
Pali: “yaṁ kho tvaṁ, āvuso, imassa bhikkhuno diṭṭhena pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapesi, kiṁ te diṭṭhaṁ, kinti te diṭṭhaṁ, kadā te diṭṭhaṁ, kattha te diṭṭhaṁ, pārājikaṁ ajjhāpajjanto diṭṭho, saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpajjanto diṭṭho, thullaccayaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): “Since you’re canceling this monk’s invitation because of what you’ve seen, what have you seen? How did you see it? When did you see it? Where did you see it? Did you see him commit an offense entailing expulsion? Did you see him commit an offense entailing suspension? Did you see him commit a serious offense, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.17
Pali: pācittiyaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.18
Pali: pāṭidesanīyaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing acknowledgment, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.19
Pali: dukkaṭaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense of wrong conduct, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.20
Pali: dubbhāsitaṁ ajjhāpajjanto diṭṭho, kattha ca tvaṁ ahosi, kattha cāyaṁ bhikkhu ahosi, kiñca tvaṁ karosi, kiṁ cāyaṁ bhikkhu karotī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): or an offense of wrong speech? Where were you? Where was this monk? What were you doing? What was this monk doing?” 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.21
Pali: So ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If he says, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.22
Pali: “na kho ahaṁ, āvuso, imassa bhikkhuno diṭṭhena pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapemi, api ca sutena pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapemī”ti, so evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): “I didn’t cancel this monk’s invitation because of what I’ve seen, but because of what I’ve heard,” he should be asked, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.23
Pali: “yaṁ kho tvaṁ, āvuso, imassa bhikkhuno sutena pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapesi, kiṁ te sutaṁ, kinti te sutaṁ, kadā te sutaṁ, kattha te sutaṁ, pārājikaṁ ajjhāpannoti sutaṁ, saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpannoti sutaṁ, thullaccayaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): “Since you’re canceling this monk’s invitation because of what you’ve heard, what have you heard? How did you hear it? When did you hear it? Where did you hear it? Did you hear that he has committed an offense entailing expulsion? Did you hear that he has committed an offense entailing suspension? Did you hear that he has committed a serious offense, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.24
Pali: pācittiyaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.25
Pali: pāṭidesanīyaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing acknowledgment, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.26
Pali: dukkaṭaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense of wrong conduct, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.27
Pali: dubbhāsitaṁ ajjhāpannoti sutaṁ, bhikkhussa sutaṁ, bhikkhuniyā sutaṁ, sikkhamānāya sutaṁ, sāmaṇerassa sutaṁ, sāmaṇeriyā sutaṁ, upāsakassa sutaṁ, upāsikāya sutaṁ, rājūnaṁ sutaṁ, rājamahāmattānaṁ sutaṁ, titthiyānaṁ sutaṁ, titthiyasāvakānaṁ sutan”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): or an offense of wrong speech? Did you hear it from a monk, a nun, a trainee nun, a novice monk, a novice nun, a male lay follower, or a female lay follower? Or did you hear it from kings, a king’s officials, the monastics of another religion, or the lay followers of another religion?” 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.28
Pali: So ce evaṁ vadeyya—
English (brahmali, published): If he says, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.29
Pali: “na kho ahaṁ, āvuso, imassa bhikkhuno sutena pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapemi, api ca parisaṅkāya pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapemī”ti, so evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): “I didn’t cancel this monk’s invitation because of what I’ve heard, but because of what I suspect,” he should be asked, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.30
Pali: “yaṁ kho tvaṁ, āvuso, imassa bhikkhuno parisaṅkāya pavāraṇaṁ ṭhapesi, kiṁ parisaṅkasi, kinti parisaṅkasi, kadā parisaṅkasi, kattha parisaṅkasi, pārājikaṁ ajjhāpannoti parisaṅkasi, saṅghādisesaṁ ajjhāpannoti parisaṅkasi, thullaccayaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): “Since you’re canceling this monk’s invitation because of suspicion, what do you suspect? How do you suspect it? When did you suspect it? Where did you suspect it? Do you suspect that he has committed an offense entailing expulsion? Do you suspect that he has committed an offense entailing suspension? Do you suspect that he has committed a serious offense, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.31
Pali: pācittiyaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing confession, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.32
Pali: pāṭidesanīyaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense entailing acknowledgment, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.33
Pali: dukkaṭaṁ … 
English (brahmali, published): an offense of wrong conduct, 

pli-tv-pvr15:31.34
Pali: dubbhāsitaṁ ajjhāpannoti parisaṅkasi, bhikkhussa sutvā parisaṅkasi, bhikkhuniyā sutvā parisaṅkasi, sikkhamānāya sutvā parisaṅkasi, sāmaṇerassa sutvā parisaṅkasi, sāmaṇeriyā sutvā parisaṅkasi, upāsakassa sutvā parisaṅkasi, upāsikāya sutvā parisaṅkasi, rājūnaṁ sutvā parisaṅkasi, rājamahāmattānaṁ sutvā parisaṅkasi, titthiyānaṁ sutvā parisaṅkasi, titthiyasāvakānaṁ sutvā parisaṅkasī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): or an offense of wrong speech? Do you suspect it after hearing about it from a monk, a nun, a trainee nun, a novice monk, a novice nun, a male lay follower, or a female lay follower? Or do you suspect it after hearing about it from kings, a king’s officials, the monastics of another religion, or the lay followers of another religion?” 

pli-tv-pvr15:32.1
Pali: Diṭṭhaṁ diṭṭhena sameti, 
English (brahmali, published): “If what he saw agrees with what he says he saw, 

pli-tv-pvr15:32.2
Pali: diṭṭhena saṁsandate diṭṭhaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If they correspond with each other, 

pli-tv-pvr15:32.3
Pali: Diṭṭhaṁ paṭicca na upeti, 
English (brahmali, published): But what was seen isn’t adequate to prove the accusation, 

pli-tv-pvr15:32.4
Pali: asuddhaparisaṅkito; 
English (brahmali, published): Then the one suspecting impurity 

pli-tv-pvr15:32.5
Pali: So puggalo paṭiññāya, 
English (brahmali, published): Should admit it, 

pli-tv-pvr15:32.6
Pali: kātabbā tena pavāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): And they should then do the invitation ceremony with him. 

pli-tv-pvr15:33.1
Pali: Sutaṁ sutena sameti, 
English (brahmali, published): If what he heard agrees with what he says he heard, 

pli-tv-pvr15:33.2
Pali: sutena saṁsandate sutaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If they correspond with each other, 

pli-tv-pvr15:33.3
Pali: Sutaṁ paṭicca na upeti, 
English (brahmali, published): But what was heard isn’t adequate to prove the accusation, 

pli-tv-pvr15:33.4
Pali: asuddhaparisaṅkito; 
English (brahmali, published): Then the one suspecting impurity 

pli-tv-pvr15:33.5
Pali: So puggalo paṭiññāya, 
English (brahmali, published): Should admit it, 

pli-tv-pvr15:33.6
Pali: kātabbā tena pavāraṇā. 
English (brahmali, published): And they should then do the invitation ceremony with him. 

pli-tv-pvr15:34.1
Pali: Mutaṁ mutena sameti, 
English (brahmali, published): If what he sensed agrees with what he says he sensed, 

pli-tv-pvr15:34.2
Pali: Mutena saṁsandate mutaṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): If they correspond with each other, 

pli-tv-pvr15:34.3
Pali: Mutaṁ paṭicca na upeti, 
English (brahmali, published): But what was sensed isn’t adequate to prove the accusation, 

pli-tv-pvr15:34.4
Pali: Asuddhaparisaṅkito; 
English (brahmali, published): Then the one suspecting impurity 

pli-tv-pvr15:34.5
Pali: So puggalo paṭiññāya, 
English (brahmali, published): Should admit it, 

pli-tv-pvr15:34.6
Pali: Kātabbā tena pavāraṇāti. 
English (brahmali, published): And they should then do the invitation ceremony with him.” 

pli-tv-pvr15:35.0
Pali: 7. Pucchāvibhāga 
English (brahmali, published): 7. The details of asking 

pli-tv-pvr15:35.1
Pali: Kiṁ te diṭṭhanti katamā pucchā? 
English (brahmali, published): “What are the questions in regard to ‘What have you seen?’ 

pli-tv-pvr15:35.2
Pali: Kinti te diṭṭhanti katamā pucchā? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the questions in regard to ‘How did you see it?’ 

pli-tv-pvr15:35.3
Pali: Kadā te diṭṭhanti katamā pucchā? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the questions in regard to ‘When did you see it?’ 

pli-tv-pvr15:35.4
Pali: Kattha te diṭṭhanti katamā pucchā? 
English (brahmali, published): What are the questions in regard to ‘Where did you see it?’ 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.1
Pali: Kiṁ te diṭṭhanti 
English (brahmali, published): In regard to ‘What have you seen?’ 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.2
Pali: vatthupucchā, vipattipucchā, āpattipucchā, ajjhācārapucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are questions on the actions that are the bases for offenses, there are questions on failure, there are questions on offenses, and there are questions on misconduct. 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.3
Pali: Vatthupucchāti—
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on the actions that are the bases for offenses’: 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.4
Pali: aṭṭhapārājikānaṁ vatthupucchā, tevīsasaṅghādisesānaṁ vatthupucchā, dveaniyatānaṁ vatthupucchā, dvecattārīsanissaggiyānaṁ vatthupucchā, aṭṭhāsītisatapācittiyānaṁ vatthupucchā, dvādasapāṭidesanīyānaṁ vatthupucchā, dukkaṭānaṁ vatthupucchā, dubbhāsitānaṁ vatthupucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are questions on the actions that are the bases for the eight offenses entailing expulsion, on the actions that are the bases for the twenty-three offenses entailing suspension, for the two undetermined offenses, for the forty-two offenses entailing relinquishment, for the one hundred and eighty-eight offenses entailing confession, for the twelve offenses entailing acknowledgment, for the offenses of wrong conduct, and for the offenses of wrong speech. 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.5
Pali: Vipattipucchāti—
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on failure’: 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.6
Pali: sīlavipattipucchā, ācāravipattipucchā, diṭṭhivipattipucchā, ājīvavipattipucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are questions on failure in morality, on failure in conduct, on failure in view, and on failure in livelihood. 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.7
Pali: Āpattipucchāti—
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on the offenses’: 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.8
Pali: pārājikāpattipucchā, saṅghādisesāpattipucchā, thullaccayāpattipucchā, pācittiyāpattipucchā, pāṭidesanīyāpattipucchā, dukkaṭāpattipucchā, dubbhāsitāpattipucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are questions on the offenses entailing expulsion, on the offenses entailing suspension, on the serious offenses, on the offenses entailing confession, on the offenses entailing acknowledgement, on the offenses of wrong conduct, and on the offenses of wrong speech. 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.9
Pali: Ajjhācārapucchāti—
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on misconduct’: 

pli-tv-pvr15:36.10
Pali: dvayaṁdvayasamāpattipucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are questions on that which is done wherever there are couples. 

pli-tv-pvr15:37.1
Pali: Kinti te diṭṭhanti 
English (brahmali, published): In regard to ‘How did you see it?’ 

pli-tv-pvr15:37.2
Pali: liṅgapucchā, iriyāpathapucchā, ākārapucchā, vippakārapucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are questions on characteristics, postures, attributes, and modes. 

pli-tv-pvr15:37.3
Pali: Liṅgapucchāti—dīghaṁ vā rassaṁ vā kaṇhaṁ vā odātaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on characteristics’: tall, short, dark-skinned, or light-skinned. 

pli-tv-pvr15:37.4
Pali: Iriyāpathapucchāti gacchantaṁ vā ṭhitaṁ vā nisinnaṁ vā nipannaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on postures’: walking, standing, sitting, or lying down. 

pli-tv-pvr15:37.5
Pali: Ākārapucchāti gihiliṅge vā titthiyaliṅge vā pabbajitaliṅge vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on attributes’: the characteristics of a householder, of a monastic of another religion, or of one gone forth. 

pli-tv-pvr15:37.6
Pali: Vippakārapucchāti gacchantaṁ vā ṭhitaṁ vā nisinnaṁ vā nipannaṁ vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on modes’: walking, standing, sitting, or lying down. 

pli-tv-pvr15:38.1
Pali: Kadā te diṭṭhanti 
English (brahmali, published): In regard to ‘When did you see it?’ 

pli-tv-pvr15:38.2
Pali: kālapucchā, samayapucchā, divasapucchā, utupucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are questions on time, on occasion, on day, and on season. 

pli-tv-pvr15:38.3
Pali: Kālapucchāti pubbaṇhakāle vā majjhanhikakāle vā sāyanhakāle vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on time’: in the morning, at midday, or in the evening. 

pli-tv-pvr15:38.4
Pali: Samayapucchāti pubbaṇhasamaye vā majjhanhikasamaye vā sāyanhasamaye vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on occasion’: in the morning, at midday, or in the evening. 

pli-tv-pvr15:38.5
Pali: Divasapucchāti purebhattaṁ vā pacchābhattaṁ vā rattiṁ vā divā vā kāḷe vā juṇhe vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on day’: before the meal, after the meal, at night, by day, during the waning moon, or during the waxing moon. 

pli-tv-pvr15:38.6
Pali: Utupucchāti hemante vā gimhe vā vasse vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions on season’: in winter, in summer, or during the rainy season. 

pli-tv-pvr15:39.1
Pali: Kattha te diṭṭhanti 
English (brahmali, published): In regard to ‘Where did you see it?’ 

pli-tv-pvr15:39.2
Pali: ṭhānapucchā, bhūmipucchā, okāsapucchā, padesapucchā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are questions about place, about elevation, about location, and about region. 

pli-tv-pvr15:39.3
Pali: Ṭhānapucchāti bhūmiyā vā pathaviyā vā dharaṇiyā vā jagatiyā vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions about place’: in the ground, on the earth, on the planet, or in the world. 

pli-tv-pvr15:39.4
Pali: Bhūmipucchāti bhūmiyā vā pathaviyā vā pabbate vā pāsāṇe vā pāsāde vā. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions about elevation’: in the ground, on the the earth, on a mountain, on a rock, or in a stilt house. 

pli-tv-pvr15:39.5
Pali: Okāsapucchāti puratthime vā okāse pacchime vā okāse uttare vā okāse dakkhiṇe vā okāse. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions about location’: to the east, to the west, to the north, or to the south. 

pli-tv-pvr15:39.6
Pali: Padesapucchāti puratthime vā padese pacchime vā padese uttare vā padese dakkhiṇe vā padeseti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Questions about region’: to the east, to the west, to the north, or to the south.” 

pli-tv-pvr15:40.1
Pali: Mahāsaṅgāmaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The great chapter on the resolution of conflicts is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr15:41.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr15:42.1
Pali: Vatthu nidānaṁ ākāro, 
English (brahmali, published): “Basis for an offense, origin story, attribute, 

pli-tv-pvr15:42.2
Pali: pubbāparaṁ katākataṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): The right order, what has and has not been done; 

pli-tv-pvr15:42.3
Pali: Kammādhikaraṇañceva, 
English (brahmali, published): Legal procedure and legal issue, 

pli-tv-pvr15:42.4
Pali: samatho chandagāmi ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Settling, and biased by favoritism. 

pli-tv-pvr15:43.1
Pali: Dosā mohā bhayā ceva, 
English (brahmali, published): By ill will, confusion, and fear, 

pli-tv-pvr15:43.2
Pali: saññā nijjhāpanena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Persuasion, and by making understand; 

pli-tv-pvr15:43.3
Pali: Pekkhā pasāde pakkhomhi, 
English (brahmali, published): Looking on, inspiration, I have supporters, 

pli-tv-pvr15:43.4
Pali: sutatheratarena ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Learned, and with more senior. 

pli-tv-pvr15:44.1
Pali: Asampattañca sampattaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): And not reached, reached, 

pli-tv-pvr15:44.2
Pali: dhammena vinayena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): According to the Teaching, and the Monastic Law; 

pli-tv-pvr15:44.3
Pali: Satthussa sāsanenāpi, 
English (brahmali, published): Also according to the Teacher’s instruction—

pli-tv-pvr15:44.4
Pali: mahāsaṅgāmañāpanāti. 
English (brahmali, published): The explanation in the great chapter on the resolution of conflicts.”
