Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr16:0.2
Pali: 16. Kathinabheda 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony 

pli-tv-pvr16:0.3
Pali: 1. Kathinaatthatādi 
English (brahmali, published): 1. Participated in the robe-making ceremony, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr16:1.1
Pali: Kassa kathinaṁ anatthataṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony? 

pli-tv-pvr16:1.2
Pali: Kassa kathinaṁ atthataṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): Who has participated in the robe-making ceremony? 

pli-tv-pvr16:1.3
Pali: Kinti kathinaṁ anatthataṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): How has the robe-making ceremony not been performed? 

pli-tv-pvr16:1.4
Pali: Kinti kathinaṁ atthataṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): How has the robe-making ceremony been performed? 

pli-tv-pvr16:2.1
Pali: Kassa kathinaṁ anatthatanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Who has not participated in the robe-making ceremony? 

pli-tv-pvr16:2.2
Pali: Dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ anatthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people: 

pli-tv-pvr16:2.3
Pali: anatthārakassa ca ananumodakassa ca. 
English (brahmali, published): those who have not performed the robe-making ceremony and those who have not expressed their appreciation. 

pli-tv-pvr16:2.4
Pali: Imesaṁ dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ anatthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr16:3.1
Pali: Kassa kathinaṁ atthatanti? 
English (brahmali, published): Who has participated the robe-making ceremony? 

pli-tv-pvr16:3.2
Pali: Dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of people: 

pli-tv-pvr16:3.3
Pali: atthārakassa ca anumodakassa ca. 
English (brahmali, published): those who have performed the robe-making ceremony and those who have expressed their appreciation. 

pli-tv-pvr16:3.4
Pali: Imesaṁ dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.1
Pali: Kinti kathinaṁ anatthatanti? 
English (brahmali, published): How has the robe-making ceremony not been performed? 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.2
Pali: Catuvīsatiyā ākārehi anatthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): There are twenty-four ways in which the robe-making ceremony has not been performed: 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.3
Pali: na ullikhitamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony has not been performed merely by marking the cloth, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.4
Pali: na dhovanamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by washing the cloth, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.5
Pali: na cīvaravicāraṇamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by planning the robe, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.6
Pali: na chedanamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by cutting the cloth, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.7
Pali: na bandhanamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by tacking the cloth, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.8
Pali: na ovaṭṭiyakaraṇamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by sewing a hem, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.9
Pali: na kaṇḍusakaraṇamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by marking with a strip of cloth, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.10
Pali: na daḷhīkammakaraṇamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by strengthening, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.11
Pali: na anuvātakaraṇamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by adding a border lengthwise, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.12
Pali: na paribhaṇḍakaraṇamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by adding a border crosswise, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.13
Pali: na ovaddheyyakaraṇamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by patching, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.14
Pali: na kambalamaddanamattena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): merely by partial dyeing; 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.15
Pali: na nimittakatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): nor has it been performed if a monk has made an indication, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.16
Pali: na parikathākatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if a monk has given a hint, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.17
Pali: na kukkukatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if the robe-cloth has been borrowed, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.18
Pali: na sannidhikatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it has been stored, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.19
Pali: na nissaggiyena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is to be relinquished, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.20
Pali: na akappakatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it has not been marked, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.21
Pali: na aññatra saṅghāṭiyā atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if it is not an outer robe 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.22
Pali: na aññatra uttarāsaṅgena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): or an upper robe 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.23
Pali: na aññatra antaravāsakena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): or a sarong; 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.24
Pali: na aññatra pañcakena vā atirekapañcakena vā tadaheva sañchinnena samaṇḍalīkatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): nor has it been performed if the robe has not been made on that very day with five or more cut sections with panels, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.25
Pali: na aññatra puggalassa atthārā atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): if the robe-making ceremony was not performed by an individual, 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.26
Pali: sammā ce atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, tañce nissīmaṭṭho anumodati. 
English (brahmali, published): or if the robe-making ceremony has been performed correctly but the appreciation for the ceremony was expressed outside the monastery zone. 

pli-tv-pvr16:4.27
Pali: Evampi anatthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.1
Pali: Nimittakammaṁ nāma nimittaṁ karoti—
English (brahmali, published): Making an indication: one makes an indication, thinking, 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.2
Pali: “iminā dussena kathinaṁ attharissāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “I’ll perform the robe-making ceremony with this cloth.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.3
Pali: Parikathā nāma parikathaṁ karoti—
English (brahmali, published): Hinting: one gives a hint, thinking, 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.4
Pali: “imāya parikathāya kathinadussaṁ nibbattessāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “With this hint, I’ll make a cloth for the robe-making ceremony appear.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.5
Pali: Kukkukataṁ nāma anādiyadānaṁ vuccati. 
English (brahmali, published): Borrowed: a gift not to be taken as one’s own is so called. 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.6
Pali: Sannidhi nāma dve sannidhiyo—
English (brahmali, published): Stored: there are two kinds of storing: 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.7
Pali: karaṇasannidhi vā nicayasannidhi vā. 
English (brahmali, published): storing for the purpose of making and storing for the purpose of accumulation. 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.8
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ nāma kayiramāne aruṇaṁ uṭṭhahati. 
English (brahmali, published): To be relinquished: dawn arrives while it is being made. 

pli-tv-pvr16:5.9
Pali: Imehi catuvīsatiyā ākārehi anatthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr16:6.1
Pali: Kinti kathinaṁ atthatanti? 
English (brahmali, published): How has the robe-making ceremony been performed? 

pli-tv-pvr16:6.2
Pali: Sattarasahi ākārehi atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony has been performed through seventeen aspects: 

pli-tv-pvr16:6.3
Pali: Ahatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, ahatakappena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, pilotikāya atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, paṁsukūlena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, pāpaṇikena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, animittakatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, aparikathākatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, akukkukatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, asannidhikatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, anissaggiyena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, kappakatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, saṅghāṭiyā atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, uttarāsaṅgena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, antaravāsakena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, pañcakena vā atirekapañcakena vā tadaheva sañchinnena samaṇḍalīkatena atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, puggalassa atthārā atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, sammā ce atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, tañce sīmaṭṭho anumodati, evampi atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony has been performed if the cloth is brand new, if it is nearly new, if it is old, if it is a rag, if it is from a shop; it has been performed if a monk has not made an indication, if a monk has not given a hint, if the robe-cloth has not been borrowed, if it has not been stored, if it is not to be relinquished, if it has been marked, if it is an outer robe or an upper robe or a sarong; it has been performed if the robe has been made on that very day with five or more cut sections with panels, if the robe-making ceremony was performed by an individual, if the robe-making ceremony has been performed correctly and if the appreciation for the ceremony was expressed inside the monastery zone. 

pli-tv-pvr16:6.4
Pali: Imehi sattarasahi ākārehi atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr16:7.1
Pali: Saha kathinassa atthārā kati dhammā jāyanti? 
English (brahmali, published): How many things are produced together with the participation in the robe-making ceremony? 

pli-tv-pvr16:7.2
Pali: Saha kathinassa atthārā pannarasa dhammā jāyanti—
English (brahmali, published): Fifteen things: 

pli-tv-pvr16:7.3
Pali: aṭṭha mātikā, dve palibodhā, pañcānisaṁsā. 
English (brahmali, published): eight key phrases, two obstacles, and five benefits. 

pli-tv-pvr16:7.4
Pali: Saha kathinassa atthārā ime pannarasa dhammā jāyanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr16:8.0

English (brahmali, published): Which things are a condition for the object by being a proximity condition, an immediacy condition, a support condition, a decisive support condition, a pre-arising condition, a post-arising condition, and a co-arising condition?

pli-tv-pvr16:9.24
Pali: Pannarasa dhammā sahajātapaccayena paccayo. 
English (brahmali, published): The fifteen things are a condition by being a co-arising condition. 

pli-tv-pvr16:10.0
Pali: 3. Pubbakaraṇanidānādivibhāga 
English (brahmali, published): 3. The details on the source of the prior duties, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr16:10.1
Pali: Pubbakaraṇaṁ kiṁnidānaṁ, kiṁsamudayaṁ, kiṁjātikaṁ, kiṁpabhavaṁ, kiṁsambhāraṁ, kiṁsamuṭṭhānaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): What is the source, the origin, the birth, the arising, the production, the origination of the prior duties? 

pli-tv-pvr16:10.2
Pali: Paccuddhāro kiṁnidāno, kiṁsamudayo, kiṁjātiko, kiṁpabhavo, kiṁsambhāro, kiṁsamuṭṭhāno? 
English (brahmali, published): What is the source, the origin, the birth, the arising, the production, the origination of relinquishment? 

pli-tv-pvr16:10.3
Pali: Adhiṭṭhānaṁ kiṁnidānaṁ, kiṁsamudayaṁ, kiṁjātikaṁ, kiṁpabhavaṁ, kiṁsambhāraṁ, kiṁsamuṭṭhānaṁ? 
English (brahmali, published): What is the source, the origin, the birth, the arising, the production, the origination of determining? 

pli-tv-pvr16:10.4
Pali: Atthāro kiṁnidāno, kiṁsamudayo, kiṁjātiko, kiṁpabhavo, kiṁsambhāro, kiṁsamuṭṭhāno? 
English (brahmali, published): What is the source, the origin, the birth, the arising, the production, the origination of performing the robe-making ceremony? 

pli-tv-pvr16:10.5
Pali: Mātikā ca palibodhā ca kiṁnidānā, kiṁsamudayā, kiṁjātikā, kiṁpabhavā, kiṁsambhārā kiṁsamuṭṭhānā? 
English (brahmali, published): What is the source, the origin, the birth, the arising, the production, the origination of the key phrases and the obstacles? 

pli-tv-pvr16:10.6
Pali: Āsā ca anāsā ca kiṁnidānā, kiṁsamudayā, kiṁjātikā, kiṁpabhavā, kiṁsambhārā, kiṁsamuṭṭhānā? 
English (brahmali, published): What is the source, the origin, the birth, the arising, the production, the origination of expectation and non-expectation? 

pli-tv-pvr16:11.1
Pali: Pubbakaraṇaṁ payoganidānaṁ, payogasamudayaṁ, payogajātikaṁ, payogapabhavaṁ, payogasambhāraṁ, payogasamuṭṭhānaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The prior duties have effort as their source, origin, birth, arising, production, and origination. 

pli-tv-pvr16:11.2
Pali: Paccuddhāro pubbakaraṇanidāno, pubbakaraṇasamudayo, pubbakaraṇajātiko, pubbakaraṇapabhavo, pubbakaraṇasambhāro, pubbakaraṇasamuṭṭhāno.

pli-tv-pvr16:11.3

English (brahmali, published): The prior duties are the beginning, the performing is the middle, the robe-making ceremony is the end. 

pli-tv-pvr16:22.1

pli-tv-pvr16:22.10

English (brahmali, published): It is effective for three kinds of people.

English (brahmali, published): For which three kinds of people is it not effective? 

pli-tv-pvr16:23.6

pli-tv-pvr16:24.10
Pali: ime tayo kathinatthārā ruhanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr16:25.0
Pali: 4. Kathinādijānitabbavibhāga 
English (brahmali, published): 4. The details on what is to be known about the robe-making ceremony, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr16:25.1
Pali: Kathinaṁ jānitabbaṁ, kathinatthāro jānitabbo, kathinassa atthāramāso jānitabbo, kathinassa atthāravipatti jānitabbā, kathinassa atthārasampatti jānitabbā, nimittakammaṁ jānitabbaṁ, parikathā jānitabbā, kukkukataṁ jānitabbaṁ, sannidhi jānitabbā, nissaggiyaṁ jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony is to be known. Participation in the robe-making ceremony is to be known. The month of the robe-making ceremony is to be known. Failure of the robe-making ceremony is to be known. Success of the robe-making ceremony is to be known. The giving of an indication is to be known. A hint is to be known. Borrowing is to be known. Storing is to be known. Relinquishment is to be known. 

pli-tv-pvr16:26.1
Pali: Kathinaṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:26.2
Pali: tesaññeva dhammānaṁ saṅgaho samavāyo nāmaṁ nāmakammaṁ nāmadheyyaṁ nirutti byañjanaṁ abhilāpo yadidaṁ kathinanti. 
English (brahmali, published): “the robe-making ceremony” is the grouping and coming together of just those things—their name, appellation, label, terminology, wording, designation. 

pli-tv-pvr16:27.1
Pali: Kathinassa atthāramāso jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): The month of the robe-making ceremony is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:27.2
Pali: vassānassa pacchimo māso jānitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): the last month of the rainy season. 

pli-tv-pvr16:28.1
Pali: Kathinassa atthāravipatti jānitabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): Failure of the robe-making ceremony is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:28.2
Pali: catuvīsatiyā ākārehi kathinassa atthāravipatti jānitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): the robe-making ceremony fails in twenty-four ways. 

pli-tv-pvr16:29.1
Pali: Kathinassa atthārasampatti jānitabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): Success of the robe-making ceremony is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:29.2
Pali: sattarasahi ākārehi kathinassa atthārasampatti jānitabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony is successful through seventeen aspects. 

pli-tv-pvr16:30.1
Pali: Nimittakammaṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): The giving of an indication is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:30.2
Pali: nimittaṁ karoti iminā dussena kathinaṁ attharissāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): one makes an indication, thinking, “I’ll perform the robe-making ceremony with this cloth.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:31.1
Pali: Parikathā jānitabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): A hint is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:31.2
Pali: parikathaṁ karoti imāya parikathāya kathinadussaṁ nibbattessāmīti. 
English (brahmali, published): one gives a hint, thinking, “With this hint, I’ll make a cloth for the robe-making ceremony appear.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:32.1
Pali: Kukkukataṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): Borrowing is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:32.2
Pali: anādiyadānaṁ jānitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): a gift not to be taken as one’s own. 

pli-tv-pvr16:33.1
Pali: Sannidhi jānitabbāti 
English (brahmali, published): Storing is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:33.2
Pali: dve sannidhiyo jānitabbā—karaṇasannidhi vā nicayasannidhi vā. 
English (brahmali, published): there are two kinds of storing: for the purpose of making and for the purpose of accumulation. 

pli-tv-pvr16:34.1
Pali: Nissaggiyaṁ jānitabbanti 
English (brahmali, published): Relinquishment is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:34.2
Pali: kariyamāne aruṇaṁ uṭṭhahati. 
English (brahmali, published): dawn arrives while it is being made. 

pli-tv-pvr16:35.1
Pali: Kathinatthāro jānitabboti 
English (brahmali, published): Participation in the robe-making ceremony is to be known: 

pli-tv-pvr16:35.2
Pali: sace saṅghassa kathinadussaṁ uppannaṁ hoti, saṅghena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ, atthārakena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ, anumodakena kathaṁ paṭipajjitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): If cloth has been given to the Sangha for the robe-making ceremony, how should the Sangha proceed? How should the one who performs the robe-making ceremony proceed? How should one who expresses their appreciation proceed? 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.1
Pali: Saṅghena ñattidutiyena kammena kathinatthārakassa bhikkhuno dātabbaṁ, tena kathinatthārakena bhikkhunā tadaheva dhovitvā vimajjitvā vicāretvā chinditvā sibbetvā rajitvā kappaṁ katvā kathinaṁ attharitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): The Sangha should give the cloth, through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, to the monk who is performing the robe-making ceremony. The monk who is performing the robe-making ceremony should wash the cloth, iron it, plan it, cut it, sew it, dye it, mark it, and then perform the robe-making ceremony. 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.2
Pali: Sace saṅghāṭiyā kathinaṁ attharitukāmo hoti, porāṇikā saṅghāṭi paccuddharitabbā, navā saṅghāṭi adhiṭṭhātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): If he wishes to perform the robe-making ceremony with an outer robe, he should relinquish his old outer robe and then determine the new one. 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.3
Pali: Imāya saṅghāṭiyā kathinaṁ attharāmīti vācā bhinditabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): He should then say, “I perform the robe-making ceremony with this outer robe.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.4
Pali: Sace uttarāsaṅgena kathinaṁ attharitukāmo hoti, porāṇako uttarāsaṅgo paccuddharitabbo, navo uttarāsaṅgo adhiṭṭhātabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he wishes to perform the robe-making ceremony with an upper robe, he should relinquish his old upper robe and then determine the new one. 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.5
Pali: Iminā uttarāsaṅgena kathinaṁ attharāmīti vācā bhinditabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): He should then say, “I perform the robe-making ceremony with this upper robe.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.6
Pali: Sace antaravāsakena kathinaṁ attharitukāmo hoti, porāṇako antaravāsako paccuddharitabbo, navo antaravāsako adhiṭṭhātabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): If he wishes to perform the robe-making ceremony with a sarong, he should relinquish his old sarong and then determine the new one. 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.7
Pali: Iminā antaravāsakena kathinaṁ attharāmīti vācā bhinditabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): He should then say, “I perform the robe-making ceremony with this sarong.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.8
Pali: Tena kathinatthārakena bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): After approaching the Sangha, that monk who is performing the robe-making ceremony should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.9
Pali: “atthataṁ, bhante, saṅghassa kathinaṁ, dhammiko kathinatthāro, anumodathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable sirs, the Sangha’s robe-making ceremony has been performed. The robe-making ceremony is legitimate. Please express your appreciation.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.10
Pali: Tehi anumodakehi bhikkhūhi ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): The monks who are expressing their appreciation should arrange their upper robes over one shoulder, raise their joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.11
Pali: “atthataṁ, āvuso, saṅghassa kathinaṁ, dhammiko kathinatthāro, anumodāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha’s robe-making ceremony has been performed. The robe-making ceremony is legitimate. We express our appreciation.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.12
Pali: Tena kathinatthārakena bhikkhunā sambahule bhikkhū upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassu vacanīyā—
English (brahmali, published): Or: after approaching several monks, that monk who is performing the robe-making ceremony should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.13
Pali: “atthataṁ, bhante, saṅghassa kathinaṁ, dhammiko kathinatthāro, anumodathā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable sirs, the Sangha’s robe-making ceremony has been performed. The robe-making ceremony is legitimate. Please express your appreciation.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.14
Pali: Tehi anumodakehi bhikkhūhi ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): The monks who are expressing their appreciation should arrange their upper robes over one shoulder, raise their joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.15
Pali: “atthataṁ, āvuso, saṅghassa kathinaṁ, dhammiko kathinatthāro anumodāmā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha’s robe-making ceremony has been performed. The robe-making ceremony is legitimate. We express our appreciation.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.16
Pali: Tena kathinatthārakena bhikkhunā ekaṁ bhikkhuṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): Or: after approaching a single monk, that monk who is performing the robe-making ceremony should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.17
Pali: “atthataṁ, āvuso, saṅghassa kathinaṁ, dhammiko kathinatthāro, anumodāhī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha’s robe-making ceremony has been performed. The robe-making ceremony is legitimate. Please express your appreciation.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.18
Pali: Tena anumodakena bhikkhunā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
English (brahmali, published): The monk who is expressing his appreciation should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, raise his joined palms, and say: 

pli-tv-pvr16:36.19
Pali: “atthataṁ, āvuso, saṅghassa kathinaṁ, dhammiko kathinatthāro, anumodāmī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha’s robe-making ceremony has been performed. The robe-making ceremony is legitimate. I express my appreciation.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.0
Pali: 5. Puggalassevakathinatthāra 
English (brahmali, published): 5. The robe-making ceremony for an individual 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.1
Pali: Saṅgho kathinaṁ attharati, gaṇo kathinaṁ attharati, puggalo kathinaṁ attharatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Does the Sangha perform the robe-making ceremony? Does a group perform the robe-making ceremony? Does an individual perform the robe-making ceremony?” 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.2
Pali: Na saṅgho kathinaṁ attharati, na gaṇo kathinaṁ attharati, puggalo kathinaṁ attharatīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha does not perform the robe-making ceremony, nor does a group, but an individual does.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.3
Pali: Hañci na saṅgho kathinaṁ attharati, na gaṇo kathinaṁ attharati, puggalo kathinaṁ attharati. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha does not perform the robe-making ceremony, nor a group, but an individual does, then 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.4
Pali: Saṅghassa anatthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, gaṇassa anatthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, puggalassa atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the Sangha has not performed the robe-making ceremony, nor has a group, but an individual has. 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.5
Pali: Saṅgho pātimokkhaṁ uddisati gaṇo pātimokkhaṁ uddisati puggalo pātimokkhaṁ uddisatīti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Does the Sangha recite the Monastic Code? Does a group recite the Monastic Code? Does an individual recite the Monastic Code?” 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.6
Pali: Na saṅgho pātimokkhaṁ uddisati, na gaṇo pātimokkhaṁ uddisati, puggalo pātimokkhaṁ uddisatīti. 
English (brahmali, published): “The Sangha does not recite the Monastic Code, nor does a group, but an individual does.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.7
Pali: Hañci na saṅgho pātimokkhaṁ uddisati, na gaṇo pātimokkhaṁ uddisati, puggalo pātimokkhaṁ uddisati. 
English (brahmali, published): If the Sangha does not recite the Monastic Code, nor a group, but an individual does, then 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.8
Pali: Saṅghassa anuddiṭṭhaṁ hoti pātimokkhaṁ, gaṇassa anuddiṭṭhaṁ hoti pātimokkhaṁ, puggalassa uddiṭṭhaṁ hoti pātimokkhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the Sangha has not recited the Monastic Code, nor has a group, but an individual has. 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.9
Pali: Saṅghassa sāmaggiyā gaṇassa sāmaggiyā puggalassa uddesā saṅghassa uddiṭṭhaṁ hoti pātimokkhaṁ, gaṇassa uddiṭṭhaṁ hoti pātimokkhaṁ, puggalassa uddiṭṭhaṁ hoti pātimokkhaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “Yet when an individual recites to unite the Sangha, to unite the group, then the Monastic Code has been recited by the Sangha, by the group, and by the individual. 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.10
Pali: Evameva na saṅgho kathinaṁ attharati, na gaṇo kathinaṁ attharati, puggalo kathinaṁ attharati. 
English (brahmali, published): It is in this way that the Sangha does not perform the robe-making ceremony, nor does a group, but an individual does. 

pli-tv-pvr16:37.11
Pali: Saṅghassa anumodanāya gaṇassa anumodanāya puggalassa atthārā saṅghassa atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, gaṇassa atthataṁ hoti kathinaṁ, puggalassa atthataṁ hoti kathinanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Yet when an individual performs the robe-making ceremony, and the Sangha expresses its appreciation, the group expresses its appreciation, then the robe-making ceremony has been performed by the Sangha, by the group, and by the individual.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:38.0
Pali: 6. Palibodhapañhābyākaraṇa 
English (brahmali, published): 6. Questions and answers regarding the obstacles 

pli-tv-pvr16:38.1
Pali: Pakkamanantiko kathinuddhāro, 
English (brahmali, published): “The robe season ends when one departs from the monastery. 

pli-tv-pvr16:38.2
Pali: Vutto ādiccabandhunā; 
English (brahmali, published): So said the Kinsman of the Sun. 

pli-tv-pvr16:38.3
Pali: Etañca tāhaṁ pucchāmi, 
English (brahmali, published): And about this I ask you: 

pli-tv-pvr16:38.4
Pali: Katamo palibodho paṭhamaṁ chijjati. 
English (brahmali, published): Which obstacle is removed first? 

pli-tv-pvr16:39.1
Pali: Pakkamanantiko kathinuddhāro, 
English (brahmali, published): The robe season ends when one departs from the monastery. 

pli-tv-pvr16:39.2
Pali: Vutto ādiccabandhunā; 
English (brahmali, published): So said the Kinsman of the Sun. 

pli-tv-pvr16:39.3
Pali: Etañca tāhaṁ vissajjissaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): And about this I answer you: 

pli-tv-pvr16:39.4
Pali: Cīvarapalibodho paṭhamaṁ chijjati; 
English (brahmali, published): The robe obstacle is removed first. 

pli-tv-pvr16:39.5
Pali: Tassa saha bahisīmagamanā, 
English (brahmali, published): The monastery obstacle is removed when one goes outside the monastery zone.

pli-tv-pvr16:53.4
Pali: Dve palibodhā apubbaṁ acarimaṁ chijjantīti. 
English (brahmali, published): The two obstacles are removed simultaneously.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.1
Pali: Kati kathinuddhārā saṅghādhīnā? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many kinds of endings of the robe season depend on the Sangha? 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.2
Pali: Kati kathinuddhārā puggalādhīnā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of endings of the robe season depend on an individual? 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.3
Pali: Kati kathinuddhārā neva saṅghādhīnā na puggalādhīnā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of endings of the robe season depend neither on the Sangha nor on an individual? 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.4
Pali: Eko kathinuddhāro saṅghādhīno—
English (brahmali, published): One kind of ending of the robe season depends on the Sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.5
Pali: antarubbhāro. 
English (brahmali, published): when the robe season ends midway. 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.6
Pali: Cattāro kathinuddhārā puggalādhīnā—
English (brahmali, published): Four kinds of endings of the robe season depend on an individual: 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.7
Pali: pakkamanantiko, niṭṭhānantiko, sanniṭṭhānantiko, sīmātikkamanantiko. 
English (brahmali, published): when one departs from the monastery, when the robe is finished, when one makes a decision, and when one is outside the monastery zone. 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.8
Pali: Cattāro kathinuddhārā neva saṅghādhīnā na puggalādhīnā—
English (brahmali, published): Four kinds of endings of the robe season depend neither on the Sangha nor on an individual: 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.9
Pali: nāsanantiko, savanantiko, āsāvacchediko, sahubbhāro. 
English (brahmali, published): when the robe-cloth is lost, when one hears about the end of the robe season, when an expectation of more robe-cloth is disappointed, and when the robe season ends together. 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.10
Pali: Kati kathinuddhārā antosīmāya uddhariyyanti? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of endings of the robe season happen inside the monastery zone? 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.11
Pali: Kati kathinuddhārā bahisīmāya uddhariyyanti? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of endings of the robe season happen outside the monastery zone? 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.12
Pali: Kati kathinuddhārā siyā antosīmāya uddhariyyanti siyā bahisīmāya uddhariyyanti? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of endings of the robe season may happen either inside or outside the monastery zone? 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.13
Pali: Dve kathinuddhārā antosīmāya uddhariyyanti—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of endings of the robe season happen inside the monastery zone: 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.14
Pali: antarubbhāro, sahubbhāro. 
English (brahmali, published): when the robe season ends midway, and when the robe season ends together. 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.15
Pali: Tayo kathinuddhārā bahisīmāya uddhariyyanti—
English (brahmali, published): Three kinds of endings of the robe season happen outside the monastery zone: 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.16
Pali: pakkamanantiko, savanantiko, sīmātikkamanantiko. 
English (brahmali, published): when one departs from the monastery, when one hears about the end of the robe season, and when one is outside the monastery zone. 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.17
Pali: Cattāro kathinuddhārā siyā antosīmāya uddhariyyanti siyā bahisīmāya uddhariyyanti—
English (brahmali, published): Four kinds of endings of the robe season may happen inside or outside the monastery zone: 

pli-tv-pvr16:54.18
Pali: niṭṭhānantiko, sanniṭṭhānantiko, nāsanantiko, āsāvacchediko. 
English (brahmali, published): when the robe is finished, when one makes a decision, when the robe-cloth is lost, and when an expectation of more robe-cloth is disappointed. 

pli-tv-pvr16:55.1
Pali: Kati kathinuddhārā ekuppādā ekanirodhā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of endings of the robe season arise together and end together? 

pli-tv-pvr16:55.2
Pali: Kati kathinuddhārā ekuppādā nānānirodhā? 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of endings of the robe season arise together and end separately? 

pli-tv-pvr16:55.3
Pali: Dve kathinuddhārā ekuppādā ekanirodhā—
English (brahmali, published): Two kinds of endings of the robe season arise together and end together: 

pli-tv-pvr16:55.4
Pali: antarubbhāro, sahubbhāro. 
English (brahmali, published): when the robe season ends midway, and when the robe season ends together. 

pli-tv-pvr16:55.5
Pali: Avasesā kathinuddhārā ekuppādā nānānirodhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): The rest of the endings of the robe season arise together but end separately.” 

pli-tv-pvr16:56.1
Pali: Kathinabhedo niṭṭhito. 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr16:57.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr16:58.1
Pali: Kassa kinti pannarasa, 
English (brahmali, published): “Who has, how, fifteen, 

pli-tv-pvr16:58.2
Pali: dhammā nidānahetu ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Things, source, and cause; 

pli-tv-pvr16:58.3
Pali: Paccayasaṅgahamūlā, 
English (brahmali, published): Condition, grouped with, roots, 

pli-tv-pvr16:58.4
Pali: ādi ca atthārapuggalā. 
English (brahmali, published): And beginning, persons who perform the robe-making ceremony. 

pli-tv-pvr16:59.1
Pali: Tiṇṇaṁ tayo jānitabbaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): For three, three, to be known, 

pli-tv-pvr16:59.2
Pali: atthāraṁ uddesena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): The robe-making ceremony, and with recitation; 

pli-tv-pvr16:59.3
Pali: Palibodhādhinā sīmāya, 
English (brahmali, published): Obstacles, depend on, in a monastery zone, 

pli-tv-pvr16:59.4
Pali: uppādanirodhena cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): And with arise and end.”

pli-tv-pvr17:0.1
Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr17:0.2
Pali: 17. Upālipañcaka 
English (brahmali, published): Ven. Upāli questions the Buddha 

pli-tv-pvr17:0.3
Pali: 1. Anissitavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The subchapter on “without formal support” 

pli-tv-pvr17:1.1
Pali: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. 
English (brahmali, published): At one time the Buddha was staying at Sāvatthī in the Jeta Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery. 

pli-tv-pvr17:1.2
Pali: Atha kho āyasmā upāli yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. 
English (brahmali, published): Venerable Upāli went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down, 

pli-tv-pvr17:1.3
Pali: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā upāli bhagavantaṁ etadavoca—
English (brahmali, published): and said, 

pli-tv-pvr17:1.4
Pali: “katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ nānissitena vatthabban”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Venerable sir, what sort of monk should live with formal support for life?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:2.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ nānissitena vatthabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities, Upāli: 

pli-tv-pvr17:2.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:2.3
Pali: Uposathaṁ na jānāti, uposathakammaṁ na jānāti, pātimokkhaṁ na jānāti, pātimokkhuddesaṁ na jānāti, ūnapañcavasso hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t know about the observance-day ceremony; (2) he doesn’t know the observance-day procedure; (3) he doesn’t know the Monastic Code; (4) he doesn’t know the recitation of the Monastic Code; (5) he has less than five years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr17:2.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ nānissitena vatthabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:2.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ anissitena vatthabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities may live without formal support for life: 

pli-tv-pvr17:2.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:2.7
Pali: Uposathaṁ jānāti, uposathakammaṁ jānāti, pātimokkhaṁ jānāti, pātimokkhuddesaṁ jānāti, pañcavasso vā hoti atirekapañcavasso vā—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows about the observance-day ceremony; (2) he knows the observance-day procedure; (3) he knows the Monastic Code; (4) he knows the recitation of the Monastic Code; (5) he has five or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr17:2.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ anissitena vatthabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:3.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ nānissitena vatthabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who has five other qualities should also live with formal support for life: 

pli-tv-pvr17:3.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:3.3

English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t know about the invitation ceremony; (2) he doesn’t know the invitation procedure; (3) he doesn’t know the Monastic Code; (4) he doesn’t know the recitation of the Monastic Code; (5) he has less than five years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr17:3.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ nānissitena vatthabbaṁ.

pli-tv-pvr17:3.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:3.7
Pali: Pavāraṇaṁ jānāti, pavāraṇākammaṁ jānāti, pātimokkhaṁ jānāti, pātimokkhuddesaṁ jānāti, pañcavasso vā hoti atirekapañcavasso vā—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows about the invitation ceremony; (2) he knows the invitation procedure; (3) he knows the Monastic Code; (4) he knows the recitation of the Monastic Code; (5) he has five or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr17:3.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ anissitena vatthabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:4.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ nānissitena vatthabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who has five other qualities should also live with formal support for life: 

pli-tv-pvr17:4.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:4.3
Pali: Āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, ūnapañcavasso hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t know the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he doesn’t know the light and heavy offenses; (3) he doesn’t know the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he doesn’t know the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has less than five years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr17:4.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ nānissitena vatthabbaṁ.

Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ anissitena vatthabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities may live without formal support for life: 

pli-tv-pvr17:4.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:4.7
Pali: Āpattānāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, pañcavasso vā hoti atirekapañcavasso vā—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he knows the light and heavy offenses; (3) he knows the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he knows the grave and minor offenses; (5) he has five or more years of seniority. 

pli-tv-pvr17:4.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā yāvajīvaṁ anissitena vatthabbaṁ”. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:5.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā na upasampādetabbaṁ, na nissayo dātabbo, na sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, what sort of monk shouldn’t give the full ordination or formal support, nor have a novice monk attend on him?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:6.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā na upasampādetabbaṁ, na nissayo dātabbo, na sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:6.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:6.3
Pali: Na paṭibalo hoti antevāsiṁ vā saddhivihāriṁ vā gilānaṁ upaṭṭhātuṁ vā upaṭṭhāpetuṁ vā, anabhirataṁ vūpakāsetuṁ vā vūpakāsāpetuṁ vā, uppannaṁ kukkuccaṁ dhammato vinodetuṁ, abhidhamme vinetuṁ, abhivinaye vinetuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): He’s incapable of three things in regard to a student: (1) of nursing him or having him nursed when he’s sick; (2) of sending him away or having him sent away when he’s discontent with the spiritual life; and (3) of using the Teaching to dispel anxiety. And (4) he is incapable of training him in the Teaching; and (5) he is incapable of training him in the Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-pvr17:6.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā na upasampādetabbaṁ, na nissayo dātabbo, na sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:6.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ, nissayo dātabbo, sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities may give the full ordination and formal support, and he may have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr17:6.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:6.7
Pali: Paṭibalo hoti antevāsiṁ vā saddhivihāriṁ vā gilānaṁ upaṭṭhātuṁ vā upaṭṭhāpetuṁ vā, anabhirataṁ vūpakāsetuṁ vā vūpakāsāpetuṁ vā, uppannaṁ kukkuccaṁ dhammato vinodetuṁ, abhidhamme vinetuṁ, abhivinaye vinetuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): He’s capable of three things in regard to a student: (1) of nursing him or having him nursed when he’s sick; (2) of sending him away or having him sent away when he’s discontent with the spiritual life; and (3) of using the Teaching to dispel anxiety. And (4) he is capable of training him in the Teaching; and (5) he is capable of training him in the Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-pvr17:6.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ, nissayo dātabbo, sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:7.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā na upasampādetabbaṁ, na nissayo dātabbo, na sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who has five other qualities also shouldn’t give the full ordination or formal support, nor have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr17:7.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:7.3
Pali: Na paṭibalo hoti antevāsiṁ vā saddhivihāriṁ vā abhisamācārikāya sikkhāya sikkhāpetuṁ, ādibrahmacāriyakāya sikkhāya vinetuṁ, adhisīle vinetuṁ, adhicitte vinetuṁ, adhipaññāya vinetuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): He’s incapable of five things in regard to a student: (1) of training him in good conduct; (2) of training him in the basics of the spiritual life; (3) of training him in the higher morality; (4) of training him in the higher mind; (5) of training him in the higher wisdom. 

pli-tv-pvr17:7.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā na upasampādetabbaṁ, na nissayo dātabbo, na sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:7.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ, nissayo dātabbo, sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities may give the full ordination and formal support, and he may have a novice monk attend on him: 

pli-tv-pvr17:7.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:7.7
Pali: Paṭibalo hoti antevāsiṁ vā saddhivihāriṁ vā abhisamācārikāya sikkhāya sikkhāpetuṁ, ādibrahmacāriyakāya sikkhāya vinetuṁ, adhisīle vinetuṁ, adhicitte vinetuṁ, adhipaññāya vinetuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): He’s capable of five things in regard to a student: (1) of training him in good conduct; (2) of training him in the basics of the spiritual life; (3) of training him in the higher morality; (4) of training him in the higher mind; (5) of training him in the higher wisdom.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:7.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā upasampādetabbaṁ, nissayo dātabbo, sāmaṇero upaṭṭhāpetabbo”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:8.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabban”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, against what sort of monk should a legal procedure be done?”

English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk, and he has wrong view, and he has failed in livelihood.

English (brahmali, published): he has failed in the higher morality; he has failed in the higher conduct; he has failed in the higher view; he has wrong view; and he has failed in livelihood.

pli-tv-pvr17:12.2

English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:12.3

English (brahmali, published): he is improperly behaved by body; he is improperly behaved by speech; he is improperly behaved by body and speech; he has wrong view; and he has failed in livelihood.

Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ.

English (brahmali, published): his bodily conduct is harmful; his verbal conduct is harmful; his bodily and verbal conduct are harmful; he has wrong view; and he has failed in livelihood.

Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:14.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal procedure should also be done against a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:14.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:14.3
Pali: Kāyikena micchājīvena samannāgato hoti, vācasikena micchājīvena samannāgato hoti, kāyikavācasikena micchājīvena samannāgato hoti, micchādiṭṭhiko ca hoti, ājīvavipanno ca—
English (brahmali, published): he has wrong livelihood by body; he has wrong livelihood by speech; he has wrong livelihood by body and speech; he has wrong view; and he has failed in livelihood. 

pli-tv-pvr17:14.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:15.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal procedure should also be done against a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:15.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:15.3
Pali: Āpattiṁ āpanno kammakato upasampādeti, nissayaṁ deti, sāmaṇeraṁ upaṭṭhāpeti, bhikkhunovādakasammutiṁ sādiyati, sammatopi bhikkhuniyo ovadati—
English (brahmali, published): if, after committing an offense and having had a legal procedure done against him, he: (1) gives the full ordination, (2) gives formal support, (3) has a novice monk attend on him, (4) accepts being appointed as an instructor of the nuns, (5) instructs the nuns, whether appointed or not. 

pli-tv-pvr17:15.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:16.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal procedure should also be done against a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:16.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:16.3
Pali: Yāya āpattiyā saṅghena kammaṁ kataṁ hoti taṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati, aññaṁ vā tādisikaṁ, tato vā pāpiṭṭhataraṁ, kammaṁ garahati, kammike garahati—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he commits the same offense for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him; (2) he commits an offense similar to the one for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him; (3) he commits an offense worse than the one for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him; (4) he criticizes the procedure; (5) he criticizes those who did the procedure. 

pli-tv-pvr17:16.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ.

Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal procedure should also be done against a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:17.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:17.3
Pali: Buddhassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, dhammassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, saṅghassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, micchādiṭṭhiko ca hoti, ājīvavipanno ca—
English (brahmali, published): he disparages the Buddha; he disparages the Teaching; he disparages the Sangha; he has wrong view; and he has failed in livelihood.”

Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ kātabban”ti.

Pali: Anissitavaggo niṭṭhito paṭhamo.

English (brahmali, published): This is the summary:

English (brahmali, published): And offense, one who is sick;

pli-tv-pvr17:22.0
Pali: 2. Nappaṭippassambhanavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 2. The subchapter on not lifting 

pli-tv-pvr17:22.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabban”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, for what sort of monk should a legal procedure not be lifted?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:23.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “For one who has five qualities, Upāli: 

pli-tv-pvr17:23.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:23.3
Pali: Āpattiṁ āpanno kammakato upasampādeti, nissayaṁ deti, sāmaṇeraṁ upaṭṭhāpeti, bhikkhunovādakasammutiṁ sādiyati, sammatopi bhikkhuniyo ovadati—
English (brahmali, published): if, after committing an offense and having had a legal procedure done against him, he: (1) gives the full ordination, (2) gives formal support, (3) has a novice monk attend on him, (4) accepts being appointed as an instructor of the nuns, (5) instructs the nuns, whether appointed or not. 

pli-tv-pvr17:23.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabbaṁ.

pli-tv-pvr17:24.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal procedure should also not be lifted for a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:24.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:24.3
Pali: Yāya āpattiyā saṅghena kammaṁ kataṁ hoti taṁ āpattiṁ āpajjati, aññaṁ vā tādisikaṁ, tato vā pāpiṭṭhataraṁ, kammaṁ garahati, kammike garahati—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he commits the same offense for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him; (2) he commits an offense similar to the one for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him; (3) he commits an offense worse than the one for which the Sangha did the legal procedure against him; (4) he criticizes the procedure; (5) he criticizes those who did the procedure. 

pli-tv-pvr17:24.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabbaṁ.

pli-tv-pvr17:25.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal procedure should also not be lifted for a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:25.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi?

pli-tv-pvr17:25.3
Pali: Buddhassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, dhammassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, saṅghassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, micchādiṭṭhiko ca hoti, ājīvavipanno ca—
English (brahmali, published): he disparages the Buddha; he disparages the Teaching; he disparages the Sangha; he has wrong view; and he has failed in livelihood. 

pli-tv-pvr17:25.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:26.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): A legal procedure should also not be lifted for a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:26.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:26.3
Pali: Alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca, omaddakārako ca hoti, vattesu sikkhāya ca na paripūrakārī—
English (brahmali, published): he is shameless, ignorant, and not a regular monk, and he is a bully, and he doesn’t fulfill the training in proper conduct.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:26.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno kammaṁ nappaṭippassambhetabban”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:27.1
Pali: “Saṅgāmāvacarena, bhante, bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamantena kati dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā saṅgho upasaṅkamitabbo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, when a monk is involved in a conflict and is about to approach the Sangha, how many qualities should he first set up in himself?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:28.1
Pali: “Saṅgāmāvacarena, upāli, bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamantena pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā saṅgho upasaṅkamitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “He should set up five qualities in himself: 

pli-tv-pvr17:28.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:28.3
Pali: Saṅgāmāvacarena, upāli, bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamantena nīcacittena saṅgho upasaṅkamitabbo, rajoharaṇasamena cittena, āsanakusalena bhavitabbaṁ nissajjakusalena, there bhikkhū anupakhajjantena, nave bhikkhū āsanena appaṭibāhantena yathāpatirūpe āsane nisīditabbaṁ, anānākathikena bhavitabbaṁ atiracchānakathikena, sāmaṁ vā dhammo bhāsitabbo, paro vā ajjhesitabbo, ariyo vā tuṇhībhāvo nātimaññitabbo, sace, upāli, saṅgho samaggakaraṇīyāni kammāni karoti; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) he should be humble; (2) he should be intent on removing defilements; (3) he should be skilled in appropriate seating and where to sit down, taking a seat without encroaching on the senior monks and without blocking the junior monks; (4) he shouldn’t ramble or engage in worldly talk, but should speak according to the Teaching or invite others to speak or value noble silence; (5) if the Sangha is doing legal procedures requiring unity, but the monk doesn’t approve, then he should reveal his view but think, ‘I shouldn’t be at variance with the Sangha,’ and unity can then be announced.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:28.4
Pali: tatra ce, upāli, bhikkhuno nakkhamati, api diṭṭhāvikammaṁ katvā ñāpetabbā sāmaggī. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:28.5
Pali: Taṁ kissahetu? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:28.6
Pali: Māhaṁ saṅghena nānatto assanti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:28.7
Pali: Saṅgāmāvacarenupāli, bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamantena ime pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā saṅgho upasaṅkamitabbo”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:29.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanaakanto ca hoti bahujanaamanāpo ca bahujanaarucito cā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What sort of monk does the majority dislike and disapprove of when he speaks in the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:30.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanaakanto ca hoti bahujanaamanāpo ca bahujanarucito ca. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:30.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:30.3
Pali: Ussitamantī ca hoti, nissitajappī ca, na ca bhāsānusandhikusalo hoti, na yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā codetā hoti, na yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā kāretā hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he’s arrogant; (2) he repeats what others say; (3) he doesn’t keep to the topic; (4) he doesn’t accuse others according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, or their offense; (5) he doesn’t act according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, or his offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr17:30.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanaakanto ca hoti bahujanaamanāpo ca bahujanaarucito ca. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:30.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanakanto ca hoti bahujanamanāpo ca bahujanarucito ca. 
English (brahmali, published): But when a monk has five qualities, the majority likes and approves of him when he speaks in the Sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr17:30.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:30.7
Pali: Na ussitamantī ca hoti, na nissitajappī ca, bhāsānusandhikusalo ca hoti, yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā codetā hoti, yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā kāretā hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he’s not arrogant; (2) he doesn’t repeat what others say; (3) he keeps to the topic; (4) he accuses others according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and their offense; (5) he acts according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and his offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr17:30.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanakanto ca hoti bahujanamanāpo ca bahujanarucito ca. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:31.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanaakanto ca hoti bahujanaamanāpo ca bahujanaarucito ca. 
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has five other qualities, the majority dislikes and disapproves of him when he speaks in the Sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr17:31.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:31.3
Pali: Ussādetā ca hoti, apasādetā ca, adhammaṁ gaṇhāti, dhammaṁ paṭibāhati, samphañca bahuṁ bhāsati—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he praises; and (2) he blames; (3) he maintains what is contrary to the Teaching; (4) he obstructs what is in accordance with the Teaching; and (5) he often speaks frivolously. 

pli-tv-pvr17:31.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanaakanto ca hoti bahujanaamanāpo ca bahujanaarucito ca. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:31.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanakanto ca hoti bahujanamanāpo ca bahujanarucito ca. 
English (brahmali, published): But when a monk has five qualities, the majority likes and approves of him when he speaks in the Sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr17:31.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:31.7
Pali: Na ussādetā ca hoti, na apasādetā ca, dhammaṁ gaṇhāti, adhammaṁ paṭibāhati, samphañca na bahuṁ bhāsati—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t praise; and (2) he doesn’t blame; (3) he maintains what is in accordance with the Teaching; (4) he obstructs what is contrary to the Teaching; and (5) he rarely speaks frivolously. 

pli-tv-pvr17:31.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanakanto ca hoti bahujanamanāpo ca bahujanarucito ca. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:32.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanaakanto ca hoti bahujanaamanāpo ca bahujanaarucito ca. 
English (brahmali, published): When a monk has five other qualities, the majority dislikes and disapproves of him when he speaks in the Sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr17:32.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:32.3
Pali: Pasayhapavattā hoti, anokāsakammaṁ kāretvā pavattā hoti, na yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā codetā hoti, na yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā kāretā hoti, na yathādiṭṭhiyā byākatā hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he speaks forcefully; (2) he speaks without having gotten permission; (3) he doesn’t accuse others according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, or their offense; (4) he doesn’t act according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, or his offenses; (5) he doesn’t explain things according to his own view. 

pli-tv-pvr17:32.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanaakanto ca hoti bahujanaamanāpo ca bahujanaarucito ca. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:32.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanakanto ca hoti bahujanamanāpo ca bahujanarucito ca. 
English (brahmali, published): But when a monk has five qualities, the majority likes and approves of him when he speaks in the Sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr17:32.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:32.7
Pali: Na pasayhapavattā hoti, okāsakammaṁ kāretvā pavattā hoti, yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā codetā hoti, yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā kāretā hoti, yathādiṭṭhiyā byākatā hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t speak forcefully; (2) he doesn’t speak without having gotten permission; (3) he accuses others according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and their offense; (4) he acts according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and his offenses; (5) he explains things according to his own view.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:32.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu saṅghe voharanto bahujanakanto ca hoti bahujanamanāpo ca bahujanarucito cā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:33.1
Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, ānisaṁsā vinayapariyattiyā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how many benefits are there of studying the Monastic Law?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:34.1
Pali: “Pañcime, upāli, ānisaṁsā vinayapariyattiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are these five benefits: 

pli-tv-pvr17:34.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:34.3
Pali: Attano sīlakkhandho sugutto hoti surakkhito, kukkuccapakatānaṁ paṭisaraṇaṁ hoti, visārado saṅghamajjhe voharati, paccatthike sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggaṇhāti, saddhammaṭṭhitiyā paṭipanno hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) your own morality is well guarded; (2) you’re a refuge for those who are habitually anxious; (3) you speak with confidence in the midst of the Sangha; (4) you can legitimately and properly refute an opponent; (5) you’re practicing for the longevity of the true Teaching.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:34.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcānisaṁsā vinayapariyattiyā”. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:35.1
Pali: Nappaṭippassambhanavaggo niṭṭhito dutiyo. 
English (brahmali, published): The second subchapter on not lifting is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr17:36.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr17:37.1
Pali: Āpanno yāyavaṇṇañca, 
English (brahmali, published): “Committing, for which, and praise, 

pli-tv-pvr17:37.2
Pali: alajjī saṅgāmena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Shameless, and with conflict; 

pli-tv-pvr17:37.3
Pali: Ussitā ussādetā ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Arrogant, and praises, 

pli-tv-pvr17:37.4
Pali: pasayha pariyattiyāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Forcefully, studying.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:38.1
Pali: Paṭhamayamakapaññatti. 
English (brahmali, published): The first pairs have been laid down. 

pli-tv-pvr17:39.0
Pali: 3. Vohāravagga 
English (brahmali, published): 3. The subchapter on speech 

pli-tv-pvr17:39.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā saṅghe na voharitabban”ti 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, what sort of monk shouldn’t speak in the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:40.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā saṅghe na voharitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities, Upāli: 

pli-tv-pvr17:40.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:40.3
Pali: Āpattiṁ na jānāti, āpattisamuṭṭhānaṁ na jānāti, āpattiyā payogaṁ na jānāti, āpattiyā vūpasamaṁ na jānāti, āpattiyā na vinicchayakusalo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t know the offenses; (2) he doesn’t know the origination of the offenses; (3) he doesn’t know the kind of effort required to commit the offenses; (4) he doesn’t know the settling of offenses; (5) he’s not skilled in deciding on offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr17:40.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā saṅghe na voharitabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:40.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā saṅghe voharitabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities may speak in the Sangha: 

pli-tv-pvr17:40.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:40.7
Pali: Āpattiṁ jānāti, āpattisamuṭṭhānaṁ jānāti, āpattiyā payogaṁ jānāti, āpattiyā vūpasamaṁ jānāti, āpattiyā vinicchayakusalo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses; (2) he knows the origination of the offenses; (3) he knows the kind of effort required to commit the offenses; (4) he knows the settling of offenses; (5) he’s skilled in deciding on offenses.

English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t know the legal issues; (2) he doesn’t know the origination of the legal issues; (3) he doesn’t know the kind of effort that is the source of the legal issues; (4) he doesn’t know the settling of legal issues; (5) he’s not skilled in deciding legal issues.

English (brahmali, published): (1) he speaks forcefully; (2) he speaks without having gotten permission; (3) he doesn’t accuse others according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, or their offense; (4) he doesn’t act according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, or his offenses; (5) he doesn’t explain things according to his own view.

pli-tv-pvr17:54.0
Pali: 4. Diṭṭhāvikammavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 4. The subchapter on revealing one’s view 

pli-tv-pvr17:54.1
Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, adhammikā diṭṭhāvikammā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how many illegitimate kinds of revealing one’s view are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:55.1
Pali: “Pañcime, upāli, adhammikā diṭṭhāvikammā. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are five, Upāli: 

pli-tv-pvr17:55.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:55.3
Pali: Anāpattiyā diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti, adesanāgāminiyā āpattiyā diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti, desitāya āpattiyā diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti, catūhi pañcahi diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti, mano mānasena diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) one reveals a view about a non-offense; (2) one reveals a view about an offense that isn’t clearable by confession; (3) one reveals a view about an offense that has been confessed; (4) one reveals a view to four or five people; (5) one reveals a view by mind. 

pli-tv-pvr17:55.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca adhammikā diṭṭhāvikammā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:56.1
Pali: Pañcime, upāli, dhammikā diṭṭhāvikammā. 
English (brahmali, published): But there are five legitimate kinds of revealing one’s view: 

pli-tv-pvr17:56.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:56.3
Pali: Āpattiyā diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti, desanāgāminiyā āpattiyā diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti, adesitāya āpattiyā diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti, na catūhi pañcahi diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti, na mano mānasena diṭṭhiṁ āvi karoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) one reveals a view about an offense; (2) one reveals a view about an offense that’s clearable by confession; (3) one reveals a view about an offense that hasn’t been confessed; (4) one doesn’t reveal a view to four or five people; (5) one doesn’t reveal a view by mind.

English (brahmali, published): (1) when someone gives by body and one doesn’t receive by body; (2) when someone gives by body and one doesn’t receive with something connected to the body; (3) when someone gives with something connected to the body and one doesn’t receive by body; (4) when someone gives with something connected to the body and one doesn’t receive with something connected to the body; (5) when someone gives by releasing and one doesn’t receive by body or with something connected to the body.

English (brahmali, published): (1) the making it left over is done with food that’s unallowable; (2) it’s done with food that hasn’t been received; (3) it’s done with food that’s not held in hand; (4) it’s done by one who’s not within arm’s reach; (5) ‘I’ve had enough,’ hasn’t been said.

English (brahmali, published): (1) A monk has committed an offense entailing expulsion. When he’s accused of having committed such an offense, he admits to committing an offense entailing suspension. English (brahmali, published): one asks (1) because of stupidity and folly; (2) because one is overcome by bad desires; (3) because of contempt; (4) because one desires to know; (5) because of the thought, ‘If he explains correctly when I ask him, all is well, but if he doesn’t, I’ll explain it correctly to him.’”

pli-tv-pvr17:81.3
Pali: Nidānaṁ uddisitvā avasesaṁ sutena sāvetabbaṁ ayaṁ paṭhamā visuddhi, nidānaṁ uddisitvā cattāri pārājikāni uddisitvā avasesaṁ sutena sāvetabbaṁ ayaṁ dutiyā visuddhi, nidānaṁ uddisitvā cattāri pārājikāni uddisitvā terasa saṅghādisese uddisitvā avasesaṁ sutena sāvetabbaṁ ayaṁ tatiyā visuddhi, nidānaṁ uddisitvā cattāri pārājikāni uddisitvā terasa saṅghādisese uddisitvā dve aniyate uddisitvā avasesaṁ sutena sāvetabbaṁ ayaṁ catutthā visuddhi, vitthāreneva pañcamī—
English (brahmali, published): (1) After reciting the introduction, the rest is announced as if heard. (2) After reciting the introduction and the four rules entailing expulsion, the rest is announced as if heard. (3) After reciting the introduction, the four rules entailing expulsion, and the thirteen rules entailing suspension, the rest is announced as if heard. (4) After reciting the introduction, the four rules entailing expulsion, the thirteen rules entailing suspension, and the two undetermined rules, the rest is announced as if heard. (5) In full is the fifth.”

English (brahmali, published): “There are five: 

pli-tv-pvr17:83.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:83.3
Pali: Odano, kummāso, sattu, maccho, maṁsaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): cooked grain, porridge, flour products, fish, and meat.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:83.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca bhojanā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:84.1
Pali: Diṭṭhāvikammavaggo niṭṭhito catuttho. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourth subchapter on revealing one’s view is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr17:85.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr17:86.1
Pali: Diṭṭhāvikammā apare, 
English (brahmali, published): “Revealing one’s view, other, 

pli-tv-pvr17:86.2
Pali: paṭiggahānatirittā; 
English (brahmali, published): Receiving, not left over; 

pli-tv-pvr17:86.3
Pali: Pavāraṇā paṭiññātaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Refusing an invitation to eat more, according to what has been admitted, 

pli-tv-pvr17:86.4
Pali: okāsaṁ sākacchena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Permission, and with discussion; 

pli-tv-pvr17:86.5
Pali: Pañhaṁ aññabyākaraṇā, 
English (brahmali, published): Question, declarations of perfect insight, 

pli-tv-pvr17:86.6
Pali: visuddhi cāpi bhojanāti. 
English (brahmali, published): And also purification, cooked food.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:87.0
Pali: 5. Attādānavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 5. The subchapter on raising an issue 

pli-tv-pvr17:87.1
Pali: “Codakena, bhante, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena kati dhamme ajjhattaṁ paccavekkhitvā paro codetabbo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how many qualities should a monk see in himself before accusing another?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:88.1
Pali: “Codakenupāli, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ paccavekkhitvā paro codetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “He should see five qualities in himself: 

pli-tv-pvr17:88.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:88.3
Pali: Codakenupāli, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (1) He should reflect: 

pli-tv-pvr17:89.1
Pali: ‘Parisuddhakāyasamācāro nu khomhi, parisuddhenamhi kāyasamācārena samannāgato acchiddena appaṭimaṁsena, saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Is my bodily conduct pure and flawless? Is this quality found in me or not?’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:89.2
Pali: No ce, upāli, bhikkhu parisuddhakāyasamācāro hoti, parisuddhena kāyasamācārena samannāgato acchiddena appaṭimaṁsena, tassa bhavanti vattāro—
English (brahmali, published): If it’s not, there will be those who say, 

pli-tv-pvr17:89.3
Pali: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā kāyikaṁ sikkhassū’ti itissa bhavanti vattāro. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please train your own bodily conduct first.’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:90.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, codakena bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (2) He should reflect: 

pli-tv-pvr17:90.2
Pali: ‘parisuddhavacīsamācāro nu khomhi, parisuddhenamhi vacīsamācārena samannāgato acchiddena appaṭimaṁsena, saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Is my verbal conduct pure and flawless? Is this quality found in me or not?’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:90.3
Pali: No ce, upāli, bhikkhu parisuddhavacīsamācāro hoti, parisuddhena vacīsamācārena samannāgato acchiddena appaṭimaṁsena, tassa bhavanti vattāro—
English (brahmali, published): If it’s not, there will be those who say, 

pli-tv-pvr17:90.4
Pali: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā vācasikaṁ sikkhassū’ti itissa bhavanti vattāro. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please train your own verbal conduct first.’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:91.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, codakena bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (3) He should reflect: 

pli-tv-pvr17:91.2
Pali: ‘mettaṁ nu kho me cittaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ sabrahmacārīsu anāghātaṁ, saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Do I have a mind of good will toward my fellow monastics, a mind free from anger? Is this quality found in me or not?’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:91.3
Pali: No ce, upāli, bhikkhuno mettaṁ cittaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti sabrahmacārīsu anāghātaṁ, tassa bhavanti vattāro—
English (brahmali, published): If it’s not, there will be those who say, 

pli-tv-pvr17:91.4
Pali: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā sabrahmacārīsu mettaṁ cittaṁ upaṭṭhāpehī’ti itissa bhavanti vattāro. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please set up a mind of good will toward your fellow monastics first.’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:92.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, codakena bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (4) He should reflect: 

pli-tv-pvr17:92.2
Pali: ‘bahussuto nu khomhi sutadharo sutasannicayo, ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpā me dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā, saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Have I learned much and do I retain and accumulate what I’ve learned? Those teachings that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, that have a true goal and are well articulated, and that set out the perfectly complete and pure spiritual life—have I learned many such teachings, retained them in mind, recited them verbally, mentally investigated them, and penetrated them well by view? Is this quality found in me or not?’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:92.3
Pali: No ce, upāli, bhikkhu bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo, ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpassa dhammā na bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā, tassa bhavanti vattāro—
English (brahmali, published): If it’s not, there will be those who say, 

pli-tv-pvr17:92.4
Pali: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā āgamaṁ pariyāpuṇassū’ti itissa bhavanti vattāro. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please learn the tradition first.’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:93.1
Pali: Puna caparaṁ, upāli, codakena bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (5) He should reflect: 

pli-tv-pvr17:93.2
Pali: ‘ubhayāni kho me pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso, saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Have I properly learned both Monastic Codes in detail; have I analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, and investigated them well, both in terms of the rules and their detailed exposition? Is this quality found in me or not?’ 

pli-tv-pvr17:93.3
Pali: No ce, upāli, bhikkhuno ubhayāni pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso, ‘idaṁ panāvuso kattha vuttaṁ bhagavatā’ti iti puṭṭho na sampāyati, (…) tassa bhavanti vattāro—
English (brahmali, published): If it’s not, then when he’s asked, ‘Where was this said by the Buddha?’ he won’t be able to reply. And there will be those who say, 

pli-tv-pvr17:93.4
Pali: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā vinayaṁ pariyāpuṇassū’ti itissa bhavanti vattāro. 
English (brahmali, published): ‘Please learn the Monastic Law first.’” 

pli-tv-pvr17:93.5
Pali: Codakenupāli, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena ime pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ paccavekkhitvā paro codetabbo”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:94.1
Pali: “Codakena, bhante, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena kati dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā paro codetabbo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how many qualities should a monk set up in himself before accusing another?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:95.1
Pali: “Codakenupāli, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā paro codetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “He should set up five qualities in himself: 

pli-tv-pvr17:95.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:95.3
Pali: Kālena vakkhāmi no akālena, bhūtena vakkhāmi no abhūtena, saṇhena vakkhāmi no pharusena, atthasaṁhitena vakkhāmi no anatthasaṁhitena, mettācitto vakkhāmi no dosantaroti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) ‘I’ll speak at an appropriate time, not at an inappropriate one; (2) I’ll speak the truth, not falsehood; (3) I’ll speak gently, not harshly; (4) I’ll speak what’s beneficial, not what’s unbeneficial; (5) I’ll speak with a mind of good will, not with ill will.’” 

pli-tv-pvr17:95.4
Pali: codakenupāli, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena ime pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā paro codetabbo”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:96.1
Pali: “Codakena, bhante, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena kati dhamme ajjhattaṁ manasi karitvā paro codetabbo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many qualities should a monk attend to in himself before accusing another?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:97.1
Pali: “Codakenupāli, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ manasi karitvā paro codetabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “He should attend to five qualities in himself: 

pli-tv-pvr17:97.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:97.3
Pali: Kāruññatā, hitesitā, anukampatā, āpattivuṭṭhānatā, vinayapurekkhāratā—
English (brahmali, published): compassion, being of benefit, sympathy, the idea of clearing offenses, and the idea of prioritizing the training.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:97.4
Pali: codakenupāli, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena ime pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ manasi karitvā paro codetabbo”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:98.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno okāsakammaṁ kārāpentassa nālaṁ okāsakammaṁ kātun”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What sort of monk is unqualified to ask for permission to correct someone?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:99.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno okāsakammaṁ kārāpentassa nālaṁ okāsakammaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:99.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:99.3
Pali: Aparisuddhakāyasamācāro hoti, aparisuddhavacīsamācāro hoti, aparisuddhājīvo hoti, bālo hoti abyatto, na paṭibalo anuyuñjiyamāno anuyogaṁ dātuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he’s impure in bodily conduct; (2) he’s impure in verbal conduct; (3) he’s impure in livelihood; (4) he’s ignorant and incompetent; (5) he’s incapable of answering properly when questioned.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:99.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno okāsakammaṁ kārāpentassa nālaṁ okāsakammaṁ kātuṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:100.1
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno okāsakammaṁ kārāpentassa alaṁ okāsakammaṁ kātuṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities is qualified to ask for permission to correct someone: 

pli-tv-pvr17:100.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:100.3
Pali: Parisuddhakāyasamācāro hoti, parisuddhavacīsamācāro hoti, parisuddhājīvo hoti, paṇḍito hoti byatto paṭibalo anuyuñjiyamāno anuyogaṁ dātuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he’s pure in bodily conduct; (2) he’s pure in verbal conduct; (3) he’s pure in livelihood; (4) he’s knowledgeable and competent; (5) he’s capable of answering properly when questioned.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:100.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno okāsakammaṁ kārāpentassa alaṁ okāsakammaṁ kātun”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:101.1
Pali: “Attādānaṁ ādātukāmena, bhante, bhikkhunā katihaṅgehi samannāgataṁ attādānaṁ ādātabban”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, if a monk wishes to raise an issue, what factors should be fulfilled?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:102.1

Pali: Katame pañca? 
English:
