English (brahmali, published): He should reflect 

pli-tv-pvr17:102.4
Pali: ‘yaṁ kho ahaṁ imaṁ attādānaṁ ādātukāmo, kālo nu kho imaṁ attādānaṁ ādātuṁ udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): whether it’s the right time to raise it. 

pli-tv-pvr17:102.5
Pali: Sace, upāli, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): If he knows 

pli-tv-pvr17:102.6
Pali: ‘akālo imaṁ attādānaṁ ādātuṁ no kālo’ti, na taṁ, upāli, attādānaṁ ādātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it’s the wrong time, he shouldn’t raise it. 

pli-tv-pvr17:103.1
Pali: Sace panupāli, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) But if he knows 

pli-tv-pvr17:103.2
Pali: ‘kālo imaṁ attādānaṁ ādātuṁ no akālo’ti, tenupāli, bhikkhunā uttari paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): it’s the right time, he should reflect further 

pli-tv-pvr17:103.3
Pali: ‘yaṁ kho ahaṁ imaṁ attādānaṁ ādātukāmo bhūtaṁ nu kho idaṁ attādānaṁ udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): whether it’s a real issue. 

pli-tv-pvr17:103.4
Pali: Sace, upāli, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): If he knows 

pli-tv-pvr17:103.5
Pali: ‘abhūtaṁ idaṁ attādānaṁ no bhūtan’ti, na taṁ, upāli, attādānaṁ ādātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it’s not, he shouldn’t raise it. 

pli-tv-pvr17:104.1
Pali: Sace panupāli, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (2) But if he knows 

pli-tv-pvr17:104.2
Pali: ‘bhūtaṁ idaṁ attādānaṁ no abhūtan’ti, tenupāli, bhikkhunā uttari paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): it is, he should reflect further 

pli-tv-pvr17:104.3
Pali: ‘yaṁ kho ahaṁ imaṁ attādānaṁ ādātukāmo, atthasaṁhitaṁ nu kho idaṁ attādānaṁ udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): whether raising the issue will be beneficial. 

pli-tv-pvr17:104.4
Pali: Sace, upāli, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): If he knows 

pli-tv-pvr17:104.5
Pali: ‘anatthasaṁhitaṁ idaṁ attādānaṁ no atthasaṁhitan’ti, na taṁ, upāli, attādānaṁ ādātabbaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): it won’t, he shouldn’t raise it. 

pli-tv-pvr17:105.1
Pali: Sace panupāli, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (3) But if he knows 

pli-tv-pvr17:105.2
Pali: ‘atthasaṁhitaṁ idaṁ attādānaṁ no anatthasaṁhitan’ti, tenupāli, bhikkhunā uttari paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): it will, he should reflect further 

pli-tv-pvr17:105.3
Pali: ‘imaṁ kho ahaṁ attādānaṁ ādiyamāno labhissāmi sandiṭṭhe sambhatte bhikkhū dhammato vinayato pakkhe udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): whether the monks who are on the side of the Teaching and the Monastic Law will support him. 

pli-tv-pvr17:105.4

English (brahmali, published): that they won’t, he shouldn’t raise it. 

pli-tv-pvr17:106.1
Pali: Sace panupāli, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (4) But if he knows 

pli-tv-pvr17:106.2
Pali: ‘idaṁ kho ahaṁ attādānaṁ ādiyamāno labhissāmi sandiṭṭhe sambhatte bhikkhū dhammato vinayato pakkhe’ti, tenupāli, bhikkhunā uttari paccavekkhitabbaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): that they will, he should reflect further 

pli-tv-pvr17:106.3
Pali: ‘imaṁ kho me attādānaṁ ādiyato bhavissati saṅghassa tatonidānaṁ bhaṇḍanaṁ kalaho viggaho vivādo saṅghabhedo saṅgharāji saṅghavavatthānaṁ saṅghanānākaraṇaṁ udāhu no’ti. 
English (brahmali, published): whether raising the issue will lead to arguments and disputes, to fracture and schism in the Sangha.

English (brahmali, published): it will, he shouldn’t raise it. 

pli-tv-pvr17:107.1
Pali: Sace panupāli, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (5) But if he knows

English (brahmali, published): it won’t, he may raise it. 

pli-tv-pvr17:107.3
Pali: Evaṁ pañcaṅgasamannāgataṁ kho, upāli, attādānaṁ ādinnaṁ pacchāpi avippaṭisārakaraṁ bhavissatī”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, when five factors are fulfilled, he won’t regret raising that issue.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:108.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu adhikaraṇajātānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bahūpakāro hotī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What sort of monk is of great help to monks involved in a legal issue?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:109.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu adhikaraṇajātānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bahūpakāro hoti. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:109.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:109.3
Pali: Sīlavā hoti, pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī, samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu; 
English (brahmali, published): (1) He’s virtuous and restrained by the Monastic Code. His conduct is good, he associates with the right people, and he sees danger in minor faults. He undertakes and trains in the training rules. 

pli-tv-pvr17:109.4
Pali: bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo; 
English (brahmali, published): (2) He has learned much, and he retains and accumulates what he has learned. 

pli-tv-pvr17:109.5
Pali: ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti tathārūpassa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā, ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso; 
English (brahmali, published): (3) Those teachings that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, that have a true goal and are well articulated, and that set out the perfectly complete and pure spiritual life—he has learned many such teachings, retained them in mind, recited them verbally, mentally investigated them, and penetrated them well by view. 

pli-tv-pvr17:109.6
Pali: vinaye kho pana ṭhito hoti asaṁhīro; 
English (brahmali, published): (4) He’s firmly committed to the Monastic Law. 

pli-tv-pvr17:109.7
Pali: paṭibalo hoti ubho atthapaccatthike assāsetuṁ saññāpetuṁ nijjhāpetuṁ pekkhetuṁ pasādetuṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (5) He’s capable of making both sides relax, of persuading them, of convincing them, of making them see, of reconciling them. 

pli-tv-pvr17:109.8

English (brahmali, published): he’s pure in bodily conduct; he’s pure in verbal conduct; he’s pure in livelihood; he’s knowledgeable and competent; he’s capable of answering properly when questioned.

pli-tv-pvr17:126.0
Pali: 6. Dhutaṅgavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 6. The subchapter on ascetic practices 

pli-tv-pvr17:126.1
Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, āraññikā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how many kinds of wilderness dwellers are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:126.2
Pali: “Pañcime, upāli, āraññikā. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are five kinds: 

pli-tv-pvr17:126.3
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:126.4
Pali: Mandattā momūhattā āraññiko hoti, pāpiccho icchāpakato āraññiko hoti, ummādā cittakkhepā āraññiko hoti, vaṇṇitaṁ buddhehi buddhasāvakehīti āraññiko hoti, api ca appicchaññeva nissāya santuṭṭhiññeva nissāya, sallekhaññeva nissāya pavivekaññeva nissāya idamatthitaññeva nissāya āraññiko hoti—
English (brahmali, published): those who are wilderness dwellers: (1) because of stupidity and folly; (2) because they are overcome by bad desires; (3) because of insanity and derangement; (4) because it is praised by the Buddhas and their disciples; (5) because of fewness of wishes, contentment, self-effacement, seclusion, and not needing anything else.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:126.5
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca āraññikā”ti. 
English:

Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, piṇḍapātikāti …pe…

pli-tv-pvr17:131.0
Pali: 7. Musāvādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 7. The subchapter on lying 

pli-tv-pvr17:131.1
Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, musāvādā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how many kinds of lying are there?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:131.2
Pali: “Pañcime, upāli, musāvādā. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are five kinds: 

pli-tv-pvr17:131.3
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:131.4
Pali: Atthi musāvādo pārājikagāmī, atthi musāvādo saṅghādisesagāmī, atthi musāvādo thullaccayagāmī, atthi musāvādo pācittiyagāmī, atthi musāvādo dukkaṭagāmī—
English (brahmali, published): (1) there’s lying that leads to an offense entailing expulsion; (2) there’s lying that leads to an offense entailing suspension; (3) there’s lying that leads to a serious offense; (4) there’s lying that leads to an offense entailing confession; (5) there’s lying that leads to an offense of wrong conduct.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:131.5
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca musāvādā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:132.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno saṅghamajjhe uposathaṁ vā pavāraṇaṁ vā ṭhapentassa—‘alaṁ, bhikkhu, mā bhaṇḍanaṁ, mā kalahaṁ, mā viggahaṁ, mā vivādan’ti omadditvā saṅghena uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā kātabbā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “A monk may be canceling someone’s observance day or invitation in the midst of the Sangha. Among such monks, what sort should be pressed: ‘Enough. No more arguing and disputing,’ with the Sangha then doing the observance-day ceremony or the invitation ceremony?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:133.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno saṅghamajjhe uposathaṁ vā pavāraṇaṁ vā ṭhapentassa—‘alaṁ, bhikkhu, mā bhaṇḍanaṁ, mā kalahaṁ, mā viggahaṁ, mā vivādan’ti omadditvā saṅghena uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā kātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “A monk who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:133.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:133.3
Pali: Alajjī ca hoti, bālo ca, apakatatto ca, cāvanādhippāyo vattā hoti, no vuṭṭhānādhippāyo—
English (brahmali, published): he’s shameless; he’s ignorant; he’s not a regular monk; he speaks to make someone disrobe, not with the aim of clearing their offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr17:133.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno saṅghamajjhe uposathaṁ vā pavāraṇaṁ vā ṭhapentassa—
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:133.5
Pali: ‘alaṁ, bhikkhu, mā bhaṇḍanaṁ, mā kalahaṁ, mā viggahaṁ, mā vivādan’ti omadditvā saṅghena uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā kātabbā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:134.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno saṅghamajjhe uposathaṁ vā pavāraṇaṁ vā ṭhapentassa—‘alaṁ, bhikkhu, mā bhaṇḍanaṁ, mā kalahaṁ, mā viggahaṁ, mā vivādan’ti omadditvā saṅghena uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā kātabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): The same procedure should be followed also for a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:134.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:134.3
Pali: Aparisuddhakāyasamācāro hoti, aparisuddhavacīsamācāro hoti, aparisuddhājīvo hoti, bālo hoti abyatto, bhaṇḍanakārako hoti kalahakārako—
English (brahmali, published): he’s impure in bodily conduct; he’s impure in verbal conduct; he’s impure in livelihood; he’s ignorant and incompetent; he’s quarrelsome and argumentative.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:134.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno saṅghamajjhe uposathaṁ vā pavāraṇaṁ vā ṭhapentassa—‘alaṁ, bhikkhu, mā bhaṇḍanaṁ, mā kalahaṁ, mā viggahaṁ, mā vivādan’ti omadditvā saṅghena uposatho vā pavāraṇā vā kātabbā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:135.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno anuyogo na dātabbo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What sort of monk shouldn’t be allowed to question?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:136.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno anuyogo na dātabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:136.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:136.3
Pali: Āpattānāpattiṁ na jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti, sappaṭikammāpaṭikammaṁ āpattiṁ na jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t know the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he doesn’t know the light and heavy offenses; (3) he doesn’t know the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he doesn’t know the grave and minor offenses; (5) he doesn’t know the offenses that are clearable by making amends and the offenses that are not clearable by making amends. 

pli-tv-pvr17:136.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno anuyogo na dātabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:137.1
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno anuyogo dātabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five other qualities may question: 

pli-tv-pvr17:137.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:137.3
Pali: Āpattānāpattiṁ jānāti, lahukagarukaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sāvasesānavasesaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, duṭṭhullāduṭṭhullaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti, sappaṭikammāpaṭikammaṁ āpattiṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the offenses and non-offenses; (2) he knows the light and heavy offenses; (3) he knows the curable and incurable offenses; (4) he knows the grave and minor offenses; (5) he knows the offenses that are clearable by making amends and the offenses that are not clearable by making amends.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:137.4

English:

pli-tv-pvr17:138.2
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, ākārehi bhikkhu āpattiṁ āpajjati.

Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English:

English (brahmali, published): because of shamelessness; because of ignorance; because of being overcome by anxiety; because of perceiving what’s unallowable as allowable; because of perceiving what’s allowable as unallowable.

English (brahmali, published): because of not seeing; because of not hearing; because of sleeping; because of perceiving it as allowable; because of absentmindedness.”

pli-tv-pvr17:147.0
Pali: 8. Bhikkhunovādavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 8. The subchapter on instructing the nuns 

pli-tv-pvr17:147.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunisaṅgheneva kammaṁ kātabban”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, against what sort of monk should the Sangha of nuns do a legal procedure?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:148.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunisaṅgheneva kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, avandiyo so bhikkhu bhikkhunisaṅghena. 
English (brahmali, published): “They should do a legal procedure, prohibiting the Sangha of nuns from paying respect to him, against a monk who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:148.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:148.3
Pali: Vivaritvā kāyaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ dasseti, ūruṁ dasseti, aṅgajātaṁ dasseti, ubho aṁsakūṭe dasseti, obhāsati, gihī sampayojeti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he exposes his body to the nuns; (2) he exposes his thighs to the nuns; (3) he exposes his genitals to the nuns; (4) he exposes both shoulders to the nuns; (5) he speaks indecently to the nuns; he associates inappropriately with householders. 

pli-tv-pvr17:148.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunisaṅgheneva kammaṁ kātabbaṁ. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:148.5
Pali: Avandiyo so bhikkhu bhikkhunisaṅghena. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:149.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunisaṅgheneva kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, avandiyo so bhikkhu bhikkhunisaṅghena. 
English (brahmali, published): They should do the same legal procedure also against a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:149.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:149.3
Pali: Bhikkhunīnaṁ alābhāya parisakkati, bhikkhunīnaṁ anatthāya parisakkati, bhikkhunīnaṁ avāsāya parisakkati, bhikkhuniyo akkosati paribhāsati, bhikkhū bhikkhunīhi bhedeti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he’s trying to stop nuns from getting material support; (2) he’s trying to harm nuns; (3) he’s trying to get nuns to lose their place of residence; (4) he abuses and reviles nuns; (5) he causes division between the monks and the nuns. 

pli-tv-pvr17:149.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunisaṅgheneva kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, avandiyo so bhikkhu bhikkhunisaṅghena. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:150.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunisaṅgheneva kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, avandiyo so bhikkhu bhikkhunisaṅghena. 
English (brahmali, published): They should do the same legal procedure also against a monk who has five other qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:150.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:150.3
Pali: Bhikkhunīnaṁ alābhāya parisakkati, bhikkhunīnaṁ anatthāya parisakkati, bhikkhunīnaṁ avāsāya parisakkati, bhikkhuniyo akkosati paribhāsati, bhikkhū bhikkhunīhi sampayojeti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he’s trying to stop nuns from getting material support; (2) he’s trying to harm nuns; (3) he’s trying to get nuns to lose their place of residence; (4) he abuses and reviles nuns; (5) he causes the monks to associate inappropriately with the nuns.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:150.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatassa bhikkhuno bhikkhunisaṅgheneva kammaṁ kātabbaṁ, avandiyo so bhikkhu bhikkhunisaṅghenā”ti.

pli-tv-pvr17:156.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgatena bhikkhunā bhikkhunīnaṁ ovādo na ṭhapetabbo.

English (brahmali, published): he’s impure in bodily conduct; he’s impure in verbal conduct; he’s impure in livelihood; he’s ignorant and incompetent; he’s incapable of answering properly when questioned.

English (brahmali, published): he’s improperly behaved by body; he’s improperly behaved by speech; he’s improperly behaved by body and speech; he abuses and reviles nuns; he socializes improperly with the nuns. 

pli-tv-pvr17:158.4

English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:161.3
Pali: Kāyikena anācārena samannāgato hoti, vācasikena anācārena samannāgato hoti, kāyikavācasikena anācārena samannāgato hoti, bhikkhunīnaṁ akkosakaparibhāsako hoti, bhikkhunīhi saddhiṁ saṁsaṭṭho viharati ananulomikena saṁsaggena—
English (brahmali, published): he’s improperly behaved by body; he’s improperly behaved by speech; he’s improperly behaved by body and speech; he abuses and reviles nuns; he socializes improperly with the nuns. 

pli-tv-pvr17:161.4

pli-tv-pvr17:169.0
Pali: 9. Ubbāhikavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 9. The subchapter on committees 

pli-tv-pvr17:169.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya na sammannitabbo”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What sort of monk shouldn’t be appointed to a committee?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:170.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya na sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:170.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:170.3
Pali: Na atthakusalo hoti, na dhammakusalo hoti, na niruttikusalo hoti, na byañjanakusalo hoti, na pubbāparakusalo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): he’s not skilled in the meaning, the Teaching, the terminology, the wording, or the right order. 

pli-tv-pvr17:170.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya na sammannitabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:170.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities may be appointed to a committee: 

pli-tv-pvr17:170.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:170.7
Pali: Atthakusalo hoti, dhammakusalo hoti, niruttikusalo hoti, byañjanakusalo hoti, pubbāparakusalo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): he’s skilled in the meaning, the Teaching, the terminology, the wording, and the right order. 

pli-tv-pvr17:170.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya sammannitabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:171.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya na sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who has five other qualities also shouldn’t be appointed to a committee: 

pli-tv-pvr17:171.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:171.3
Pali: Kodhano hoti kodhābhibhūto, makkhī hoti makkhābhibhūto, paḷāsī hoti paḷāsābhibhūto, issukī hoti issābhibhūto, sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he’s angry, overcome by anger; (2) he’s denigrating, overcome by denigration; (3) he’s domineering, overcome by being domineering; (4) he’s envious, overcome by envy; (5) he obstinately grasps his own views and only gives them up with difficulty. 

pli-tv-pvr17:171.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya na sammannitabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:171.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities may be appointed to a committee: 

pli-tv-pvr17:171.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:171.7
Pali: Na kodhano hoti na kodhābhibhūto, na makkhī hoti na makkhābhibhūto, na paḷāsī hoti na paḷāsābhibhūto, na issukī hoti na issābhibhūto, asandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti anādhānaggāhī suppaṭinissaggī—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he’s not angry or overcome by anger; (2) he’s not denigrating or overcome by denigration; (3) he’s not domineering or overcome by being domineering; (4) he’s not envious or overcome by envy; (5) he doesn’t obstinately grasp his own views and gives them up with ease. 

pli-tv-pvr17:171.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya sammannitabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:172.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya na sammannitabbo. 
English (brahmali, published): A monk who has five other qualities also shouldn’t be appointed to a committee: 

pli-tv-pvr17:172.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:172.3
Pali: Kuppati, byāpajjati, patiṭṭhiyati, kopaṁ janeti, akkhamo hoti apadakkhiṇaggāhī anusāsaniṁ—
English (brahmali, published): he (1) gets agitated, (2) has ill will, (3) becomes hardhearted, (4) gives rise to anger, and (5) is resistant and doesn’t receive instructions respectfully. 

pli-tv-pvr17:172.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya na sammannitabbo.

English (brahmali, published): he (1) doesn’t get agitated, (2) doesn’t have ill will, (3) doesn’t become hardhearted, (4) doesn’t give rise to anger, and (5) isn’t resistant but receives instructions respectfully. 

pli-tv-pvr17:172.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya sammannitabbo. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:173.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya na sammannitabbo.

pli-tv-pvr17:173.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:173.3
Pali: Pasāretā hoti no sāretā, anokāsakammaṁ kāretvā pavattā hoti, na yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā codetā hoti, na yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā kāretā hoti, na yathādiṭṭhiyā byākatā hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he causes confusion, not recollection; (2) he speaks without having gotten permission; (3) he doesn’t accuse others according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, or their offense; (4) he doesn’t act according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, or his offenses; (5) he doesn’t explain things according to his own view.

Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:173.7
Pali: Sāretā hoti no pasāretā, okāsakammaṁ kāretvā pavattā hoti, yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā codetā hoti, yathādhamme yathāvinaye yathāpattiyā kāretā hoti, yathādiṭṭhiyā byākatā hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he causes recollection, not confusion; (2) he speaks after getting permission; (3) he accuses others according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and their offense; (4) he acts according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and his offenses; (5) he explains things according to his own view. 

pli-tv-pvr17:173.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu ubbāhikāya sammannitabbo.

pli-tv-pvr17:176.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu bālotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchatī”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “What sort of monk is considered ignorant?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:177.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu bālotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.

pli-tv-pvr17:177.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:177.3
Pali: Suttaṁ na jānāti, suttānulomaṁ na jānāti, vinayaṁ na jānāti, vinayānulomaṁ na jānāti, na ca ṭhānāṭhānakusalo hoti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t know the Monastic Code; (2) he doesn’t know what’s in accordance with the Monastic Code; (3) he doesn’t know the Monastic Law; (4) he doesn’t know what’s in accordance with the Monastic Law; (5) he’s not skilled in what is and is not possible. 

pli-tv-pvr17:177.4

pli-tv-pvr17:178.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu paṇḍitotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.

pli-tv-pvr17:179.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu bālotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.

pli-tv-pvr17:179.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:179.3
Pali: Vatthuṁ na jānāti, nidānaṁ na jānāti, paññattiṁ na jānāti, padapaccābhaṭṭhaṁ na jānāti, anusandhivacanapathaṁ na jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he doesn’t know the actions that are the bases for offenses; (2) he doesn’t know the origin stories; (3) he doesn’t know the rules; (4) he doesn’t know the right order of words; (5) he doesn’t know the sequence of statements.

English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:179.5
Pali: Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu paṇḍitotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati. 
English (brahmali, published): But a monk who has five qualities is considered learned: 

pli-tv-pvr17:179.6
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:179.7
Pali: Vatthuṁ jānāti, nidānaṁ jānāti, paññattiṁ jānāti, padapaccābhaṭṭhaṁ jānāti, anusandhivacanapathaṁ jānāti—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he knows the actions that are the bases for offenses; (2) he knows the origin stories; (3) he knows the rules; (4) he knows the right order of words; (5) he knows the sequence of statements. 

pli-tv-pvr17:179.8
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu paṇḍitotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.

English:

Pali: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, saṅgharāji hoti no ca saṅghabhedo? 
English (brahmali, published): But how is there fracture in the Sangha, yet not schism? 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.3
Pali: Kittāvatā ca pana saṅgharāji ceva hoti saṅghabhedo cā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): And how is there both fracture and schism in the Sangha?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.4
Pali: “Paññattetaṁ, upāli, mayā āgantukānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ āgantukavattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): “(1) Upāli, I’ve laid down the proper conduct for newly-arrived monks. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.5
Pali: Evaṁ supaññatte kho, upāli, mayā sikkhāpade āgantukā bhikkhū āgantukavatte na vattanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Even though I’ve carefully laid down the training rules, the newly-arrived monks don’t practice that proper conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.6
Pali: Evampi kho, upāli, saṅgharāji hoti no ca saṅghabhedo. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, there’s fracture in the Sangha, but not schism. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.7
Pali: Paññattetaṁ, upāli, mayā āvāsikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ āvāsikavattaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (2) I’ve laid down the proper conduct for resident monks. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.8
Pali: Evaṁ supaññatte kho, upāli, mayā sikkhāpade āvāsikā bhikkhū āvāsikavatte na vattanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Even though I’ve carefully laid down the training rules, the resident monks don’t practice that proper conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.9
Pali: Evampi kho, upāli, saṅgharāji hoti no ca saṅghabhedo. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, there’s fracture in the Sangha, but not schism. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.10
Pali: Paññattetaṁ, upāli, mayā bhikkhūnaṁ bhattagge bhattaggavattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (3) I’ve laid down the proper conduct for monks in the dining hall: 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.11
Pali: yathāvuḍḍhaṁ yathārattaṁ yathāpatirūpaṁ aggāsanaṁ aggodakaṁ aggapiṇḍaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): the best seat, the best water, and the best almsfood is to be given out according to seniority and according to what’s proper. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.12
Pali: Evaṁ supaññatte kho, upāli, mayā sikkhāpade navā bhikkhū bhattagge therānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ āsanaṁ paṭibāhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Even though I’ve carefully laid down the training rules, the junior monks block the senior monks from seats. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.13
Pali: Evampi kho, upāli, saṅgharāji hoti no ca saṅghabhedo. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, there’s fracture in the Sangha, but not schism. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.14
Pali: Paññattetaṁ, upāli, mayā bhikkhūnaṁ senāsane senāsanavattaṁ—
English (brahmali, published): (4) I’ve laid down the proper conduct for the monks in regard to dwellings: 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.15
Pali: yathāvuḍḍhaṁ yathārattaṁ yathāpatirūpaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): they’re to be given out according to seniority and according to what’s proper. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.16
Pali: Evaṁ supaññatte kho, upāli, mayā sikkhāpade navā bhikkhū therānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ senāsanaṁ paṭibāhanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Even though I’ve carefully laid down the training rules, the junior monks block the senior monks from dwellings. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.17
Pali: Evampi kho, upāli, saṅgharāji hoti no ca saṅghabhedo. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, there’s fracture in the Sangha, but not schism. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.18
Pali: Paññattetaṁ, upāli, mayā bhikkhūnaṁ antosīmāya ekaṁ uposathaṁ ekaṁ pavāraṇaṁ ekaṁ saṅghakammaṁ ekaṁ kammākammaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): (5) For monks within the same monastery zone, I’ve laid down this: a joint observance-day ceremony; a joint invitation ceremony; joint legal procedures of the Sangha; joint legal procedures of whatever kind. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.19
Pali: Evaṁ supaññatte kho, upāli, mayā sikkhāpade tattheva antosīmāya āvenibhāvaṁ karitvā gaṇaṁ bandhitvā āveniṁ uposathaṁ karonti āveniṁ pavāraṇaṁ karonti āveniṁ saṅghakammaṁ karonti āveniṁ kammākammāni karonti. 
English (brahmali, published): Even though I’ve carefully laid down the training rules, they form a faction, a subgroup, right there within the monastery zone. They then do a separate observance-day ceremony, a separate invitation ceremony, separate legal procedures of the Sangha, or separate legal procedures of whatever kind. 

pli-tv-pvr17:197.20
Pali: Evaṁ kho, upāli, saṅgharāji ceva hoti saṅghabhedo cā”ti. 
English (brahmali, published): In this way, there’s both fracture and schism in the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:198.1
Pali: Adhikaraṇavūpasamavaggo niṭṭhito dasamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The tenth subchapter on the resolving of legal issues is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr17:199.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary: 

pli-tv-pvr17:200.1
Pali: Āpattiṁ adhikaraṇaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): “Offenses, legal issues, 

pli-tv-pvr17:200.2
Pali: chandā appassutena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Desire, and with ignorant; 

pli-tv-pvr17:200.3
Pali: Vatthuñca akusalo ca, 
English (brahmali, published): And the actions that are the bases, and unskilled, 

pli-tv-pvr17:200.4
Pali: puggalo āmisena ca; 
English (brahmali, published): Individual, and with worldly things; 

pli-tv-pvr17:200.5
Pali: Bhijjati saṅgharāji ca, 
English (brahmali, published): Schism, and fracture in the Sangha, 

pli-tv-pvr17:200.6
Pali: saṅghabhedo tatheva cāti. 
English (brahmali, published): And so too schism in the Sangha.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:201.0
Pali: 11. Saṅghabhedakavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 11. The subchapter on schism in the Sangha 

pli-tv-pvr17:201.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgato saṅghabhedako āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, what sort of monk who has caused a schism in the Sangha is irredeemably destined to an eon in hell?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:202.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato saṅghabhedako āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities, Upāli: 

pli-tv-pvr17:202.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:202.3
Pali: Idhupāli, bhikkhu adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti, avinayaṁ vinayoti dīpeti, vinayaṁ avinayoti dīpeti, vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ kammena—
English (brahmali, published): (1) a monk proclaims what’s contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it, (2) what’s in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it, (3) what’s contrary to the Monastic Law as being in accordance with it, (4) what’s in accordance with the Monastic Law as contrary to it, and (5) he misrepresents his view of what’s true during the legal procedure. 

pli-tv-pvr17:202.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato saṅghabhedako āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:203.1
Pali: Aparehipi, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato saṅghabhedako āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho. 
English (brahmali, published): When one who has caused a schism in the Sangha has five other qualities, he’s also irredeemably destined to an eon in hell: 

pli-tv-pvr17:203.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:203.3
Pali: Idhupāli, bhikkhu adhammaṁ dhammoti dīpeti, dhammaṁ adhammoti dīpeti, avinayaṁ vinayoti dīpeti, vinayaṁ avinayoti dīpeti, vinidhāya diṭṭhiṁ uddesena—
English (brahmali, published): (1) a monk proclaims what’s contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it, (2) what’s in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it, (3) what’s contrary to the Monastic Law as being in accordance with it, (4) what’s in accordance with the Monastic Law as contrary to it, and (5) he misrepresents his view of what’s true during the recitation. 

pli-tv-pvr17:203.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato saṅghabhedako āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.

English:

English (brahmali, published): (1) a monk proclaims what’s contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it, (2) what’s in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it, (3) what’s contrary to the Monastic Law as being in accordance with it, (4) what’s in accordance with the Monastic Law as contrary to it, and (5) he misrepresents his view of what’s true while he speaks.

pli-tv-pvr17:205.4

pli-tv-pvr17:213.0

pli-tv-pvr17:216.3

English (brahmali, published): (1) a monk proclaims what’s contrary to the Teaching as being in accordance with it, (2) what’s in accordance with the Teaching as contrary to it, (3) what’s contrary to the Monastic Law as being in accordance with it, (4) what’s in accordance with the Monastic Law as contrary to it, but (5) he doesn’t misrepresent his view of what’s true while he speaks. 

pli-tv-pvr17:216.4
Pali: Imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato saṅghabhedako na āpāyiko na nerayiko na kappaṭṭho na atekiccho.

pli-tv-pvr17:226.0
Pali: 13. Āvāsikavagga 
English (brahmali, published): 13. The subchapter on resident monks 

pli-tv-pvr17:226.1
Pali: “Katihi nu kho, bhante, aṅgehi samannāgato āvāsiko bhikkhu yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, what sort of resident monk is dumped in hell?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:227.1
Pali: “Pañcahupāli, aṅgehi samannāgato āvāsiko bhikkhu yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye. 
English (brahmali, published): “One who has five qualities: 

pli-tv-pvr17:227.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:227.3
Pali: Chandāgatiṁ gacchati, dosāgatiṁ gacchati, mohāgatiṁ gacchati, bhayāgatiṁ gacchati, saṅghikaṁ puggalikaparibhogena paribhuñjati—
English (brahmali, published): he’s biased by desire, ill will, confusion, or fear, and he uses what belongs to the Sangha as if belonging to an individual. 

pli-tv-pvr17:227.4
Pali: imehi kho, upāli, pañcahaṅgehi samannāgato āvāsiko bhikkhu yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.

pli-tv-pvr17:228.2
Pali: Katamehi pañcahi? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:228.3

pli-tv-pvr17:240.0
Pali: 14. Kathinatthāravagga 
English (brahmali, published): 14. The subchapter on the robe-making ceremony 

pli-tv-pvr17:240.1
Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, ānisaṁsā kathinatthāre”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, how many benefits are there in participating in the robe-making ceremony?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:241.1
Pali: “Pañcime, upāli, ānisaṁsā kathinatthāre. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are five benefits, Upāli: 

pli-tv-pvr17:241.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:241.3
Pali: Anāmantacāro, asamādānacāro, gaṇabhojanaṁ, yāvadatthacīvaraṁ, yo ca tattha cīvaruppādo so nesaṁ bhavissati—
English (brahmali, published): (1) going without informing; (2) going without taking; (3) eating in a group; (4) as much extra robe-cloth as you need; and (5) whatever robe-cloth is given there is for you.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:241.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca ānisaṁsā kathinatthāre”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:242.1
Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, ādīnavā muṭṭhassatissa asampajānassa niddaṁ okkamato”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many drawbacks are there in falling asleep absentminded and heedless?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:243.1
Pali: “Pañcime, upāli, ādīnavā muṭṭhassatissa asampajānassa niddaṁ okkamato. 
English (brahmali, published): “There are these five drawbacks: 

pli-tv-pvr17:243.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:243.3
Pali: Dukkhaṁ supati, dukkhaṁ paṭibujjhati, pāpakaṁ supinaṁ passati, devatā na rakkhanti, asuci muccati—
English (brahmali, published): you don’t sleep well; you wake up feeling miserable; you have nightmares; the gods don’t guard you; you emit semen. 

pli-tv-pvr17:243.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca ādīnavā muṭṭhassatissa asampajānassa niddaṁ okkamato. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:244.1
Pali: Pañcime, upāli, ānisaṁsā upaṭṭhitassatissa sampajānassa niddaṁ okkamato. 
English (brahmali, published): But there are five benefits in falling asleep mindfully, with clear awareness: 

pli-tv-pvr17:244.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:244.3
Pali: Sukhaṁ supati, sukhaṁ paṭibujjhati, na pāpakaṁ supinaṁ passati, devatā rakkhanti, asuci na muccati—
English (brahmali, published): you sleep well; you wake up feeling good; you don’t have nightmares; the gods guard you; you don’t emit semen.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:244.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca ānisaṁsā upaṭṭhitassatissa sampajānassa niddaṁ okkamato”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:245.1
Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, avandiyā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “How many kinds of people should one not pay respect to?”

Pali: “Pañcime, upāli, avandiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These five: 

pli-tv-pvr17:246.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:246.3
Pali: Antaragharaṁ paviṭṭho avandiyo, racchagato avandiyo, otamasiko avandiyo, asamannāharanto avandiyo, sutto avandiyo—
English (brahmali, published): one who has entered an inhabited area; one who’s walking along a street; one who’s in the dark; one who’s not paying attention; one who’s asleep. 

pli-tv-pvr17:246.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca avandiyā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:247.1
Pali: Aparepi, upāli, pañca avandiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five other kinds of people you also shouldn’t pay respect to: 

pli-tv-pvr17:247.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:247.3
Pali: Yāgupāne avandiyo, bhattagge avandiyo, ekāvatto avandiyo, aññavihito avandiyo, naggo avandiyo—
English (brahmali, published): one who’s drinking congee; one who’s in the dining hall; one who has turned away; one who’s preoccupied with something else; one who’s naked. 

pli-tv-pvr17:247.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca avandiyā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:248.1
Pali: Aparepi, upāli, pañca avandiyā.

pli-tv-pvr17:248.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:248.3
Pali: Khādanto avandiyo, bhuñjanto avandiyo, uccāraṁ karonto avandiyo, passāvaṁ karonto avandiyo, ukkhittako avandiyo—
English (brahmali, published): one who’s eating fresh food; one who’s eating cooked food; one who’s defecating; one who’s urinating; one who has been ejected. 

pli-tv-pvr17:248.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca avandiyā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:249.1
Pali: Aparepi, upāli, pañca avandiyā.

pli-tv-pvr17:249.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:249.3
Pali: Pure upasampannena pacchā upasampanno avandiyo, anupasampanno avandiyo, nānāsaṁvāsako vuḍḍhataro adhammavādī avandiyo, mātugāmo avandiyo, paṇḍako avandiyo—
English (brahmali, published): (1) one who has been given the full ordination after you; (2) one who isn’t fully ordained; (3) one who belongs to a different Buddhist sect who’s senior to you, but who speaks contrary to the Teaching; (4) a woman; (5) a <i lang='pi' translate='no'>paṇḍaka</i>. 

pli-tv-pvr17:249.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca avandiyā. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:250.1
Pali: Aparepi, upāli, pañca avandiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are five other kinds of people you also shouldn’t pay respect to: 

pli-tv-pvr17:250.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:250.3
Pali: Pārivāsiko avandiyo, mūlāyapaṭikassanāraho avandiyo, mānattāraho avandiyo, mānattacāriko avandiyo, abbhānāraho avandiyo—
English (brahmali, published): (1) one who’s on probation; (2) one who deserves to be sent back to the beginning; (3) one who deserves the trial period; (4) one who’s undertaking the trial period; (5) one who deserves rehabilitation.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:250.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca avandiyā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:251.1
Pali: “Kati nu kho, bhante, vandiyā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “But how many kinds of people should one pay respect to?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:252.1
Pali: “Pañcime, upāli, vandiyā. 
English (brahmali, published): “These five: 

pli-tv-pvr17:252.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:252.3
Pali: Pacchā upasampannena pure upasampanno vandiyo, nānāsaṁvāsako vuḍḍhataro dhammavādī vandiyo, ācariyo vandiyo, upajjhāyo vandiyo, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya tathāgato arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vandiyo—
English (brahmali, published): (1) one who has been given the full ordination before you; (2) one who belongs to a different Buddhist sect who’s senior to you and who speaks in accordance with the Teaching; (3) your teacher; (4) your preceptor; and (5) in this world with its gods, lords of death, and supreme beings, in this society with its monastics and brahmins, its gods and humans, you should pay respect to the Buddha, perfected and fully awakened.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:252.4
Pali: ime kho, upāli, pañca vandiyā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:253.1
Pali: “Navakatarena, bhante, bhikkhunā vuḍḍhatarassa bhikkhuno pāde vandantena kati dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā pādā vanditabbā”ti? 
English (brahmali, published): “Sir, when a monk is paying respect to a more senior monk, how many qualities should he first set up in himself?” 

pli-tv-pvr17:254.1
Pali: “Navakatarenupāli, bhikkhunā vuḍḍhatarassa bhikkhuno pāde vandantena pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā pādā vanditabbā. 
English (brahmali, published): “He should set up five qualities in himself: 

pli-tv-pvr17:254.2
Pali: Katame pañca? 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:254.3
Pali: Navakatarenupāli, bhikkhunā vuḍḍhatarassa bhikkhuno pāde vandantena ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā, añjaliṁ paggahetvā, ubhohi pāṇitalehi pādāni parisambāhantena, pemañca gāravañca upaṭṭhāpetvā pādā vanditabbā—
English (brahmali, published): (1) he should arrange his upper robe over one shoulder; (2) he should raise his joined palms; (3) he should stroke the feet with the palms of both hands; (4) he should set up a sense of affection; (5) he should set up a sense of respect.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:254.4
Pali: navakatarenupāli bhikkhunā vuḍḍhatarassa bhikkhuno pāde vandantena ime pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā pādā vanditabbā”ti. 
English: 

pli-tv-pvr17:255.1
Pali: Kathinatthāravaggo niṭṭhito cuddasamo. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourteenth subchapter on the robe-making ceremony is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr17:256.1
Pali: Tassuddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary:

Pali: Kathinatthāraniddā ca, 
English (brahmali, published): “The robe-making ceremony, and asleep,

English (brahmali, published): Inhabited, congee, when eating fresh food; 

pli-tv-pvr17:257.3
Pali: Pure ca pārivāsi ca, 
English (brahmali, published): And before, and one on probation, 

pli-tv-pvr17:257.4
Pali: vandiyo vanditabbakanti. 
English (brahmali, published): Should pay respect to, to be paid respect.” 

pli-tv-pvr17:258.1
Pali: Upālipañcakaṁ niṭṭhitaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): Ven. Upāli questions the Buddha is finished. 

pli-tv-pvr17:259.1
Pali: Tesaṁ vaggānaṁ uddānaṁ 
English (brahmali, published): This is the summary of these subchapters:

English (brahmali, published): “Without formal support, and legal procedure,

Pali: vohārāvikammena ca;

English (brahmali, published): And accusing, and ascetic practices,

Pali: musā bhikkhunimeva ca. 
English (brahmali, published): Lying, and nun.

Pali: Ubbāhikādhikaraṇaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): Committee, legal issue, 

pli-tv-pvr17:261.2
Pali: bhedakā pañcamā pure; 
English (brahmali, published): Schism, the fifth before; 

pli-tv-pvr17:261.3
Pali: Āvāsikā kathinañca, 
English (brahmali, published): Resident monks, and robe-making ceremony—

pli-tv-pvr17:261.4
Pali: cuddasā suppakāsitāti. 
English (brahmali, published): The fourteen have been well proclaimed.”

Pali: 1. Pārājika 
English (brahmali, published): 1. The offenses entailing expulsion 

pli-tv-pvr18:1.1
Pali: Atthāpatti acittako āpajjati, sacittako vuṭṭhāti. 
English (brahmali, published): There are offenses that are committed unintentionally, but cleared intentionally.

English (brahmali, published): There are offenses that are committed intentionally, but cleared unintentionally.

pli-tv-pvr19
Root: root/pli/ms/vinaya/pli-tv-pvr/pli-tv-pvr19_root-pli-ms.json

English translation sources:
  - brahmali [published]: translation/en/brahmali/vinaya/pli-tv-pvr/pli-tv-pvr19_translation-en-brahmali.json

pli-tv-pvr19:0.1
Pali: Parivāra 
English (brahmali, published): The Compendium 

pli-tv-pvr19:0.2
Pali: 19. Dutiyagāthāsaṅgaṇika 
English (brahmali, published): Verses on offenses, training rules, and legal procedures 

pli-tv-pvr19:0.3
Pali: 1. Kāyikādiāpatti 
English (brahmali, published): 1. Offenses by body, etc. 

pli-tv-pvr19:1.1
Pali: Kati āpattiyo kāyikā, 
English (brahmali, published): “How many kinds of offenses are there by body? 

pli-tv-pvr19:1.2
Pali: Kati vācasikā katā; 
English (brahmali, published): How many are done by speech? 

pli-tv-pvr19:1.3
Pali: Chādentassa kati āpattiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses are there for concealing? 

pli-tv-pvr19:1.4
Pali: Kati saṁsaggapaccayā. 
English (brahmali, published): How many because of contact? 

pli-tv-pvr19:2.1
Pali: Chāpattiyo kāyikā, 
English (brahmali, published): There are six kinds of offenses by body, 

pli-tv-pvr19:2.2
Pali: Cha vācasikā katā; 
English (brahmali, published): Six are done by speech; 

pli-tv-pvr19:2.3
Pali: Chādentassa tisso āpattiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of offenses for concealing, 

pli-tv-pvr19:2.4
Pali: Pañca saṁsaggapaccayā. 
English (brahmali, published): Five because of contact. 

pli-tv-pvr19:3.1
Pali: Aruṇugge kati āpattiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses are there at dawn? 

pli-tv-pvr19:3.2
Pali: Kati yāvatatiyakā; 
English (brahmali, published): How many after the third announcement? 

pli-tv-pvr19:3.3
Pali: Katettha aṭṭha vatthukā, 
English (brahmali, published): How many here have eight parts? 

pli-tv-pvr19:3.4
Pali: Katihi sabbasaṅgaho. 
English (brahmali, published): Through how many are they all included? 

pli-tv-pvr19:4.1
Pali: Aruṇugge tisso āpattiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of offenses at dawn, 

pli-tv-pvr19:4.2
Pali: Dve yāvatatiyakā; 
English (brahmali, published): Two after the third announcement; 

pli-tv-pvr19:4.3
Pali: Ekettha aṭṭha vatthukā, 
English (brahmali, published): One here has eight parts, 

pli-tv-pvr19:4.4
Pali: Ekena sabbasaṅgaho. 
English (brahmali, published): They are all included through one. 

pli-tv-pvr19:5.1
Pali: Vinayassa kati mūlāni, 
English (brahmali, published): How many roots are there of the Monastic Law, 

pli-tv-pvr19:5.2
Pali: Yāni buddhena paññattā; 
English (brahmali, published): Which were laid down by the Buddha? 

pli-tv-pvr19:5.3
Pali: Vinayagarukā kati vuttā, 
English (brahmali, published): How many are said to be heavy in the Monastic Law? 

pli-tv-pvr19:5.4
Pali: Duṭṭhullacchādanā kati. 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of concealing are there of grave offenses? 

pli-tv-pvr19:6.1
Pali: Vinayassa dve mūlāni, 
English (brahmali, published): There are two roots of the Monastic Law, 

pli-tv-pvr19:6.2
Pali: yāni buddhena paññattā; 
English (brahmali, published): Which were laid down by the Buddha; 

pli-tv-pvr19:6.3
Pali: Vinayagarukā dve vuttā, 
English (brahmali, published): Two are said to be heavy in the Monastic Law, 

pli-tv-pvr19:6.4
Pali: dve duṭṭhullacchādanā. 
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of concealing of grave offenses. 

pli-tv-pvr19:7.1
Pali: Gāmantare kati āpattiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses are there concerning the next inhabited area? 

pli-tv-pvr19:7.2
Pali: Kati nadipārapaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): How many because of crossing a river? 

pli-tv-pvr19:7.3
Pali: Katimaṁsesu thullaccayaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): How many serious offenses are there in relation to meat? 

pli-tv-pvr19:7.4
Pali: Katimaṁsesu dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): How many offenses of wrong conduct are there in relation to meat? 

pli-tv-pvr19:8.1
Pali: Gāmantare catasso āpattiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): There are four kinds of offenses concerning the next inhabited area, 

pli-tv-pvr19:8.2
Pali: Catasso nadipārapaccayā; 
English (brahmali, published): Four because of crossing a river; 

pli-tv-pvr19:8.3
Pali: Ekamaṁse thullaccayaṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): There is one serious offense in relation to meat, 

pli-tv-pvr19:8.4
Pali: Navamaṁsesu dukkaṭaṁ. 
English (brahmali, published): And nine offenses of wrong conduct. 

pli-tv-pvr19:9.1
Pali: Kati vācasikā rattiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses are there by speech at night? 

pli-tv-pvr19:9.2
Pali: Kati vācasikā divā; 
English (brahmali, published): How many are there by speech by day? 

pli-tv-pvr19:9.3
Pali: Dadamānassa kati āpattiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): How many kinds of offenses are there for one who’s giving? 

pli-tv-pvr19:9.4
Pali: Paṭiggaṇhantassa kittakā. 
English (brahmali, published): And how many for one who’s taking? 

pli-tv-pvr19:10.1
Pali: Dve vācasikā rattiṁ, 
English (brahmali, published): There are two kinds of offenses by speech at night, 

pli-tv-pvr19:10.2
Pali: Dve vācasikā divā; 
English (brahmali, published): Two by speech by day; 

pli-tv-pvr19:10.3
Pali: Dadamānassa tisso āpattiyo, 
English (brahmali, published): There are three kinds of offenses for one who is giving, 

pli-tv-pvr19:10.4
Pali: Cattāro ca paṭiggahe.

Pali: Kāyikā vācasikā ca, 
English (brahmali, published): By body, and by speech, 

pli-tv-pvr20:50.2
Pali: tissitthī cāpi mātaraṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): And three kinds of women, mother; 

pli-tv-pvr20:50.3
Pali: Kuddho ārādhako tuṭṭho, 
English (brahmali, published): An angry person who is approved of, contented, 

pli-tv-pvr20:50.4
Pali: saṅghādisesā ca ubho. 
English (brahmali, published): And an offense entailing suspension, both. 

pli-tv-pvr20:51.1
Pali: Akappakataṁ na deti, 
English (brahmali, published): Not marked, she neither gives, 

pli-tv-pvr20:51.2
Pali: Na detāpajjatī garuṁ; 
English (brahmali, published): She neither gives, one commits a heavy offense—

pli-tv-pvr20:51.3
Pali: Sedamocanikā gāthā, 
English (brahmali, published): The sudorific verses: 

pli-tv-pvr20:51.4
Pali: Pañhā viññūhi vibhāvitāti. 
English (brahmali, published): Questions explained by the wise.”
